AVEVA™ Engineering
Contents

Page 109
Welcome to AVEVA Engineering 
Welcome to the AVEVA Engineering documentation! 
This publication contains comprehensive information about AVEVA Engineering. It does not, however, contain 
information about individual applications AVEVA Engineering works with. You must refer to the respective 
components’ documentation for information about the individual applications. 
This publication is primarily intended for multi-disciplinary teams of engineers to work effectively together to 
develop and maintain the detailed definition of all the key engineering items involved in plant or marine 
projects. 
As projects progress, engineers build a database of its engineering objects, such as lines, equipment and valves, 
and all their key attributes. This object data is stored and managed in the project information model as part of 
the Digital Asset, enabling its integration with schematic and 3D design data and supporting global project 
collaboration where required. 
This publication assumes that you have access to AVEVA Licensing System (ALS) and the appropriate license to 
install AVEVA Engineering. Further, this publication assumes that the administrators know their organization's 
requirements and are familiar with the set up of your work environment in AVEVA Administration 
We hope that you will find this publication useful and that it answers all your queries about AVEVA Engineering.

AVEVA™ Engineering
Welcome to AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 111
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering 
AVEVA Engineering provides the tools to to develop and maintain the detailed definition of all the key 
engineering items involved in an Engineering project. 
Common Functionality 
Describes functionality, which is common to several disciplines within Engineering. 
Model is the main, graphically driven constructor module. It enables a full sized three dimensional model to be 
defined in the design database, with selected views of the current state of the design shown on the graphics 
screen as the design progresses. 
All parts of the design can be created. Component selection is provided through Specifications that dictate which 
Catalog Components can be used. Each part of the design model can be displayed in colour-shaded solid colour-
coded representations for ease of interpretation. 
The Engineering module can check for interferences (clashes) between items created in the design. There is a 
very flexible reporting capability that can be used to produce a wide variety of design documents - from bulk 
Material Take Off to detailed nozzle schedules. 
Quick Access Toolbar 
Options on the tabs and the Project menu can be added to the Quick Access Toolbar for quick and convenient 
access. 
By default, the toolbar includes the following options. 
Save Work 
Used to save any changes made to data in the module. 
Get Work 
Used to refresh data in the module with any changes 
that other users have made since the module was 
opened. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 112
Session Comment 
Used to save any comments made during the work 
session. 
Use the Savework/Session Comment window to 
record the comment. 
If no changes have been made during the session, a 
message displays at the top of the window. 
Reset Cell Feedback Colours and Tooltips (F7) 
Used to reset feedback colours and tooltips. 
Undo 
Undoes the last change made in the module. 
Multiple changes can be undone, each subsequent 
undo rolls back the previous change. 
Changes made prior to the last save cannot be 
undone. 
Redo 
Reverses the last undo made in the module. 
Multiple undoes can be reversed. 
To add a tab or menu option to the toolbar, right-click on it and select the Add to Quick Access Toolbar option 
from the menu that is then displayed. 
The option is then added to the toolbar. 
By default, the toolbar is located above the tabs. If required it can be moved below the tabs by selecting the 
Show Quick Access Toolbar Below the Ribbon option from the right-click menu, or the Show Below the Ribbon 
option from the Customise Quick Access Toolbar menu displayed by clicking the arrow at the end of the toolbar. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 113
Once the toolbar is below the ribbon, the menu options become Show Quick Access Toolbar Above the Ribbon 
and Show Above the Ribbon, and are used to move the toolbar back to its original position. 
The Customise Quick Access Toolbar menu also includes options for removing and returning the default buttons 
from and to the toolbar. 
Messages and Message Log 
As actions are carried out on data in AVEVA Engineering, messages relating to these actions, such as whether 
they have succeeded or failed, are displayed in the status bar. A record of all the messages issued in a session can 
be viewed in the Message Log. 
Messages in the Status Bar 
When a user carries out, or attempts to carry out such actions as claiming and releasing, undoing and redoing, 
copying records, refreshing grid data and so on, messages indicating the success or failure of the actions are 
issued by the application. 
Message Log 
The Message Log displays all messages issued during the current session (unless the log has been cleared, as 
explained below). To display the log, click on the message text in the status bar, the Message Log window is 
displayed above the status bar. 
Command Window 
The Command Window is used for typing standard commands and executing Programmable Macro Language 
(PML) functions and is displayed above the status bar. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 114
Grids Explorer 
Provides an easy to use graphical method for exploring the grids database. The Grids Explorer displays the grids 
database elements in a hierarchy. Click on an element in the tree view to navigate to the element in the database 
and make it the current element. 
To expand an element hierarchy, click  to the left of the element name, to contract an expanded element, click 
to the left of the element name. 
The diagram illustrates the grids database hierarchy and the relationship between grid elements. 
The uppermost element is the World element, the principal elements are the owning Category element and 
member Grid element. A category can contain one or more Grids and Categories. 
Categories 
To create and modify Category elements in the Grids database, right-click a Category element to display a drop-
down menu. 
New 
Category 
Creates a new Category element below the currently 
selected Category element. 
Grid 
Creates a new Grid element below the currently 
selected Category element. 
Properties 
Edits the currently selected Category element. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 115
Delete 
Deletes the currently selected Category element. 
Grids 
To create and modify Grid elements in the Grids database, right-click a Grid element to display a drop-down 
menu. 
Open 
Opens the currently selected Grid element. 
Properties 
Edits the currently selected Grid element. 
Delete 
Deletes the currently selected Grid element. 
Delete 
Copies the currently selected Grid element. 
Refer to Create or Edit Category, Create Grid, Copy Selected Grid, Delete Category, Delete Grid, Edit Grid and 
Open Grid for further information. 
Autonaming Setup 
Use the Name Settings window to define sets of rules which can be used to allocate logical names automatically 
to newly created elements of specified types. 
Naming Settings 
Auto Naming On/Off 
Click to select the check box to 
switch the Auto naming 
functionality on. By default, the 
functionality is not switched on. 
Define Naming Rules 
Displays the Naming Rules window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 116
Using User defined naming rules 
The statement indicates which set 
of rules has been opened for 
example: User defined, Project 
defined or System defined. 
Naming Mode 
Infill 
Use the numbers made available by 
deleting elements, therefore the 
number used for a new element is 
the lowest available. 
Append 
Add all elements to the end of the 
numbering scheme. 
Naming Rules Window 
Use the functionality available from the Naming Rules window to define or modify autonaming rules. 
Data Input 
Key 
Displays the current autonaming rule 
Scan Level 
Use to determine the top level in the hierarchy for the 
element rule must start from, for example: world, 
dept, reg. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 117
Rule Name 
Populated with the name of the rule which is used as 
a reference to its respective element rule. 
To change the rule name, click to select the Rule 
Name drop-down list, select a rule name from the 
displayed list. 
Element Rules 
Displays the Define Rules window. 
Description 
Populated with the description of the autonaming 
rule. 
Format 
Use to define the format for the name (PML 
expression can be used to call a function or method, 
which can then ascertain attributes from the drawing 
to use in the construction of the name). 
Indices 
Use to assign the maximum number of significant 
figures to the autonaming rule. 
Cut 
Removes the currently selected naming rule from the 
list. 
Copy 
Copies the currently selected naming rule, the Naming 
Rules window is then populated with its properties. 
Replace 
Replaces the currently selected naming rule with the 
modified version. 
Insert 
Places a new naming rule into the current list, the 
naming rule can be placed then be placed before or 
after the currently selected one. 
Data Display 
Key field 
Displays the currently selected autonaming rules. 
Data files 
Displays all the attributes for the selected rule. 
Test 
Test the naming rule on the currently selected 
element, if successful it returns a constructed name. If 
the rule is not valid to the element then a warning 
window is displayed. 
Use Rule 
The rule attribute in the element rule is tested on the 
currently selected element. 
Define Rules Window 
For use by a system administrator, use the functionality available from the Define Rules window to add new 
element rules to all locations (users, project or default) or modify existing element rules located in the users, 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 118
project or default area. 
The Define Rules window is multi functional, enter new details or modify details which automatically populate 
the window once selected. 
Element Rules 
Definition 
Location 
Select the area where the element rules are located 
(user, project or default). 
Name 
Enter a new name or modify the existing name of the 
element rule. 
Types 
Enter a new type or modify the existing type of the 
type of the element rule. 
Rule 
Enter a new rule or modify the existing rule. 
Description 
Enter a new description or modify the existing 
description. 
Include 
Includes the element rule in the selected area. 
Replace 
Replaces the element rule in the selected area. 
Remove 
Removes the element rule in the selected area. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 119
Defined 
The Defined part of the Define Rules window is 
populated with the element rules from all the 
locations. 
Database Views 
The Database Views functionality available in the Configuration module provides the administrator with the 
ability to map element data into sets of related tables (Database View Sets). The database view sets are 
populated with rows that exist in a table for each element that meets specified criteria. The columns are derived 
from attributes, PML 1 or XPath1.0 expressions. Attributes and expressions for the columns are specified from 
the elements defined in the view definition (that is from multiple elements not just the row element). 
Database Views are managed in Definition Sets, these include: 
• Element View - Database views defined automatically to represent the database hierarchy. 
• Project Views - Database views defined in the dictionary data bases for a project. 
• User Views - Database views defined as a file in the users project settings. 
It is advised to create Database Views as User Views with the Database Views Editor in the relevant module. 
If the editor is not available it can be added with the customization command Aveva.Core.Database View. The 
customization command allows the opportunity to test the view as a user view with full data access. 
Once a view is stable, it is easily copied with the editor from the User Views to the Project Views which adds it to 
the Dictionary database definitions. 
Create Administrative Elements 
The administrative elements must be created first, make sure that the correct element in the dictionary 
hierarchy is selected, right-click and select Create from the drop-down menu, then select the required database 
element. 
Database View World Element 
Database View World (DBVWWL) element - the DBVWWL element must be located in the database for the 
master databases requiring the view. Use the New Database View World window to create a new database view 
world element in the Database View Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with default attributes for the element, enter a name, lock and 
description attributes for the new database view world element. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 120
Database View Group Element 
Database View Group (DBVGR) element - is used to manage definitions. Use the New Database View Group
window to create a new database view group element in the Database View Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with default attributes for the element, enter a name, lock and 
description attributes for the new database view group element. 
Database View Set Element 
Database View Set (DBVSE) element - is a storage area for views that are used for the same purpose or 
workflow. Use the New Database View Set window to create a new database view set element in the Database 
View Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with default attributes for the element, enter a name, lock and 
description attributes for the new database view set element. 
Database View Element 
A database view defines a table definition for the display of element data. The data can be derived from a single 
element or multiple elements. Make sure that the correct element in the Database View Explorer is selected. 
Use the Database View window to create a new database view element in the Database View element in the 
Database Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with the default attributes, enter a name, lock, description, user-
defined name, element types, database view sets, auto creation and allow definition attributes for the new 
database view element. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 121
Beneath the database view element, the hierarchy can include an attribute column, attribute filter, create rules, 
expression column, expression filter and source element elements. 
Attribute Column (ATTCOLUMN) Element 
The ATTCOLUMN element defines the attribute column display in the database view, the columns operate on the 
SRCELEMENT that contains them. 
Attribute Filter (ATTFILTER) Element 
Rows are present in the resulting Database View table for each element that has a particular element type (ELEL) 
and meets any defined criteria (ATTFILTER and EXPFILTER). The Attribute Filter element is used to define 
matching criteria. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 122
Create Rule (CRERULES) Element 
A CRERULES element defines the rules for the creation of the context element. If owned by a DBVW element, 
the rules define row element creation, if owned by a SCRELEMENT the rules define column element creation. 
Expression Column (EXPCOLUMN) Element 
A EXPCOLUMN element defines a column in the database view is based on an expression result, by default these 
columns are read only. 
Expression Filter (EXPFILTER) Element 
The Expression Filter element is used to define matching criteria, rows are present in the resulting Database 
View table for each element that has a particular element type (ELEL) and meets any defined criteria (ATTFILTER 
and EXPFILTER). Additionally if element match criteria cannot be expressed using attribute filters then it is 
possible to define an expression for evaluation. All EXPFILTER criteria must be met for a row to be present in the 
table. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 123
Source Element (SCRELEMENT) 
If attributes or expressions from other elements are needed, a source element must be created. A Source 
Element defines a step from the current context to another element from which column values can be derived. 
The equivalent in terms of the data display: 
• Option 1: use of expression columns 
• EXPCOL ( Expression : NAME OF OWNER ) 
• EXPCOL ( Expression : TEMP OF OWNER ) 
• Option 2: use of attribute columns 
• SRCELE ( Expression : OWNER ) 
• ATTCOL ( Database Attribute : NAME ) 
• ATTCOL ( Database Attribute : TEMP ) 
The difference is that the columns inside the SRCELEMENT are editable and allows the units of measure for 
display to be chosen. The columns based on expressions are Read only and do not allow the end user to choose 
units of measure for display. These can report errors if they contain UDAs that do not have their default values 
configured. 
Database Views Editor 
The Database Views Editor is available in Lexicon, AVEVA Engineering and AVEVA Engineering Configuration (for 
AVEVA Administrator users) and can be added using the customization menus to all other modules. 
The Database Views Editor provides quick access to all the tasks associated with Database View definitions. All 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 124
Database Views Editor functionality is provided through selections made by the administrator in the 
Configuration module. 
By default the Database Views Editor is populated with the Project Views which are the Database Views defined 
in the Dictionary databases. 
View Definition Set 
Use the View Definition Set part of the Database Views Editor to select and manage the source for the Database 
View definitions being modified. By default there are three Definition Sets available for selection from the drop-
down menu. 
Element Views 
A read-only set of Database Views which are 
automatically generated from the database 
definitions. A default Database View is provided for 
every system defined element type and User defined 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 125
element UDET for each database type it is allowed in. 
Project Views 
All of the Database Views that exist in the Lexicon 
hierarchy. 
User Views 
The Database Views for the current project as defined 
for this user, these views are stored in 
%AVEVA_DESIGN_USER%. 
Additional Definition Sets can be created or added into the list with the use of the toolbar provided. 
Toolbar 
The View Definition Set toolbar provides the ability to manage the Definition Sets available for selection from 
the drop-down list. Use the functionality available to create, load or delete any file based definitions. 
Open 
Displays an Open window, navigate to and open a 
Definition Set. 
Close 
Closes the currently selected file based Definition Set, 
the default Definitions Sets cannot be closed. 
New 
Displays the Create Definition Set window, use the 
functionality available to create a new Definition Set. 
Delete 
Deletes the currently selected Definition Set from the 
file system and removes it from the list. 
Save 
Saves the currently selected file based Definition Set. 
If required, select from the drop-down menu which View Definition Set to use. 
Create Definition Set 
To create a Definition Set, from the View Definition Set toolbar, select New to display the Create Definition Set 
window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 126
Enter a Name and Description for the new Definition Set. 
The location of the definition file must now be identified, click the icon next to the Filename field to display the 
Save Definition File As window. 
Navigate to the required location, name the file and click Save, or click Cancel to return to the Create Definition 
Set window without saving the file. 
The Filename field in the Create Definition Set window is automatically populated. 
Views 
The Views part of the Database View Editor window displays the Database Views and Database View Sets in 
the selected Definition Set, subject to the criteria selected from the Database Types Filter. The filter restricts the 
information that displays in the Views tab, for example: only the information relevant for the selected database 
types display. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

When a Database View or Database View Set is selected from the View tab, the View Properties, Data Sources, 
Columns and a Preview display in the View Detail on the right hand side of the Database Views Editor. Each 
time a new Database Views or Database View Sets is selected, the information in the View Detail is 
automatically updated to reflect the change.

Use the right-click menu on the View tab to manage the Database Views and Database View Sets:

New > View

context.

View Set Detail 
If a Database View Set is selected in the Database Views Editor then View Detail displays. If the definition can 
be modified, the display name and the description of the Database View Set can also be modified. 
A Database View Set is a set of Database Views that have been defined for a related purpose and can be used to 
group Database Views for easy navigation. A Database View belongs to a Database View Set if it is a member of 
the Database View Set definition or if its DBVWSTREF attribute refers to the Database View Set. 
Note: 
The DBVWSTREF attribute is not editable when the Database Views Editor is being used. 
If required the display name, description and element type of the Database View Set can be modified. 
User-defined name 
The display name for the Database View Set, the 
name needs to be unique. The display name is 
situated at the top of the View Set Detail by the 
padlock for easy reference. 
Description 
A description of the Database View Set. 
Element types 
Displays the element types included in the database 
view set, click Browse to display the Select element 
type window. 
View Detail 
If a Database View is selected, from the Views in the Database Views Editor, View Detail displays. The Database 
View can be modified with limitations: 
• Editing of Column Relations and Test Expressions are not supported 
• Editing of the DBVWSTREF attribute is not supported 
The View Detail part of the Database Views Editor window displays four tabs populated with: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 131
View Properties 
Supports modification of the primary attributes of a Database View to define the display name and the elements 
that the Database View rows are based on. 
Data Sources 
Defines rules for database element creation and navigation steps to additional information elements such as an 
items Catalog specification. 
Columns 
The columns display each Data Source, these can be attribute values or expressions evaluated on the selected 
Data Source. 
Preview 
A grid displaying column data defined by the Database View. The grid only displays data that the current user 
has access to. By default, data access is restricted in Lexicon, it appears as an empty grid for most element types. 
View Properties 
The View Properties tab is populated with the information which defines the display name and description for 
the Database View. As well as the element selection criteria for the data rows. By default, the View Properties 
tab is selected. 
If required, modify the primary attributes of a Database View and the elements that the Database View rows 
are based on. 
User-defined name 
The display name for the Database View, the name 
needs to be unique or the Database View is listed 
with a derived name of the form Tablen. The display 
name is situated at the top of the View Detail by the 
padlock for easy reference 
Description 
A description of the Database View. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 132
Element types 
The types of elements to include in the Database 
View. 
Attribute Filters 
Attribute filters restrict the display content in the Database View to only the elements that meet the stated 
criteria. For a filter to be active it must have the Attribute Operator and Value columns completed. All the 
elements that meet the criteria of the Attribute filters display. 
Pseudo attributes and hidden attributes do not display in the right click drop-down menu on the Attribute 
column but can be input in the Attribute cell. 
Expression Filters 
Expression filters restrict the display content in the Database Views to only the elements that meet the stated 
criteria. For an expression filter to be active it must have a valid expression that evaluates to a Boolean/LOGICAL 
result. All the elements that meet the criteria of the Expression filters display. 
Note: 
Expression filters can be used with global variables (created in Lexicon while creating a Database DB view) to 
filter grids in Engineer, thus reducing the number of DB views required. Once the DB view is selected the global 
variables for the selected DB view are automatically populated. Enter the Filter Value for the Filter Variable 
Name, these will be saved as part of the grid definition. 
Data Sources 
Data Sources are elements that data columns can be based on. An Attribute column based on the Database 
View (DBVW) Data Source displays the value of the attribute from the elements selected by the view definition. 
An attribute column based on a Source Element (SRCELE) Data Source displays the value of the attribute 
associated with the element defined by that data source. Select the Data Sources tab to display the Data Sources 
for the Database View. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 133
For the purposes of this user guide, an example Data Sources hierarchy is described: 
• If a Database View is created that has the Element Types (ELEL) attribute set to UDA then the node at the 
top of the Data Sources tree represents the collection of UDA elements. 
• If a SRCELEMENT node is created below this with its Expression set to OWNER, then the Source Element 
represents UGROs, the OWNER elements of the selected UDAs 
• Since SRCELEMENTs are allowed to contain SRCELEMENTs navigation steps are incremental. Another node 
with its Expression set to OWNER represents UWRLs, the OWNERs of the OWNERs of the selected UDAs. 
• The NAME attribute is present on UDAs, UGRO and UWRLs. Creating an Attribute column for the NAME 
attribute and selecting different Data Sources changes the element the NAME attribute is being displayed/
edited on. 
The user can create a new Data Source, delete, navigate to, cut, copy or paste a Data Source from the right-click 
menu. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

New SRCELEMENT 
Creates a new Data Source in the current context

Sets the Current Element (CE) to the definition 
element for the Data Source, this expands the 
Dictionary Explorer if displayed 
Cut 
Places the selected item on the Database Views 
Editor clip board ready for a Paste operation. Pasting 
the item moves it. 
Copy

Views Editor clip board. 
Paste

board into the current context 
Properties 
The Properties part of the Data Sources tab displays the significant properties of the selected Data Source. If a 
DBVW is selected then only its description can be modified on the Data Sources tab. The Expression field must 
be filled out with a valid expression for SRCELE Data Sources to define the element navigation. 
Allow row creation 
To create new rows for the Database View or to enable the creation of the Data Source element in the event 
that it doesn't exist and a column value requires setting. 
Note: 
Dependent upon which element is selected as the Current Element CE determines if new rows or Data Sources 
element can be created. 
Click to select the Allow element creation to enable the Creation Rules part of the Data Sources tab. The rules 
defined in the Creation Rules part of Data Sources tab are used for the element creation. If insufficient 
information is defined for the element creation then a prompt is displayed stating, this can include information 
such as OWNER and TYPE. 
Allow row deletion 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 135
Deletes rows or data source elements from the Database View. Dependent upon which element is selected as 
the CE determines if rows or Data Sources element can be deleted. 
Creation Rules 
The creation rules define the information to create an element when requested and set up the initial state of the 
element created by setting attribute values and assigning status. The user must populate the Creation Rules part 
of the Data Sources tab to make sure additional information is not required during the element creation. 
Auto assign or create owner - To create the new element in the first available owner or an ownership hierarchy if 
one isn't present, click to select the Auto assign or create owner check box. 
Note: 
As ownership is usually significant for an element, this must be used with caution. 
Owner Expression - In the Owner Expression field, input the OWNER for the element being created, if left unset 
then the user is prompted for the OWNER. The expression can be that returns either the element such as ‘id 
/Equipment’ or a string expression that returns the name or identity of the element such as ‘/SITE_' + $!Param2. 
Type Expression - In the Type Expression field, input a string expression returning the type of the element to 
create such as ';MyUdet'. If this isn't set then the user is prompted for the type of element to create. 
Note: 
A prompt is displayed t if the type can be derived. 
Additional rules can be used to request information from the user (Parameters), set attribute values at the time 
of creation and assign status to the newly created element. 
Create Parameters 
Parameters are user defined variables that can be used in the create rule expressions. A parameter can either be 
a value derived from an expression or information requested from the user. If the Expression field is left unset 
then the user is prompted at the time of creation. When using a parameter it should be treated as if the value is 
being substituted into the expression before execution rather than as a PML variable. 
The Create Parameters part of the Data Sources tab is populated with attributes: 
User-defined name 
The name of the parameter, as used in the 
expressions. 
User Prompt 
The prompt to the user to request a value. 
Expression 
The expression used to derive a value for the 
parameter, if unset the user is prompted. If the 
element is being created for a SRCELE Data Source 
then the expression is executed on the element 
defined by the parent Data Source otherwise it is 
executed on the Current Element (CE). 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 136
Expression Type 
The syntax of the expression (PML or XPATH), PML 
expression must be PML 1 only. 
UDA Type 
The data type for the parameter, this affects user 
entry for the parameter and not the parameter use. 
Required 
Indicates the parameter is required and whether 
element creation can succeed without it. 
If a parameter is used to request the fluid from a user, the attributes in the Create Parameters part of the Data 
Sources tab must be populated. 
User-defined name 
Fluid 
User Prompt 
Fluid Code 
Expression 
Expression Type 
PML 
UDA Type 
TEXT 
Required 
True 
The value input by the user at the time of creation can be used in the Owner Expression and in attribute the 
rule: 
Owner Expression '/Equipment_' + '$!Fluid' 
Attribute Rule 
Attribute 
:Fluid 
Expression 
‘$!Fluid’ 
Note: 
The example expressions contains $!Fluid in '' characters since the value is substituted into the expression before 
the expression is evaluated. The parameter is not a PML variable. 
Attribute Rules 
The Attribute Rules define the initial values of attributes when the element is created. These can be fixed by 
specifying expressions to derive the value or the user can be forced to input a value at the time of element 
creation. 
The Attributes Rules part of the Data Sources tab is populated with attributes: 
AVEVA™ Engineering

User Prompt 
The prompt which displays to the user. 
Attribute 
The attribute to set the value of. 
Expression 
Expression to derive a value, if left unset the user is 
prompted for a value. 
Expression Type 
The syntax of the expression (PML or XPATH). 
Required 
Indicates whether a value is required and whether 
element creation can succeed without it. 
Status Rules 
The Status Rules assign the identified status definition to the element on creation or prompts the user for a 
status definition to apply if the status definition is not defined. 
The Create Status part of the Data Sources tab is populated with attributes: 
User Prompt 
The prompt which displays to the user. 
Status Definition Ref 
The status to assign to the created element, if left 
unset then a prompt is displayed. 
Required 
Indicates whether a status must be assigned for the 
element creation to succeed. 
Columns 
The Columns tab displays the column definitions for the Database View. A column can be based on a database 
attribute or expression. Attribute columns are editable in the Database View by the user unless the Readonly 
property is set. Columns based on expressions are not editable by the user when accessed in the data grid. It 
may be helpful when managing the columns to drag the Data Source field onto the grouping section in order to 
quickly view what columns are defined from the different Data Sources. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 138
The drop-down list at the top of the Columns tab control contains all the Data Sources defined in the grid. The 
functionality of the toolbar which contains the New, Add Standard Attributes and Add Status Column options 
are affected by which Data Source is selected from drop-down list. 
New 
Creates a column definition for the selected Data 
Source and selects it in the grid. 
Delete 
Deletes the selected column(s). 
Add Standard Attributes 
Adds all of the common attributes for the elements of 
the selected data source. Add Standard Attributes is 
enabled if columns don't already exist for all of the 
common attributes and the element type for the 
selected Data Source can be identified. 
Add Distributed Attributes 
Adds all of the distributed attributes for the elements 
of the selected data source. Add Distributed 
Attributes is enabled if columns don't already exist for 
all of the distributed attributes and the element type 
for the selected Data Source can be identified. 
Add Status Column 
Adds a column to display the status for the selected 
Data Source. The Qualifier must then be set for the 
column to the status definition to present to the user. 
The fields in the grid are enabled/disabled depending on whether the column is based on an attribute or an 
expression.

Data Source 
The element the attribute or expression is obtained 
from. 
Attribute/Expression 
The database attribute or expression used to get the 
value of the column. PML 2 is not supported. 
Column Name 
The default display name for the column, if unset then 
the display name is derived from the attribute or the 
expression used to get the column value. If duplicate 
names are input then the first column has the 
requested name, subsequent columns have an index 
added (for example: 'Name (2)') 
Description 
The data type of the column, this is known for the 
columns based on attributes but must be set for 
columns based on expressions. If set to the wrong 
type, errors display when the data is accessed. 
Type 
The data type of the column, this is known for the 
columns based on attributes but must be set for the 
columns based on expressions. If set to the wrong 
type, errors display when the data is accessed. 
Read Only 
If set the user is not able to modify the values in the 
column. 
Expression Syntax 
The syntax of the expression if the column is

Qualifier 
A qualifier used for attributes that require 
qualification such as STVVAL. 
Note: 
If a column is a case variant (as defined by the new flag on User Defined Attributes UDAs), specify a specific (case 
sensitive) Case name in the qualifier column of the Database View Editor for that column. As a result the column 
in that view becomes specific for just that case. 
Preview 
The Preview tab displays the data defined in the selected Database View definition. The preview can only display 
the data for elements the user has access to. In Lexicon, the contents of the grid may be significantly limited 
since Lexicon only has access to elements in Dictionary Databases.

An Administrative user is able to configure the rules governing the details of the information available for the

database definition.

All unit sets definition changes are stored after Save Work.

Delete

Once a new unit has been added to the unit set, it is displayed in a grid in the Configure Unit Sets window. 
Unit 
The database name of the unit 
Display Label 
The label that will be displayed throughout the 
system. Unicode characters can be used. Each display 
label should be unique per unit set. Refer to Display 
Label for further information. 
Precision 
The number of decimal digits that will be used. 
Display Label 
In the Display Label field, any Unicode character can be entered, in any language. If there is a need for special 
characters, then the relevant Unicode can be used, by entering a backslash (\ ) followed by the letter u or U and 
the code of the character.For example: the symbol 1/3 will be \u2153 or \U2153. 
Alternatively, special characters can be copied and pasted from another application or by using the available ALT 
codes, for example: by holding the ALT key and entering 0178, produces the ² (superscript two) character. 
By default, every number that is entered in the display label box is converted to a superscript. To change to a 
normal or subscript character of that number, use the Up and Down arrows on the keyboard. 
Set Units priority for grids workflow 
Use this functionality to select which units are displayed for selection from the drop list in a grid. In the 
Configuration module on the Rules tab in the Units groups, click to display the Unit Sets Configuration window. 
1. Click Create Unit Set to add a unit set the list displayed in the window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 143
2. In the New Unit Set 1 row, type Length from the Dimension drop down list select Length. 
3. In the Unit pane of the Unit Sets Configuration window, type MM into the Unit field and click Add unit to 
add the unit to the unit set. 
4. In the Unit field, type MM and click Add unit, the display name is shown as MM[1]. By default , every 
number is converted to a subscript, refer to Display Label or further information. 
5. Repeat the step to add a CM unit. 
6. Repeat the step to add a KM unit. 
7. Repeat the step to add a CMM unit. 
8. Repeat the step to add a CMM unit. 
9. Repeat the step to add a KMM unit. 
10. Add a unit set to the list of unit sets, name the unit set as Length-Max. 
11. Add a KM unit and modify the display name to KM. 
12. Add a Yard unit and modify the display name to YRD. 
13. Add a Mile unit and modify the display name to Mil. 
14. Add a Yard unit and modify the display name to YD. 
15. Click Apply

16. Click Save Work 
17. In the Tools group, click Database View Editor to display the Database Views Editor window. 
18. From the Database Views Editor window, select Pump Unit sets. 
19. Select the Grids tab, from the Grids Explorer window, right click the Project folder. 
20. From the right -click menu , select New then Grid to display the Create Grid window. 
21. Enter Pump Unit sets in the Name field. 
22. From the Views pane, in the Description field type Pump Unit sets to filter the list of views. 
23. Select Pump Unit sets.

24. Select the Fields tab, on the Length row, in the unit set column select Length from the drop down list. 
25. On the Length row, select Length from the drop down list. 
26. On the Width row, select Length-Max from the drop down list. 
27. On the Height row, select Length from te drop down list. 
28. Select Save Work 
29. Select Get Work 
30. Select the Home tab, then from the View group, click Explorer and select Grids. 
31. Select Default grids then Project and Pump Unit sets to display the grid. 
32. From the Height column in the grid, click the ruler to display the drop down list populated with the 
applicable unit sets for this attribute.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering

33. From the drop down list, select Precision, to display the Precision window where the number of decimals

Basic Engineering 
Use the Basic Engineering Data window to set the units of Measure Constants (absolute/gauge pressure units) 
for the project. 
Collections 
The Collections window is used to group data into collections and display search results., implement operations 
on all the items in a collection in one action. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 147
Use the Collections window to generate collections of database elements which are needed to work on. Each 
time the Collections window is accessed, in any of the modules that use it (Draw, Modeland Spool), the data 
collections generated is displayed enabling elements to be easily transferred between these modules. 
The contents of Collections persists between applications and module switching, as well as between sessions. 
The data stored is per user, per project in the AVEVA_DESIGN_USER directory, for example, in Model elements 
can be added to Collections and then switched to Draw to use the same data. 
Note: 
If the AVEVA_DESIGN_USER directory is shared between users, the users are able to view the same data. 
The Collections window consists of two parts: 
The top part of the window is the Collections grid and displays the Collections folders. 
The lower part of the window is the Contents grid, which displays the status of each item in the collection 
currently selected in the Collections grid. The Contents grid can be customized. 
When a search is carried out, the upper pane is minimized to show only search results. Click Show All Collections 
to display more collections and to show only the current collection, click Current Collection. Relative size of each 
pane can be adjusted to display more or fewer collections. 
To save search results as a collection rename the search results using right click menu. A new search results will 
be created when a new search is run. New collections are created on right click menu. 
Create and Rename a Collection 
To create a collection, right-click on a folder in the Collection grid and select New > New Collection from the 
right click menu. A new collection is then created and displayed in the Collections grid with a default name, for 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 148
example, NewCollection1. 
To change the name of a collection, right-click once on the collection name and select Rename enter the new 
name. 
Note: 
Collection names must be unique. 
Add Elements to My Collections 
To add elements to a collection, use any of the following methods: 
Select the required element in the displayed Explorer and either right-click and select Add to Collections from 
the menu, or drag and drop the element into the Collections grid or the contents grid. 
If the Add to Collections or Add Members to Collections menu option functionality has been used, the element 
is added to the current collection. 
If the element has been dragged and dropped from the displayed Explorer, the element can be dropped directly 
onto a Collection in Collections list in order to include it in that collection. If the element is dropped outside the 
list of collections a new collection containing the element is created. 
If the element was dropped onto the contents grid and a collection is currently selected, the element is added to 
that collection. If no collection is selected, elements cannot be dropped into the contents grid. 
Alternately, select the required element in the Drawlist, and proceed as described for the Explorers. 
Unlike the Explorers, multiple elements can be selected in the Drawlist, and added to the Collections grid. From 
the Drawlist window right-click Add to My Data. A new collection is automatically created containing the 
selected elements. 
Remove Elements from Collection 
To remove an element in a collection from the Collections window, select the item row, then right click and 
select Remove Selected Rows from the displayed menu. To remove a collection, right-click on the collection and 
select Delete from the displayed menu. 
Commands 
To give commands that act on the current list: type the command into the text box, commands can be applied to 
all contents in a collection (including Search Results) through the text box in the middle of the Collections 
window. Refer to Data Model Reference Manual, Introduction for further information on commands. 
Drag and Drop 
The contents of one collection can be added to another by dragging one collection on top of another. Elements 
can be dragged from the Explorer directly into collection by dropping them in the contents grid. Elements can be 
dragged to the 3D view from the contents grid. Collections can be copied between My Collections and System 
Collections through drag and drop. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 149
Move and Copy to and from Collections 
To move an element from one collection to another, select the item in the contents grid of the source collection. 
Either drag the element onto the destination collection in the Collections grid. 
To copy elements between collections, right click on an element, select Copy and then right-click on the 
Collection and select Paste. 
Note: 
Orphaned elements can be copied or moved into a collection, except that the elements are selected from the 
Collections grid. Elements in collections cannot be added to the Collections grid as orphaned elements. 
Navigate to an Element 
An element in a collection can be navigated to (for example in the displayed Explorer) and made the current 
element, select the element from the contents grid, right-click and select Navigate To. 
Perform Actions on My Collection Elements 
Several different actions can be performed on My Collections elements: 
Copy and Paste 
Moves and Copies to and from Collections. 
Remove Selected Rows 
Removes the selected row from the table grid. 
Navigate To 
Navigates to selected element in the displayed 
Explorer. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 150
3D View 
The 3D View has the following options: 
Add - Adds the current element to the 3D View. 
Add Only - Will add the significant element to the 
drawlist but only add the given primitive(s) to the 3D 
view rather than all the primitives below the 
significant element. 
Add Connected - Adds the CE and all the elements 
that are connected to the 3D View. 
Add Within Volume - Adds the CE plus all the 
elements that occupy the same volume as the current 
element to the 3D View. 
Remove - Removes the current element from the 3D 
View. 
Remove Only - Will remove the significant element 
from the drawlist but only removes the given 
primitive(s) to the 3D view rather than all the 
primitives below the significant element. 
Highlight Elements - Highlights the current element in 
the 3D View. 
Unhighlight Elements - Removes the highlight from 
the selected element. 
Note: 
If a collection was selected, the action is performed 
for all elements in that collection. 
Current Collection 
The Current Collection is selected by double-clicking on a collection and will be indicated with an asterisk icon in 
the Collections pane. 
Shared Collections 
To create Shared Collections in the Shared Collections folder, which will create Groups and Groupsets in the 
Explorer, both of which are created through the right-click menu. 
System Collections 
System Collections are read only and are the following: 
• Drawlist 
• Claimlist 
• Current Selection 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 151
Add to Collections 
To add elements from the displayed Explorer to the Collections window, right click on an element and select Add 
To Collections or Add Members to Collections, which will add the selected element to the current collection. 
Note: 
Double-click on the collection to make it active before adding elements. 
Languages 
Use the Languages functionality to define the default and available languages for the project. 
Note: 
This functionality is only available to the system administrator. 
Note: 
Only one language can be set as the default language, the first added language is automatically set as the default 
language. 
1. Launch the Configuration module. 
2. Create a language attribute, add a name, description and attribute properties as shown below. 
Note: 
Language variant attributes can only be String type. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 152
3. Select the Project tab. 
4. Select Languages, the Project Deliverable Languages window is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 153
5. Click to select the Show language when there is no current language value check box. 
6. Click to select the down arrow next to the Language field. 
7. From the list of available languages select English (World), the Default radio button is automatically 
selected. 
8. Click Add. 
9. Click to select the down arrow next to the Language field. 
10. From the list of available languages select German (Germany) 
11. Click Add. 
12. Click to select the down arrow next to the Language field. 
13. From the list of available languages select Spanish (Brazil). 
14. Click Save. 
15. Exit the Configuration module. 
16. Launch the Engineer module. 
17. Select Project then Languages, the Deliverable Languages page is displayed. 
18. Use the radio button to specify the current language. 
Note: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 154
By default, the operating system's active input language is the current language. If the operating system's 
active input language is not in the valid set of input languages, the default project language is used as the 
current language. If a default language setting is not found, the language variant functionality is not 
available. 
Language Variants 
Language Variants for Grids 
If the Language Variant attribute is selected for an element that represents a grid column in the conceptual 
model, the grid column data is displayed using the current language when opened. 
Language Variants for Datasheets 
If the Language Variant attribute is selected for an element that represents a datasheet cell in the conceptual 
model, the data is displayed using the current language when opened. 
Language Variants for Tag Editor 
If the Language Variant attribute is selected for an element that represents a tag property in the conceptual 
model, the tag property data is displayed using the current language when opened. 
Language Variants for Drawings 
If the Language Variant attribute is selected for intelligent text on a drawing, the text is displayed using the 
intelligent text formula. 
If an attribute is marked as language variant only, the following text scenarios are available: 
• Display value for current language: 
@Attribute {{Current Language}}@ 
• Display value for specific language: 
@Attribute {Language{IETF-Tag}}@ 
If an attribute is marked as both language and case variant, the following text scenarios are available: 
• Display value for active case and current language: 
@Attribute {{Active Case}} | {{Current Language}}@ 
• Display value for specific case and current language: 
@Attribute {Case{Specific_Case}} | {{Current Language}}@ 
• Display value for specific case and specific language: 
@Attribute {Case{Specific_Case}} | {Language{IETF-Tag}}@ 
• Display value for active case and specific language: 
@Attribute {{Active Case}} | {Language{IETF-Tag}}@ 
Refer to Attributes,and Database Definitions Implementation Preferences for further information. 
Drawing Options 
In the Configuration module or Engineer module, use the drawing options functionality to define the animation/
colour display options and the object selection/object snap settings. Sheet defaults can also be defined in the 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 155
Configuration module. 
Animation and Colour Display Options 
Sets the default animation, colours, axis options and crosshair options. 
Animation 
Animate zoom changes 
If the check box is selected, animate zoom changes is 
applied. 
If the check box is not selected, animate zoom 
changes is not applied. 
Animation Time (seconds) 
Specifies the animation duration. 
Colours 
Custom colours can be specified for various elements of the application's interface. 
Select the element to modify from the list and click Modify to display the Color window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 156
Use the Color window to define and select the colour for the currently selected element. 
Basic colors 
Specifies a basic colour for the element. 
Custom colors 
Specifies a custom colour for the element. 
Crosshair 
Specifies a colour for the element using a crosshair on 
the palette. 
Slider 
Specifies a colour for the element using a slider. 
Hue, Sat, Lum 
Specifies explicit colour values. 
Red, Green, Blue 
Specifies a colour using explicit values. 
Add to Custom Colors 
Adds the currently selected colour to the Custom 
colors palette. 
Axis Options 
Specifies the size and position of the Axis icon. 
If the Draw Axis Icon check box is selected, the Axis icon is displayed in the viewport. 
If the Draw Axis Icon check box is not selected, the Axis icon is not displayed in the viewport. 
Crosshair Colour Mode 
Controls the appearance of the crosshair. 
If the RGB option button is selected, the crosshair is displayed in colour mode, the X axis is red, the Y axis is 
green and the Z axis is blue. 
If the Grayscale option button is selected, the crosshair is displayed in grayscale mode. 
The Crosshair size slider defines the size of the crosshair. 
Reset Configuration 
Restores project configuration settings to their default settings. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 157
The check boxes specify the settings to be restored to their defaults. 
Select all 
Selects all available settings. 
Clear all 
De-selects all available settings. 
Object Selection and Object Snap Settings 
Sets the default object selection, object snap, dynamic hint and anti aliasing options. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 158
Selection Settings 
Defines the way in which objects are selected. 
Pickbox Size 
Specifies the display size of the pickbox. 
Grip Size 
Specifies the display size of the grip. 
Max number of Grips 
Specifies the maximum number of grips that can be 
displayed simultaneously. 
Single selection mode 
If the check box is selected, holding down Shift adds 
or removes selected objects. 
If the check box is not selected, holding down Shift 
does not add or remove selected objects. 
Object Snap Settings 
Defines the snap marker size and aperture box size. Functionality is also available to specify the Grid Settings and 
Object Snap settings. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 159
Snap Marker SIze 
Specifies the size of the snap marker. 
Aperture Box Size 
Specifies the size of the aperture box. 
Display snap aperture box 
If the check box is selected, the snap aperture box is 
drawn while specifying coordinates with the mouse. 
If the check box is not selected, the snap aperture box 
is not drawn while specifying coordinates with the 
mouse. 
Advanced 
Specifies the Object Snap settings and Grid settings. 
Dynamic Hints Settings 
If the Animate dynamic hints check box is selected, hints with full opacity are displayed only when the an 
activity is not being carried out (for example, the mouse is not being moved). 
If the Animate dynamic hints check box is not selected, hints are displayed at all times, regardless of the activity. 
Anti-aliasing 
If the Use anti-aliasing check box is selected, anti-aliasing is on. 
If the Use anti-aliasing check box is not selected, anti-aliasing is off. 
Context Editor Precision 
Specifies the number of decimal characters displayed on the context editor. The default value is 0. 
In the example, the Decimal precision is 2 which displays two decimal characters on the context editor. 
Reset Configuration 
Restores project configuration settings to their default settings. 
Sheet Defaults 
Sets the width, height and description of available sheets. 
Note: 
The sheet defaults functionality is only available in the Configuration module. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 160
New sheets can be added and existing sheets can be modified using the grid. 
Save 
Saves the sheet defaults with the current settings. 
Configuration 
This guide describes the functionality available from the configuration module. 
New and Deleted Items Available 
By default, the New and Deleted Items Available window displays when the Configuration Module is launched. 
Note: 
The examples display created, deleted and previously found items. Other created, deleted or previously found 
items are also displayed in the New and Deleted Items Available window, for example, units of measure if a new 
Torque has been added using Admin. 
The New Items tab displays all items created in Admin since the last session. Select the required items to add 
them to the Configuration Module. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 161
The Deleted Items tab displays all items deleted from Admin since the last session. Select the required items to 
remove them from the Configuration Module. 
The Previously Found tab displays all items found in previous sessions that have not been added to the 
Configuration Module. Select the required items to add them to the Configuration Module.

Use the Notifications functionality to select which tag modifications notifications will be displayed for: logical

AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 172
Logically Delete 
Enables the notification functionality for parent tags 
on logical delete and purged of the child tag. 
Notes: 
A SaveWork must be carried out for the notification 
functionality to be enabled. 
If any changes are made, the application needs to be 
relaunched. 
Rename 
Enables the rename notification functionality for all 
written attributes of child tag(s). 
Notes: 
A SaveWork must be carried out for the notification 
functionality to be enabled. 
To see the notifications tag(s) must have a status for 
example: working. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 173
Status Change 
Enables the status change notification functionality. 
On changing of status of an owning tag, a notification 
is displayed on effected parent tag in the grid if the 
changed status notification is set true and its parts 
have a lower status notification is set false. 
Notifications are displayed for all the affected tags 
irrespective of who changed status them. 
Clicking on the notification icon in the grid, displays a 
dock window populated with details of all status 
changed tag(s) and other appropriate information. 
Raising notification\Reset notification will occur only 
when a status change is carried out on owning tag\
Part. 
Notes: 
This feature doesn’t work based on tag status value/
status definition. 
A SaveWork must be carried out for the notification 
functionality to be enabled.

New Elements

Refer to Status Definitions, Revisions, Templates, Database View Elements and Datasheet Template Elements for 
further information. 
Status Definitions 
Status Control is the ability to control and report on the status of individual model objects as they progress their 
lifecycles. It can be applied to any model objects, for example, tagged items, catalog components, drawings. 
The desired project configuration is created with the use of a hierarchy of Status Configuration World, Status 
Definition Group, Status Definition and Status Value elements. A Status Configuration World element is a 
container for status definitions used to control and report on the status of individual model objects as they 
progress their life cycles. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 192
Status Configuration World 
Use the New Status Configuration World window, to create a new status configuration world element in the 
Workflow Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with the default attributes for the element, enter a name and 
description for the new status configuration world element. 
Status Definitions Group 
Use the New Status Definitions Group window, to create a new status definitions group element in the 
Workflow Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with the default attributes for the element, enter a name and 
description for the new status definition group element. 
Status Definition 
Use the New Status Definition window to create a new status definition element in the Workflow Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with the default attributes for the element, enter a name, description, 
initial status and controllable element type. 
Revisions 
Controlled Object Revisioning (COR) is used to control when and if users are alerted to changes to objects made 
by disciplines other than their own. When such changes are made, and the objects are released via status 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 193
control, a notification is sent to any users that have subscribed to that object. Users may then decide if and when 
to adopt the change for their own discipline. Refer to the Lexicon User Guide for further information. 
Use the COR functionality to create a revision configuration and a hierarchy at the same time: 
• Revision Configuration World (REVCWL) 
• Revision Configuration Group (REVCGP) 
• Version Control Configuration (VERCON) 
• Revision Number Definition (REVNOD) 
• Revision Block Definition (REVBLD) 
• Revision Control Configuration (REVCON). 
Revision Configuration World 
Use the New Revision Configuration World window to create a new revision configuration world element in the 
Workfow Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor, is populated with the default attributes for the element, enter a name and lock for 
the new revision configuration world element. 
Revision Configuration Group 
Use the New Revision Configuration Group window to create a new revision configuration group element in the 
Workflow Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with the default attributes for the element, enter a name and lock for 
the new revision configuration group element. 
Revision Block Definition 
Use the New Revision Block Definition window to create a new revision block definition element in the 
Workflow Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with the default attributes for the element, enter a name, description, 
lock and displaytag for the new revision block definition element. 
Revision Configuration 
Use the New Revision Configuration window to create a new revision configuration element in the Workflow

The Current Element Editor is populated with default attributes for the element, enter a name, description, lock

Structure 
Use the available functionality from the Structure tab to view and define the Project Breakdown Structure 
which presents the database elements of a specific project in a customized hierarchy structure which matches a 
companies working practices. 
The database elements can be arranged by class, system, site, zone or some other user-defined structure. By 
default, the Structure Explorer, Current Element Editor and Project Structure Preview (Read Only) windows are 
displayed. 
Structure Explorer 
The Structure Explorer displays the Project Breakdown Structure in a hierarchical format. 
Current Element Editor 
The attributes of the currently selected element in the Structure Explorer are displayed in the Current Element 
Editor. If required these attributes can be modified. 
Project Structure Preview (Read Only) 
The Project Structure Preview (Read Only) displays a read only preview of the currently selected element in the 
Project Breakdown Structure (PBS). 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 197
To display PBS data, a template definition must be created, the template definitions are known as Template 
Definitions (PBSTPL). Template definitions must be created below Templates World elements (PBSWLD). Each 
PBSTPL represents a separate PBS hierarchy and is presented in a PBS as a single root node. 
Templates 
Use the New functionality to create the required templates hierarchy. 
Template World Element 
Use the New Templates World window to create a new Template World element in the Structure Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with the default attributes for the element, enter a name and 
description for the new template world element. 
Template Definition 
Use the New Template Definition window to create a new Template Definition element in the Structure 
Explorer. 
The Current Element Editor is populated with the default attributes for the element, enter a name and 
description, flexible explorer text, flexible explorer sorting, flexible explorer context and flexible explorer icon for 
the new template definition element. Refer to the Lexicon User Guide for further information.

Use the New Object Node window to create a new Object Node element in the Structure Explorer.

Text Node

Edit Formula

Lexicon User Guide for further information.

Rules 
The available functionality from the Rules tab provides the AVEVA Engineering System Administrator the ability 
to map element data into tables (Database Views) for a user workflow or report. 
A Database View definition includes the criteria for selecting a set of elements from which the rows are 
constructed. Refer to the Engineering Common Functionality and the LEXICON Command Reference Manual for 
further information. 
Use the Rules functionality to view Database View Explorer and Current Element Editor, create the database 
views hierarchy, configure unit sets, configure cable sizes, set basic engineering data, name/edit tags and 
configure the Tags Management System. 
Database View Explorer 
The Database View Explorer displays the database view elements in a hierarchical format. 
Element Editor 
The attributes of the currently selected element in the Structure Explorer are displayed in the Current Element 
Editor. If required these attributes can be modified. 
Database View Elements 
The desired project configuration is created in the Database View Explorer with the use of a hierarchy of 
Database View World (DBVWWL), Database View Group (DBVWGR), Database View Sets (DBVWSE), Database 
Views (DBVW) and Column Relations (COLREL) elements. 
Note: 
Dependent which elements is selected on the Element Editor determines which elements are listed in the drop 
down list. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 200
Delete Rules Element 
Use to delete the currently selected element in the Database View Explorer. 
Components 
Refer to the Unified Engineering documentation for further information. 
Refer to Autonaming Setup, Unit Sets, Basic Engineering and New Elements for further information. 
Controlled Object Revision 
Use this functionality to enable had parts and can have parts associations to issue parts automatically, click to 
check the Issue part items check box. Multiple tags can be issued with parts and the status of the parent 
cascades down to the parts. 
Note: 
This functionality is only available for Controlled Object Revisioning (COR) enabled projects and is readable in the 
Engineering module. 
Storage Rules 
Storage Rules 
Use the storage rules and unreserve tags functionality to restrict access to databases and to unreserve partially 
reserved tags in the TMR database (both of these require administrative rights). 
AVEVA Engineering allows project data to be stored in multiple databases. Project administrators can put access 
control on each database thus allowing/denying access to the database for project teams. 
This allows project administrators to keep tight control on data segregation when a project has to be worked 
upon by various teams simultaneously. For an application to make use of this feature it needs some capability by 
which creation of element can be redirected to a given database in case a user has write access to multiple 
databases. 
Storage rules are required to figure out the Engineering database where a tag will be created. These rules are 
based on the class model, or on conditions on class attributes. 
Storage rule creation 
From the Storage rules window, click to select the database from the list of available databases. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 201
Use the functionality available from the Storage configuration rules window to add a new storage configuration 
rule. Storage rules can be based on a class without any conditions (these will be used in creating Admin 
elements, for example: Plant, System and so on) or the rules are based on a class and some conditions on class 
attributes.

Select class 
Specifies the class of the new enclosure. 
A top level enclosure class can be selected, or the Enclosure node can be expanded to display sub-classes (some 
of which also have sub-classes), which can be selected instead: 
Search 
Searches the list of available enclosure classes, the Select class list displays the classes that have names matching 
the characters typed in this field.

Use the Enclosures grid to view, select and edit the enclosures in the enclosures library.

The upper grid is used to display the enclosures, the middle grid is used to display the enclosure accessories 
assigned to the enclosure selected in the upper grid, and the lower grid displays the cable glands and gland

or terminal strip.

AVEVA™ Engineering

Page 245
Associated Class 
Specifies the class of equipment that the terminal arrangement will be used for. The ... control opens the 
Associated Class window, used to select the class. 
Name 
Specifies the display name of a new terminal arrangement. 
Description 
Specifies a description of a the terminal arrangement. 
Number of Terminals 
Specifies the number of Top, Bottom. Left and Right terminals that the terminal arrangement has (at least 1 
terminal must be specified in one of these fields). 
Updates the display in the Preview area as the numbers of terminals are entered. 
Power Attributes 
If appropriate for the selected equipment class, used to specify the power attributes of the terminal selected in 
the Preview area, namely: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 246
• Core Type, 
• Phase Identifier Set (not active if Core Type "GC" is selected), 
• Phase ID, 
• Core Markings, 
• Cable Power Flow (select "To" or "From"), 
• Core Marking, and 
• Terminal Type. 
Allow use on project 
Specifies whether or not the terminal arrangement is to be available for use in the project. 
Preview 
The preview area specifies the internal connections, signal orders, and the plugs/sockets of the terminal 
arrangement. 
It initially displays a picture of some terminal arrangements. It updates to display a representation of the 
terminal arrangement as terminal numbers are specified in the Number of Terminals fields, and as internal 
connections, signal orders, channel numbers, and the plugs/sockets are defined. 
Terminal fields on the preview image are used to specify terminal identifiers. 
Left-click on terminal fields while pressing Ctrl to select them. Multiple fields may be selected at the same time. 
Plug/Socket Pin View 
If the arrangement includes any plugs or sockets, the preview image changes to display details of the assigned 
plugs and sockets. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 247
Fields and controls not relevant to this view and not available on the window when this view is displayed. The 
other fields and controls work in the same way. 
Terminal Arrangement View 
Changes the preview image back to the terminal arrangement view from the Plugs/Sockets Pin view. 
Internal Connection + 
Makes an internal connection between two terminals with markings selected on the preview image, or between 
two plugs or sockets in the Plugs/Sockets Pin view: 
The terminals at each end of a connection are highlighted in yellow if the mouse cursor is hovered over it: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 248
Internal Connection - 
Removes an internal connection between two terminals selected on the preview image, or between two plugs or 
sockets in the Plugs/Sockets Pin view. 
Signal Order + 
If a terminal or terminals are selected, displays the Signal Order window. 
The preview image updates to reflect the entered values: 
Terminals with signal order numbers defined are colour coded. Terminals with the same numbers are coded with 
the same colour, as are terminals with the same combination of signal number and channel number: 
Signal Order - 
Removes the signal order numbers of terminals selected on the preview image. 
Channel Number + 
If a terminal or terminals are selected, displays the Channel Number window. 
The preview image updates to reflect the entered numbers (prefixed with "Chnl"): 
Terminals with channel numbers defined are colour coded. Terminals with the same numbers are coded with the 
same colour, as are terminals with the same combination of signal number and channel number. 
Channel Number - 
Removes the channel numbers of terminals selected on the preview image. 
Plug/Socket + 
If a terminal or terminals without internal connections or signal order numbers is selected, displays the Select 
Plug and Socket window. 
The preview image updates to reflect the selected plug or socket. Any terminal identifiers in the selected 
terminals are replaced by the name of the plug or socket. 
Sockets: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 249
Plugs: 
Plug/Socket - 
Removes the plug or socket from terminals selected on the preview image.

Library items

Preview

Only displayed if the selected class is one for which a library exists. 
For other items, displays a preview of the terminal arrangement of the selected item under Library items in the

Number of items to add 
Multiples of certain library items can be added (various device types for example). When such a library item is 
selected, an additional field is displayed next to Add >>. 
Specifies the number of the library items to be added. 
Add >> 
Adds the new library item to the compartment. 
If the item being added is an earth bar, displays the Earth Bar window. 
If the item being added is a terminal strip, displays the Terminal Pattern window. 
The preview on the compartment is updated to display the item. 
Back 
Used to display the items added to the compartment. 
Selected items are highlighted with a blue outline. 
Rotate item +90° / -90° 
Rotates the selected item plus or minus 90°.

Copy

Link Device
