device in the same compartment. 
A sub-menu is displayed from which the direction of 
the link can be selected (i.e. to or from): 
Unlink Device 
Used to remove links to other devices. A sub-menu is 
displayed from which link to be removed can be 
selected:

to other devices.

Scale To Fit

-, + and Slider 
Zooms the display of the compartment preview in and out. 
Overview

Top level item classes can be selected, or a class node can be expanded to display sub-classes (some of which

Wiring Rules 
Use the Wiring Rule window to create and edit a library of wiring rules. 
Name 
Specifies the display name of a new wiring rule. 
Description 
Specifies a description of a the wiring rule. Optional. 
Use parent as ‘From’ 
Sets the wiring rule to use the parent equipment and the "from" equipment rather than the "to" equipment. 
Preview area 
The preview area specifies the equipment and cables of the wiring rule. 
It initially displays a picture of some cable and wires. It updates to display a representation of the wiring rule 
equipment and cables are specified. 
Primary equipment + 
Displays the Primary equipment window, used to add primary equipment to the wiring rule. 
When primary equipment (and its terminal arrangement) is added, the preview on the Wiring Rule window is 
updated accordingly: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 261
Secondary equipment + 
Displays the Secondary equipment window, used to add secondary equipment to the wiring rule. 
When secondary equipment is added, the preview on the Wiring Rule window is updated to include it: 
Cable + 
Displays the Cable window, used to select a cables to link primary and secondary equipment. 
When a cable is added to the wiring rule, the preview on the Wiring Rule window is updated to include it: 
Terminals and From/To Cable Ends on the Preview 
Used to link equipment terminals to cable ends by clicking on one and dragging to other so that both are 
highlighted. For example: 
This displays the Available Cores to Connect window, used to select the cable cores that connect a cable to an 
equipment terminal. 
The preview on the Wiring Rule window is updates to include connections between terminals and cables: 
Tied back cores are depicted as follows: 
Remove - 
Removes a cable selected in the preview window from the wiring rule, including any terminal connections. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 262
Right-click menu options for cables on the Preview 
Edit 
Displays the Cable window. 
Remove 
Removes the cable, including any terminal 
connections. 
Swap Ends 
Swaps to the From and To ends of the cable. 
Show Cores 
Displays the Available Cores to Connect window, used 
to select the cable cores that connect a cable to an 
equipment terminal. 
Right-click menu option for cable core terminal connections on the Preview 
Remove 
Removes the connection. The cable is not removed. 
Other Preview right-click menu options 
These options are displayed if the window is right-clicked any where except on a cable of cable core terminal 
connection. They perform the same functions as the controls along the top of the preview area. 
Primary Equipment 
Use the Primary Equipment window to add primary equipment to the wiring rule. 
Equipment Number

Copy Datasheet Template 
Copy Datasheet Template 
Use the Copy option to copy a datasheet template. Paste the template into a template group within the same 
project. 
The copy retains all the properties, mappings and data of the source template. 
Copy and Paste Datasheet Template 
1. Click Datasheet on the Libraries tab. 
2. Select the datasheet template to copy. 
3. Right-click the template and select Copy. 
Alternatively, click Copy on the ribbon. 
4. Select the template group in which to place the copy. 
The template must be in the same project as the source template. 
5. Right-click and select Paste. 
Alternatively, click Paste on the ribbon. 
6. Edit the template's name and content as you require. 
Copied templates have the same name as their source, followed by an incremental number. For example, 
TemplateName(1) 
Datasheet Template Editor 
Datasheet Template Editor 
Use the Datasheet Template Editor window to design a new datasheet template or edit an existing one with the 
use of attribute mapping and various editing tools and controls. A basic template can be designed in Excel then 
the Datasheet Template Editor can be used to edit, map attributes and add controls to the datasheet. 
All of the tasks that are associated with the creation or modification of a datasheet template are initiated from a 
central window which acts as a task hub. Depending on selections made in the Datasheet Template Editor

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Map Free-text Cells to Tag Attributes in Datasheets 
Map Free-text Cells to Tag Attributes in Datasheets 
If a datasheet template contains one or more free-text cells, you can map each of those cells to an attribute. For 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 305
example, to update the description of a tag from within a datasheet, link a free-text cell in the datasheet to the 
Description attribute. 
If the structure of a datasheet template changes (for example, by the addition of a further free-text cell), and you 
open a datasheet based on that template, you are prompted to upgrade the datasheet instance. 
Click Yes to apply the changed template to the existing datasheet instance. Click No to continue working with the 
unchanged template. 
This functionality is available only to administrators or users with access to all databases. 
If you use a free-text cell to update an attribute that already has a value, an error is logged. Subsequent updates 
to that attribute do not generate errors. 
You can specify a location in which to store error logs. If you do not specify a location, logs are sent to the local 
temp directory. 
Errors are also logged if, for example, you log in as a non-administrator, or try to use an invalid type to update a 
value. 
Specify the Location of Error Logs 
Log in to the Engineer module as an administrator. 
1. Click the Admin tab. 
2. Click Settings in the Datasheets group. 
3. In the Logs field specify the location in which to store error logs. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 306
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 307
Example of Linking a Free-text Cell to an Attribute 
This example describes how to create a datasheet template with a free-text cell and link that cell to an attribute. 
This example uses the Description attribute of a reciprocating pump. 
1. Log in to the Configuration module as an administrator. 
2. Click the Libraries tab. 
3. In the View group, click Datasheet. 
4. In the Datasheet pane, select the node in which to create a datasheet template. 
5. Right-click and select New Datasheet Template. 
6. Specify a name and description for the template and click the plus (+) icon in the Data source field. 
7. Select a data source and click Add. 
8. Select the Edit the template when finished check box and click OK. 
9. From the Standard Controls pane, drag the Free Text control to the template. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 308
10. Click in the free-text cell. In the Data Sources pane, navigate to the Data Sources node and double-click the 
required attribute. 
11. Save the template. 
12. Log into the Engineer module as an administrator. 
13. In the Grids Explorer, open the grid for the data source that you selected for the datasheet template. 
14. Select the tag to which to link the template and select Datasheet>New in the Datasheets group. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 309
15. Select the datasheet template to use and specify a description. Click OK. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 310
16. In the template, enter a value in the free-text cell. 
The tag attribute is updated with the entered value. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 311
Continuation Area 
Continuation Area 
Use the continuation area functionality to identify a horizontal or vertical area in the datasheet template in 
which to display data related to a sub element of a datasheet tag (parent-child relation), for example: a pump 
and nozzles. Highlight multiple cells in the template and click Add Area in the toolbar of the datasheet template 
editor. 
You can nest multiple continuation areas within another. In a datasheet instance, right-click options to control 
data sorting and filtering are available. You specify default filter and sort options in the properties of the 
template. If the attribute in a cell returns a numerical value, there are also Display Rule and Precision options. 
Once an attribute from the nozzle data source (child) has been mapped on to the first row within the continuous 
area, the mapping is then repeated horizontally or vertically for each nozzle associated with the datasheet tag. 
If the nozzles outnumber the number of rows or columns selected in the continuous area, new sheets are 
generated until all the nozzles display. 
Cases can also be displayed in a continuation area. 
Define the continuation area type, and default filtering and sorting options, using the Properties window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 312
Orientation 
Select Horizontal to define a horizontal continuation 
area or select Vertical to define a vertical continuation 
area. 
Type 
Select Sub Items to apply the continuation area to 
data related to a sub element or select Cases to apply 
the continuation area to defined Cases. 
Filter 
In the Filter field, specify the default filtering criteria. 
In a datasheet instance, users can select different 
filtering if required. 
This option is available only if you have highlighted an 
entire continuation area in the template. 
When you right-click a continuation area in a 
datasheet instance, a context menu displays a Filters
option. The option opens the Filter Records form. 
If two or more continuation areas are nested, you can 
select the area to filter. 
Sort 
In the Sort field, specify the default sorting criteria. In 
a datasheet instance, users can sort the data 
differently if required. 
This option is available only if you have highlighted an 
entire continuation area in the template. 
When you right-click a continuation area in a 
datasheet instance, a context menu displays a Sort
option. Select the order in which to sort the data. 
Display Rule 
A display rule specifies that the cell on which it is applied displays, for example, only values that are between 
specified limits. 
Use the Display Rule Editor to configure a display rule on cell. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 313
In a continuation area, click a cell containing an attribute. 
1. In the Display Rule Editor, select the Display Rule check box. 
The check box is enabled only for attributes that return numerical values. 
2. Select a prefix, such as At least or Greater than. 
3. Select a suffix, such as and above. 
4. Optionally, select a comment to be displayed when the condition of the rule is met. Optionally, select the 
Use Comment Only check box to display only the comment when the condition of the rule is met. 
5. Select an operator. 
6. Specify a value. 
7. Select a unit measure. 
If the operator is =, then you must specify a tolerance. 
In a datasheet instance, if a cell has, or could have, a display rule defined on it, the Display Rule option is 
available from the cell's context menu. Select Display Rule to access these options: 
Option 
Description 
Show Editor 
Opens the Display Rule Editor. You can specify a new 
display rule or modify an existing rule. 
Disable and Enable 
Prevents application of the rule until you select 
Display Rule>Enable.This option is persistent across 
sessions. 
Remove 
Removes the rule from the datasheet instance for the 
current session. 
Copy Rule 
Copies the rule so that it can be pasted to another 
cell. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 314
Paste Rule 
Pastes a copied rule into another cell during the 
current session. You can independently enable and 
disable copied and pasted rules. 
A separate context menu option, Precision, is also enabled if a cell has, or could have, a display rule defined on 
it. 
Select the number of decimals to display for values returned by the display rule. Optionally, choose to remove 
trailing values. 
Filters 
Use the Filter form to define one or more filters which can then be selected in a datasheet instance. 
Select the entire continuation area and then click the continuation area cell on which to create a filter. In the 
Properties pane, click the ellipsis in the Filter Conditions field. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 315
In a datasheet instance, right-click the cell to filter and select Filters and the area to filter (for example, Current 
Level). The Filter records form is displayed. 
If a filter has been defined in the template, you can use it with or without modifying it. If no filter has been 
defined, you can create a filter. 
Click Clear to clear the form fields. 
If a filter has been defined in the template, click Default to load the filter. 
Click Filter to apply the filter. 
Attribute Mapping 
Attribute Mapping 
Use the datasheet functionality to auto-map or manually map attributes to the cell(s) of a datasheet template. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 316
Auto-map Attributes 
Auto-map Attributes 
The Auto-map functionality searches for the attribute with the same name as the label text in the data source of 
the datasheet template. If an attribute cannot be located in the data source, the dictionary database for the UDA 
is searched. 
Once the searches have been completed, the Create and Map window is populated with the results. The results 
can be accepted to continue with the mapping process. 
The Create and Map window is populated with the results of the searches. All results can be accepted and 
sorted. 
Matched 
The Label matches an attribute in the data source of 
the datasheet template. Select the Accept check box 
to map the templates’ cell to the attribute. 
If required the Attribute Name can be modified. 
Matched - Multiple data source 
The Label matches more than one attribute in the data 
source (database views and dictionary database. From 
the Data Source column, select the required data 
source, then select the Accept check box to map the 
templates’ cell to the attribute. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 317
Not Matched 
The Label does not match an attribute, a new attribute 
must be created or the data source modified. 
If an error occurs, it is highlighted in the Create and Map window along with a message describing the error. 
Manual Map 
Manual Map 
If required, the attribute from the data source can be mapped to a cell(s) in the datasheet template. 
From the Template Explorer, select the required attribute, drag and drop the attribute into the cell adjacent to 
the matching label text in the datasheet template. 
Or 
From the Datasheet Template Editor window, select the cell adjacent to the required label text, then double-
click the required attribute in the Template Explorer. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 318
Map Cell Format 
Mapped cells retain the formatting specified in the Datasheet Template Editor and supplied in the Excel 
template. 
Revision Block Addition 
Revision blocks can be mapped to the datasheet template, to create and configure a revision block. 
From the Template Explorer, select the element beneath the revision block (in this case: generic pump), drag and 
drop the attribute into the datasheet template. 
Or 
From the Datasheet Template Editor window, select the cell then double-click the required attribute in the 
Template Explorer. 
The selected cell in the datasheet template requires enough area to the right and bottom to accommodate the 
revision block. For example: the number of revisions (rows) and the number of attributes added (columns). 
Repetition Area 
Repetition Area 
A repetition area is an area on a principal sheet of a datasheet template that can be associated to multiple 
associated sheets. 
Note: 
Multiple sheets must be available to associate with the principal sheet. 
Add Area 
Add Area 
Select a cell range (repetition area) in the datasheet template from the Datasheet Template Editor toolbar, select 
Add Area. The repetition area is added to the datasheet template and the area is represented with a black 
border. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 319
The Properties part of the datasheet template is populated with the attributes of the repetition area. 
By default the Area Type attribute is Continuous, select Repeating. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 320
Add Mappings to Repetition Area 
Add Mappings to Repetition Area 
Mappings can be added to the repetition area before or after associating it to other sheets, the mappings from 
the repetition area will automatically added to the associated sheets. 
Note: 
Mappings can only be added to the primary sheet, if an attempt is made to add mappings to the primary sheet, 
an error stating that ‘The operation is not allowed since the current sheet is not a primary sheet’. 
Remove mapping from the repetition sheet 
Remove mapping from the repetition sheet 
All Mappings can removed from the primary sheet and associated sheets, highlight the repetition area on the 
primary sheet, right click and select Delete. 
Note: 
Mappings can only be added to the primary sheet, if an attempt is made to add mappings to the primary sheet, 
an error stating that ‘The operation is not allowed since the current sheet is not a primary sheet’. 
Associated Sheets 
Associated Sheets 
Once the area is set to repeating the Associated Sheets functionality is available for selection. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 321
In the Properties part of the datasheet template, in the AssociatedSheets attribute field select Browse the String 
Collection Editor window is displayed populated with sheets which can be added to the template. In this 
example the primary sheet is page 1 and the available associated pages are pages 2, 3 4 and 5. 
To add the repetition area to associated sheets, select the check box adjacent to the sheet. 
Remove Repetition Area 
Remove Repetition Area 
To remove a repetition area from its primary sheet and associated sheets, in the primary sheet of the datasheet 
template, highlight the repetition area right click and select Remove Area. 
Note: 
The functionality to remove the repetition is only enabled when the area is highlighted. The border and all 
mappings (if present) are removed from the primary and associated sheets. 
Remove Repetition Area from Selection of 
Associated Sheets 
Remove Repetition Area from Selection of 
Associated Sheets 
To remove a repetition area from a selection of associated sheets, in the Properties part of the Datasheet 
Template Editor window, in the AssociatedSheets field select Browse. From the displayed String Collection 
Editor window populated with the associated sheets with the selected repetition area. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 322
Select to uncheck the check box adjacent to the individual or a selection of associated sheets. 
Standard Tool with a Repetition Area 
Standard Tool with a Repetition Area 
When standard tool functionality is used (such as a check box, radio button and so on) which contains a 
repetition area, it gets created w ith all the primary and associated sheets of the repetition area. The values of 
the mappings (if present) are populated for the selected tag with respect to the mapped attributes. 
Note: 
If any value or colour modification for an editable cell in the repetition area is modified, the modification is also 
carried out on any associated sheets. 
Formatting Tools 
Formatting Tools 
A datasheet template appearance can be controlled using the formatting functionality such as borders, font, 
format alignment and text colour. 
Behaviour 
Behaviour 
Use the behaviour functionality to identify the properties of an attribute as ‘Provided by supplier’ which can be 
edited after export the datasheet to Excel.

Border Editor

Text Colour 
Use the text colour functionality in the Appearance part of the Properties window to set the colour of the text in

Palettes. Click to select the desired colour for the font in the datasheet template. 
Text Formatting 
Text Formatting 
Use the text formatting functionality in the Appearance part of the Properties window to set the font style and 
size for a selected cell in the datasheet template. 
From the Properties part of the template editor, in the Format field, click Browse to display the Font window 
(the functionality available from the Font window is standard).

Symbols 
Symbols 
This functionality is an administrative and can be used to add pre-defined shapes (circle square triangle or 
hexagon) which are then available for selection to the end user. The administrator can add a shape with text, a 
shape and a mapped attribute or if the shape is filled or left blank. 
From the Toolbox, drag and drop the required shape into the desired cell in the datasheet template. 
The Properties part of the datasheet template editor is now populated with the attributes of the pre-defined 
shape. 
The attributes displayed are identical to all the other with the exception of the appearance attributes fill shape or 
lock fill shape attributes. The default for Fill State is None and Lock Fill State is False (if set to True the fill state 
cannot be modified by the end user). 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 326
Pre-defined shapes can be added to a cell which has text or a mapped attribute in the datasheet template.

Datasheet Template Properties 
Use the Datasheet Template Properties window to modify the name, description, datasource and path of the 
currently selected database template in the Datasheets Explorer. 
Name 
Enter a name for the new datasheet template element (special characters or spaces are not permitted). 
Description 
Enter a brief description for the new datasheet template element. 
Data Source 
Select Add to display the Add Data Source window, then select the data source from the Add Data Source 
window. 
Template File 
Browse and navigate to the location of the file to be used as a basis of the new datasheet. 
Disciplines 
Select the discipline or disciplines that the template is to be used in. 
Template File 
Specifies the location of the Excel file that the template is to be based. 
For External Calc Template 
Defines if the template is to be used for external calculations. 
Edit the template when finished 
Specifies whether the template will be edited (i.e. whether the Database Template Editor will be opened) once 
finished.

Default Cases 
Once the case control is mapped under Items properties the user set case values to be auto-populated, thus 
enabling the administrator to select a default case. Refer to Default Cases and Cases for further information. 
Note: 
The cases are preferably created and added to the MDB in the Engineer module before logging in the 
Configuration module to map the cases under Items properties. 
Example 
Open a Datasheet template and map a case control available from the Item properties ( in this example - Case1, 
Case2 or Case3).Set the default case as Case3, save and close. 
Note: 
The selected Case is treated as the default case. 
The selected Case needs to be saved and while re-opening the Datasheet Template the earlier selected Case 
should be pre-populated. 
The selected Case needs to be saved and while re-opening the Datasheet Template the earlier selected Case 
should be pre-populated but can be updated later. 
For the existing Datasheet Templates, upon clicking of Case Controls or while loading DST we fill-in the Items 
property values. 
If no selection is made, then the behavior should be as existing that is left blank if the Items field is empty else by 
default show the first case from the array list. 
If the MDB doesn’t have the configured Cases or there are no Cases configured, then the Items should have 
"Base Case" as it is today 
In scenarios when Case values are changed by the user, then while loading a DST necessary validation messages

The functionality available is identical to that described in New Calculation Template Item. 
Export and Import 
You can export and import libraries and catalogues. The process is similar to that for exporting and importing 
grids in the Engineer module. 
Export and Import Catalogues and Libraries 
You can export and import libraries and catalogues. The process is similar to that for exporting and importing 
grids in the Engineer module. The key difference that Element type is the only mode in which you can export and 
import libraries and catalogues. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 336
Mandatory Elements 
If the Excel file to be imported lacks a mandatory attribute, the file must be edited and imported again. If a file 
must be re-imported in these circumstances, additional attributes become mandatory. 
The CLANAM attribute is always mandatory. 
Which other attributes are mandatory depend on whether a project is conceptual or non-conceptual, on the 
import mode, and on whether the tags are being imported are new or amended. Depending on these conditions, 
you may be prompted for the CLANAM, NAME, or TYPE attributes, which must be added to the Excel file before 
it is re-imported. 
Export Catalogue 
1. On the Libraries tab, click Catalogue in the View group. 
2. Click the catalogue to export. 
3. Select Export>Select from Database. 
4. Optionally, if the details of a previous export have been saved in a configuration file, select the configuration. 
5. Click Next. 
6. From the Element type list, select the type of element to export. 
Add a row to enable the list: 
7. Optionally, select the option to include UDETs (user-defined element types) 
8. Click Next. 
9. From the Attribute list, select the element attribute to export. 
Add rows to add further attributes, or click the Add all attributes icon. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 337
10. Optionally, select the Use reporter text for Excel column option. 
This exports column headings in a user-friendly format. 
11. Click Next. 
12. Optionally, remove leading slashes from one or more of the mandatory attributes: Name, Owner, Reference. 
13. Click Next. 
14. Optionally, save the configuration. 
Configurations are saved in User, Project, or Product locations. User configurations are stored locally and can 
only be used locally. Saved Project and Product configurations can be accessed by other users. 
15. Click Finish. 
16. Browse to the location in which to save the export. 
17. Dismiss the Exporting to Excel File dialog. 
Import Catalogue 
1. On the Libraries tab, click Catalogue in the View group. 
2. Select Import>List. 
3. Browse to and open the Excel file to import. 
4. Optionally, if the details of a previous export have been saved in a configuration file, select the configuration. 
5. Click Next. 
6. From the Element type list, select the appropriate element type to import for each worksheet. 
7. Optionally, select the option to include UDETs (user-defined element types) in the import. 
8. Click Next. 
9. Click Next. 
You might receive this error: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 338
To resolve this error, check whether CLANAM is listed in the Excel Column list. If it is listed, then select 
CLANAM from the Attribute column and click Next. 
If CLANAM is not listed in the Excel Column list, this suggests that the CLANAM attribute was not included in 
the export. In this case, you must cancel the import. Then, open the Excel file and add a CLANAM column. 
Check that the Excel file also includes a NAME column and a TYPE column. If it does not, add them and re-
import the file. 
10. Optionally, choose to remove carriage returns from all text elements. 
This ensures that element names, for example, display on one line. 
11. Click Next. 
12. Optionally, save the configuration for use with future imports. 
Configurations are saved in User, Project, or Product locations. User configurations are stored locally and can 
only be used locally. Saved Project and Product configurations can be accessed by other users. 
13. Click Finish. 
14. Dismiss the Importing Excel File dialog. 
You might receive this error: 
This indicates that the TYPE attribute is missing from the Excel file. 
Export Library and Import Library 
The procedure to export and import libraries is the same as for catalogues.

Preview 
Use the functionality available from the Preview group to view and navigate the content of the currently 
selected element in the grids explorer and view the content of the grids explorer in discipline groups. 
Grids 
Use to preview the project data from the currently selected element in the Grids Explorer in a grid format. 
Discipline Preview 
Use to view the project data in discipline groups. 
To specify which disciplines are displayed in the Preview Grids Explorer, select available disciplines from the list. 
Drawings 
Use the available functionality from the Drawings tab to define the drawings, symbol palettes, symbols, labels, 
connectors, borders and templates that are available in the Engineer module. 
Setup Explorer 
Provides an easy to use graphical method for exploring the drawing types, symbols hierarchy, assemblies, 
drawing templates, drawing borders, connection point definitions and symbol variants. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 340
The Setup Explorer displays the database elements in a hierarchy. Click on an element in the tree view to 
navigate to the element in the database and make it the current element. 
To expand an element hierarchy, click  to the left of the element name. To contract an expanded element, click 
 to the left of the element name. 
Symbol Palette 
Provides an easy to use graphical method for viewing and selecting the symbols that are associated with the 
currently selected symbol palette element in the Setup Explorer. Each symbol is displayed as an image with the 
symbol name positioned below the symbol. 
Use the Attributes window to define the associated symbols and disciplines. 
Options 
Modifies the symbol display size in the Symbol Palette window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 341
Small icons 
Displays the symbols at 64 pixels. 
Medium icons 
Displays the symbols at 96 pixels. 
Big icons 
Displays the symbols at 128 pixels which is the default 
display size. 
Search 
Type a string in the Search field, all symbols are searched for matching content. The search results are 
highlighted with a yellow background. 
The arrow on the right of the Search field displays a drop-down list of search filters that are available for 
selection. 
By name 
Filters the search results by name. 
By class name 
Filters the search results by class name. 
Press Ctrl+F to clear the Search field. 
Hover the mouse over a symbol name to display additional information for the symbol. 
Symbols with inactive in build or deleted classes in the conceptual model are displayed with a warning. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 342
Hover the mouse over the symbol to display additional warning information. 
If a symbol is created or modified, the symbol palette is refreshed when the symbol editor is closed or when a 
save work is performed. 
Attributes 
Displays the attributes that have been specified for the currently selected element (CE), dependent upon access 
rights, some attributes can be modified. Attributes can also be configured so that they are not displayed in the 
Attributes window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 343
By default, the attributes are displayed in a list format, the left-hand part of the window displays the attribute 
names, the right-hand part of the window displays the attribute values. 
Attributes can be populated explicitly, for example, the Display name and Description fields. Attributes can also 
be used to link an element to a TMS class hierarchy, for example, the Class attribute. 
Click ... adjacent to the Class field to select a class in the conceptual model.

The Search list displays the attributes that match the characters typed in the field. 
Electrical and Instrumentation Drawing Attributes 
Electrical and Instrumentation drawing attributes are set at drawing type and symbol level. For example: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 345
These include: 
• The spacing between the lines representing busbars on multi-line schematic diagrams. 
• Power connection colours, line types and line weights for busbar phases: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 346
• The symbol that is automatically placed where busbar potential lines join fed item connection lines and its 
size. 
• Connection point labels. 
• Symbol template variants (for left, right and tiers 1 to 3). 
• The sequence numbers of symbols in templates. 
• Minimum spacings between connectors. 
• Start, Middle, and End settings for Cable labels on Loop and Termination automated drawings. 
• Engineering text positions for connector item types (e.g. Cables, Cores, Wires, and Links and so on) on Loop 
and Termination automated drawings. 
• The text to be placed on top of cables, cable cores, wires, looping wires, group shield screens, and overall 
screen shields and Termination automated drawings, and the justification of this text. 
• Whether or not individual cable cores are displayed on Loop automated drawings. 
• Whether or not connections between terminals in the same and in different terminals strips, and 
connections to overall screen cable cores, are displayed on Loop automated drawings. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 347
Refer to Create Record in Tag Management System Enabled Project for further information. 
Create Node 
Creates a new node on the Setup Explorer, the available node type is dependent on the currently selected node. 
Create Drawing Type 
Use the New Drawing Type window to create a new drawing type. The drawing type is used to store drawings in 
Engineer. 
Name 
Type a unique name for the drawing type. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
Automation Type 
Use the drop down list to define the drawing type content. 
Manual (No Automation) 
Specifies a manually produced drawing. For example, 
a single line diagram. 
Insertion Template 
Specifies the use of an automatic template currently 
used for hookup drawings. The drawing is generated 
from engineering elements and refined, not manually 
drawn. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 348
Termination Template 
Specifies the use of an automatic template currently 
used for termination drawings and loop diagrams. The 
drawing is generated from engineering elements and 
refined, not manually drawn. 
Item Type 
Use the drop down list to define the item types available for use on the drawing. 
Engineering 
Specifies engineering items can be used on the 
drawing, this is the default option. 
Functional 
Specifies functional items can be used on the drawing. 
Disciplines 
Displays the associated disciplines list for the drawing type. The associated disciplines list is populated with the 
name and description of each associated discipline. 
Manage 
Use the Select disciplines window to define the associated disciplines for the drawing type. Associated 
disciplines are displayed in the disciplines list. 
The check box adjacent to the discipline name defines the disciplines that are associated with the drawing type. 
If the check box is selected, the discipline is associated with the drawing type. 
If the check box is not selected, the discipline is not associated with the drawing type. 
The drawing type is created as a member element of the drawing type folder on the Setup Explorer. 
Use the Attributes window to modify the display name, description, associated disciplines and purpose. 
The read only Item Type field can be viewed using the Attributes window. 
Default Symbols 
Use the Default symbols part of the Attributes window to define the symbols associated with classes for a 
specific drawing type. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 349
Class 
Specifies the class element. Use the Browse
functionality to display the Select class window. 
Default 
Specifies the default symbol for the class. Only one 
default symbol can be specified for each class. 
Symbol 
Specifies the the symbol associated with the class. Use 
the Browse functionality to display the Select Symbol 
or connector window. 
Add 
Adds a row to the grid. 
Remove 
Removes the currently selected row from the grid. 
Refer to Setup Explorer and Attributes for further information. 
Create Symbol Palette 
Use the Create new symbol palette window to create a symbol palette. 
Type a unique name for the symbol palette. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 350
The symbol palette is created as a member element of the currently selected drawing type on the Setup Explorer. 
Use the Attributes window to define the associated symbols and disciplines. 
Refer to Setup Explorer and Attributes for further information. 
Create Symbol 
Use the Create new symbol template window to create a symbol. 
Type a unique name for the symbol. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
Once named, draw and annotate the symbol using the blank drawing canvas, the symbol is created as a member 
element of the currently selected symbol palette on the Setup Explorer. By default, the Graphical Canvas - 
Symbol Editor tab is displayed. 
The example displays a symbol named Motor. The symbol contains drawing elements, intelligent text and 
connection points. 
Functionality is available to create symbol templates that contain extensible symbols. 
Refer to Setup Explorer, Symbol Editor, Create Symbol Template with Extensible Symbols, Create Nested 
Assembly, Create Object Assembly, Create Assembly for further information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 351
Create Symbol Template with Extensible Symbols 
Creates a base symbol template which is constructed using a set of individual symbol template elements. The 
administrator can specify which symbol template elements can and cannot be repeated. 
The symbol template elements must first be created using individual symbol template elements. The example 
displays individual symbol template elements. 
The symbol template elements are then inserted and positioned on a single base symbol template using the 
Symbol Palette. 
Use the Properties window to modify the symbol template properties for each symbol used on the base symbol. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 352
Symbol Type 
Select Top from the drop-down list to specify a top 
symbol. The top symbol is not repeated when 
stretched along the Y axis. The top symbol is 
positioned at the top of the extended symbol. 
Select Bottom from the drop-down list to specify a 
bottom symbol. The bottom symbol is not repeated 
when stretched along the Y axis. The bottom symbol is 
positioned at the bottom of the extended symbol. 
Select Left from the drop-down list to specify a left 
symbol. The left symbol is not repeated when 
stretched along the X axis. The left symbol is 
positioned at the left of the extended symbol. 
Select Right from the drop-down list to specify a right 
symbol. The right symbol is not repeated when 
stretched along the X axis. The right symbol is 
positioned at the right of the extended symbol. 
Select Repeatable from the drop-down list to specify a 
repeatable symbol. The repeatable symbols are 
repeated when extended along the X or Y axis. 
Use the Properties window to modify the base symbol properties. 
Extensibility 
Input a value in the Offset X field to specify the repeat 
distance for repeatable symbols along the X axis. 
Input a value in the Offset Y field to specify the repeat 
distance for repeatable symbols along the Y axis. 
The example displays the extensible symbol template extended along the Y axis. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 353
Refer to Symbol Palette, Properties and Extend Symbols using Grip Points for further information. 
Create Label 
Use the Create new label window to create a label. 
Type a unique name for the label. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
Once named, annotate the label on the blank drawing canvas, the label is created as a member element of the 
currently selected symbol palette on the Setup Explorer. By default, the Graphical Canvas - Symbol Editor tab is 
displayed. 
The example displays a label that contains drawing elements and intelligent text. 
Refer to Setup Explorer and Symbol Editor for further information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 354
Create Connector 
Use the Create new connector template window to create a connector. 
Type a unique name for the connector. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
Once named, use the Attributes window to define the connector name, intelligent text formula, TMS class 
hierarchy association, colour and line type. 
The connector is created as a member element of the currently selected symbol palette on the Setup Explorer. 
Note: 
Only Classes that are associated with connectors in the conceptual model can be selected. 
Refer to Setup Explorer, Attributes, and Create Record in Tag Management System Enabled Project for further 
information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 355
Create Off Page Connector 
Use the Create new off-page connector window to create an off page connector. An off page connectors is used 
to indicate the position of the connector on a different sheet. 
Once named, draw and annotate the off page connector on the blank drawing canvas. The off page connector is 
created as a member element of the currently selected symbol palette on the Setup Explorer. By default, the 
Graphical Canvas - Symbol Editor tab is displayed. 
The example displays an off page connector named Off Page Connector Left. The off page connector contains 
drawing elements, intelligent text and a connection point. 
Refer to Setup Explorer for further information. 
Create Annotation Break 
Use the Create new annotation break window to create an annotation break. An annotation break is used to 
enable two annotations for different values of an attribute. It can be used as a specification break to indicate the 
change in the value of an attribute. For example, a transition from low to high pressure or temperature. 
Type a unique name for the annotation break. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
The annotation break is created as a member element of the currently selected symbol palette on the Setup 
Explorer. By default, the Graphical Canvas - Symbol Editor tab is displayed. 
Use the Attributes window to select an attribute. Select either the Ab-1 or Ab-2 radio button to define the 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 356
attribute prefix and drag and drop the attribute on the blank drawing canvas. 
The example displays an annotation break with two annotations containing intelligent text formulas for two 
different values of the default name attribute. In the Engineer module the intelligent text is resolved and the 
appropriate values are displayed. 
Refer to Setup Explorer for further information. 
Create Nested Assembly 
Creates a nested assembly which is constructed using a set of individual symbol, drawing and annotation 
elements. 
Use the Create new assembly window to create a nested assembly. 
Type a unique name for the nested assembly. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 357
Drag and drop existing symbols from the symbol palette, draw and annotate the nested assembly using the blank 
drawing canvas, the nested assembly is created as a member element of the currently selected symbol palette 
on the Setup Explorer. By default, the Graphical Canvas - Symbol Editor tab is displayed. 
Use the Attributes window to define the associated connection point definitions for each symbol in the 
assembly. 
The example displays a nested assembly named PC-Telephone Connection. The nested assembly contains three 
symbol elements with intelligent text and intelligent connection points. 
A marker point can be added to a nested assembly to define the origin point of the assembly when placed onto 
the drawing canvas in the Engineer module. 
Refer to Add Marker Point, Setup Explorer, Symbol Editor, Create Symbol, Create Symbol Template with 
Extensible Symbols, Create Object Assembly and Create Assembly for further information. 
Create Object Assembly 
Creates an object assembly which is constructed using a set of individual symbol, drawing and annotation 
elements. The object assembly is created using a selected symbol as the initial symbol on the drawing canvas. 
Use the Create new assembly window to create an object assembly. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 358
Type a unique name for the object assembly. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
Use the Select symbol window to specify the symbol to be used as the initial symbol on the drawing canvas. 
Full class hierarchy 
Displays the full class hierarchy in the class list. 
Classes on symbols 
Displays only classes that are used by symbol templates in the class list. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 359
All Symbols 
Displays all symbols. 
Selected class symbols 
Displays only symbols that are associated with the currently selected class in the class list. 
Selected class and child symbols 
Displays only symbols and child symbols that are associated with the currently selected class in the class list. 
Search class list 
Type a string to be searched in the Search field, matching content is displayed below the field. 
Alternatively, navigate through the tree hierarchy and select a class. 
Options 
Modifies the symbol display size in the Select symbol window. 
Small icons 
Displays the symbols at 64 pixels. 
Medium icons 
Displays the symbols at 96 pixels. 
Big icons 
Displays the symbols at 128 pixels which is the default 
display size. 
Search symbols 
Type a string in the Search field, all symbols are searched for matching content. The search results are 
highlighted with a yellow background. 
The arrow on the right of the Search field displays a drop-down list of search filters that are available for 
selection. 
By name 
Filters the search results by name. 
By class name 
Filters the search results by class name. 
Press Ctrl+F to clear the Search field. 
Hover the mouse over a symbol name to display additional information for the symbol.

Use the Attributes window to define the associated connection point definitions for each symbol in the

The assembly is associated with a selected class. 
Use the Create new assembly window to create an assembly. 
Type a unique name for the assembly. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
Use the Select class window to specify the class to associate with the assembly.

drawing canvas, the assembly is created as a member element of the currently selected symbol palette on the 
Setup Explorer. By default, the Graphical Canvas - Symbol Editor tab is displayed. 
Use the Attributes window to define the associated connection point definitions for each symbol in the 
assembly.

Create Border 
Use the Create new border window to create a sheet border. 
Type a unique name for the border. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
Once named, draw and annotate the border on the blank drawing canvas. The backing sheet, border lines and 
attribute values can be defined for areas on the drawing such as the title block, revision history block and sheet 
number. These areas can be positioned anywhere on the drawing. The border is created as a member element of 
the currently selected drawing border folder on the Setup Explorer. By default, the Graphical Canvas - Symbol 
Editor tab is displayed. 
Use the Attributes window to define the associated drawing types, border grid definition and standard. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 364
Select associated drawing types 
Use the Elements window to select the drawing types to be associated with the border. 
The check box adjacent to the drawing type name defines the drawing types that are associated with the border. 
If the check box is selected, the drawing type is associated with the border. 
If the check box is not selected, the drawing type is not associated with the border. 
Select grid 
Displays the Border Grid Definition window which can be used to define and position the drawing border grid. 
Remove grid 
Deletes the drawing border grid from the drawing canvas. 
The Remove grid window is displayed. Click OK to confirm the deletion or click No to cancel the deletion. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Standard 
Displays a list of associated standards that are available for selection. 
Add 
Adds a new standard to the drop-down list. The Create new standard window is displayed. 
Input a standard name in the field and click Create to add the standard. 
Delete 
Deletes a standard from the drop-down list. 
Refer to Setup Explorer, Symbol Editor, Create Border Grid and Create Text for further information. 
Create Template 
Use the Create new drawing template window to create a template. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Type a unique name for the template. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
Once named, draw and annotate the template objects on the blank drawing canvas.Objects on the template 
drawing canvas are displayed on all drawings which are created from the template. The template is created as a 
member element of the currently selected template folder on the Setup Explorer. By default, the Graphical 
Canvas - Symbol Editor tab is displayed. 
Use the Attributes window to define the associated guard level, border and template region. 
Border 
Displays a list of associated borders that are available for selection.

Create Connection Point Definition 
Use the Create new connection point definition window to create a connection point definition. 
Type a unique name for the connection point definition. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
The connection point definition is created as a member element of the currently selected connection point 
definition folder on the Setup Explorer. 
Use the Attributes window to define the connection point type, display name and description. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 368
Colour 
Specifies the colour of the connection point when used on an engineering drawing. The default is None. 
Remove Colour 
Deletes the specified colour from the colour field. 
Select Colour 
Displays the Color window, select a colour for the connection point definition from the colour palette. 
The example displays a cabinet on an engineering drawing, the right connection point definitions are highlighted 
blue when the cabinet is selected. 
Class 
Creates a TMS class hierarchy association, the selected class is used as the default connector when the Draw 
Default Connector functionality is used on an engineering drawing. 
Use the Select class window to specify the default connector for the connection point definition. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 369
If a selected class is not a connector, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
Refer to Attributes, Create Connection Point and Draw Default Connector for further information. 
Create Symbol Variant 
Use the Create new symbol variant window to create a symbol variant. A symbol variant is used to change the 
default symbol display on a drawing. For example symbol variants can be used to display left or right pin variants 
on termination drawings. 
Type a unique name for the symbol variant. 
If the name is not unique, an error message displays summarizing the problem. 
The symbol variant is created as a member element of the currently selected symbol variants folder on the Setup 
Explorer. 
Use the Attributes window to define the symbol variant type and display name. 
The example displays four symbols, two left symbol variants and two right symbol variants. 
The pin symbol must have a left connection point and a right connection point, the sequence for both 
connection points must be 1. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 370
Use the Attributes window to define the Class and Symbol variant values for each symbol. 
The symbols can then be added to the Default Symbols for the drawing type. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 371
The example displays symbol variants on an engineering drawing. 
The available left and right symbol variants for drawings can be specified. 
Refer to Drawing Settings for further information. 
Copy Node 
Copies the currently selected node on the Setup Explorer to the clipboard, ready for pasting. Member elements 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 372
are also copied. 
For example: 
• A symbol node can be copied, ready for pasting onto the same or a different symbol palette node. 
• A symbol palette node can be copied, containing all member symbols, ready for pasting onto the same or a 
different drawing type node. 
• A drawing border node can be copied, ready for pasting onto the same drawing border container node. 
Click on a node in the Setup Explorer and on the Drawing tab, in the Node group, click Copy. 
Alternatively, right-click on a node in the Setup Explorer and select Copy from the drop down list. 
Paste Node 
Pastes nodes from the clipboard to a specified position on the Setup Explorer. 
For example: 
• A copied symbol node can can be pasted onto the same or a different symbol palette node. 
• A copied symbol palette node, containing all member symbols, can be pasted onto the same or a different 
drawing type node. 
• A copied drawing border node can be pasted onto the same drawing border container node. 
Note: 
Copied nodes cannot be pasted onto a different container node type. For example, a symbol node cannot be 
pasted onto a connection point definition container node. 
Click on a container node in the Setup Explorer and on the Drawing tab, in the Node group, click Paste. 
Alternatively, right-click on a container node in the Setup Explorer and select Paste from the drop down list. 
The copied node is pasted to the Setup Explorer as a member of the selected container node. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 373
Delete Node 
Deletes the currently selected node from the Setup Explorer, member elements are also deleted. 
Symbol Editor 
Use the functionality to modify the currently selected symbol, assembly, label, connector, off-page connector, 
template or border on the Setup Explorer. 
Refer to Create Connection Point, Add Marker Point, Add Drawing Objects, Create Border Grid, Create Border 
Grid, Create Text, Copy Objects. Paste Objects, Move Objects, Scale Objects, Rotate Objects, Create Arrays of 
Objects, Stretch Objects, Explode and Merge Objects, Change Length of Objects, Trim and Extend Objects, Fillet 
and Chamfer Objects, Delete Selection, Measure Distance between Two Points, Change Standard, Add Image, 
Revision Block, Engineering Table, Database View Table or CAD Overlay to Drawing and Zoom Options for further 
information. 
Import 
Previously created symbols, stored on the Production of Annotated and Dimensioned Drawings (PADD) database, 
or CAD symbols in the format of a .dwg, .dxf or .dwt file can be imported as member elements of the currently 
selected symbol palette. 
Previously created borders, stored on the PADD database, or CAD borders in the format of a .dwg, .dxf or .dwt 
file can be imported as member elements of the currently selected drawing borders node. 
Import Symbols 
Use the Import Existing Symbols window to import previously created symbols, stored on the Production of 
Annotated and Dimensioned Drawings (PADD) database. The symbols are created as member elements of the 
currently selected symbol palette.

Attribute Driven Mapping 
Use the Attribute driven symbols mapping window to map classes and symbols to attributes and values. The 
representation of mapped symbols on the drawing canvas can be modified by changing an attribute value using 
the Engineer module. 
For example, the representation of a valve can be modified to open or closed via the attribute Valve Position and 
the value Open or Closed. 
Note: 
The available symbols must already be associated with the class. 
Selected mapping 
Select an attribute driven mapping specification from the drop-down list to populate the Attribute driven 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 383
symbols mapping window with the mapping settings. 
Create 
Creates an attribute driven mapping specification. 
Delete 
Deletes the currently selected mapping specification. 
Classes 
Displays the currently mapped classes. Select a class to display the associated symbols. 
... 
Displays the Select Class window. Use the Select Class 
window to select the class using the TMS class 
hierarchy or search functionality. 
If a drawing type is not associated with the class, an 
error message displays summarizing the problem. 
Adds a row to the bottom of the grid. 
Deletes the currently selected row from the grid. 
Drawing Types 
The check box adjacent to each drawing type defines the drawing types that are associated with the currently 
selected class. You can select multiple drawing types for each class association. 
If the check box is selected, the drawing type is associated with the class. 
If the check box is not selected, the drawing type is not associated with the class. 
Symbol 
Displays the symbols that have been associated with the class. Select a symbol to display the mapped attributes 
and values. 
... 
Displays the Symbols Templates window. Use the 
Symbols Templates window to select a symbol. 
Moves to the next row in the grid. 
Moves to the previous row in the grid. 
Attribute 
Displays the currently mapped attributes. Select from the drop-down list to associate an attribute with a symbol. 
Value 
Displays the currently mapped attribute values. Select from the drop-down list to associate an attribute value 
with a symbol. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 384
Refer to Create Record in Tag Management System Enabled Project and Drawings for further information. 
Visual Styles 
Sets the visual style for connectors and intelligent tables which controls their appearance when added to a 
drawing. For example, the colours, line weights, styles and text sizes that are used. 
Visual styles are supplied which are initially assigned to connectors and intelligent tables by default. However, 
you can also define visual styles. A user-defined visual style can be set as the default style for connectors and 
intelligent tables. 
The assigned visual style can also be changed from the default at any time to any style after being added to a 
drawing. 
Create and Modify Visual Styles 
Sets the appearance of visual styles for connectors and intelligent tables. 
On the Drawing tab, in the Styles group, click Connector to display the Visual Styles window. 
The existing visual styles for connectors display in the Available Styles list. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 385
Select a style from the list to view and modify the visual style details. The properties display underneath the list. 
Modify the properties as required. 
Create a new style 
Displays the Create [Connector, 
Itable, Itable Cell] Style window. 
Refer to Create Visual Style for 
further information. 
Delete selected styles 
Click to delete the selected style 
from the Available Styles list. A 
confirmation window displays 
asking you to OK the deletion. Click 
Yes to delete the selected style. 
If a style which has been assigned 
to objects is deleted, those objects 
are automatically assigned to the 
AVEVA Default style. 
Explore the style hierarchy 
Click to display the Hierarchy 
Browser window, indicating the 
location of the default visual styles 
hierarchy. 
Create a new style with starting 
values created from the selected 
one 
Allows you to copy an existing 
visual style. Refer to Copy Visual 
Style for further information. 
Set Default Visual Styles 
Sets the default visual style for connectors. 
Select the check box adjacent to the required style from the Available Styles list. 
Change Visual Styles of Objects 
Applies a different visual style to a connector or intelligent table. 
Select a style in the Available Styles list. 
Use the Dynamic Properties tab to modify the style properties. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 386
Click OK to apply the visual style settings and close the Visual Styles window. 
Create Visual Style 
Creates a new visual style for connectors or intelligent tables. 
Note: 
The display of the Create Style window is dependent on which tab is selected on the Visual Styles window. 
Browse the hierarchy to select the library element that the new style is to be created under. Input a name in the 
Name field, or leave it blank if the style is to be assigned a name automatically. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 387
To open a drop-down menu consisting of elements that can be created at the current level, click New above the 
left-hand part of the window. 
The icon above the right-hand part of the window is used to specify whether styles display as thumbnails, a 
simple list, or a list with details. 
Click Create to create the new style. The new style is then created and added to the list of available styles in the 
Visual Styles window. 
Click Cancel to discard any changes and close the Create Style window. 
Copy Visual Style 
Creates a new visual style as a copy of an existing visual style. 
The Create Style window displays. Refer to Create Visual Style for further information. 
Browse the hierarchy to select the library element that the new style is to be created under. Input a name in the 
Name field, or leave it blank if the style is to be assigned a name automatically. 
Click Create to create the new style. The copy of the selected style is then created and added to the available 
styles list in the Visual Styles window. 
Line Styles Manager 
Sets the visual style for lines which controls their appearance when added to a drawing. For example, the 
pattern, text and glyph used to draw the line. 
Visual styles are supplied which are initially assigned to lines by default. However, you can also define line styles. 
Create and Modify Line Styles 
Sets the appearance of visual styles for lines. 
On the Drawing tab, in the Styles group, click Line to display the Line Styles Manager window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 388
The existing line styles display in the Available Styles list. 
Select a style from the list to view and modify the line style details. 
Create a new line style 
Displays the Create Line Style 
window. Refer to Create Line Style 
for further information. 
Create a new line style with 
starting values created from the 
selected one 
Allows you to copy an existing line 
style. Refer to Copy Line Style for 
further information. 
Delete selected line styles 
Click to delete the selected line 
style from the Available Styles list. 
A confirmation window displays 
asking you to OK the deletion. Click 
Yes to delete the selected style. 
Browse available line styles 
Click to display the Select Linestyle 
window, indicating the location of 
the currently selected line style. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 389
Change Visual Style of Lines 
Applies a different visual style to a line. 
Select a line style in the Available Styles list. 
Use the Properties tab to modify the line style properties. 
Decoration distribution 
Specifies the placement rules for the Glyph and Text 
across the line. The default value is Spaced 3. 
Decoration gap 
Specifies the clearance distance around the decoration 
within the line. The default value is 0.00. 
Decoration offset 
Adjusts the placement of the decoration elements 
with respect to each line segment. The default value is 
X 0mm Y 0mm. 
Decoration repeat 
Specifies the distance between repeated decorations 
affecting the density of the decoration when drawn. 
The default value is 0.00. 
Decoration size 
Specifies the Text and Glyph size of single decoration 
elements. The default value is 0.00. 
Glyph 
Specifies the glyph element used to decorate the line. 
The default value is 0 Off. 
Text 
Specifies the text used to decorate the line. The 
default value is empty. 
Text alignment 
Specifies the text alignment with respect to each line 
segment. The default value is Bottom. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 390
Text font 
Specifies the font for the text line decoration. The 
default value is By Template. 
Alternative definition 
Specifies an alternative line style for use in other 
features. For example, plot files. Only existing line 
styles can be selected by name. The default value is 
NullRef which specifies that no alternative line style is 
selected. 
Line style number 
Specifies an internal value that identifies the line style. 
The property is read only. 
Name 
Specifies the line style name. The value is populated 
from the line style name in the Available Styles list. 
Owner 
Specifies the line style owning element. The property 
is read only. 
Line description 
Describes the line style functionality. The default value 
is empty. 
Pattern definition 
Specifies the integer part list that defines the line 
pattern The default value is unset. 
Pattern line width 
Specifies the width of the pattern elements. The 
default value is 0.00. 
Pattern name 
Specifies the line style pattern name. The default 
value is Solid. 
Pattern name id 
Specifies the line style pattern identifier. The property 
is read only. 
Pattern repeat length 
Specifies the pattern repeat length. The default value 
is -1.0. 
Use the Preview shapes tab to view the line style as various different shapes using the currently defined 
properties. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 391
Click OK to apply the line style settings and close the Line Styles Manager window. 
Create Line Style 
Creates a new line style. 
Browse the hierarchy to select the library element that the new style is to be created under. Input a name in the 
Name field, or leave it blank if the style is to be assigned a name automatically. 
To open a drop-down menu consisting of elements that can be created at the current level, click New above the 
left-hand part of the window. 
The icon above the right-hand part of the window is used to specify whether styles display as thumbnails, a 
simple list, or a list with details. 
Click Create to create the new style. The new style is then created and added to the Available Styles list in the 
Line Styles Manager window. 
Click Cancel to discard any changes and close the Create Linestyle window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 392
Copy Line Style 
Creates a new line style as a copy of an existing line style. 
The Create Linestyle window displays. Refer to Create Line Style for further information. 
Browse the hierarchy to select the library element that the new style is to be created under. Input a name in the 
Name field, or leave it blank if the style is to be assigned a name automatically. 
Click Create to create the new style. The copy of the selected style is then created and added to the Available 
Styles list in the Line Styles Manager window. 
Transfer Configuration 
Use the Transfer configuration window to transfer elements to writable databases. 
Select destination databases 
Displays the writable databases that are available for selection. Select the writable databases, to which the 
elements will be transferred, from the-drop down lists. 
Select transfer elements 
Displays the elements which are available for transfer. Elements which exist in the destination databases do not 
display in the tree hierarchy. 
To expand an element hierarchy, click  to the left of the element name. To contract an expanded element, click 
 to the left of the element name. 
If the Select all check box is selected, all elements in the tree hierarchy are selected. 
If the Select all check box is deselected, all elements in the tree hierarchy are deselected. 
The check boxes adjacent to each element name defines the elements that are transferred. 
If the check box is selected, the element is transferred. 
If the check box is not selected, the element is not transferred. 
Transfer 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 393
Transfers the selected elements to the writable databases. 
The Transfer configuration progress window displays which tracks the progress of the element transfer. 
Click Stop to cancel the element transfer process. 
Once the element transfer has completed, click Close to close the Transfer configuration progress window. 
Create Connection Point 
Creates intelligent connection points on a symbol. Connectors can be connected to the connection point using 
the Engineering module. 
The prompt Specify the insertion point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the position of the connection point. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple connection points can be created.f 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Size 
Specifies a different size for the connection point. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
FInish 
Ends the command. 
Insert 
Adds the connection point to the symbol. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 394
The connection point extended line indicates the connection direction. The associated connector can only be 
connected using the specified direction. Use the rotate grip point to modify the direction of the connection point 
extended line. 
Use the Attributes window to define the sequence number and the associated connection point definition. 
Refer to Status Bar, Rotate Objects using Grip Points and Create Connection Point Definition for further 
information. 
Add Marker Point 
Adds a marker point to an assembly. The marker point defines the origin point of the assembly when placed onto 
the drawing canvas in the Engineer module. 
Note: 
It is not possible to add more than one marker point to an assembly. if an attempt is made to add a second 
marker point to an assembly, an error message is displayed summarising the problem. 
Add Marker Point 
Adds a marker point to an assembly. 
The prompt Specify the insertion point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the insertion point for the marker. The 
marker point is added to the assembly. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 395
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Glyph 
Specifies a different symbol for the marker point. Refer 
to Add Marker Point with Specified Glyph for further 
information. 
Size 
Specifies the size of the marker point. Refer to Add 
Marker Point with Specified Size for further 
information. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
Insert 
Adds the marker point to the assembly. 
Refer to Create Nested Assembly, Create Object Assembly and Create Assembly for further information. 
Add Marker Point with Specified Glyph 
Adds a marker point to an assembly with a specified glyph. 
The prompt Specify the insertion point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input G at the prompt and press Enter to display the Glyph Selection window.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Click to identify the required marker symbol. 
By default, the Glyph Selection window closes, the prompt Specify the insertion point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the insertion point for the marker. The 
marker point is added to the assembly. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Glyph 
Specifies a different symbol for the marker point. 
Size 
Specifies the size of the marker point. Refer to Add

information. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
Insert 
Adds the marker point to the assembly. Refer to Add 
Marker Point for further information.

Adds a marker point to an assembly with a specified size. 
The prompt Specify the insertion point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input S at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify the glyph size or <#mm> is displayed.

The prompt Specify the insertion point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the insertion point for the marker. The 
marker point is added to the assembly. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.
