Glyph 
Specifies a different symbol for the marker point. Refer 
to Add Marker Point with Specified Glyph for further 
information. 
Size 
Specifies a different size for the marker point. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
Insert 
Adds the marker point to the assembly. Refer to Add

Add Drawing Objects 
A number of drawing objects can be added to a drawing. 
Draw Lines 
Adds a line to a drawing. 
Draw Lines with Specified Points 
Draws a line with specified points. 
The prompt Specify the first point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the starting point of the line. 
The prompt Specify the next point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension and angle fields and press Enter or click to identify the point to which the line is 
drawn. A line is drawn between the two specified points. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple lines can be created. The first point of a new line is positioned at the same 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 399
point as the second point of the previous line. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Note: 
If more than one line is created, no connection is made between the lines. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Displacement 
Draws a line with a specified displacement. Refer to 
Draw Lines with Specified Displacement for further 
information. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Close 
Links the two endpoints of the drawn lines with an 
automatically drawn line and ends the command. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
Draw Lines with Specified Displacement 
Draws a line with a specified displacement. 
The prompt Specify the first point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the starting point of the line. 
The prompt Specify the next point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input D at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify displacement or <finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension and angle fields and press Enter or click to identify the point to which the line is 
drawn, with reference to the zero point of the user coordinates system. A line is drawn between the two 
specified points. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple lines can be created. The first point of a new line is positioned at the same 
point as the second point of the previous line. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Note: 
If more than one line is created, no connection is made between the lines. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 400
Next point 
Draws a line without specifying a displacement. Refer 
to Draw Lines with Specified Points for further 
information. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Close 
Links the two endpoints of the drawn lines with an 
automatically drawn line and ends the command. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
Draw Clouds and Outlines 
Adds a cloud or an outline to a drawing. 
Draw Clouds 
Draws a cloud. The cloud is created by drawing individual arcs. 
The prompt Specify the first vertex or <Default> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the first point of the cloud. 
The prompt Specify the next vertex or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the cloud. An arc is 
drawn between the first and second points. 
The prompt Specify the next vertex or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the cloud. An arc is 
drawn between the specified points. 
The prompt Specify the next vertex or <Close> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the cloud. An arc is 
drawn between the specified points. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple arcs can be created. The first point of a new arc is positioned at the same point 
as the second point of the previous arc. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 401
Cloud Inward 
Draws a cloud with inward facing arcs. 
Default 
Draws an outline. Refer to Draw Outlines for further 
information. 
Cubic 
Draws an outline that consists of polynomial curves. 
Revision 
Draws a revision cloud. Refer to Draw Revision Clouds 
for further information. 
Cloud Outward 
Draws a cloud with outward facing arcs. 
Close 
Links the two endpoints of the cloud with an 
automatically drawn arc and ends the command. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
Draw Revision Clouds 
Draws a revision cloud. The cloud is created with multiple arc segments, vertices are placed at each change of 
direction. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 402
The prompt Specify the first vertex or <Default> is displayed. 
Input R at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify the first vertex or <Default> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the first point of the cloud. 
The prompt Specify the next vertex or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the cloud. Multiple arcs 
are drawn between the first and second points. 
The prompt Specify the next vertex or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the cloud. Multiple arcs 
are drawn between the specified points. 
The prompt Specify the next vertex or <Close> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the cloud. Multiple arcs 
are drawn between the specified points. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple vertices can be created. The first point of a new arc vertex is positioned at the 
same point as the second point of the previous arc vertex. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Cloud Inward 
Draws a cloud with inward facing arcs. Refer to Draw 
Clouds for further information. 
Default 
Draws an outline. Refer to Draw Outlines for further 
information. 
Cubic 
Draws an outline that consists of polynomial curves. 
Revision 
Draws a revision cloud. 
Segment Depth 
Specifies the depth of each cloud segment. The 
default value is 1. The allowed values are in the range 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 403
of -2 to 2. 
Segment Distance 
Specifies the distance between each cloud segment. 
The default value is 10. The allowed values are in the 
range of 5 to 30. 
Cloud Outward 
Draws a cloud with outward facing arcs. Refer to Draw 
Clouds for further information. 
Close 
Links the two endpoints of the cloud with 
automatically drawn arcs and ends the command. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
Draw Outlines 
Draws an outline. The vertices of an outline can consist of a mixture of straight lines and arcs. However, outlines 
can also be drawn which consist of polynomial curves. 
The prompt Specify the first vertex or <Cloud Outward> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the first point of the outline. 
The prompt Specify the next vertex or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension and angle fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the outline. A 
vertex is drawn between the first and second points. 
The prompt Specify the next vertex or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension and angle fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the outline. A 
vertex is drawn between the specified points. 
The prompt Specify the next vertex or <Close> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension and angle fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the outline. A 
vertex is drawn between the specified points. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple outlines can be created. The first point of a new outline is positioned at the 
same point as the second point of the previous outline. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 404
Cubic 
Draws an outline that consists of polynomial curves. 
Cloud Outward 
Draws a cloud with outward facing arcs. Refer to Draw 
Clouds for further information. 
Cloud Inward 
Draws a cloud with inward facing arcs. Refer to Draw 
Clouds for further information. 
Revision 
Draws a revision cloud. Refer to Draw Revision Clouds 
for further information. 
Arc 
Draws an arc. 
Default 
Draws an outline. 
Line 
Draws a line.

Links the two endpoints of the outline with an 
automatically drawn line and ends the command.

Page 405
Modify Outline Type 
Clouds, revision clouds and outlines are all created as OUTL elements. When an OUTL element is selected, 
functionality is available to modify the outline type using the Properties window. 
Convert Outline to Revision Cloud 
The OUTL is converted to a revision cloud, the default 
segment depth and segment distance is used. 
Convert Outline to Cloud 
The OUTL is converted to a cloud, the default arc 
segment dimensions are used. 
Convert Outline to Polygon 
The OUTL is converted to an outline, straight line 
segments are used. 
Functionality is available to modify revision cloud segments using the Properties window. 
Segment Depth 
Specifies the depth of each cloud segment. The 
default value is 1. The allowed values are in the range 
of -2 to 2. 
Segment Distance 
Specifies the distance between each cloud segment. 
The default value is 10. The allowed values are in the 
range of 5 to 30.

Adds a circle to a drawing. 
Draw Circle with Specified Radius 
Draws a circle with a specified radius.

The circle is drawn and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.

Draws a circle with a specified diameter using a distance or reference point. 
The prompt Specify the centre point or <Circumference> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the center point of the circle. 
The prompt Specify the diameter or <Radius> is displayed. 
Input C at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify distance or reference point or <Radius> is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter or click to identify a reference point, with relation to the 
center point of the circle. 
The prompt Specify the diameter <or Radius> is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the diameter of the circle, with respect 
to the reference point. 
The circle is drawn and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Circumference 
Draws a circle with a specified circumference. Refer to 
Draw Circle with Two Specified Points for further 
information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 410
Radius 
Draws a circle with a specified radius. Refer to Draw 
Circle with Specified Radius for further information. 
Change reference point 
Specifies a radius distance or reference point. Refer to 
Draw Circle with Specified Radius using a Distance or 
Reference Point for further information. 
Draw Rectangles and Diamonds 
Adds a rectangle or a diamond to a drawing. 
Draw Rectangle with Specified Corners 
Draws a rectangle with specified corners. 
The prompt Specify the first corner or <Centre> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the first corner of the rectangle. 
The prompt Specify the opposite corner or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the opposite corner of the rectangle. 
The rectangle is drawn and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Fillet 
Sets the fillet radius of the corners. Refer to Draw 
Rectangle with Specified Corners and Set Fillet Radius 
for further information. 
Centre 
Draws a rectangle with a specified center point. Refer 
to Draw Rectangle with Specified Corners and Set 
Fillet Radius for further information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 411
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Draw Rectangle with Specified Centre Point 
Draws a rectangle with a specified center point. 
The prompt Specify the first corner or <Centre> is displayed. 
Input C at the prompt or press Enter. 
The prompt Specify centre point or <Fillet> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the center of the rectangle. 
The prompt Specify the width is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter or move the cursor left or right to define the width of the 
rectangle. Click to confirm the required width. 
The prompt Specify the height is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter or move the cursor up or down to define the height of the 
rectangle. Click to confirm the required height. 
The rectangle is drawn and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Fillet 
Sets the fillet radius of the corners. Refer to Draw 
Rectangle with Specified Centre Point and Set Fillet 
Radius for further information. 
Back 
Undoes the last action.

Draws a rectangle with specified corners and a set fillet radius.

The prompt Specify the fillet radius <#mm> is displayed. 
Input a value in the dimension field and press Enter. The radius is applied to all rectangles drawn in the session. 
The prompt Specify the first corner or <Centre> is displayed. 
Click to identify the first corner of the rectangle. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the first corner of the rectangle. 
The prompt Specify the opposite corner or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the opposite corner of the rectangle. 
The rectangle is drawn and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.

Sets the fillet radius of the corners.

Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the center point of the diamond.

Draw Arcs 
Adds an arc to a drawing. 
Draw Arc with Specified Center, End and Angle 
Draws an arc with a specified center point, end point and sweep angle. 
The prompt Specify the centre point or <Through> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the center point of the arc. 
The prompt Specify the start point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the start point of the arc. 
The prompt Specify the sweep angle is displayed. 
Input an angular value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the end point of the arc. 
An arc is then drawn between the specified start point and the sweep angle point. The command ends 
automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Through 
Draws an arc with specified start, end and through 
points. Refer to Draw Arc with Specified Start, End and 
Through Points for further information. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Draw Arc with Specified Start, End and Through Points 
Draws an arc with specified start, end and through points. 
The prompt Specify the start point or <Angle> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the start position of the arc. 
The prompt Specify the end point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the end position of the arc. 
The prompt Specify the through point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the mid-point of the arc. 
An arc is drawn between the start point, through point and end point. The command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.

Draw Elliptical Arc with Specified Axes, Start and End Angle 
Draws an elliptical arc with specified axes, start and end angle. 
The prompt Specify the start of the first axis or <Centre> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the start point of the first axis of the 
ellipse that the arc is derived from. 
The prompt Specify the first axis length is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the length of the first axis. 
The prompt Specify the second axis length or <#mm> is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the length of the first axis. 
The prompt Specify the start angle or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input an angular value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the start position of the arc.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

The prompt Specify the end angle or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input an angular value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the end position of the arc. 
The arc is drawn and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.

Input an angular value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the rotation angle around the first axis. 
The prompt Specify the start angle or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input an angular value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the start position of the arc. 
The prompt Specify the end angle or <Finish> is displayed. 
The arc is drawn and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.

Adds an equilateral triangle to a drawing.

Draws an equilateral triangle with specified vertices.

triangle. 
The prompt Specify the second vertex or side length or is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the position of the second corner of the 
triangle, therefore also specifying the length of all sides of the triangle. 
The triangle is drawn and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.

Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.

Use the Border Grid Definition window to define and position a drawing border grid. The drawing border is 
defined using a grid template. The grid section coordinates can be used to identify the position of symbols, for 
example, an off page connector.

Grid Template

Add

Delete 
Deletes the currently selected grid template. 
Font Size 
Specifies the font size for the grid template text. 
Create mode 
Specifies the grid creation actions that are available for selection. 
Draw new grid 
Creates a border grid with a generated internal 
coordinates system.

coordinates system.

Starting point of template

Specifies the horizontal grid section annotation direction. 
Left 
The annotations start at the left side of the horizontal 
grid. 
Right 
The annotations start at the right side of the 
horizontal grid. 
Horizontal Template Type 
Specifies the character type for horizontal grid section annotations. 
Letter 
Populates the horizontal grid with alphabetical 
characters. 
Number 
Populates the horizontal grid with numerical 
characters. 
Starting value 
Specifies the start value for the horizontal grid annotations, subsequent grid sections are populated in ascending 
order.

summarizing the problem.

Letter

characters. 
Number 
Populates the vertical grid with numerical characters. 
Starting value

order. 
If the specified value is not compatible with the Vertical Template Type, an error message displays summarizing 
the problem. 
Horizontal Start Distance

Drawing Length

Vertical End Distance 
Specifies the distance from the end of the grid template to the edge of the vertical grid section. 
Vertical Mid Distance 
Specifies the height of the vertical grid sections. 
Vertical Start Distance

Drawing Height

Copies a selected object.

Copy and Paste Objects 
Copies objects to a specified position on the same sheet. 
The prompt Select objects to copy is displayed. 
Note: 
The prompt does not display if one or more objects are already selected. 
Click to identify the objects to copy and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify base point or <Displacement> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify a base point relative to which the objects 
are copied. 
The prompt Specify the second point or <First point as displacement> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the new location of the objects. The 
objects are copied to the new position relative to the specified base point. 
If single mode is currently selected, the objects are moved and the command ends automatically. If multiple 
mode is selected, the prompt Specify the second point or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the new location of the objects. The 
objects are copied to the new position relative to the specified base point. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple copies of the objects from the clipboard can be pasted. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.

Page 448
To display the document information in grids, sub-grids and data sheets add the DRWGIN attribute to the 
Database (DB) View. 
Manage Subscriptions 
When a discipline issues data and it becomes available to be consumed by other disciplines, those other 
disciplines need to know when the data is available. Notifications are used to indicate that updated data is 
available. 
Not all disciplines are interested in changes to all attributes on all element types. In order to control the 
notifications received by a discipline, a subscription mechanism is available that works on a per-attribute level 
and is set up, team by team. 
A set of information is available that describes the set of attributes, by element type, which teams may be 
interested in viewing. Database Views, and the Database View definitions are immediately available to use in 
AVEVA Engineering Configuration and Lexicon. 
The same Database View can be used to subscribe to more than one team. Their does not remain an automatic 
persistence between Database Views and Teams. The subscribed Database Views for a team are retained, only to 
support Unsubscription. Changes to Database Views without re-creating the subscription do not reflect any 
changes in the subscriptions for teams. Further, only Attribute Columns are used to create subscriptions. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 449
Expression Columns are ignored for the purpose of subscription, in this context. 
Define Team View Subscriptions 
The Team-View Subscriptions window is used to create the subscriptions that define which teams (for example, 
targets) receive notifications when an attribute change in a (subscribed) Database View is approved in the 
Controlled Object Revisioning (COR) workflow. 
From the Team-View Subscriptions window the following functionality is available: 
Note: 
If the Shared Service is not available or configured correctly, an error message displays summarizing the 
problem. Check that Shared Services is installed and the AVEVA.SharedServices.Client.dll.config is set-up 
correctly. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 450
Project Teams 
Select a Project Team from the list, the current Team 
View Subscriptions display on the right hand side of 
the window. 
Input a value in the Project Teams field and press 
Enter to filter the list of Project Teams. The default is 
all. 
Project Teams that display in the list with an asterisk 
suffix contain pending changes (for example, A 
Database View that has been added or removed) that 
have not yet been committed to the Shared Services. 
The asterisk suffix also appears in the title of the 
window to indicate that pending changes are present. 
Subscribed Views 
A list of Subscribed Views displays for the currently 
selected Project Team. 
Click to move the currently selected item(s) from the 
Project Views list to the Subscribed Views list. 
Click to move the currently selected item(s) from the 
Subscribed Views list to the Project Views list. 
Project Views 
A list of Project Views displays for the currently 
selected Project Team. 
Select from the drop-down list to filter the list of 
Project Views. The default is all. If an item is removed 
from the Subscribed Views list and does not match the 
current filter in the Project Views list, it does not 
display in the Project Views list. 
Delete all Subscriptions 
Click to remove all items from the Subscribed Views 
list. 
Note: 
Team View subscriptions are committed to the Shared Service at attribute level. The procedure of breaking down 
the view into its individual attributes and committing them to the Shared Services is an intensive process that 
can impact User Interface performance. Committing a number of views to a number of teams as a single process 
(for example, making a number of pending changes and then pressing OK or Apply) can cause a delayed 
response to the User Interface and the Team-View Subscriptions window may indicate that it is not responding. If 
this happens, do not try to close the window, wait for the User Interface to respond. The suggested best practice 
is to commit a small number of views to a single team (for example, add up to four database views to one team) 
and then press apply to commit the change. The process can be repeated to commit further subscriptions. 
Reclassification Notifications 
Use the Subscribe to Cross-discipline Notifications window to subscribe discipline teams to receive notifications 
for cross-discipline teams. All members of the subscribed teams will receive notifications and only teams 
managing distributed attributes should be subscribed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 451
Subscriptions and notifications are stored independently of engineering project data and are not deleted when a 
version of a project is deleted. Inconsistencies with notifications may arise in some circumstances: 
• in evaluation phases where customers reuse and restore different versions of the same project, for example: 
when a tag created in a copy of a project does not exist in a restored copy of a project. 
• when databases are reconfigures and database ids are changed 
In these cases, it is advisable to clear notifications and subscriptions before the project is changed or the 
databases are reconfigured. 
Class & Attributes 
Use the functionality to map ETAP elements types to AVEVA Engineering classes and SEE3D e-catalog attributes 
to library items in AVEVA Engineering. 
Import/ExportToFromETAP 
The Import/ExportToFromETAP tab is populated with the ‘out of the box’ mappings of ETAP element types and 
AVEVA Engineering Class types. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 452
ETAP has a set number of element types which are mapped to the equivalent class in AVEVA Engineering. 
However there are three cases in the ‘out of box’ mappings provided by AVEVA, where the ETAP Element cannot 
be mapped directly to the equivalent class in AVEVA Engineering or it does not exist in ETAP. 
In AVEVA Engineering, there is no single object called transformer, there is a transformer with classes as a single 
object with one or many windings. 
For example: a XFORM2W (ETAP Transformer element) has three classes created for AVEVA Engineering. A 
transformer, a primary and secondary winding, as windings do not exist in ETAP the windings have the prefix ENG 
which are can be seen in the Mappings window below and the same prefix is used for pseudo attributes (which 
are not actual element types). 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 453
Other cases are Cable Data Size (ENGCDS) where the ETAP data is imported into AVEVA Engineering as a cable 
sizing data record (this is not the physical sizing cable data but the data that is required to size the cable) and 
connecting objects together in the database (ENGFEDITEM). 
Note: 
The import of ETAP data to AVEVA Engineering data is governed by a style sheet provided by AVEVA. Refer to 
ETAP Integration for more information, for the other tabs displayed in the Mappings window. Refer to the AVEVA 
Simulation Integration Setup User Guide. 
Create a new ETAP mapping 
Select + to display the Create New Mapping window, enter the details for the new mapping into the Create New 
Mapping window, ETAP to Engineering Tag is the default. 
ETAP Dumpster 
Within ETAP application there is a section that is titled dumpster, this is effectively the holding area for all 
logically deleted tags in the application. The ETAP interface in AVEVA Engineering supports the transfer of 
logically deleted tags between AVEVA Engineering and ETAP. 
Within the mappings for ETAP interface there is a new attribute that appears against all the mapped Engineering 
Tag classes. This is called ENGLogicalDelete for ETAP element type and ETAP Logical Delete for AVEVA 
Engineering class. 
ETAP Mapping Attributes 
The following is an example of the key ETAP mapping attributes and how the style sheet is used to translate the 
data between ETAP and AVEVA Engineering. The example describes the import of the tag's Connection Voltage 
attribute value from ETAP to AVEVA Engineering. The style sheet used is ETAPImportTrans.xslt. 
The GetFormatedConnectionVoltage method converts ETAP voltage into engineering format. It requires two 
input attributes values from ETAP to passed into the style sheet. These attributes are ETAP tag's voltage and 
project frequency values. 
ENGConnectionVoltage holds the converted engineering formatted voltage value. The code snippet below

based on the number of phases from ETAP.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 455
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 456
The method displayed below sets the other parameters used in Connection Voltage that is, 'V' unit, 'Hz' unit and 
Phase Set for example: Normal. 
The window displayed below creates Connection Voltage values with AVEVA Engineering and displays the

The example displayed below shows the attribute being called for WTGEN element type. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 457
SEE3D 
Use this functionality to map attributes from the SEE Electrical web catalog to AVEVA Engineering library item 
attributes. Once the attributes have been mapped, the data can be downloaded from the SEE Electrical web 
catalog to populate the AVEVA Engineering library attributes. This library data will propagate to the AVEVA 
Engineering when the library items are assigned to AVEVA Engineering instances. 
Note: 
This first release is for evaluation purposes only. 
Free Text Cells Mapping to Database Attributes Upgrade 
If a datasheet template contains one or more free-text cells, you can map each of those cells to an attribute. For 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 458
example, to update the description of a tag from within a datasheet, link a free-text cell in the datasheet to the 
Description attribute. 
As an Administrator, I would like the ability for free text cells that are mapped to a database attribute to set the 
values (free text cells) to the database, so that it will assist in the transfer of existing datasheet templates. 
Because the free text cell still has the value, If we have a free text cell subsequently mapped to the database, it 
will write that cell's value to the database. 
Free text cells need to be mapped to the desired user defined or database attributes in the datasheet templates. 
Instances when database attributes already contain values and also free text cells hold data, such cells need to 
be highlighted in red with data in it as free text cell's data. This is to let the Administrator know the update has 
failed during upgrade. From the next open onwards, the DB values will be shown just like today. 
The attribute mapping to free text cell doesn’t support inside continuation area.

Adds a counter rule to the naming 
rule. 
Delete Naming Part 
Deletes the selected naming part. 
Move Naming Part Up 
Moves the naming part higher in 
the order. 
Move Naming Part Down 
Moves the naming part lower in 
the order. 
Preview 
The Preview part of the Naming Rule window displays the currently selected naming rule. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 463
A 
Alphanumeric part of the naming rule 
N 
Numeric part of the naming rule 
(N) 
Not required part of the naming rule 
Restricted Characters 
The default list of restricted characters for naming parts in the naming rules is displayed in the Restricted 
character window. If required the system administrator can add to this list. 
When the user enters a character from this list the field is highlighted with a red outline, along with a tooltip 
‘The value X must only contain allowable name characters’. This functionality can be observed in Engineer, 
Datasheets and Grid and Tag Editor. 
Note: 
This functionality is only available in TMS enabled projects. 
Range Configurations 
Use the functionality to define and configure counter ranges for a class and associated set of rules to adhere in 
order to apply the defined ranges, to be configured by a project administrator. Tag creation for the class with an 
associated counter range is constrained to use counter within the defined range. 
The Counter Range Reservation window is used for listing and /or editing counter range configurations so that 
engineer user can create, edit or delete counter range configuration(s.) 
Note: 
Upon first use the Range reservation window is displayed as below: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 464
All the counter range configurations are displayed in the Configuration pane of the Range configurations
window. The New/Edit configuration pane displays the name, class, type, counter range and defined rules for 
the currently selected configuration. 
Range Reservation 
Each configuration is based on a class, attributes can be added based on the class selected for the configuration. 
Each configuration can have multiple rules, each rules or multiple conditions are composed together with an 
And/Or. These range configuration will apply to all the naming rules for the class where the naming rules uses 
the attribute in the configuration and has a counter. Child class will inherit the range configuration from the 
parent class. 
Add a configuration 
1. From the Range configurations window, click Add New Range Configuration the New/edit configuration 
pane of the Range configurations is populated as displayed below. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 465
2. Enter a name for the new range configuration in the Name field. 
3. From the Select Class window, select a class from the list of available classes, then press select (once a class 
has been selected, it cannot be changed). 
4. Select the + tab, to add a rule to the configuration. 
5. Enter a Min value in the Min value field. 
6. Enter a Max value in the Max value filed. 
Note: 
The minimum value cannot be equal to or more than maximum value 
7. Define the rule using attributes, click browse next to the Attributes field. The Select Attributes window is 
displayed. 
8. Create expressions using attributes, select an attribute from the list of available attributes displayed in the 
Select Attributes window. Attributes can be added or deleted using the Select Attribute window. 
9. Enter a value for the selected attribute in the Value field. 
Note: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 466
Once a rule is satisfied, the defined counter range is applied during tag creation. 
Note: Within a configuration no two rules can have an overlapping range. For example: Rule1 has Min 201 
and Max 300. All other rules in this configuration should not use a range between 201 and 300. A rule cannot 
be set with Min 250 and Max 300 because that will create an overlap of reserved range. 
Rules 
• Every rule will have at-least 1 condition at any time 
• Condition will be composed of following: 
• condition composer - And/Or 
• an attribute belonging to the class of config 
• comparison operator - =, <>, <, <=, >, >= 
• value for the condition 
• Attribute can be set by selecting an attribute from the Select Attribute window. Only attributes valid for an 
configuration class are displayed in the Select Attribute window. 
• The condition composer for the first condition is empty and can never be set, every other condition 
composer in the rule must be set 
• Depending on the attribute's data type the comparison operators should be filtered out or in for example: if 
the attribute is of string data type then <, <=, >, >= should not be shown/selectable because they are not 
valid for string data type but they should be shown/selectable if attribute is of numeric data type 
• There will be an option to remove a condition, the last condition cannot be removed 
• An attribute value cannot be empty. 
Copy a rule 
Select Copy to copy selected rule, the copied rule is appended to the list of rules and becomes the selected rule. 
Delete a rule 
Select Delete to delete selected rule. 
Edit a range configuration 
Select a configuration from the Configuration list, modify as required. 
Delete a range configuration 
Select a configuration from the Configuration list, then select delete, the selected configuration from the list 
after confirmation. 
Sequence Rules in the Rules list 
A selected rule can be moved up or down in the list in order to set the execution order of the rule within the list. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 467
Moving the rule up swaps the position of selected rule with the rule just above it. If selected rule is topmost in 
the list then option to move up is disabled. 
Moving the rule down swaps the position of selected rule with the rule just below it. If selected rule is at bottom 
of the list then option to move down is disabled. 
Search Configuration 
Use the filter in the Configuration pane of the Range configurations window to filter out range configurations 
based on Class Name or Class Type. 
Update Tags Registry 
An existing Tag Management System (TMS) project can be structurally changed to be compatible with TMR by 
carrying out a project upgrade. 
Such projects have tags that were created using Engineering service, these tags are available in the Engineering 
database but they wont be reserved in the TMR service. Any tags that don't have a reservation in TMR service 
can end up having duplicates, for example: 05-FT-01 is a flow transmitter created in TMS, it's counter is 01, 
because it is not reserved with TMR so TMR logic treats 01 as an available counter for flow transmitters. In such 
cases another user can create a duplicate flow transmitter with tag number 05-FT-01. To avoid duplicates, all 
tags whose naming rule either writes to NAME attribute or has a counter should be reserved with TMR service. 
The update registry functionality provides the ability to reserve existing tags with TMR service, it scans all 
existing ENGITE and FUNITE based elements and then updates the TMR service with the tag number and counter 
information extracted from these elements. 
A message window is displayed Update Tags Registry with project data, click Yes to start the update operation. 
The Update registry window is displayed and a progress bar displays the progress of the update process. 
Note: 
Scanning and updating the tags registry with project data can take a long time. 
Once the update of tag registry has been completed, the Update registry window is populated with the total 
number of elements scanned, tag numbers processed and the tags numbers not processed (information is also 
provided for why tag numbers have not been processed). Log files for the tag numbers processed or not 
processed can be exported. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 468
Consider the following before using the update registry functionality: 
• In a Multiple Database (MDB) that has access to all the engineering databases with tags to be updated- the 
functionality scans and updates all ENGITE and FUNITE based elements in the current MDB (even if they are 
read-only). Existing tag numbers are expected to be in update with their naming rules. 
• Any tags that have a naming rule with a counter are expected to have corresponding counter element(s). 
Tags that have a naming rule with a counter but not the counter element(s) will not be updated with TMR 
service. 
• Tags created prior to the introduction of counter options will not have counter elements. These tags need to 
be purged and re-created with Counter override option. 
• On a live customer project, if purging and recreating is not an option then the correct counter elements can 
be created with an ad-hoc script or add-in, contact your local AVEVA Technical Support via the AVEVA 
Knowledge & Support Center website https://softwaresupport.aveva.com/. 
Note: 
Any tags that have a tag number not conforming to its naming rule has the potential to create garbage data 
in TMR service. For example - a naming rule is composed of Area Number, Name Precedent and Counter 
attributes. For such a naming rule a tag number 05-FT-01 should have "05" in Area number attribute, FT in 
the Name Precedent attribute and 01 as the counter value. If these values are not as expected then wrong 
data will be updated with TMR service. 
Unreserve 
Use the functionality to unreserve the partially reserved tags in the TMR database. Partially reserved tags are a 
result of the TMR not being available (for example: quit work or a crash) where the counters are partially 
reserved during the creation of the tags. 
This is an administration function. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 469
Click to select the tag(s) in the Partially reserved tags in TMR pane, then click Release selected tags. Upon 
completion, a message ‘Tags released successfully’ is displayed in the Messages window. If the TMR is 
unavailable an error message ‘TMR service is unavailable Failed to get partially reserved tags’ is displayed in the 
Messages window. 
Click to highlight the tags in the Unreserve window then click Release selected tags. If successful, a message is 
displayed in the Messages window: ‘Tags released successfully’. 
SEE Electrical 3D Panel+ Set Up 
For further information on the set up and the configuration refer to the support and training sites. 
Data Model 
As a user with administrative rights, use Data Model to create a conceptual model from a base model with some 
predefined definitions or based on an imported external file. Once a conceptual model has been created the 
user can build their database definition model for use in AVEVA Engineering. Implementation models can be 
created or added based on an imported external file (model). There will always be a Database Definitions model 
which reflects the current Dabacon Database structure. 
The Data Model functionality is available in the AVEVA Engineering Configuration module. 
Welcome 
When entering Data Model for the first time, the Welcome to DATA MODEL window is displayed. As a 
conceptual model has not been imported or created, all of the functionality is unavailable for selection. 
The main part of the window is populated with short descriptions of the main tools of Data Model. 
Select Import Model to create a model based on an imported external file or select Base Model to create a 
model with some preset definitions (units of measure, measure classes and disciplines). 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 470
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 471
Work Area 
Use the Start functionality to get started and to create the conceptual model: 
• Home - use this page to get started with the Data Model functionality. 
• Import - use the drop-down menu to import a conceptual model, create an implementation model or to 
preview the conceptual model content. 
Use the View functionality to manage opened or created models: 
• Diagram View - use this diagrammatic view to see the structure of the model. 
• Grid View - use this view to see a grid of details about the objects in the model. 
• Mappings - use this view to see and edit mappings between the models. 
• Restricted Lists - use this view to see and edit restricted lists between attributes. 
• Properties - use this window to modify model properties. 
Use the Diagram functionality to view and modify objects when using the diagram view. 
Use the Grid functionality to view and modify objects when using the grid view. 
Use the Evaluation functionality to validate the vocabularies and to to display suggestions on how to streamline 
the data model. 
Use the Contextual View functionality to create and define contextual views. 
Use the Finish functionality to generate export files or to build the model. 
Use the Post Build functionality to create DBVIEWs from the conceptual model. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering

Page 472
Home 
The Home window displays detailed information about the conceptual model. 
Use the Base Definitions functionality to add engineering definitions to the conceptual model or to add items to 
the model base definitions. 
Select the Active Vocabularies check boxes to define the available vocabularies for the conceptual model. Refer 
to Vocabulary for further information. By default, the available vocabularies are defined from the settings of the 
most recent saved session. 
Select Validate to run an automatic validation process on the active vocabularies. Use the Validation window to 
add missing items and fix validation errors. Refer to Validate Vocabularies for further information. 
Use the Identities functionality to define identities to which model items can be associated. Refer to Identities 
for further information. 
Use the Contextual Views functionality to define contextual views to which model items can be associated. Refer 
to Contextual Views for further information. 
The Schema part of the window displays the version of the data modelling schema. 
The Model Statistics part of the window displays the number of classes, attributes and class associations in the 
model. 
The Learn part of the window displays an overview of the diagram, grid, mapping and vocabulary functionality. 
Import 
The models that already exist are listed, selecting one will import into that model.

Conceptual Model 
A conceptual model displays the model that will be used in AVEVA Engineering. The conceptual model can be 
created from scratch using the base model option (includes units of measure, measure classes and disciplines in 
line with those in Dabacon) or by creating a conceptual model based on an imported file. If based on an 
imported file, the diagram view is populated with a view of the classes in the imported model. Otherwise, the 
diagram view will initially be empty. Refer to Diagram View for further information. 
Conceptual Model Properties 
When using the diagram view or grid view, select the Conceptual Model properties pen to display the 
conceptual model properties. 
Enter a name and description for the conceptual model. By default, the Model Statistics, Data Types and 
Mapped Models tabs are collapsed.

information.

currently selected model.

Select from the drop-down list to define the number 
of levels for classes to be active in the build. For

leaf classes, plus one level of superclasses ,will be 
active have instances created. It is beneficial to

Diagram View 
Use the Diagram View to display the structure of a conceptual model, database definitions or contextual view. 
Use the functionality available to navigate the structure, relationships between objects and their connections. 
Search and filter the objects shown, add, edit and connect objects and their properties. 
The diagram displays the following classes and the parent/child relationships between them: 
• Active in build classes (blue) 
• Inactive in build classes (white) 
• Read only classes (grey) 
A list of all the classes in the model are displayed in a list format on the top left hand side of the diagram. 
To navigate to a selected class from the list in the diagram view, double click it or select it from the displayed 
model, once selected the Properties pane tracks the selection. 
Note: 
The properties pen does not display when viewing a contextual view.

associations. 
Show Overview

orange box can be moved around the model, the Diagram Overview pane can be docked if required. This option 
is also available using the right click menu.

Use the Switch Layout functionality to view the relationships from the top down, by default the relationships are

AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering

Page 482
Show All Objects 
Displays all the objects in the model in the diagram view. This option can also be used to return all the objects to 
the diagram view when using the Set As Focus functionality. Refer to Right Click Menu for further information. 
Show All Root Objects 
Displays all of the root classes (classes without parents) with all child classes collapsed. This option can also be 
used to return all the root objects to the diagram view when using the Set As Focus functionality. Refer to Right 
Click Menu for further information. 
Note: 
By default, the diagram view displays all root objects when the application is started. 
Automatic Refresh 
The Automatic Refresh toggle at the bottom of the diagram can be turned off to suppress diagram view refresh 
when changes are made to the model. For example, automatic refresh will automatically update the class 
arrangement when a new object is created. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 483
The Refresh command, available from the ribbon and the right click menu can be used to manually refresh the 
diagram view. 
Enable Manual Movement 
The Enable Manual Movement toggle at the bottom of the diagram can be turned on to allow manual 
positioning of diagram items. 
Note: 
The class arrangement is not preserved by the application between sessions. 
Editing the Model using the Diagram View 
The classes in the diagram view can be selected and moved so that new relationships can be made more easily. 
Connecting Classes 
The relationship lines in the Diagram View represent parent child (inheritance) relationships. Each oval of a class 
has one square on either side, left and right or top and bottom, depending on the selected layout. These are 
what we call expanders and display as empty if there are no relationships from that expander, a + if the class 
relationships are not shown and a - if the classes are all shown. 
The expanders can be dragged and dropped from one class to another to create new relationships. Dragging the 
expander from the left or bottom of a class will make the class you drop the expander on a parent of the class 
you started from. Dragging the right or top expander onto another class will make the class you drop the 
expander on a child of the class you started from. 
A class cannot be set as a child of a drawing class. If an attempt is made to set a class as the child of a drawing 
class, an error message is displayed summarizing the problem. 
Mouse Controls 
Use the mouse functionality to select objects and modify the view. 
Left Mouse Click 
Selects the object. If an object is already selected, click 
on a different object to select that object instead. 
Ctrl + Left Mouse Click 
Adds the object to a multiple selection group of 
objects. Ctrl + left click again to remove the object 
from the multiple selection group of objects. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 484
Shift + Left Mouse Drag 
Dragging a rectangular marquee selection around a 
group of objects includes all objects totally enclosed 
within the marquee selection, or all objects totally 
enclosed plus all objects the marquee selection 
crosses to a multiple selection group of objects. 
To include objects totally enclosed in a marquee 
selection, begin the rectangular marquee selection by 
clicking top or bottom left and then releasing top or 
bottom right to complete the rectangle. 
To include objects totally enclosed plus all objects the 
marquee selection crosses, begin the rectangular 
marquee selection by clicking top or bottom right and 
then releasing top or bottom left to complete the 
rectangle. 
Middle Mouse Drag 
Moves the view. 
Shift + Middle Mouse Drag 
Dragging a rectangular marquee selection zooms to a 
selected area. 
Middle Mouse Scroll Up/Down 
Zooms in/out. 
Right Mouse Click 
Displays a contextual menu for the selected object

Toggle Active in Build/Inactive in Build 
Turns the class active in build status to active which 
means it will be instantiated when the model is built, 
or inactive which means it will not be instantiated 
when the model is built. 
Active in build classes are displayed blue and inactive 
in build classes are displayed white on the diagram 
view. 
Inactive in build classes will not have a User Defined 
Element (UDET) created for them and will not be 
displayed in the Engineer module. 
Cascade Active in Build status down to children 
Inherits the active in build status from the currently 
selected class to all descendent classes in the diagram 
view. 
Toggle Pin 
Turns the object pin on or off. If an object is pinned, it 
remains visible regardless of the current layout option. 
Delete Object 
Deletes the currently selected object. 
Delete Highlighted 
Deletes all highlighted objects. 
Delete Object and Descendants 
Deletes the currently selected item and all 
descendants. 
Collapse All 
Collapses all descendants for the currently selected 
item. 
Expand All 
Displays all descendants for the currently selected 
item. 
Set As Focus 
Zooms to the currently selected object in the diagram 
view, and hides all objects that are not in its hierarchy. 
Highlight Ancestors 
Highlights the ancestors of the currently selected 
object in the diagram view. 
Highlight Descendants 
Highlights the descendants of the currently selected 
object in the diagram view. 
Forwards Associated Objects 
Select All Forwards 
Selects all associated objects with an association from 
the currently selected object in the diagram view. 
Select All References 
Selects all associated objects with a reference 
association from the currently selected object in the 
diagram view. 
Note: 
Other options are available dependent on the

Selects all associated objects with a reference 
association to the currently selected object in the 
diagram view. 
Note: 
Other options are available dependent on the 
association types applicable to the currently selected 
object. For example, Select All Can Be Part Of.

Active in Build 
Class is set to Active in Build, which means it will be 
instantiated when the model is built. By default, the 
Class is set to Active in Build when a new object is 
created. 
Inactive in Build

for them and will not be displayed in the Engineerr 
module.

Inherited Attributes 
When relationships are created in the diagram between classes, a parent and child relationship is formed. This 
means that the attributes and associations are inherited from the parent class to the child. These are then 
grouped into the inherited part of the Properties pane for the class in the attribute and associations sections. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 491
The grouping can be turned off using the Group attributes by owning class functionality. As a result, the 
inherited attributes and associations are displayed with ones actually on the class. The elements will have the 
attributes displayed, both inherited and local. 
Note: 
The inherited attributes and associations can be hidden from the view. This doesn't remove them from the class 
but allows the user to cut down the numbers shown. 
Class Associations 
Use the Class Associations part of the Classes window to add, view and edit class association properties. Refer to 
Class Associations for further information.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 492
Inherited Associations 
Use the Class Associations part of the Classes window to add, view, edit and delete inherited class association 
properties. 
When relationships are created in the diagram between classes, a parent and child relationship is formed. This 
means that the attributes and associations are inherited from the parent class to the child. These are then 
grouped into the inherited part of the Properties pane for the class in the attribute and associations sections. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 493
The grouping can be turned off using the Group associations by owning class functionality, as a result the 
inherited attributes and associations are displayed with ones actually on the class. The elements will have the 
attributes displayed, both inherited and local. 
Note: 
The inherited attributes and associations can be hidden from the view. This doesn't remove them from the class 
but allows the user to cut down the numbers shown. 
Associated items can be added, deleted and all associated items can be selected for deletion. 
The check boxes adjacent to associated items can be individually selected to mark them for deletion. 
Note: 
When overriding associations associated with a vocabulary, the Cardinality of the overridden association can be 
modified if the Cardinality value of the parent is * without causing a vocabulary validation error. If the parent 
Cardinality value is an integer, is associated with a vocabulary and is modified, an error is displayed when the 
vocabularies are validated. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 494
System Generated Inverses 
Whenever an association to a class is added, an inverse association is automatically added to that class, pointing 
to the source of the added association. Only the name of the inverse class association can be edited. 
Requirement 
Use the Requirement functionality to classify conceptual model classes and attributes with an engineering 
requirement value; either Functional, Physical, Functional and Physical or Installed. 
These are industry recognized terms that communicates the context of the engineering object with respect to its 
current life cycle status as an indicator of the state of its maturity with respect to data integrity and engineering 
completeness. 
The requirement definition is part of the base definitions in a new modelling project. It is automatically added to 
existing projects. The requirement value is set from the Properties pane or the Grid View, where 'fill down' 
editing is available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 495
In the Engineer module, it is possible to filter tags and attributes on the requirement value. A pseudo-attribute 
(ENGREQ) is provided on engineering, functional and simulation tags. This takes the tag element and returns the 
requirement value of the class which the tag is created from. The pseudo-attribute can be added to database 
views and engineer grids to appear as a grid column. Database view filters can be used to create pre-filtered 
views. 
Attribute metadata filtering includes the built requirement definition. The name may include a numerical suffix if 
it clashes with an existing attribute named Requirement, for example, Requirement1. 
In the underlying Dabacon model, the requirement definition is built as a UDA on UDETs and UDAs. The Dabacon 
value for Functional And Physical is FunctionalAndPhysical. 
Attributes 
Use the Attributes functionality to view and or edit the attribute properties and to define configurable attribute 
metadata. 
Attributes can be used on one or more classes, this means that any changes made to an attribute will affect all 
classes that it is used on. Deleting a class does not delete the attributes on that class. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 496
Requirement 
Classifies conceptual model classes with an 
engineering requirement value; either Functional, 
Physical, Functional and Physical or Installed. Refer to 
Requirement for further information. 
Attribute Properties 
Domain 
The classes the attribute is on. 
Data Type 
The type of data for the attribute. 
Unit 
The unit value for the attribute. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 497
Discipline 
The discipline that will own this attribute. 
Valid Values 
Refer to Lists Of Values for further information. 
Closed Enumeration 
Refer to Lists Of Values for further information. This 
check box must be selected to enable the list of values 
to be used in list restrictions. Refer to Restricted Lists 
for further information. 
All attributes can have a category which is displayed in the Properties window. 
String attributes 
Default Value 
The default value, the default is No default value. 
Length 
Length of the string (maximum). 
Category 
The attribute category. 
Numeric attributes 
Default Value 
The default unit of measure, the default is No default 
value. 
Minimum/Maximum 
Minimum and maximum values. The fields are 
disabled if the attribute has both a Closed 
Enumeration and a Valid Enumeration, as they are no 
longer relevant. 
Array Length 
The array length. 
The field cannot be set to a value greater than 124 if 
the Store a Tag name check box is selected. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 498
Category 
The attribute category. 
Allow attribute to 
Hold multiple Case values 
Allows the attribute to have case data. 
Synchronise across Linked objects 
The value in this attribute will be synchronised with 
the other linked objects 
Store a Tag Name 
Available to be used as a Tag Management System 
naming attribute. 
The option is disabled if the Be Language Variable 
check box is selected because attributes can only have 
one string format property. 
Be Language Variable 
Available to be used with multiple language strings. 
The option is disabled if the Store a Tag Name check 
box is selected because attributes can only have one 
string format property. 
Be a Pseudo 
The attribute value can be supplied by a pseudo 
attribute function. 
Be Hidden 
The attribute value can be hidden from the view. 
Be Copied 
Specifies if the attribute is copied when a class is 
copied. By default, the check box is selected. 
Attribute Metadata 
Allows configurable attribute metadata. The 
configurable attribute metadata is stored as text and 
does not support restricted lists. 
When the model is built, the configurable attribute 
metadata is created as User Defined Attributes (UDAs) 
with a type of UDA, as they are UDAs on UDAs. 
Metadata 
The attribute metadata entry. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 499
Item 
The attribute metadata item. Selection is handled by 
the check boxes to the left of the item, only one 
metadata item can be selected. When a check box is 
selected, the previously selected check box is 
deselected. By default, the check boxes are 
deselected. 
Aspects 
Aspects allow an attribute to hold multiple different 
values, one value for each aspect. For example, the 
specification of the maximum operating temperature 
on a piece of equipment can have different values: 
• As Installed 
• As Purchased 
• As Required. 
Aspects can be defined for the different criteria, the 
attribute can then hold separate values for each. 
There is a single list of defined aspect items which can 
be added to and modified from any attribute in the 
Properties window. Selection is handled by the check 
boxes to the left of the item, multiple aspect items can 
be selected. 
The aspect values for an attribute are stored in 
separate UDAs. The properties that identify a UDA 
(Name, UDA Name, Description and Reporter Text) 
have the aspect name appended. 
If an attempt is made to add an attribute to a parent class where the attribute has already been added to a child 
class, a warning message is displayed summarizing the problem. 
Settings for attribute reflection 
Where Class A has Attribute X and Class B has Attribute Y, the model can be set up so that an instance of B can 
have its value of Y reflect the value of X on an instance of A. If Y is set to a value on B then that value is used, but 
if Y is unset on B it appears to have the value of X on A. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 500
In this example, the definition of Attribute Y must set which association to follow in order to get the value it is 
reflecting, and then which attribute to reflect. 
Associations 
Use the Associations functionality to describe relationships between classes. Associations can be created and 
will then be available to use when defining a class association. 
Name of Inverse 
The name of the auto-generated inverse of this 
association, which is used to generate inverse class 
associations. This name can be edited from the 
default. 
The base vocabulary defines a set of associations that determine behaviour in the application. New associations 
can be created, and if they are decorated with these vocabulary items, the behaviour is enabled. 
• Has Part - creates the parts automatically and parts are deleted with the main item. 
• Can Have Part - the parts are not created automatically, but parts are deleted with the main item. 
• References - the parts are not created automatically or deleted with the main item. 
• Realized By - the parts are not created automatically or deleted with the main item. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 501
Associations Example 
In this example, in the model there is an association ‘Has Part’ 
The association Has Part has been added to the Pump class in the model. Pump Has Part Impeller. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 502
The classes that are subclasses of the Pump will inherit the association, in this case Dynamic Pump, Axial Flow 
Pump and Centrifugal Pump. 
This association can be overridden on the classes that inherit it by adding or changing a range class. 
In the example, the association has been overridden on the Dynamic Pump by changing the range class to be Fan 
Impeller instead of Impeller. This override is also inherited by any subclasses, in this case Centrifugal Pump and 
Axial Flow Pump. 
A class can have a local Has Part association. For example, on Centrifugal Pump there is a local Has Part 
association to the Pump Impeller. The Centrifugal Pump has two Has Part associations, the one defined on it 
directly and the inherited one. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 503
This means that two reference attributes will be created for use in AVEVA Engineering as follows: 
• Pump | Has Part 
• On Element Types - Pump, Dynamic Pump, Centrifugal Pump, Axial Flow Pump 
• Allowed Reference Types - Impeller, Compressor Impeller, Fan Impeller, Pump Impeller 
Note: 
This reference attribute is the association on the pump which is inherited by the Dynamic Pump and also 
the Axial Flow Pump and Centrifugal Pump. The references are the Impeller and all of its children. 
• Centrifugal | Pump Has Part 
• On Element Type - Centrifugal Pump 
• Allowed Reference Types - Compressor, Dynamic Compressor, Pump Impeller 
Note: 
This reference attribute is the association on the centrifugal pump and it can only reference items of 
type Pump Impeller. It also has the member Compressor and Dynamic Compressor, which is a child of 
Compressor, because of the Has Part association on Centrifugal Pump 
Note: 
If the same association is added to a class twice and not given a different name, the second association is 
ignored on build (even if they are different disciplines). If the same association is added to a class twice 
and they have a different name, both associations are created on build. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 504
Class Associations 
Use the Class Associations functionality to create associations between classes. 
Active in Build 
Identifies the element as active in the build. 
Domain 
The classes which the class associations are on. 
Range 
The classes which are associated to the domain 
classes via the class association. 
Discipline 
Select a discipline for the class association from the 
populated Discipline drop down list. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 505
Cardinality 
The reference User Defined Attribute (UDA) that is 
created has a UDA length set to value in cardinality if 
there is one. If no cardinality is set on the class 
association then the UDA length for the referenced 
UDA created is 1. 
The UDA length is the number of elements that can be 
referenced, for example: if the UDA length is 2 then 
the reference UDA can hold 2 references. 
Class associations in restricted lists must have a 
cardinality value of 1. 
If the cardinality value is modified on a class 
association which is used in a restricted list, an error 
message displays indicating that the cardinality value 
is invalid. 
Category 
The class association category. 
Be a Pseudo 
Select the Be a Pseudo check box to mark the UDA 
that is built from the class association as a Pseudo. 
All class associations are built into a UDA. These UDAs 
contain a boolean property that indicate if they are 
Pseudo UDAs. 
Class associations decorated with Realized By from the 
Base Vocabulary are implemented as a pseudo-
attribute UDA. The Be a Pseudo option is set 
automatically and cannot be changed. The inverse of 
the class association (Specified By) is built as a 
standard UDA. 
Be Copied 
Specifies if the class association is copied when a class 
is copied. By default, the check box is selected. 
Be Hidden 
The class association can be hidden from the view. 
Build Inverse 
Select the Build Inverse check box to create additional 
UDAs during the Build Model process. The created 
UDAs can be added to DBviews which evaluate the 
inverse class association from the BACKREF attribute 
on an instance of an object. 
Discipline 
Disciplines are setup using AVEVA Administration and read into the data model. As a result, disciplines are read 
only. Refer to Multi-Disciplinary Project Data for further information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 506
If a new discipline is created, the New and Deleted Items Available window displays when the Configuration 
module is launched. Refer to New and Deleted Items Available for further information. 
The Discipline functionality is used for the distributed attributes set up. Setting a discipline on an attribute 
causes the appropriate distributed attribute set up for the attribute and any classes that it is used on. 
If required, select the preferred language. Enter the name and description for the discipline. 
Lists Of Values 
The Lists of Values functionality populates the conceptual model with any lists from an imported ISM file. Refer 
to ISM Integration for further information. 
Lists of Values that are created in the conceptual model are built into the Dabacon model. Each list contains a 
number of items. If the attribute Data Type is set to String, the predefined lists are available for selection as Valid 
Values and the user can choose a List Of Values to use in the Valid Values field. 
Select the Closed Enumeration check box to restrict the attribute in AVEVA Engineering so that only a value in 
the list of values can be entered. This also sets the Valid Values optional flag to false on the UDA that is built for 
the attribute. 
Deselect the Closed Enumeration check box to allow the use of free text in the attribute in AVEVA Engineering. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 507
When built, attributes with a selected Valid Values list create User defined Attributes (UDAs) that contain User 
defined valids (UVALIDs) for each item in the list. 
Each defined List of Values creates a User Defined List Of Values (UDLOV) in the database. 
Measure Classes 
Adding or updating the base definitions populates the measure classes by reading the Dabacon dimensions and 
defined unit sets. The Build translates these and sets the appropriate dimension on the UDA. 
Use the Measure Classes functionality to view and or edit the unit sets used and also the attributes that are 
using the measure class. 
Note: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 508
The Data Type value, defined in the Attribute properties,includes the measure classes. The selected Data Type
changes the available values in the Units drop-down list to reflect the measure class units. 
Units of Measure 
Adding or updating the base definitions populates the units of measure by reading the Dabacon Units. 
Use the Units of Measure functionality to view and or edit the units used and which measure class they are used 
on. 
The functionality is used to populate the Unit drop-down list in the Attribute properties and relates to the 
chosen Data Type. 
Grid View 
Use the Grid View to display the properties of a conceptual model, database definitions or conceptual view. 
Functionality is available to add new objects, edit or delete them and search and filter the contents of the grid 
view window. 
To add or delete an object, right click in the grid pane or use the buttons available for selection on the ribbon. 
The icons across the top of the grid view are the views for the model. 
Note: 
Only the object properties and classes views are available when viewing a contextual view. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 509
Object properties can be viewed, selected or highlighted. 
The object properties view shows all attributes and associations for item(s) that are currently selected or 
highlighted in the diagram. 
Attributes are represented by an in-built association called Can Have As Attribute, while inheritance 
relationships are also shown using Sub Class Of. The in-built associations are not displayed in the Associations 
grid. 
Multiple items can be selected on the diagram and displayed in this grid by either using shift + click, or using the 
options on the right click menu. 
The name, discipline and other properties of an object can be edited in the grid. If displayed, the Properties
window shows all details of the selected object on the grid. 
The classes are the same objects that can that can be displayed in the diagram view. The other views show other 
types not appropriate to be viewed in the diagram view as they do not have relationships with one another. 
Column Settings 
Right click in the grid pane and select Column Settings to display the Data Grid Settings window, use the Data 
Grid Settings window to add or remove columns in the grid view window. 
The example displays the column settings for a class object. Specific column settings are available for each of the 
different grids.
