Add New Property 
Click New Object from the ribbon when viewing the Object Properties grid to display the Add New Property 
window which can be used to add a new attribute, existing attribute, or class association to multiple classes. 
From the Class drop-down list, select the check box adjacent to each class to be modified. Multiple classes can 
be selected. The selected classes display at the top of the Class drop-down list. 
Select the New Attribute radio button to add a new attribute to the selected classes. Refer to Attributes for

Select the Existing Attribute radio button to add an existing attribute to the selected classes. The attribute can be

Multiple Copy Attribute Properties 
Select an attribute cell and hover the mouse over the bottom right corner of the cell to display the multiple copy 
crosshair. 
Drag the crosshair downwards to copy the attribute value to multiple cells. 
Note: 
If an attribute value cannot be copied, the multiple copy crosshair is not displayed. 
Mappings 
The Mappings window is populated with the mappings between the two selected models. By default, the View 
pane of the window is displayed which can be used to view the mappings, search for a particular mapping 
context, automap objects, sort the mappings and view all mapped or unmapped objects. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 513
The Edit pane of the Mappings window is populated with the list of the mappings, suggested mappings and 
current mappings. Use the Edit pane of the Mappings window, to remove mappings from the current mappings 
area, add new mappings from the mappings list or from suggested mappings, edit properties, search, sort, 
automap and clear all mappings. 
Restricted Lists 
Use the functionality available from the Setup Restricted Lists window to configure two attributes with lists of 
values thus enabling the available values on one attribute (the secondary) to be restricted by the selection from 
the values available on the other attribute (the primary). 
Note: 
When a secondary value is not secondary for any primary value, that secondary value is always available. Thus 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 514
allowing any new value added to the secondary list to be immediately available in all selections, unless it is set to 
secondary for at least one of the primary values. 
Note: 
Restricted lists do not work with cases. 
In the example shown below, the Oil Filter class and Rating attribute are used as the attribute on which its list of 
values are restricted. Both primary and secondary list of values are not in the same class and requires a 
connection through an association. Thus enabling the selection of an attribute from a different class. 
If required, check to select Same Class check box to display the other attributes of the Oil Filter. 
On selection of the second attribute, the primary list of values and sub-list restricting values are displayed in the 
Setup Restricted Lists window. 
In the Conceptual Model grid and List of Values views, the restricted list icon is displayed on the secondary list 
attribute (in this example - Oil System Type). 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 515
Grids 
From the oil filter instance, if Mounted On Baseplate is assigned to the Oil Filter attribute (primary list of values), 
the available values that can be assigned to Oil System Type (secondary list of values) are 1500 and 900 (always 
available) and 300. 
Datasheets 
Restricted list of values attributes can be mapped as drop down lists or radio buttons on datasheet instances. The 
values for both radio buttons and drop down lists are refreshed and restricted as per the LOV. Restricted LOVs are 
only available on datasheet instances, templates remain unchanged. 
Note: 
All of the values for the attributes on the datasheet instance are imported/exported to Excel (restricted LOV 
functionality is not applied). Modifications can be made in excel and imported back using the Datasheet Excel 
Import Wizard, only modified values in the LOV are imported. 
Implementation Models 
Use the Implementation functionality to create new implementation models or import others. The most 
common will be AVEVA AIM models which will probably be created when importing a file from ISM. 
Functionality is available from the Diagram View. Refer to Diagram View for further information.

Use the Implementation Preferences part of the Properties window to select the preferred language, define if 
the model is an AVEVA AIM model and also define the functional, document and data set root.

Preferred Language 
Select a preferred language from the preferred 
language drop-down list.

AVEVA AIM Models 
If required other Implementation Models can be imported and created. The most common will be AVEVA AIM

Database Definitions Model 
The Database Definitions window displays the Database Definitions Model which shows the underlying 
Dabacon databases. The elements are displayed as classes with Dabacon hierarchy relationships. For example, an 
owner and a member. 
Use the functionality available to navigate the structure, relationships between objects and their connections 
and search and filter the objects shown. 
Limited functionality is available. Refer to Diagram View for further information. 
Database Definitions Implementation Preferences 
When using the diagram view or grid view, select the Database Definitions properties pen to display the 
database definitions properties. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 519
Use the Implementation Preferences functionality to select the preferred language and tag creation discipline. 
The Model Statistics, Data Types, Mapped Modules and Set Active Levels options work in the same way for the 
database definitions properties as they do for the conceptual model properties. Refer to Conceptual Model 
Properties for further information. 
Preferred Language 
Select a preferred language from the drop down list. 
In this case, it will be used by the build model 
functionality to set names for the User Defined 
Elements (UDETs) and User Defined Attributes (UDAs) 
that are created. 
Build Location 
There is a limit to the number of User defined 
Elements (UDETs) and User Defined Attributes (UDAs) 
allowed in a single dictionary database and this is 
easily exceeded for a large conceptual model. A 
Database Set (DBSET) can be created in AVEVA 
Administration with several dictionary databases 
added (the recommendations is five). The DBSET is 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 520
current on the Multiple Database (MDB) along with 
the conceptual model database. 
Select the DBSET from the drop-down list The build 
process uses as much of the set of databases as it 
needs to build the model. It is also prompted for on 
build if not set. 
Note: 
It is recommended to treat this set of databases as 
reserved for the conceptual model and to use other 
dictionary databases for non-model generated 
purposes. 
Tag Creation Discipline 
For use with Tags Management System. Select a 
discipline from the drop down list which will be used 
for tag creation. The selected discipline is used to 
create the attributes displayed as ‘Allow attribute to: 
Store a Tag Name’ 
Clear Build Definitions 
Removes UDETs and UDAs from the database that are 
no longer used. For example, a related class has been 
deleted from the model. 
Note: 
This option should be used with care as it invalidates 
instantiated data. 
Run Analysis 
Use the Run Analysis functionality to display suggestions on how to streamline the data model. The model 
analysis can be used to suggest: 
• Where attributes can be moved to a superclass. 
• Where there are duplicates in the model. 
• Where there are empty classes. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 521
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 522
Model Analysis Rules 
Select the required check boxes to define the rules for 
the model analysis. 
The following rules can be selected for model analysis: 
• Properties That Can Be Moved - the rule 
determines if all the subclasses of a class have the 
same property. If they do, that property can be 
moved from the subclasses to the class. 
• Duplicate Properties On Classes - the rule 
determines if a class has multiple copies of an 
identical property. 
• Duplicate Items - the rule determines if there are 
multiple classes with the same name. 
• Duplicate List Of Values Items - the rule 
determines if a list of values contains multiple 
identical values. 
• Leaf Classes with no Attributes or Associations - 
the rule determines if a leaf class (a class with no 
children) has no attributes or associations. 
• Intermediate Classes with no Attributes - the rule 
determines if an intermediate class (a class with 
children) has no attributes or associations. 
Run Analysis 
Performs the model analysis. The suggested 
improvements display at the bottom of the Model 
Analysis window. 
Note: 
When the model analysis is running, all other windows 
are closed and the Fluent UI is disabled. 
If no suggested improvements are found, the Analysis 
Passed window displays. 
Cancel 
Cancels the process. The process can be cancelled at 
any time during the model analysis. 
Action All 
Applies all suggested improvements to the model. 
Navigate to Item 
Selects the item on the diagram view and the grid 
view. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 523
Fix 
Applies the suggested improvement to the item. 
displays adjacent to the suggested improvement and a 
message displays at the bottom of the window 
indicating the action taken. For example: 
If the suggested improvement cannot be fixed 
automatically, the option is disabled. 
Vocabulary 
The vocabulary functionality is a set of concepts that are fixed for AVEVA Engineering. One such concept is 
decorating, decorating can be thought of as a mapping between the conceptual model and vocabulary elements. 
Note: 
There are different types of vocabularies that enable business logic for different domains. A functional item is a 
decoration from the simulation vocabulary, it enables a class to produce a physical and functional 
representation. 
Decorating that class with the vocabulary entry for instrument allows AVEVA Engineering to work with the 
customer’s model. The customer can extend the model with subclasses of instrument and add attributes to meet 
their needs and all instances of all those subclasses will be handled as instruments by AVEVA Engineering. 
Vocabularies and decorations can be specified for classes, attributes, associations, class associations and lists of 
values using the Properties window or the Grid View. 
Functionality is available to display and fix vocabulary errors. Refer to Validate Vocabularies for further 
information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 524
Validate Vocabularies 
Use the Validate Vocabularies functionality to display and fix vocabulary errors. 
If the validation passes, the Validation Passed window displays. 
If the validation fails, the Validation Failed window displays. 
Click OK to display the Validation window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 525
Add All 
Adds all missing items. 
Fix All 
Fixes all validation errors. 
Messages 
Select Show All Errors from the drop-down list to 
display all validation error messages 
Select Show Only Missing Items from the drop-down 
list to display only missing items error messages. 
Select Show Only Validation Errors from the drop-
down list to display only validation error messages. 
Identities 
Identities can be added to model items in the conceptual model, allowing them to be associated with different 
owners and versions. 
Identities can be created using the functionality available on the Home window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 526
Owner 
Specifies the owner of the identity. Owners can be 
created, edited and deleted. An owner can have 
multiple versions. Deleting an owner deletes all 
versions and identities related to the owner. 
Note: 
Deleting an owner does not delete the model item, 
the identity value is deleted from the model item. 
Publisher 
Specifies the publisher of the identity. 
Version 
Specifies the version of the identity. Versions can be 
created, edited and deleted. A version must always 
belong to an owner. Deleting a version deletes all 
identities related to the owner. 
Note: 
Deleting a version does not delete the model item, the 
identity value is deleted from the model item. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 527
Date 
Specifies the version date. 
Description 
Displays the description of the identity. 
Namespace 
Displays the namespace of the version. This is 
displayed in the Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) of 
the identity when data is exported. 
Is Editable 
Select the check box to allow the version and identity 
value to be edited on the Properties window. 
Deselect the check box to lock the version and identity 
value for editing on the Properties window. The 
version and identity values display greyed out. 
Enforce Unique IDs 
Select the check box to enable unique identity values 
on the Properties window. 
If the identity value is not unique, an error message 
displays summarizing the problem. 
Deprecated 
Select the check box to label the version as 
deprecated. The version does not display on the 
Properties window. 
Identities can be added to model items in the conceptual model using the Grid View or Properties window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 528
Identities can be created, edited and deleted. 
Select Versions 
Select a version from the drop-down list. 
Identity Value 
Specifies the identity value associated to the model 
item. An alpha numeric character string can be used. 
Contextual Views 
The contextual views functionality provides a programmatic mechanism to determine logical and intelligent 
contextual views (sub-models) from an initial input or class selection. The initial selection criteria represents the 
context in the contextual view. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 529
On the Data Model tab, in the Contextual View group, click Create to display the Create Contextual View 
window. 
Use the Create Contextual View window to create a contextual view. A contextual view can be created using a 
marquee selection and the right click menu on the diagram view. Refer to Mouse Controls for further 
information. Alternatively, a contextual view can be created from existing identities, vocabularies or database 
views when the Create Contextual View window is opened from the ribbon or the Home window. 
Name 
Input a name for the contextual view in the field. 
Description 
Input a description for the contextual view in the field. 
Include sub classes 
Select the check box to include sub classes of the 
selected classes in the contextual view. 
Include all sub-classes 
Select the check box to include all sub classes and 
associated classes in the contextual view. 
Include required classes 
Select the check box to include classes required to 
specify a complete contextual view sub-model, 
including associated class hierarchies and parent 
classes. 
Include associations 
Select the required association types from the drop-
down menu to filter the associated classes. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 530
Filters 
Select the required check boxes to add classes to the 
selected classes column that are associated with 
identities vocabularies or database views. 
Note: 
Associated vocabularies are selected when certain 
vocabularies are selected. For example, when the E&I 
Vocabulary is selected, the Base Vocabulary and 
Engineering Vocabulary are also selected. 
Note: 
Associated vocabularies are deselected when certain 
vocabularies are deselected. For example, when the 
Base Vocabulary is deselected, the E&I Vocabulary and 
Engineering Vocabulary are also deselected. 
The database views filter displays all database view 
sets as top-level items, with the database views as 
child items. The filter is populated the first time the 
database views filter is expanded, so there may be a 
delay while the data loads. 
A database view can be a member of more than one 
database view set. In this case, all instances of the 
same database view in the filter are selected or 
deselected at the same time. 
When a database view is selected, classes are added 
to the selected classes column based on the element 
types defined for the view. The element types (UDETs) 
can be any types that map to a model class. For 
example, the element types :Vessel, :VesselMECH and 
:FUNVessel will all select the Vessel class. 
Selected Classes 
Displays the classes to be added to the contextual 
view. 
 Indicates the classes added from a selection in the 
diagram view. 
 Indicates the classes added from an identity filter. 
 Indicates the classes added from a vocabulary 
filter. 
 Indicates the classes added from a database view 
filter. 
Populate 
Adds the selected classes to the contextual view 
contents column. Sub classes and required classes are 
also added to the contextual view contents column, if 
selected. 
Contextual View Contents 
Displays all classes to be added to the contextual view. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 531
The top of the column displays the total number of 
classes contained in the contextual view compared to 
the total number of classes in the model, this is also 
indicated as a percentage value. 
Create 
Creates the contextual view. 
Refresh 
Updates the filters to reflect changes made outside of 
the Create Contextual View window. 
Close 
Closes the Create Contextual View window. 
The Creating Contextual View Preview window displays the progress of the classes added to the Contextual 
View Contents column. 
Click Show Details to expand the Creating Contextual View Preview window which displays a summary of the 
process. 
Click Save Errors and Warnings to save the errors and warnings to a log file. Input a file name and define a 
location for the saved file. The file is saved to CSV format. 
Click Hide Details to contract the Creating Contextual View Preview window which removes the summary of the 
process from the display. 
Click Close to close the Creating Contextual View Preview window. 
Contextual views can be created, modified and deleted using the functionality available on the Home window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 532
Add a new contextual view 
Creates a contextual view. 
Name 
Specifies the name of the contextual view. 
Description 
Specifies the description of the contextual view. 
Use the Delete functionality to delete the currently selected contextual view. A confirmation window displays 
asking you to confirm the deletion. 
If the currently selected contextual view is open in a diagram view or grid view, a message displays summarizing 
the problem. 
Export 
Functionality is available to export the following: 
• Export conceptual model 
• Export database definitions 
• Export model differences file 
• Export sub-model using a contextual view 
Export Conceptual Model 
Use the Save As window to navigate to the location, name the model and select the format to export the model. 
Functionality is available to export data model relationships from the conceptual model. Refer to Export Data 
Model Relationships for further information. 
Export Data Model Relationships 
Use the Save As window to navigate to the location and name the data model relationships export file. Select 
Data Model Relationships (*.csv) from the Save as type drop down list. 
Data model relationships are comprised of the following: 
• Class hierarchy 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 533
• Attributes 
• Class associations 
The relationships are exported to a single .csv format file. 
The following fields are exported (column headers): 
• Is Multi Domain 
• Is Multi Range 
• Domain 
• Association 
• Range 
• Association URI 
• Base Association 
• Base Association URI 
• Domain URI 
• Domain Group URI 
• Range URI 
• Range Group URI 
Export Database Definitions 
Use the Save As window to navigate to the location, name the database definitions and select the format to 
export the database definitions. 
Export Model Differences File 
Compares two different versions of a conceptual model and creates a file containing the differences. 
Use the Model Differences Export Form window to define the conceptual model files for comparison. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 534
Base Model 
Specifies the base model for comparison. 
Select a model from the drop-down list or use the 
Browse functionality to navigate to a location, and 
select the base model for comparison. 
Swap 
Swaps the values in the Base Model and Changed 
Model fields. 
Changed Model 
Specifies the changed model for comparison. 
Select a model from the drop-down list or use the 
Browse functionality to navigate to a location, and 
select the model for comparison. 
Alternatively, select Current Model from the drop-
down list to select the current model for comparison. 
Output file 
Specifies the location and filename of the file which 
contains the model comparison results. 
Use the Browse functionality to select a location, and 
export format for the file. 
Format 
Displays the export format of the file which contains 
the model comparison results. 
Log File 
Specifies the location and filename of the export log 
file. 
Use the Browse functionality to select a location, and 
format for the log file. 
The Create Model Differences Export File window displays the progress of the model comparison. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 535
Click Show Details to expand the Create Model Differences Export File window which displays a summary of the 
export process. 
Click Save Errors and Warnings to save the errors and warnings to a log file. Input a file name and define a 
location for the saved file. The file is saved to CSV format. 
Click Hide Details to contract the Create Model Differences Export File window which removes the summary of 
the export process from the display. 
Click Close to close the Create Model Differences Export File window. 
Items which are found to have been deleted are recorded, and in the case of the TTL export format, are emitted 
as specialized comments in the model differences file. If the model differences file is imported into AVEVA 
Engineering, the deleted items can be flagged for deletion in the model. No other format (apart from TTL format) 
supports this functionality. 
Export Sub-model using a Contextual View 
Use the Export Contextual View as window to navigate to the location, name the contextual view and select the 
format to export the sub-model. 
Build Model 
Saves any change made to data in the module and begins the build process. User Defined Elements (UDETs) and 
User Defined Attributes (UDAs) that have been manually mapped will not be updated during the build process. 
These UDETs and UDAs remain unchanged and will not become controlled by Data Model. 
Any manual edit to the UDETs and UDAs created during the build process are outside the control of Data Model 
and are done so at the user’s risk. Edits to these UDETs and UDAs may cause the conceptual model to be 
inaccurate and any subsequent build may undo the changes. 
If active claims exist, the Active Claims Check window is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 536
Active claims can exist when two users have separate instances of the application open at the same time and are 
using the same project. If one user modifies elements and builds the model, the elements are claimed. If the 
other user modifies elements and builds the model, the Active Claims Check window is displayed, indicating that 
elements are currently claimed by another user. 
Click Cancel to cancel the build. 
Click Export to export a list of claimed elements to a text file. Use the Save File window to navigate to the 
location and name the file. 
Click Build to continue building the model, the Confirm window is displayed. 
Select the Remove Unused Definitions check box to remove definitions that are not used after completion of the 
build process. 
The LOCK attribute is set/unset on the Dabacon elements (UDET, UDA) which are created during the build 
process. 
If the LOCK attribute was previously set, the locks are removed prior to commencing the build process. 
Select the Lock Built Database Definitions check box to lock Dabacon elements (UDET, UDA) which were created 
during the build process after completion of the build process. 
Click Yes to confirm the build or click No to cancel the build. 
The Build Database Definitions window displays the progress of the build. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 537
Click Show Details to expand the Build Database Definitions window which displays a summary of the build 
process. 
Click Save Error Log to save the errors and warnings to a log file. Input a file name and define a location for the 
saved file. The file is saved to CSV format. 
Click Hide Details to contract the Build Database Definitions window which removes the summary of the build 
process from the display. 
Click Close to close the Build Database Definitions window. 
Create DBVIEWs from the conceptual model 
Use the wizard functionality to create a dbview based on the conceptual model. 
Note: 
The Edit functionality has been removed for AVEVA Engineering 15.1, to edit a db view, use the dbview editor 
functionality available from Configuration to change it in terms of the User defined Elements (UDET) and User 
Defined Attribute (UDA) details. 
1. In the Configuration module, in the Post Build group select Database View. The Data Model Database View 
Creator window is displayed and the Database View Details page is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 538
2. Enter a name for the Database View Name field. 
3. Enter a description for the database view in the Database View Description field. 
4. Click Next, the Class(es) for Database View page is displayed populated with the classes available for 
selection. 
5. In the Select Classes pane, navigate to the required class(es), if required enter a key word in to the Search 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 539
field to the filter the list of classes. 
6. Click to check the required check box(es) for the attributes to be displayed as columns in the database view. 
7. Click Next, The Attributes for Database View page is displayed populated the attributes available for 
selection. 
8. In the Select Associations to become Columns pane, click to check the Select All check box. 
9. Click Next, the Associations Columns for Database View page is displayed populated with the associations 
available for selection.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 540
10. In the Select Associations to be Columns pane, click to check the Select All check box. All of the selections

11. Click Next, the Associations for Database View page is populated with the attributes on associated classes 
available for selection. The selected associations will be represented as columns in the db view. 
12. In the Associations pane, click +, the Select Associations to use as Source Element in Database View 
AVEVA™ Engineering

window is displayed. 
13. To view the associations on the class, click to expand the list. 
14. Select the required association(s), once selected the associations are displayed in the Associations pane. 
Note: 
Only the blue test in the list can be selected. 
15. In the Attributes pane, click to check the Select All check box. 
16. Click Next, the Build Database View page is displayed populated with a summary of the selections made.

Engineer 
This guide describes the functionality available from the engineer module. 
Engineering Defaults 
Use the Options functionality to view and edit default module preferences. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 543
Default presentation for Units of Measure 
Sets the default units of measure used for values in grids and drawings. 
Save Grid Layout Changes 
Defines the settings for saving grid layout changes when the module is exited. 
Yes 
Automatically saves grid layout changes when the 
module is exited. 
No 
Does not save grid layout changes when the module is 
exited. 
Ask 
Displays the Confirm Save Grid Layout window when 
the module is exited if a grid layout has changed. 
Prompt for Session Comment during Save Work 
Defines the session comment settings when a Save Work is performed. 
If the check box is selected, the Save Work Comment window is displayed prompting for a session comment. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 544
If the check box is not selected, a session comment is not required when a Save Work is performed. 
Highlight Changes after Get Work 
Defines the highlighting of changes when a Get Work is performed. 
If the check box is selected, changes are highlighted when a Get Work is performed. 
If the check box is not selected, changes are not highlighted when a Get Work is performed. 
Highlight Changes after Refresh from Parent Extract 
Defines the highlighting of changes when a Refresh from Parent Extract is performed. 
If the check box is selected, changes are highlighted when a Refresh from Parent Extract is performed. 
If the check box is not selected, changes are not highlighted when a Refresh from Parent Extract is performed. 
Highlight Changes after Refresh from All Ancestors 
Defines the highlighting of changes when a Refresh from All Ancestors is performed. 
If the check box is selected, changes are highlighted when a Refresh from All Ancestors is performed. 
If the check box is not selected, changes are not highlighted when a Refresh from All Ancestors is performed. 
Auto renaming active on attribute changes 
Defines the of autonaming of engineering items when attributes are changed. 
If the check box is selected, engineering items are autonamed when attributes are changed. 
If the check box is not selected, engineering items are not autonamed when attributes are changed. 
Note: 
The functionality is only available when Auto Naming is set to On in the Configuration module. Refer to the 
Configuration for further information. 
Show database errors on grid 
Defines the display of database errors on grids. 
If the check box is selected, database errors on grids are displayed. 
If the check box is not displayed, database errors on grid are not displayed. 
Refresh PBS Explorer on Events 
Defines the flexible explorer refresh settings on events. 
If the check box is selected, the flexible explorer is refreshed on events. 
If the check box is not selected, the flexible explorer is not refreshed on events. 
Selection direction after cell edit 
Specifies the direction of the next selected cell after a cell is edited. 
Up 
Selects the cell above the edited cell when editing is 
complete. 
Down 
Selects the cell below the edited cell when editing is 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 545
complete. 
Left 
Selects the cell to the left of the edited cell when 
editing is complete. 
Right 
Selects the cell to the right of the edited cell when 
editing is complete. 
Auto Save Work every 
Defines the auto save settings for the session. The minute(s) from last Save Work and Prompt before auto save 
options become active. 
Minute(s) from last Save Work 
Specifies the duration in minutes when an auto save is performed, measured from the last Save Work. 
Prompt before Auto Save 
Defines the auto save prompt settings. 
If the check box is selected, the Auto Save Work window is displayed prompting for confirmation of a Save 
Work. 
If the check box is not selected, the session is auto saved without a prompt. 
Automatically release claimed elements on SaveWork 
Set Default Units of Measure 
Use the Current Session Units window to set the default units of measure used for values in grids. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 546
The left-hand part of the window lists each dimension and the current default units of measure. 
To change the default unit of measure for a dimension, click on it in the list. A list of units of measure is then 
displayed on the right-hand part of the window. 
Click on the unit of measure to be used on the right-hand side of the window and click Set. The default unit is 
displayed on the left-hand part of the window and is changed for the selected dimension. 
Project Defaults 
Applies the default units of measure for the project. 
Restore 
Applies the last saved set of default units of measure. 
Reset Units to Default 
Use the Reset units to default window to return the units of measure for grid values to the default settings for: 
• Junction boxes 
• Pump datasheets 
• Mechanical 
• Instrumentation 
• Electrical 
• Valves 
• HVAC 
• Process lines 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 547
Session Default Values for New Records 
Default values for new records can be defined for a session. These values automatically populate fields in the 
Create element information window which is used to create a record. 
Click Session Defaults on the status bar to display the Session Defaults window: 
Attribute 
Defines the attribute for which the default value is set. 
Value 
Type or select a default value. 
Apply as default 
Defines the default value settings for the session default. 
If the check box is selected, the value is used as the default value. 
If the check box is not selected, the value is not used as the default value. 
A new row of blank fields (for defining a further default value) is automatically added beneath the existing rows 
when a default value is defined. 
Autofilter List 
Defines the autofilter settings. 
If the check box is selected, autofilter is turned on. 
If the check box is not selected, autofilter is turned off. 
Refer to Create Record for further information. 
Automatically release claimed elements on Save Work 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 548
Notes: 
A requirement for this functionality is that the module should be launched using a Mechanical or Process user(s) 
and the check box should be checked for each user. By default the checkbox is not checked. 
Claimed imported ETAP elements can also be automatically released on SaveWork. 
Use this functionality to automatically release claimed elements on SaveWork.The option should be saved in 
engineering defaults along with other defaults in Project Options of the Engineering module. 
On checking the checkbox, all the tag elements (that is ENITE, SIMITE, FUNITE, XPITE) which are claimed by the 
user will be released on SaveWork. If the checkbox is not checked, elements claimed by the user will not be 
released on SaveWork and the user must release them explicitly whenever required. The current grid will reflect 
the change of released elements by removing the bold on tags if any of the elements are bold. 
Note: 
In multiuser environment if user A updates an element and carried out SaveWork then user B will be able to 
work on same element. 
Work Area 
By default, the AVEVA Engineering - Engineer window is displayed with the Home tab selected. The main parts 
of the AVEVA Engineering - Engineer window are: 
The title bar of the module window: this displays the code of the current project. 
Quick Access Toolbar - tab options can be added to the quick access toolbar for convenient access. Some 
commands are located here by default. The default location of this toolbar is above the tabs to the left of the 
title bar, but it can be moved below them if required. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 549
Engineer Tabs - most commands, facilities and utilities of the Engineer module are accessed from these tabs. 
Overall Project Functionality - accessed from the Project tab, the displayed fluent User Interface (UI) provides 
access to a number of key commands and facilities that define the Engineer module defaults. 
Grids Explorer - provides an easy-to-use, graphical method of exploring the engineering grids. 
Grids - Data in the Engineer module is viewed and edited using a number of grids which can be located using the 
Grids Explorer. 
Message Log window - displays the user and system messages created by the application including alerts and 
errors. 
Command Window - used for executing standard commands and Programmable Macro Language (PML) 
functions. 
Status Bar - located at the bottom of the module window, this displays status information, (for example, the 
number of records in the current grid) error, warning and information messages and provides access to the 
message log. Functionality is also available to increase or decrease the magnification of the view using the zoom 
functionality. 
Refer to Quick Access Toolbar, Engineering Defaults, Grids Explorer, Grids, Messages and Message Log and 
Command Window for further information. 
Project 
Use the Project tab to access a number of key commands and facilities. 
Save Work 
Saves any changes made to data in the Engineer module. By default, this facility is also available on the quick 
access toolbar. 
Engineering users of the same discipline in two separate sessions (A and B), are able to: 
• Update the distributed data (new/existing XPITEM data) on two different tagged items simultaneously 
without any claim issues. 
• If a user performs a Save Work within a session (Session A), an automatic Get Work is triggered from the 
other sessions (Session B), without the need for a manual Get Work operation. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 550
Engineering users of different disciplines in two separate sessions (A and B) are able to: 
• Update the distributed data (new/existing XPITEM data) on the same tagged item simultaneously without 
any claim issues. 
• If a user performs a Save Work within a session (Session A), an automatic Get Work is not triggered from 
other sessions (Session B). Updates are only visible using a manual Get Work operation. The automatic Get 
Work operation on an XPITEM when a Save Work is performed is only applicable to XPITEMs in the same 
database. The functionality is not available if the XPITEMs are in different databases, for example, the 
Process data Database (DB) and the Electrical data DB. 
Get Work 
Refreshes data in any opened grids to include changes that have been made by other users since the grids were 
opened. By default, this facility is also available on the quick access toolbar. 
Print 
Displays a sub-menu which is used to print the current grid, diagram. or datasheet. 
The Print Grid option displays the Print Preview window which displays a screen capture of the current grid, 
various printing options are available. 
The Print Diagram option displays the Print window which allows the user to print the displayed diagram. 
The Print Datasheet option displays the Print window which allows the user to print the displayed datasheet. 
The Print Preview Datasheet option displays the Print Preview window which displays a print preview of the 
currently selected datasheet. 
Print Grid 
Use Print Grid to preview and print the currently displayed grid. 
The Print Preview window is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 551
The Print Preview window displays a preview of the grid. 
The File menu includes a Page Setup option, which displays the standard Windows Page Setup window. 
Thumbnail images of each page are displayed on the left-hand pane of the window. The section of the page 
currently being viewed is enclosed in the thumbnail. 
The thumbnail part of the Print Preview window can be opened or closed by selecting Thumbnails from the 
View menu. 
The toolbar and menus can be used to view and define the printed area. 
Print 
Prints the currently displayed grid. 
Hand Tool 
Drags the page view in any direction. 
Click on the page with the tool activated, drag the 
page until the required view is visible and release the 
mouse button. 
Also available on the Tools menu. 
Snapshot Tool 
Takes a screen capture of a page, or part of a page. 
Captures are copied to the clipboard. 
To take a capture of an entire page, click on the page 
with the tool activated. To take a capture of a part of a 
page, click on one corner of the area to be captured, 
then drag the cursor to the opposite corner of the 
area and release the mouse button. 
Also available on the Tools menu. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 552
Dynamic Zoom Tool 
Increases the magnification of the view when the 
cursor is dragged up the page or the mouse wheel is 
rolled up. Decreases the magnification of the view 
when the cursor is dragged down or the mouse wheel 
is rolled down. 
Also available on the Tools menu. 
Zoom In Tool 
Zooms into the selected page to the next standard 
zoom level. 
Also available on the Tools menu. 
Zoom Out Tool 
Zoom out of the selected page to the next standard 
zoom level. 
Also available on the Tools menu. 
Page Width 
Sets the view to the same width as the current page. 
Also available on the View menu. 
Margin Width 
Sets the view to the width of the margins of the grid 
image on the current page. 
Also available on the View menu. 
Whole Page 
Sets the view to display the entire current page. 
Also available on the View menu. 
Zoom Out 
Zooms out from the page to the next standard zoom 
level. 
Also available on the View menu. 
Zoom 
Modifies the magnification of the view using a typed 
value or a value selected from the list adjacent to the 
field. 
Also available on the View menu. 
Zoom In 
Zooms into the page to the next standard zoom level. 
Also available on the View menu. 
Continuous 
Removes the gaps between different pages in the 
main view. 
Repeat the command to re-insert the gaps. 
Page Layout 
Opens a drop-down list with various page layout 
options. 
Close Preview 
Closes the Print Preview window. 
Also available from the File menu (Exit).

First Page 
Displays the first page of the preview. 
Also available on the View menu. 
Previous Page 
Displays the previous page of the preview. 
Also available on the View menu. 
Next Page 
Displays the next page of the preview 
Also available on the View menu. 
Last Page 
Displays the last page of the preview 
Also available on the View menu. 
Previous View 
Displays the previous view. 
Also available on the View menu. 
Next View 
Displays the next view. 
Also available on the View menu. 
Print Diagram or Datasheet 
Use Print Diagram to print the currently displayed diagram or Print Datasheet to print the currently displayed 
datasheet. 
The Print window is displayed. 
Select a printer, set the number of pages and copies to produce and set the properties of the printer. 
Print Preview Datasheet 
Use Print Preview Datasheet to preview and print the currently displayed datasheet. 
The Print Preview window is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering

The toolbar and menus can be used to view and define the printed area. 
Print 
Prints the currently displayed datasheet. 
Page Setup 
Displays the standard Windows Page Setup window. 
Zoom 
Modifies the magnification of the view using a value 
selected from the list adjacent to the option. 
First Page 
Displays the first page of the preview. 
Previous Page 
Displays the previous page of the preview. 
Next Page 
Displays the next page of the preview 
Last Page 
Displays the last page of the preview 
Close 
Closes the preview 
Help 
Launches the Online help, opens the AVEVA product support Contact Us page, opens the AVEVA product support 
Downloads page and displays information about AVEVA Engineer. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 555
AVEVA Engineering Help 
Launches the AVEVA Engineering online help with the Contents displayed. Functionality is available to navigate 
to the required topic from the contents list or specify a keyword(s) and search for all instances of that keyword 
within the documentation suite. 
Contact AVEVA and Check for Updates 
Opens the AVEVA product support Contact Us page which provides information on how to contact AVEVA. 
Check for Updates 
Opens the AVEVA product support Downloads page, a product download can be searched for. 
About 
Displays the current version of installed software and product licence details. 
Show more displays the Additional Information window which reports all the details related to the version.dat 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 556
files, which are located in the PMLLIB and PMLUI search paths. 
Copy 
Copies the information to the clipboard. The 
information can be pasted into a file and saved. 
Appware 
Scans all the application appware to check if any 
changes have been made. All changes are displayed in 
the Appware Changes from Installed window. 
Copy 
Copies the information to the clipboard. The 
information can be pasted into a file and saved. 
Modules 
Switches to a different module (Configuration or Monitor) in AVEVA Engineering. The Engineer module is closed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 557
Exit 
Exits the application module. 
All work done in the current session can be saved before exiting or the module can be exited directly without 
updating any databases. 
If changes have been made and Exit is selected without doing a Save Work, a Question window is displayed 
prompting to save changes to the appropriate database. 
If a Save Work has been done (and no subsequent changes made), or if no changes have been made during the 
session, a Confirm window is displayed. 
Explorers 
Provides an easy to use graphical method of exploring the databases. 
Refer to Grids Explorer, Project Breakdown Structure, Drawings Explorer and Database Explorer for further 
information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 558
Categories 
Project data grids in the Engineer module are grouped into defined categories. A category can contain a number 
of grids and categories. 
Create or Edit Category 
A Category element can be created below the currently selected Grid World element or the currently selected 
Category element on the Grids Explorer. Use right click functionality to select New then Category to display the 
Manage Grid Category window. Use the Manage Grid Category window to create or edit a grid category. 
Category 
Type the category name in the field. 
Delete Category 
Use the Delete functionality to delete the currently selected Category element on the Grids Explorer. 
Note: 
When a category is deleted, all grids contained in that category are also deleted. 
The Confirm Delete window is displayed. Click Yes to confirm the deletion or click No to cancel the deletion. 
Grids 
Project data in the Engineer module is viewed and edited using grids. A grid is created from a project database 
view. Fields that are included in the grid can be specified. The grid layout and sort order can be filtered. 
Create Grid 
A grid element can be created below the currently selected category element on the Grids Explorer, use the 
Create Grid window to define the grid properties. 
Specify Name and Database View 
Use the View tab to define the grid. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 559
Name 
Type the grid name in the field. 
Description 
Type the grid description in the field. 
Category 
Displays the category that the grid is associated with. 
Can be published to AVEVA NET 
Defines export settings to AVEVA NET. 
If the check box is selected, grid data can be exported to AVEVA NET. 
If the check box is not selected, grid data cannot be exported to AVEVA NET. 
Visible in grid explorer 
Defines the display settings in the Grids Explorer. 
If the check box is selected, the grid displays in the Grids Explorer. 
If the check box is not selected, the grid does not display in the Grids Explorer. 
The functionality of the View Definition Set and Views part of the Create Grid window is identical to the 
Database Views Editor window. 
Note: 
The selected view cannot be changed once the grid has been created. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 560
Specify Fields 
Use the Fields tab to define the columns that are included in the grid. 
Select 
Defines the columns that are included in the grid. 
If the check box is selected, the column is included in the grid. 
If the check box is not selected, the column is not included in the grid. 
Use the check box in the header of the Select column to select or deselect all checkboxes. 
Layout Order 
Defines the column display order in the grid. 
Specify Layout 
Use the Layout tab to specify the order in which the columns of the grid are displayed and set the column 
headers and cell colors. 
Order of Columns 
Specifies the order in which the columns of the grid are displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 561
Change the order of columns either by: 
• dragging and dropping them up and down the list of column names in the left-hand part of the window, 
• or by dragging and dropping them to the left and right in the graphical representation of columns in the 
right-hand part of the window. 
Multi-level column headers can be created, columns can be grouped together under defined column headers. 
Add Group 
Right-click on a column name in the left-hand part of the window and select Add Group. 
A column group header is added under the column name that is right-clicked with the default name of Group 1 
(assuming it is the first group to be created). 
If required, drag and drop the columns that are to be grouped under the new header onto its entry in the list. 
The new column group is displayed in the graphical representation of columns on the right-hand part of the 
window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 562
Add further groups as required. Groups can be created under other groups. 
Rename Group 
Right-click on a group and select Rename Group to rename a group. 
Delete Group 
Right-click on a group and select Delete Group to delete a group,. Columns and groups within the deleted group 
are not deleted. They are moved up a level in the list of columns/groups. 
If required, columns can be pinned by default. To a pin a column, either click on the pin icon in the graphical 
representation, or with the Multi Level Headings checkbox unchecked, right-click on the column name in the list 
of columns and select Pin Column from the menu that is then displayed. 
Options for unpinning the column and to remove the pinning icon from the column are also available on this 
menu. 
Records with common attribute values can be grouped together by default. For example, records can be grouped 
according to their Description or Tag Number. This is achieved by dragging the column header into the grouping 
bar above the column headers. 
Column Headers and Cell Color 
Controls column headers and cell colors. 
Header Color 
Right click on a column header, select one of the following from the displayed drop-down menu: 
Header Colour 
From the Color window, select a color for the 
background of the column header. 
Header Font Colour 
From the Color window, select a color for the column 
header text. 
Reset Header Colours 
Removes all colors from the column header. 
Header Text Alignment 
Defines the horizontal and vertical alignment of the 
header using a drop-down list. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 563
Cell Color 
Right click on a cell in the grid, select one of the following from the displayed drop-down menu: 
Cell Colour 
From the Color window, select a color for the 
background of the cell. 
Alternate Row Cell Colour 
From the Color window, select a color for the 
background of the alternate row. 
Cell Font Colour 
From the Color window, select a color for the cell text. 
Reset Cell Colour 
Removes all colors from the cell. 
Cell Text Alignment 
Defines the horizontal and vertical alignment of the 
cell using a drop-down list. 
Specify Sort Order 
By default, records in a grid are displayed in order of creation, with the newest listed first. The default sort order 
can be modified. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 564
View Fields 
Displays a list of available field names. 
Show Used Only 
Defines the fields displayed in the list of available field names. 
If the check box is selected, the list of available fields is restricted to display only fields that are associated with 
the current grid. 
If the check box is not selected, the list of available fields is not restricted. 
Sort by 
Double-click on a field to move it into the Sort by list. Any number of fields can be added to the Sort by list. 
Note: 
The numerical sorting option only applies to text fields, a numeric field is always numerically sorted. 
If multiple fields are displayed in the Sort by list, drag and drop fields upwards or downwards to define the sort 
priority. 
Double-click on a field in the Sort by list to move it to the View fields list. 
Note: 
Temporary sort orders can be specified when a list is open using the general list functionality. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 565
Sub-grids 
Grids can have sub-grids. If a sub-grid is set up for a grid, when an item is selected in the grid, the sub-grid 
displays the details of items associated with the selected item. 
For example, if a grid displays equipment items, a sub-grid can be set up that displays details of associated 
motors. When a pump is selected, the sub-grid displays the details of the relevant pump. 
Use the functionality available from the Sub-grids tab to customize the layout of the sub-grids window. 
Applicable sub-grids 
The Applicable sub-grids part of the Create Grid window is populated with all the available sub-grids for the 
current grid (the sub-grids must first be set up). 
Select the appropriate checkbox to add a sub-grid. If required, the layout of the sub-grid window can be 
customized. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 566
Docked 
Defines the location for the sub-grids window. The 
available options are: 
• Bottom 
• Floating 
• Left 
• LeftPriority 
• Right 
• RightPriority 
• Top 
Grouped 
Defines the display of a group sub-grid window. The 
available options are: 
• True 
• False 
Group Style 
Defines the style of the group sub-grid window. The 
available options are: 
• HorizontalSplit 
• Sliding Group 
• TabGroup 
• VerticalSplit 
Vertical 
Displays the sub-grid window in vertical mode. The 
available options are: 
• True 
• False 
Delete Grid 
Deletes the currently selected Grid element on the Grids Explorer 
The Confirm Delete window is displayed. Click Yes to confirm the deletion or click No to cancel the deletion. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 567
Edit Grid 
Edits the properties of the currently selected Grid element on the Grids Explorer. 
Note: 
The selected view cannot be changed once the grid has been created. 
The functionality available on the Grid Manager window is identical to the functionality available on the Create 
Grid window. 
Copy Selected Grid 
Copies the properties of the currently selected grid element on the Grids Explorer. The properties of the new grid 
can be edited. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 568
The functionality available on the Copy Grid window is identical to the functionality available on the Create Grid 
window. 
Open Grid 
Displays the currently selected grid element on the Grids Explorer. If sub-grids have been defined, they are 
displayed under the grid. 
The total number of records is displayed on the status bar. 
Note: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 569
By default, the title tab of each grid includes the category name and the grid name. If required, the category 
name can be hidden using the Display Short Titles functionality. 
Note: 
By default, each grid is displayed on a separate tab. If required, each grid can be displayed in separate window 
using the Tabbed View functionality. 
When an item is selected in a grid, the sub-grid displays the items associated with that item. For example, the 
motor associated with a pump. 
Sub-grid data can be viewed, formatted and edited in the same way as data in grids. If a record in a sub-grid is 
selected, the title bar of the sub-grid is highlighted in blue. 
Refer to Display Short Titles and Tabbed View for further information. 
Records 
Project data in the Engineer module is viewed and edited using a number of records (tags), each of which 
displays a category of data as a separate row in the grid. 
Create Record 
Creates a record at the bottom of the currently selected grid. 
If the grid is based on a database view which has been set up without creation rules, a new blank record is then 
added to the bottom of the grid. Enter details of the new record as required. 
If the grid is based on a database view which has been set up with creation rules, use the Create element 
information window to define the record properties. 
Enter the required information in the fields provided, and as required the optional information. 
Default values can be defined for a session. The default values automatically populate the fields in the Create 
element information window and can be overwritten as required. 
If Autonaming is in use, the name of the new record is automatically completed. In the case of grids based on 
views with creation rules setup, the Create element information window is displayed if there are rules covering 
no-name element attributes. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 570
Note: 
When tags are created they are partially reserved so if the application is closed without saving work or if the 
application crashes then partially reserved tags can be unreserved. An error is displayed if the reservation could 
not be completed during Savework. 
Refer to Set Default Units of Measure and Autonaming Setup for further information. 
Create Record in Tag Management System Enabled Project 
Creates new records in a Tag management system enabled project. Use the New Tag window to define the 
record class and the number of created records. 
Note: 
Any naming action is initiated in SEE 3D is managed by Tag Management System. 
Class 
For a TMS enabled project - Select the ellipsis to populate the field using the Select Class window. The Select 
Class window is automatically populated based on the first element type defined in the DB View which is used in 
the grid configuration. 
For example if the Instrument grid is configured to use the Database (DB) view AVEVA_LIST_Instrument, 
AbsolutePressureTransmitter as the first element type in the DB View element types and New is clicked while the 
grid is open. The Select Class window is automatically populated with the Absolute Pressure Transmitter class. 
If the class is automatically populated in the New Tag window and the ellipsis (next to the Select field) is clicked 
then the Select Class window is automatically pre populates the class as the selected class. 
If the grid displays records of a single class, then the Select Class window is automatically populated with that 
class and the naming rules of that are initialized. For example if the grid configuration of the Enclosure _lists grid 
has only one element type defined and if the grid is open and New is clicked. The Select Class window is 
automatically populated that element class. 
If the grid is configured with sub grids, the New Tag window has an additional drop down enabling the context to 
be set on creation of new tag(s). All grids (main and sub grids) for the active window are displayed as in the tabs 
of the grid(s) in the additional drop down. The main grid is the default and the New Tag window is initialized to a 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 571
class that is relevant to the main grid. 
Selection from another grid resets the New Tag window to a class that is relevant to the selected grid. If no class 
can be found for selection the window is initialized to an empty class. 
If the active window has only one grid then the additional drop down list is not displayed in the New Tag 
window. The drop down list is also not displayed when the New Tag window is displayed without an active 
window hosting a grid. 
When the class has been selected, naming rules and attribute fields may be displayed, populate as required. 
No. of Tags 
Type the required number of tags in the field or use + and - to increase or decrease the number of required 
records. 
Default Name (System) 
Used to select the system that will be part of the default name of the record, if required. 
Name Precedent 
Enter the prefix for the default name. 
Counter Type 
If the default name is to include a counter, e.g. an incrementing number, select the type of counter. 
Select Class window 
Use the Select Class window to select the record class using the class hierarchy or search functionality. 
Search 
Type a string to be searched in the Search field, matching content is displayed below the field. 
Alternatively, navigate through the tree hierarchy and select a class. 
Select 
Selects the class and closes the Select Class window, the Select class field in the New Tag window is populated 
with the selected class. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 572
Note: 
If no gird is selected and a class which is not built (inactive) is also selected, the functionality becomes 
unavailable. A not built or inactive class is represented as a folder icon in the class hierarchy (classes which are 
built (active) are represented as a document icon). This functionality can also be observed in the Configuration 
module on the Libraries tab. 
Note: 
If the class selected is active and is both an engineering and physical item or an Functional Item, an additional 
drop down list (Class type) is displayed in the Select Class window (Engineering /Physical Item is the default). 
The drop down is not displayed if the class is inactive. 
Note: 
From the Naming Rules window in the Configuration module on the Rules tab, any class can be selected 
irrespective if it is active or inactive. 
Note: 
If the selected class has a functional decoration (that is a decoration from the simulation vocabulary in the 
Configuration module) a drop down is enabled on the Class Picker window. 
Create Multiple Records 
Creates multiple records at the bottom of the currently selected grid. 
Use the Number of records window to specify the number of created records. 
Type the number of new records to be created. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 573
If the grid is based on a database view which has been set up without creation rules, new blank records are 
added to the bottom of the grid. Enter the details of the new records as required. 
If the grid is based on a database view which has been set up with creation rules, use the Create element 
information window to define the properties of each record. The functionality available on the Create element 
information window is explained as part of the Create Record process. 
Select Single Record 
Click immediately to the left of the first grid column, an arrow is displayed in the cell and the row is highlighted in 
a different color, indicating the selected row. 
Select Multiple Records 
There are a number of methods available to select multiple rows: 
• Select an individual record by clicking immediately to the left of the first grid column. Hold down Ctrl and 
select individual records by clicking immediately to the left of the first grid column. Using the Ctrl key adds 
each record to the selected records. Click on the record again to remove it from the selection. 
• Select an individual record by clicking immediately to the left of the first grid column. Hold down Shift and 
select a record by clicking immediately to the left of the first grid column. Both the selected records, and all 
the records between the two records are selected. 
Select All Records 
Selects all records in the current grid, excluding any filtered out records. 
Copy 
The properties of the currently selected record can be copied and edited to create a new record. The copied 
record is created at the bottom of the current grid. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 574
Copy Records 
Copies the currently selected records. The records are stored on the clipboard for a later paste operation. 
Copy & Assign 
Creates copies of selected tag(s) and assigns the copies as a part to the tag for which the source tag is a part. On 
a single selected tag, this functionality will only be available if the selected tag is part of another tag. If Multiple 
tags are selected, tags that are not a part of another tag are copied, tags that are part of another are assigned to 
their owners. 
Note: 
This functionality is available for a non -TMS enabled project, but not available for a project without an 
conceptual model. 
Cut Records 
Cuts and deletes the currently selected records. The records are stored on the clipboard for a later paste 
operation. 
Paste Records 
Pastes the records that are stored on the clipboard at the bottom of the grid. 
Edit Records 
The properties of the currently selected records can be edited, use the Multi record edit window to define the 
records which are edited and specify the values that are modified. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 575
The window lists all the columns in the grid. 
Selected 
Defines individual records to be edited. 
If the check box is selected, the record is marked as edited. 
If the check box is not selected, the record is not marked as edited. 
Use the Select all check box to select or deselect all check boxes. 
Value 
Type or select the required value for each edited record. 
Copy First Record’s Data to other Selected Records 
Defines the setting of values to those of the first selected record in the list. 
If the check box is selected, the values of the selected records are set to those of the first selected record in the 
list. 
Note: 
The first record does not necessarily mean the first (upper-most) listed record of those selected, it means the 
first record that was selected when the selection of multiple records was made. 
If the check box is not selected, the values of the selected records are not set to those of the first selected record 
in the list. 
Rename Record 
Renames the currently selected record. 
The functionality is not available if more than one record is selected. 
Use the Rename element window to specify the new name for the record. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 576
Type the new record name. 
Rename Record 
If Plant A is renamed and at that time Tag A1-1 is read only/claimed then the software will rename Plant A1 and 
System A1 not Tag A1-1. Renaming triggers reservation rules, if any of the rules are broken, no attribute values 
are changed and a message is displayed. 
When renaming in bulk or on import the software will skip all elements which are read only/claimed. 
Note: 
Any changes can be undone and existing counters are retained where possible. 
Auto Rename Records 
Applies autonaming rules to currently selected records. Records that are not named according to the current 
naming rules are renamed. 
Reclassify record(s), drawing elements and existing counter 
Use to modify the class of a single or multiple record(s) in the displayed grid, an element on the drawing canvas 
or an existing counter. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 577
Notes: 
Access to the functionality is defined by the administrator. 
If the drawing is read only or protected when opened, the class of the currently selected element on the drawing 
canvas cannot be modified. 
Use the Select class window to define the target class for reclassification. By default, the Select class window 
displays the current class for the selected record in the grid. 
Search 
Type a string to be searched in the Search field, matching content is displayed below the field. 
Alternatively, navigate through the tree hierarchy and select a class. 
Select 
Selects the class and closes the Select Class window. 
A progress bar is displayed during the reclassification process. 
A confirmation message is displayed indicating that the reclassification was successful. 
If the drawing is open, the drawing symbol is immediately modified to display the symbol associated with the 
mapped class on the drawing canvas. If the drawing is not open, the symbol is modified to display the symbol 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 578
associated with the mapped class when opened. 
If the grid record or drawing element is mapped to a class that is not associated with a symbol, a red box with a 
cross displays on the drawing canvas. 
If the same symbol is displayed on the drawing canvas when a drawing element is reclassified, the label 
connections are maintained. If a different symbol is displayed on the drawing canvas when a drawing element is 
reclassified, labels are disconnected. 
Reclassify Distributed Attributes 
Once the tag has been successfully reclassified, any associated attributes will also need to be reclassified. If a 
user is in a team that has subscribed to reclassification notifications, a notification will be received. 
Note: 
Not all reclassified tags have distributed attributes for any given discipline. 
A confirmation message is displayed indicating that the reclassification of distributed attributes was successful. 
Reclassify an existing counter 
If an physical object is reclassified from one class to another where the naming rules are the same then the 
existing counter is retained. 
When a physical object is reclassified from one class to another where the naming rules do not match, then the 
counter is released from the source tag and becomes available from use with another tag. 
Note: 
If the target class of tag to be reclassified has a naming rule with mandatory naming parts and values for those 
attributes are empty then the Rename Tag window is displayed, enter the required values. 
Add Parts 
Adds parts to a specified tag as defined in the conceptual model. Sub-parts can be added to classes with has Part 
or can have Part associations. 
Use the Add Parts window to display existing parts and to add parts to tags. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 579
The Add Parts window displays a list of classes that can be added as parts. 
Add Part Type 
Use the Select Class window to add a class to the list of classes. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 580
Search 
Type a string to be searched in the Search field, 
matching content is displayed below the field. 
Alternatively, navigate through the tree hierarchy and 
select a class. 
Select 
Selects the class and closes the Select Class window. 
Filter 
Class 
Filters the list of classes using a selection from the 
drop-down list. 
Has existing parts 
If the check box is selected, the list of classes is filtered 
to display only classes that have associated sub-parts. 
If the check box is not selected, the list of classes is 
not filtered to display only associated sub-parts. 
Class 
Displays the class name. 
No. of Tags 
Specifies the number of sub-parts to add to the class. 
Tags 
Displays the sub-parts that are associated with the class. The functionality is not available if there are no sub-
parts associated with a class. 
Naming Rule 
Modifies the naming convention for each associated sub-part. The Name, Default Name and Counter Type can 
be modified. 
Note: 
The functionality becomes available when the No. of Tags field is populated. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 581
Click OK to add the sub-parts to the specified class. 
A progress bar is displayed during the sub-parts association process. 
A confirmation message is displayed indicating that the sub-parts association was successful. 
Refer to Counter Options for further information. 
Add Parts and range configuration 
The Add Parts window automatically validates the range values to make sure the value entered is checked 
against the allowed range. 
On setting the values for attributes that participate in the counter range configuration, all the rules for counter 
range configuration run as per their sequence in the configuration till one of the rules passes. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
