Page 582
If the provided values passes any rule of the counter range configurations, then only a counter within the 
maximum and minimum range displayed in the New Tag window can be selected. 
If the provided values fail all the rules of the counter range configuration, then the counter type can be selected 
as Next, First or Override. 
If all reserved counters are already taken, then reject create/update operation with an appropriate message is 
displayed. 
Duplicate Record 
Creates and links a copy of the currently selected record. 
Link Records 
Links the currently selected records. 
Use the Link Set window to define the source record and the linked records. 
Set Source 
Defines the currently selected record in the Link Set 
list as the source record. The source record is 
displayed in the Source Tag field. 
Link Set 
Defines the records that are linked to the source 
record. 
Delete 
Deletes the currently selected record from the Link Set 
list. 
Unlink Records 
Deletes the link between the currently selected records. 
Delete Records 
Deletes the currently selected records. The Confirm Delete window is displayed. Click Yes to confirm the deletion 
or click No to cancel the deletion. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 583
Note: 
If a record is deleted that is referenced on a drawing, the symbol and all attached labels that represent the item 
are deleted from the drawing canvas. 
Note: 
Records can also be deleted using the Database Explorer. 
Refer to Database Explorer for further information. 
Related Drawings 
Use the Open Related Drawings window to view and open the drawings which are related to the currently 
selected record or drawing object. 
Name 
Displays the name of the related drawing. 
Status 
Displays the access permissions for the related drawing. 
Drawing Type 
Displays the drawing type of the related drawing. 
Refer to Duplicate Record and Unlink Records for further information. 
Counter Options 
Use to override the counter used for naming a tag. If the naming rules have counters the Counter Type 
functionality will be enabled on the New Tag window; default is Next. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 584
First 
Use to backfill counters (when a counter is overridden to a specific value, gaps in the counter values can result, 
these gaps can be backfilled using this functionality). 
The Sequential checkbox is enabled when the number of tags requested is more than one. 
How the backfill occurs can be specified - sequentially or un sequentially. To backfill sequentially, click to check 
the Sequential check box (default). 
Sequential - all gaps in the counter values are checked, to determine if there is a range equal to the number of 
tags available. If the range is not available you will be unable to create the tags. 
Non-sequential - the counter values are assigned where ever they are available. If there are no gaps the counter 
values will be assigned the next available counters. If there are gaps; but not all of the counters can be assigned, 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 585
then the gaps will be filled first and the remainder assigned the next ones available. 
Next 
Use to assign the next available counter. 
Override 
Use to specify a user defined counter, in the Counter Value field enter the required counter. As previously 
described, the Sequential check box will not be enabled if the No of tags requested is one (the number must be 
more than one). 
Note: 
If it is not possible to have the requested number, the tag request is rejected. If the tag request is sequential and 
counters are not available in sequence then whole request is rejected. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 586
Rename 
The Rename Tag window is populated with the naming rule(s) associated with the selected tag. For renaming the 
default is Override and the current counter allocated to the selected tag is displayed. 
Once the tag is renamed and a new counter allotted, the previous used counter is released for reuse. 
Purge 
Use this functionality to purge a element selected from a grid and release the counter allotted to it for re use. 
Note: 
Purge functionality is not available for a COR or Tag Management System and COR project. 
To purge an element and release the allocated counter, the element must be first selected in the grid, then 
deleted before it can be purged. 
If this functionality is greyed out you do not have the permission to carry out this task. 
Once the purge has been accomplished a message in the Messages window ‘Purge: Element purged 
successfully’ (clearly displaying the difference between purged and deleted tags). 
On purging a tag the following control elements will also be deleted: 
• Revision and status link elements controlling the tag 
• Counter elements. 
Note: 
If the tag has parts and associations, these are also deleted. 
Note: 
Older tags created will be purged of the same elements as new tags except the counter allotted will not be 
released and cannot be reused. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 587
Range Configuration 
The creation of a tag for a class is constrained to use a counter within the range defined for the class. The range 
is implicitly controlled by the range configuration on the naming rule whose class has a range configuration 
defined. 
The New Tag window validates the requested range so that a counter cannot be taken out that is reserved. 
The class and class type combination form the criteria to select which configuration is applicable for create/
update operation. 
Note: 
For creating/updating a tag whose class has a range configuration defined, a counter cannot be taken out from 
the reserved range unless one of the rules of configuration is passed. 
Minimum and Maximum Counter Range in New/Rename Tag 
The minimum and maximum range for the counter is displayed in the New Tag window. On setting the values for 
attributes that participate in the range configuration, the minimum and maximum allowed per configuration is 
displayed. 
If values entered are not in the configuration then the minimum and maximum functionality is hidden and 
counter for tag will be outside reserved range. If the values fail all rules of the range configuration, the Next, First 
or Override functionality is displayed. 
On setting the values for attributes that participate in the range configuration, all the rules for range 
configuration will run as per their sequence in the configuration until one of the rules passes. 
If any of provided values pass any rule of the range configuration: 
• Next, First or Override is not available 
• Able to choose counter within minimum and maximum range only 
If the provided values fail all rules of the range configuration: 
• Next, First or Override is available 
If all the reserved counters are already taken, then create/update operation is rejected and an error message is 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 588
displayed. 
The sequential option is considered when providing a counter value for multiple tags. If counters are not 
available sequentially, no tags are created. 
Inheritance 
The range configuration for a generalized (parent) class is applicable to all specialized (child) classes. If multiple 
ranges are found, then the closest one in hierarchy is applicable. Range configuration for a specialized (child) 
class ignores the range configuration of generalized (parent) class. The rule(s) for parent class is not used if a 
child class has defined its own range configuration. 
Silent creation/modification of tags 
Silent creation/modification of tags from Drawings, Enclosures and Terminations editor diagrams automatically 
validate the range values as per the reserved ranges. If the silent creation/modification provides a counter to 
override and other values will result in passing of a range configuration rule for the class of the tag, then the 
counter is validated within the reserved range. 
If the silent creation/modification does not provide a counter to override and other values will result in passing 
of a range configuration rule for the class of the tag, then next counter shall be taken out within the reserved 
range. 
If the silent creation/modification does not provide a counter to override and other values will not result in 
passing of a range configuration rule for the class of the tag, then the next counter will be taken out outside of 
the reserved range. 
If all counters within reserved range are already taken, then creation/modification of tag is rejected with an error 
message is displayed. 
Reclassification 
The reclassification process tries to retain the counter where possible. If a target class has a range defined and 
retention of current counter violates that range, then next available counter that is not reserved is taken. 
Range value validation from Grid, Property Palette and Datasheets 
When a tag is modified from Grid, Property Palette or Datasheets, range values are validated as per the allowed 
ranges defined in the range configuration for that tag. 
When modifying, a value on a tag triggers a change in the counter and the class of the tag has a range 
configuration defined then the counter is validated within the reserved range. If a range rule passes, a counter 
from an unreserved range is taken out if new values on tag do not pass any of the rules. The retention of counter 
is checked to make sure the range configuration rules are not violated. If all the reserved counters are already 
taken, then create/update operation will be rejected and an error message is displayed. 
Copy, duplicate, duplicate with referencing items 
The range value validations are applicable for Copy, Duplicate or Duplicate with referencing items for a tag. 
• If copying/duplicating/duplicate with referencing item results in the passing of a range configuration rule for 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 589
the class of the tag, then the next counter within the reserved range is taken out 
• If all counters within the reserved range are already taken, then creation of tag is rejected and an error 
message displayed. 
Add Parts 
The Add Parts window automatically validates the range values to make sure the entered value is validated 
against the allowed range. On the setting of the values for attributes that participate in the counter range 
configuration, all the rules for range configuration are run as per their sequence in the configuration until one of 
the rules passes. 
If the provided values pass any rule of the range configuration: 
• Next, First or Override is not available 
• The minimum and maximum values are displayed. 
• Only able to choose counter within minimum and maximum range 
If the provided values fail all rules of the range configuration, Next, First or Override is available. If all the 
reserved counters are already taken, then create/update operation is rejected and an error message is displayed. 
Refer to Create Record, Create Record in Tag Management System Enabled Project, Create Multiple Records, 
Select Single Record, Select Multiple Records, Select All Records, Copy, Cut Records, Copy Records, Paste 
Records, Edit Records, Rename Record, Auto Rename Records, Reclassify record(s), drawing elements and 
existing counter, Add Parts, Duplicate Record, Link Records, Unlink Records, Delete Records and Related 
Drawings for further information. 
Sub Tag 
Use the functionality to create or select an exisiting sub tag. 
Create New 
Creates new sub tag 
Select Exisiting 
Selects existing sub tag 
View Grid 
A number of functions are available to modify the grid view. 
Explorer Options 
Defines auto refresh and current element tracking options for the selected explorer. For example, Grids Explorer. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 590
Auto Refresh 
Turns on/off the automatic refresh of the explorer after database changes. 
Track CE 
Turns on/off tracking of the currently selected element on the explorer. 
Zoom 
Use the zoom slider on the status bar to increase or decrease the magnification of the grid. 
The current zoom percentage is displayed to the left of the slider. If the zoom percentage is clicked, the Zoom
window is displayed: 
Select a pre-defined magnification level or type a custom magnification level. 
View Horizontal Tab Group 
Displays grids separately and horizontally. The currently selected grid is displayed in the lower section of the grid 
view. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 591
If three or more grids are open when the option is selected, the additional grids are displayed as tabs in the 
upper section. 
If an additional grid is opened once the option has been selected, this grid is displayed as a tab in the same view 
as the currently selected grid. 
View Vertical Tab Group 
Displays grids separately and vertically. The currently selected grid is displayed in the right section of the grid 
view. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 592
If three or more grids are open when the option is selected, the additional grids are displayed as tabs in the left 
section. 
If an additional grid is opened once the option has been selected, this grid is displayed as a tab in the same view 
as the currently selected grid. 
Move Grid to Previous Tab Group 
Moves the current grid to the section above or section to the left if the current grid view has been split into two 
or more view sections using either New Horizontal Tab Group or New Vertical Tab Group. 
If all grids are moved from a view section, the tab is closed and the grid display reverts to the default layout. 
Move Grid to Next Tab Group 
Moves the current grid to the section below or section to the right if the current grid view has been split into two 
or more view sections using either New Horizontal Tab Group or New Vertical Tab Group. 
If all grids are moved from a view section, the tab is closed and the grid display reverts to the default layout. 
Tabbed View 
Defines the grid view tab settings. 
If the check box is selected, each grid is displayed as a separate tab. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 593
If the check box is not selected each grid is displayed as a separate window. 
Vertical View 
Turns the card view display of grids on or off. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 594
Multi-level headers cannot be displayed in vertical view mode. Only the headers of individual columns are 
displayed. Grouped column headers are not displayed. If data cannot be displayed in vertical view mode a 
message is displayed. 
Display Short Titles 
Defines the grid title display settings. 
If the check box is selected, the title tab of each grid includes the grid name. 
If the check box is not selected, the title tab of each grid includes the category name and the grid name.

Filter Grid Data 
Filters can be applied to grids to display only certain records. For example, only elements with a particular 
description. 
A filter row is displayed below the currently selected column header if the Column Filtering check box is 
selected. 
Clear All Filters 
Removes all filters from the grid. 
Filter 
Type a value in the cell to filter the column. 
Filter Criteria 
Defines additional filter criteria, the behaviour of the 
option is different in multi-select filter mode and 
alternate filter mode. 
Clear Column Filter 
Removes all filters from the column. 
When a filter is applied, this is indicated by a message displayed on the status bar. 
Multi-Select Filter Mode 
If the Multi-select Filter Style check box is selected, multi-select filter mode is activated. 
Hover the cursor over the filter row for a column and click the filter criteria arrow that is displayed to the right of 
the cell to apply a multi-select filter to a column. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 596
The filter criteria list displays all the filtered cell values, plus All and Blanks. 
Select the check box adjacent to a cell value to include the cell in the filter. 
Select the All check box to select all of the check boxes adjacent to the cell values. Select the All check box again 
to deselect all check boxes adjacent to the cell values. 
Select the Blanks check box to include rows without a value in the column in the filter. 
Text Filters 
Applies additional filter criteria to the filter. 
= 
Equals 
Includes all values that are equal to 
the filter value. 
¹ 
Not Equals 
Includes all values that are not 
equal to the filter value. 
< 
Less Than 
Includes all values that are smaller 
than the filter value. 
£ 
Less Than or Equal To 
Includes all values that are smaller 
than the filter value or is equal to 
the filter value. 
> 
Greater Than 
Includes all values that are greater 
than the filter value. 
³ 
Greater Than or Equal To 
Includes all values that are greater 
than the filter value or is equal to 
the filter value. 
Like (wildcards) 
... 
Not Like (wildcards) 
...

... 
Does Not Match 
...

(regular expression)

Includes all values that starts with 
the filter value.

Custom Filter 
Use the Custom Filter window to create a customized text filter for the column using various filter rules. 
FIlter based on 
All 
Includes values that match all of the custom rules in 
the filter. 
Any 
Includes values that match any of the custom rules in 
the filter.

Column

Page 598
Alternate Filter Mode 
If the Multi-select Filter Style check box is not selected, alternate filter mode is activated. 
Click the icon that is displayed to the left of the cell and select a filter criteria from the drop-down list. For 
example, Starts with, Contains or Does not end with. The default value is Contains. 
Type a value in the cell or select a value from a drop-down list using the arrow that is displayed to the right of the 
cell to filter the column. 
The drop-down list contains every value in the column, plus Custom, Blanks and NonBlanks. 
Select a value to display the individual record. 
Custom 
Creates a customized text filter, the Custom Filter 
window is displayed. 
Blanks 
Displays rows without a column value filter. 
NonBlanks 
Displays rows with a column value in the filter. 
Clear Filters 
Removes all filters from the grid. 
Group Grid Data 
Grid data can be grouped using either single or multiple attributes. Grouped grid data can also be ungrouped. 
Group Grid Data using Single Attribute 
The display format of grids can be changed so that records with common attributes are grouped together. For 
example, records can be grouped according to their Tag Number or Function. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 599
A grouping panel is displayed above the column headers if the Grouping Panel check box is selected. 
Drag and drop the column header of the required attribute into the grouping panel to group the data. Two 
vertical arrows are displayed to assist the positioning of the column header. 
The grid display changes to display the name of each group in the following format: 
<Attribute Name>: <Attribute Value> <(Number of records in the group)>. 
The name of the column that records are grouped by is displayed in the grouping panel. 
Click + next to the group name to expand a group and to display the records within a group. 
Note: 
The fields within a group can be fixed, sorted, moved and filtered. 
Click - next to the group name to collapse a group and to remove the records within a group from the display. 
Refer to Ungroup Grid Data for further information. 
Group Grid Data using Multiple Attributes 
The display format of grids can be changed so that records can be grouped using more than one attribute. For 
example, Function and Tag Number. 
A grouping panel is displayed above the column headers if the Grouping Panel check box is selected. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 600
Drag and drop the column header of the first required attribute into the grouping panel. Two vertical arrows are 
displayed to assist the positioning of the column header. 
Drag and drop the column header of the second required attribute to the right of the first attribute in the 
grouping panel. Two vertical arrows are displayed to assist the positioning of the column header. 
The records are grouped using the first attribute then using the second attribute. 
The grid display changes to display the name of each group in the following format: 
<Attribute Name>: <Attribute Value> <(Number of records in the group)>. 
The name of the column that records are grouped by is displayed in the grouping panel. The grouping order is 
displayed in the header of each grouped column. 
Click + next to the group name to expand a group and to display the records within a group. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 601
Note: The fields within a group can be fixed, sorted, moved and filtered. 
Click - next to the group name to collapse a group and to remove the records within a group from the display. 
Refer to Ungroup Grid Data for further information. 
Ungroup Grid Data 
Removes data from a grouped display format. 
Drag and drop the header from the grouping panel to the column header to ungroup data. 
Refer to Group Grid Data for further information. 
View Columns 
A number of functions are available to modify the column view. 
Resize Columns 
Changes the width of the currently selected column. 
Click and hold on the line between columns and drag the line left or right to make it wider or narrower. Double-
click on the line to resize the column to fit the largest string of text within the column. 
Refer to Auto Size Columns for further information. 
Change Column Order 
Moves a column to a new grid position. 
Click and hold a column header and drag it to a new position between two other columns to change the column 
order. Two vertical arrows are displayed to assist the positioning of the column. 
Combine Columns 
Combines data from multiple columns into a single column. 
Click and hold a column header and drag it on top of the header of the column with which it is to be combined. 
Two horizontal arrows are displayed to assist the positioning of the column. 
The columns are then combined. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 602
Column Summaries 
Defines the settings for the calculation of a total value for a numeric column. 
If the check box is selected, a sum icon is displayed in the column header and a column summary can be applied. 
If the check box is not selected, a sum icon is not displayed in the column header and a column summary cannot 
be applied. 
Click the sum icon in the column header to display the Select Summaries window. 
Use the Select Summaries window to define the calculations that are applied to the column. 
Average 
Defines the calculation of an average column value. 
If the check box is selected, the average column value 
is calculated. 
If the check box is not selected, the average column 
value is not calculated. 
Count 
Defines the calculation of a cell count for the column. 
If the check box is selected, the cell count value is 
calculated. 
If the check box is not selected, the cell count value is 
not calculated. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 603
Maximum 
Defines the calculation of a maximum (highest) 
column value. 
If the check box is selected, the highest column value 
is calculated. 
If the check box is not selected, the highest column 
value is not calculated. 
Minimum 
Defines the calculation of a minimum (lowest) column 
value. 
If the check box is selected, the lowest column value is 
calculated. 
If the check box is not selected, the lowest column 
value is not calculated. 
Sum 
Defines the calculation of a sum total of the column 
values. 
If the check box is selected, the sum total of the 
column values is calculated. 
If the check box is not selected, the sum total of the 
column values is not calculated. 
Column Sorting 
Defines column sorting settings according to the values in a selected column. 
Click on the column header to reverse the sort order. By default, the sort order is lowest value to highest (for 
example: A to Z, 1 to 9). 
The column sort order is indicated by an arrow in the column header. An arrow pointing upwards indicates the 
sort order is lowest to highest. An arrow pointing downwards indicates the sort order is highest to lowest. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 604
While making selections, hold Shift to sort multiple columns. The list is sorted in the order in which each column 
is selected. 
By default, the sort function is enabled for each column. If the sort function has been disabled, right-click on a 
selected cell and select Column Sorting from the drop-down list. 
Column Pins 
Automatically positions a column as the first (left) column of the grid. The column remains displayed at the left-
hand side of the grid even when the grid is scrolled to the right, so that it is always in view. 
Click on a pin in the header to pin a column. 
When a column is pinned, the pin points downwards. Click the pin again to unpin the column. 
Change Column Units 
Defines the unit of measure used to quantify values in a column. 
Click the ruler icon in the column header and select a unit of measure from the drop-down list. 
The values in the column are recalculated in accordance with the selected unit of measure. 
Show Units in Headers 
Defines the display position of units of measure in columns. 
If the check box is selected, the unit of measure is displayed in the column header. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 605
If the check box is not selected, the unit of measure is displayed in each cell. 
Wrap Text in Headers 
Defines the display of data in column headers. 
If the check box is selected, column header data which is too wide for the column is wrapped onto another line. 
If the check box is not selected, column header data which is too wide for the column is not wrapped onto 
another line. 
Auto Size Columns 
Auto-sizes all columns in the grid so that each column is wide enough to fully display the longest string of text. 
Refer to Resize Columns for further information. 
Cells 
Project data in the Engineer module is viewed and edited using a number of cells, each of which displays a value 
in the grid. 
Note: 
If the database has been set to Explicit Claim mode, before elements can be edited they must first be claimed. 
If an attempt to edit an unclaimed element in such a database is made, the edit will fail. A message is issued 
explaining why, and the cell feedback color is set to that indicating an unsuccessful edit. 
Refer to Manage Claims, Messages and Message Log and Feedback Colors for further information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 606
Select Cells and Values in Cells 
Click on a cell to select it, the selected cell is highlighted. 
Double click on a cell to highlight the cell value. 
Edit Cell Data 
Cell data can be edited directly in a list by double-clicking on the value and typing in a new value, or by selecting 
a different value from a list of values. 
Successfully edited cells, unsuccessfully edited cells and read-only cells are indicated with a feedback color. 
A failed attempt at editing the data in a cell is indicated with a background color. For example, a red background. 
Hover the mouse cursor over an edited cell to view a tooltip message indicating the cell status. 
Edit Long Text 
Functionality is available to aid in the editing of long text. Click within a cell, then click and drag the bottom-right 
hand corner of the cell down and to the right to open a larger area to edit the text. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Press Alt+Enter to create a new line of text. 
Refer to Feedback Colors for further information. 
Clear Cells 
Deletes data from any number of selected cells, right-click the selected cells and select Clear from the drop-
down list to delete the cell data. 
List of Values 
Populates cells using a list of values, cllick the arrow that is displayed when the mouse is hovered over a cell. A 
value can be selected from the drop-down list. 
Engineering Reference Browser 
Populates cells using a referenced value. 
Click the ellipsis that is displayed when the mouse is hovered over a cell to display the Engineering Reference 
Browser window.

Array of References 
Populates cells using a pre-defined reference value. 
Click the ellipsis that is displayed when the mouse is hovered over a cell to display the Reference Array Browser

Edit 
Edits the currently selected array value, use the Engineering Reference Browser window to replace the existing 
value. 
Add

Remove

Up

Down

Find and Replace Text 
Any text string within a grid can be found, highlighted and optionally replaced. Text can also be searched in 
column headers. 
Find Text 
Type a string to be searched in the Find field and click Find to search the currently displayed grid (and column 
headers) for matching content. 
The arrow on the right of the Find field displays a drop-down list of recent searches that are available for 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 610
selection. 
The arrow on the right of Find displays a drop-down list of find and replace options that are available for 
selection. 
Next 
Finds the next match. 
Previous 
Finds the previous match. 
Search for Tag 
Finds matching records in all grids, sub-grids and datasheets in the database. Results are displayed in the Find 
results window. 
Use the Find results window, to select and open the required grids, sub-grids and datasheets. 
Search in Headings 
Finds matching text in the column headers of the currently displayed grid. 
If the option is deselected, the Search in Data values option is automatically selected. 
Search in Data Values 
Finds matching text in the data cells of the currently displayed grid. 
If the option is deselected, the Search in Headings option is automatically selected. 
Find/Replace Text 
Finds customized matching content which can be optionally replaced. 
Use the Find and Replace window to define customized find and replace settings. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 611
The Find tab defines the find settings. 
Find what 
Type a string to be searched. 
The arrow on the right of the Find what field displays a drop-down list of recent searches that are available for 
selection. 
Options 
Defines the customized find settings. 
+ 
Displays the customized find settings. 
- 
Removes the customized find settings from the 
display. 
Search 
By Row 
Searches for matching text row by row. 
By Column 
Searches for matching text column by column. 
Down 
Searches for matching text from the top of the grid in 
a downward direction. 
Up 
Searches for matching text from the bottom of the 
grid in an upward direction. 
Match case 
Finds only matching text with a matching case. 
Match entire cell contexts 
Finds only matching text that matches the cell exactly. 
Use wildcards 
Finds matching text using wildcard symbols. For 
example, an asterisk can be used to find any string of 
characters or a question mark can be used to find any 
single character. 
Find Next 
Finds matching text. If no matches are found, a message is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 612
The Replace tab defines the replace settings. 
Replace with 
Type a string to be replaced. 
The arrow on the right of the Replace with field displays a drop-down list of recent replacements that are 
available for selection. 
Replace All 
Replaces all matching text, without confirmation. 
Replace 
Replaces the currently selected text. 
View Cells 
A number of functions are available to modify the cell view. 
Show Unset/Nulref Values 
Defines the display of unset and nulref values in cells. 
If the check box is selected, unset and nulref cell values are displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 613
If the check box is not selected, unset and nulref cell values are not displayed. 
Show Leading Slash 
Defines the display of cell values that are prefixed with a slash. For example, Name and SYSYFS. 
If the check box is selected, cell values that are prefixed with a slash are displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, cell values that are prefixed with a slash are not displayed. 
Show Logically Deleted 
Defines the display of elements that have been deleted but remain in the database. 
If the check box is selected, elements that have been deleted but remain in the database are displayed. The 
deleted elements are displayed with an overstrike. 
If the check box is not selected, elements that have been deleted but remain in the database are not displayed. 
Show Notifications 
Displays the notifications in the active and sub grids, if the check box is selected the notifications are displayed in 
the grids. 
Notifications are displayed in grids/sub grids on owing tags if a linked referencing tag is logically deleted or if a 
part is deleted in a sub grid. For example - A pump has a nozzle if the nozzle is logically deleted a notification is 
displayed on the pump in the grid. The notification on the pump is cleared if the nozzle is un deleted or purged. 
Notifications are also displayed on the owning tag if a linked referencing tag is renamed, all affected tags are 
displayed irrespective of who renamed them. Click on the notification in the grid to display details of all the 
renamed tag(s). 
View Attribute Metadata 
Filters grid columns or grid data based on the metadata values of the attributes on which they are based. 
Metadata 
The drop-down list contains a list of the names (the User Defined Names) of all of the User Defined Attribute 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 614
(UDAs) that apply to UDAs, plus Category and a blank option. 
Note: 
The drop-down list is only enabled when a grid is active. 
Value 
The drop-down list is populated with a list of User Defined Valids (UVALIDs) from the UDAs selected in the 
Metadata drop-down list, plus a blank option and an expression option. 
Note: 
The drop-down list is only enabled once a selection has been made in the Metadata drop-down list. 
Clear 
Removes the applied filter. 
Feedback Colors 
Indicates the status of cells using a background color. 
Use the Feedback Colours drop-down list to modify the cell color for each feedback type. 
Read Only Cell Colour 
Defines the cell feedback color for read only cells. 
Edit Fail Colour 
Defines the cell feedback color for a cell that is 
unsuccessfully edited ((for example, if an invalid entry 
is made), or required data is missing (for example, a 
blank cell that must be completed). 
Edit Success Colour 
Defines the cell feedback color for a cell that is 
successfully edited. 
Edit Highlight Colour 
Defines the cell feedback color for cells which have a 
condition status, for example, the cell contents have 
been claimed. 
Reset Default Feedback Colours 
Resets the cell feedback colors to the default colors. 
In the example below the cell with a blue background has been edited successfully, the cell with a red 
background was not edited successfully, and the cells with a grey background are read-only. 
Use the Color window to define the cell color. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 615
Select the required color from the color palette, refer to Manage Claims for further information. 
Reset Cell Feedback Colors 
Removes cell feedback cell colors from a grid. 
Project Breakdown Structure 
The project breakdown structure presents database content dependent on user needs. 
Data can be displayed as one or more user-defined structures. Each structure displays on the explorer as a 
hierarchy starting from a separate root node and can display database items organized according to, for example, 
system or class, depending on the definition in Lexicon. 
The Project breakdown structure hierarchy is generated during runtime from a Lexicon definition and its nodes 
can represent database items as well as label text generated from the definition. Selecting a node connected to a 
database item navigates the database hierarchy. 
A context menu with basic commands is available for relevant nodes. 
To expand a node in the hierarchy, click  to the left of the node name. To contract an expanded node, click  to 
the left of the node name. 
More than one node can be dragged and dropped from within or outside the system hierarchy. If required the 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 616
correct hierarchy structure is created and the moved nodes are automatically renamed to reflect their new 
owner in the hierarchy along with their details and properties. 
Split Project Breakdown Structure 
Displays flexible explorer definitions (templates) in the project in separate tabbed windows. 
Join Project Breakdown Structure 
Displays flexible explorer definitions (templates) in the project in a single window. 
Database Explorer 
The Database Explorer displays the project database hierarchy and the Database -Presentation tab. 
The Engineering tab displays the Engineering hierarchy: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 617
The System tab displays the System hierarchy: 
The hierarchies are displayed in a standard Explorer tree-view. 
Elements which have been claimed locally are displayed in bold. 
To expand a node in the hierarchy, click  to the left of the node name. To contract an expanded node, click  to 
the left of the node name. 
If the Track Current Element (CE) checkbox is selected, the attributes of the currently selected element in the 
hierarchy are displayed to the right of the explorer. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 618
The displayed attributes can be edited. 
Note: 
If the database has been set to Explicit Claim mode, before elements can be edited they must first be claimed. 
If an attempt to edit an unclaimed element in such a database is made, the edit will fail. 
A message is issued explaining why and the cell feedback color is set indicating an unsuccessful edit. 
Refer to Manage Claims, Messages and Message Log, Feedback Colors for further information. 
Create Database Element 
Opens a drop-down menu from which a new element can be created. The drop-down menu consists of element 
types which can be members of the currently selected element. 
New elements are created without a name. They are given a default identifier in the explorer consisting of the 
element type suffixed with a sequential number, for example, NOZZTAG 1. 
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select New from the drop-down menu. 
Rename Database Element 
Renames an element or an element and all its members on the database hierarchy. 
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select Rename from the drop-down menu. 
Type a new element name. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 619
Only 
The name change is applied to the current element 
only. 
Re-name all 
The name change is cascaded down to those members 
of the current element which incorporate the affected 
text within their names. 
Note: Re-name all is recommended to rename the 
entire hierarchy below. 
Un-name 
The name becomes unset (the element is then 
identified only by its reference number). 
To modify the names of other elements, select the element on the Database Explorer and click CE. 
Delete Database Element 
Deletes the currently selected database element, right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and 
select Delete from the drop-down menu. 
The Confirm window is displayed. Click Yes to confirm the deletion or click No to cancel the deletion. 
Open New Database Explorer 
Opens a new Database Explorer window, either the current element and its members, or the owner element of 
the current element and its members are displayed. 
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select New Explorer from the drop-down menu. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 620
Cut Database Element 
Cuts and deletes the currently selected database element on the Database Explorer. The element is stored on the 
clipboard for a later paste operation. 
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select Cut from the drop-down menu. 
Copy Database Element 
Copies the currently selected database element on the Database Explorer. The element is stored on the 
clipboard for a later paste operation. 
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select Copy from the drop-down menu. 
Paste Database Element 
Pastes the database element that is stored on the clipboard. 
Right-click the selected element on the Database Explorer and select Paste from the drop-down menu. 
Add Database Elements to Design Reuse Export List 
Adds the currently selected database element to the Design Reuse Export List. 
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select Add to Design Reuse Export List from the 
drop-down menu. 
Refer to Export for further information. 
Claim Elements 
Claims elements to local claims. 
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select Records or Extracts from the drop-down 
menu. 
Refer to Claim Records to Local Claims for further information. 
Release Elements 
Releases elements from local claims. 
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select Records or Extracts from the drop-down 
menu. 
Refer to Release Records from Local Claims for further information. 
Flush Extracted Database Elements 
Updates parent extracts/databases with changes made to extracted elements. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 621
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select Extracts from the drop-down menu. 
Refer to Flush Extracts for further information. 
Issue Extracted Database Elements 
Issues changes made to extracted elements to their parent extract/database and releases those elements. 
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select Extracts from the drop-down menu. 
Refer to Issue Extracts for further information. 
Drop Extracted Database Elements Changes 
Undoes all changes made to extracted elements and releases those extracts. 
Right-click a selected element on the Database Explorer and select Extracts from the drop-down menu. 
Refer to Drop Extracts for further information.

Configures the behaviour of the Explorer display.

Auto Collapse Tree 
Defines the auto collapse tree settings for all active 
explorers. 
If the check box is selected, expanded nodes not 
contained in the current element are collapsed. 
If the check box is not selected, expanded nodes not 
contained in the current element are not collapsed. 
Expand to CE 
Defines the auto expand tree settings for all changed 
elements. 
If the check box is selected, the tree is expanded when 
the current element is changed (if not already 
expanded). 
If the check box is not selected, the tree is not 
expanded when the current element is changed. 
Display Folders 
Defines the explorer settings for the display of folders. 
If the check box is selected, folders are displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, folders are not 
displayed. 
Hide non-user System Data 
Defines the explorer settings for the display of non-
user system data. 
If the check box is selected, non-user system data is 
not displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, non-user system data 
is displayed. 
Show TUBI/ROD 
Defines the explorer settings for the display of TUBI 
and ROD elements. 
If the check box is selected, TUBI and ROD elements 
are displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, TUBI and ROD 
elements are not displayed. 
Hide non-writable LIBY 
Defines the explorer settings for the display of non-
writable LIBY data. 
If the check box is selected, non-writable LIBY data is 
not displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, non-writable LIBY data 
is displayed. 
Show Point Worlds 
Defines the explorer settings for the display of point 
worlds. 
If the check box is selected, point worlds are 
displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, point worlds are not

View Attributes Alphabetically 
Displays database attributes as a grid (the default setting).

Attribute Display 
Defines the display of database attributes for the currently selected element on the Database Explorer. 
User Defined Attributes 
Defines the display of user defined attributes for the currently selected element on the Database Explorer. 
If the check box is selected, user defined attributes are displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, user defined attributes are not displayed. 
Pseudo Attributes 
Defines the display of pseudo attributes for the currently selected element on the Database Explorer.

P-points

Data Types

Show Unset/Nulref

Status Management 
Elements can be assigned a lifecycle consisting of a defined status, which it progresses through. 
Status definitions are specified and applied by Administrators in Lexicon. 
The Engineer module is provided with the following status management functionality: 
• A graphical viewer which displays the lifecycle associated with the elements in the current grid as a workflow 
diagram. 
• Elements in a grid can be selected according to their status. 
• The status of an element or elements in a grid can be changed, including adding and removing elements to/
from a lifecycle. 
• Users can be alerted to status changes on a tagged item(s) made by disciplines other than their own. 
• A status change log. 
• A graphical representation of the proportion of elements at each status in a grid. 
Refer to Configuring Status Definitions in Lexicon and Status Definitions for further information. 
Status Control 
Displays the status definitions (lifecycles) applied to the elements in the current grid, each lifecycle can be 
viewed graphically and matching status values can be displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 628
Status Definition 
Displays a list of the status 
definitions (lifecycles) applied to 
the elements in the current grid 
that are available for selection. 
The class name of the selected tag 
is appended to the Display Tag 
value of the status definition 
(specified in the Workflow Explorer
in Configuration) 
The Display Tag attribute is 
available in both conceptual and 
non-conceptual model projects. If 
no value is set for the Display Tag 
attribute, then the description of 
the status definition is used. 
Note: 
In conceptual model enabled 
projects, the display name of the 
discipline is appended to the class 
name of discipline-specific class 
types (XPITEM). This helps you to 
identify which status is being 
applied on which discipline. 
For example - Cabinet class 
discipline specific types 
(CabinetMECHUDET and 
CabinetELECUDET) are displayed 
with the discipline name and class 
name. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 629
Status Graphical Viewer 
Displays a graphical representation 
of the selected lifecycle. 
Status Values 
Displays a list of lifecycle status 
values that are available for 
selection. 
Select Rows 
Selects all the elements in a grid 
that share the same lifecycle status. 
A progress bar is displayed during 
the search process. The search can 
be cancelled if required. When the 
search is complete, items with the 
specified status are selected and 
highlighted. 
Promote Element Status 
Changes the status of selected elements to the next status in the lifecycle. 
If a database has been set to Explicit Claim mode, before the status of elements can be edited they must first be 
claimed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 630
If an attempt to change the state of an unclaimed element in such a database is made, the attempt will fail. A 
message is issued explaining why. 
The Status window displays element information together with the new status value. The functionality of the 
Status window is described in the Edit Element Status topic. 
Controlled Object Revisioning (COR) is used to control when and if users are alerted to status changes of tagged 
item(s) by disciplines other than their own. COR enables this to be implemented by deploying an extract 
hierarchy that allows users to chose when to pick up status changes from other disciplines. Refer to the 
Administration User Guide for further information. 
Note: 
As the status control being used is mapped across an extract hierarchy, it is necessary to be logged into the 
correct maturity level Multiple Database (MDB) for the team in order to carry out certain status changes. Refer 
to the Administration User Guide for further information. 
Refer to Show Extract Claim State of Records, Messages and Message Log and Notifications for further 
information. 
Demote Element Status 
Changes the status of selected elements to the previous status in the lifecycle. 
If a database has been set to Explicit Claim mode, before the status of elements can be edited they must first be 
claimed. 
If an attempt to change the state of an unclaimed element in such a database is made, the attempt will fail. A 
message is issued explaining why. 
The Status window displays element information together with the new status value. The functionality of the 
Status window is described in the Edit Element Status topic. 
Controlled Object Revisioning (COR) is used to control when and if users are alerted to status changes of tagged 
item(s) by disciplines other than their own. COR enables this to be implemented by deploying an extract 
hierarchy that allows users to chose when to pick up status changes from other disciplines. Refer to the 
Administration User Guide for further information. 
Note: 
As the status control being used is mapped across an extract hierarchy, it is necessary to be logged into the 
correct maturity level MDB for the team in order to carry out certain status changes. Refer to the Administration 
User Guide for further information. 
Refer to Show Extract Claim State of Records, Messages and Message Log and Notifications for further 
information. 
Edit Element Status 
Changes the status of selected elements to a selected status in the lifecycle. 
If a database has been set to Explicit Claim mode, before the status of elements can be edited they must first be 
claimed. 
If an attempt to change the state of an unclaimed element in such a database is made, the attempt will fail. A 
message will be issued explaining why. 
The Status window displays element information together with the new status value. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 631
Controlled Object Revisioning (COR) is used to control when and if users are alerted to status changes of tagged 
item(s) by disciplines other than their own. COR enables this to be implemented by deploying an extract 
hierarchy that allows users to chose when to pick up status changes from other disciplines. Refer to the 
Administration User Guide for further information. 
Note: 
As the status control being used is mapped across an extract hierarchy, it is necessary to be logged into the 
correct maturity level Multiple Database (MDB) for the team in order to carry out certain status changes. Refer 
to the Administration User Guide for further information. 
Value 
Specifies a status value for the selected elements. The drop-down list consists of all available statuses, according 
to the definition of the lifecycle. 
If multiple elements are selected that are different status, or had their status changed at a different time and/or 
by a different user, mixed is displayed, use promote/demote to edit the status value. 
Reasons 
If status change reasons have been defined, a drop-down list is available. The list will consist of all the reasons 
that can be assigned. Refer to the Lexicon User Guide for more information. 
Comment 
If required, type a comment in the field. 
Revision 
If the element has been revised, the Revision part of the Status window becomes active. 
Use the Major or Minor radio buttons to specify the revision type. 
Add Elements to Lifecycle 
Adds elements that are not on the lifecycle currently selected for the grid. The elements cannot be promoted or 
demoted (the options on the tab are disabled). 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

The Value of the element is set to Not Controlled. Use the Value drop-down list to apply a status value to the 
element. 
Remove Elements from Lifecycle 
Removes elements that are on the lifecycle currently selected for the grid, the status of the elements is set to 
Not Controlled.

Displays a graphical breakdown indicating the proportion of selected elements at each status.

Hover the mouse cursor over a segment to view the percentage of selected elements that the segment 
represents.

Refresh

View Changes 
Data in one session can be compared to data in another and the differences highlighted, a log of data changes is 
also available. 
Historical data for the currently selected cell or row can also be displayed 
Highlight Changes in Current Session 
Highlights changes that have been made to the currently selected grid in the current session. 
Highlight Changes Between Sessions 
Highlights changes to the currently selected grid between selected sessions or selected date stamps. 
The Select Target and Base Version window is used to select a target session/stamp and a base session/stamp 
for comparison. 
Use the Stamps tab to browse the available date stamps for selection. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 637
Use the Sessions tab to browse the available sessions for selection. 
Target Version 
Specifies the target stamp/session for comparison. Click Set to populate the field with the currently selected 
stamp/session. 
Base Version 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 638
Specifies the base stamp/session for comparison. Click Set to populate the field with the currently selected 
stamp/session. 
Filter by selected DB number 
Filters the display of available stamps/sessions. 
If the check box is selected, only the stamps/sessions that match the Database (DB) number of the currently 
selected stamp/session are displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, the display of available stamps/sessions is not filtered. 
Differences between the selected sessions or stamps are highlighted. For example, if an element was added, 
modified or deleted after the base session but during or by the time of the target session, this is highlighted. 
Different highlight colors are used for different types of changes. In the example, modifications are highlighted in 
yellow. new records are highlighted in green and deleted records are highlighted in red. 
Highlight Changes Between Last and Current Session 
Highlights changes to the currently selected grid between the last session and the current session. 
View Row or Cell History 
Displays historical data for the currently selected cell or row. The historical data is displayed in a new tab. 
Note: 
If a row is based on different database elements, a prompt is displayed to specify which element historical data is 
displayed for. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 639
View Column History 
Displays historical column data for the currently selected cell, the historical data is displayed in a new tab 
(column history). 
Active directory username in a session history (TMS) 
Changes can be viewed in the session history refer to Database Concepts for further information. The TMS 
service also records and displays in the session history the audit directory user name of the user who have 
initiated the change in TMS. 
Note: 
AD user name is only displayed for Tags controlled by TMS (ENGLTE and FUNLTE) 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 640
Manage Claims 
If a database is set to Explicit Claim mode (refer to the Administration User Guide for further information), 
before records can be edited or have their status changed they must first be claimed using a Local Claim. 
If the database is set to Explicit Claim mode and is also an Extract database, records must also be claimed to an 
Extract Claim after being claimed to a Local Claim. 
If an attempt is made to edit or change the status of a record that must be claimed either locally or to an extract, 
the edit/status change fails and a message is issued explaining why. 
Data Management facilities in the Engineer module allow the following: 
• View the current local and extract claim state of records in a grid. 
• Claim and release records from a local claim. 
• Claim, release, flush, issue and drop records from an extract claim. 
For further details of Extracts, Claims and related concepts and procedures, refer to the Administration User 
Guide for more information. 
Refer to Messages and Message Log for further information. 
Show Local Claim State of Records 
Defines the display of records that are locally claimed or unclaimed, use the Show Claim State window to modify 
the display of records with a defined claim state. 
Action 
Defines the display style for records with a specified claim state. 
Highlight 
Highlights the records in the grid display. A different 
background color is applied to records that match the 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 641
specified claim state. 
Select 
Selects the records in the grid display. Bold text is 
applied to records that match the specified claim 
state. 
Items 
Defines the claim state of the records that have a specified display style. 
Not Claimed 
Applies a display style to records that are not locally 
claimed. 
Claimed 
Applies a display style to records that are locally 
claimed. 
Claimed and modified 
Applies a display style to records that are locally 
claimed and have been modified. 
Claimed and modified 
Applies a display style to records that are locally 
claimed but have not been modified. 
User 
Defines the user status of the records that have a specified display style. 
Not Claimed 
Applies a display style to records that are locally 
claimed by the current user. 
Claimed 
Applies a display style to records that are locally 
claimed by any user. 
The records are selected/highlighted in accordance with the defined options. 
If only some of the attribute groups of an element have been claimed to a local claim and the highlight option is 
selected, only the columns in the claimed attribute groups are highlighted. 
The default highlight color can be changed using Feedback Colours option. 
The highlight color can be removed using the Reset Cell Feedback option. 
Refer to Feedback Colors and Reset Cell Feedback Colors for further information. 
Claim Records to Local Claims 
Claims the currently selected records to a local claim, use the Claim Records window to specify which of the 
currently selected records are claimed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 642
The Claim Records window displays the attribute groups associated with the selected records, and the columns 
in each attribute group. 
Select 
Defines the claim state of each record or attribute group. 
If the check box is selected, the record or attribute group is claimed to a local claim. 
If the check box is not selected, the record or attribute group is not claimed to a local claim. 
If a selected record or attribute group has already been claimed by another user, the claim will fail. An error 
message is displayed. 
Release Records from Local Claims 
Releases the currently selected records from a local claim, use the Claim Records window to specify which of the 
currently selected records are released. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 643
The Release Records window displays the attribute groups associated with the selected records, and the 
columns in each attribute group. 
Select 
Defines the claim state of each record or attribute group. 
If the check box is selected, the record or attribute group is released from a local claim. 
If the check box is not selected, the record or attribute group is not released from a local claim. 
If a selected record or attribute group has been edited, it must be saved before it can be released. If it is not, the 
release will fail. An error message is displayed. 
Release All Records from Local Claims 
Releases all currently claimed records from a local claim, the Confirm Release All window is displayed prompting 
to confirm release of the claimed elements. 
Show Extract Claim State of Records 
Defines the display of records that are claimed or unclaimed to an extract. 
Use the Show Extracts State window to modify the display of records with a defined extract claim state. 
The functionality of the Show in Grid tab of the Show Extracts State window is identical to the Show Claim State
window. 
The All Claimed Elements tab displays all records that are claimed to an extract.

Claims the currently selected records to an extract claim. 
Use the Claim Extract window to specify which of the currently selected records are claimed. 
The Claim Extract window displays the attribute groups associated with the selected records, and the columns in 
each attribute group. 
Select 
Defines the claim state of each record or attribute group. 
If the check box is selected, the record or attribute group is claimed to an extract claim. 
If the check box is not selected, the record or attribute group is not claimed to an extract claim. 
If a selected record or attribute group has already been claimed by another user, the claim will fail. An error

message is displayed. 
Release Records from Extract Claims 
Releases the currently selected records from an extract claim, use the Release Extract window to specify which 
of the currently selected records are claimed. 
The Release Extract window displays the attribute groups associated with the selected records, and the columns 
in each attribute group. 
Select 
Defines the claim state of each record or attribute group. 
If the check box is selected, the record or attribute group is released from an extract claim. 
If the check box is not selected, the record or attribute group is not released from an extract claim. 
If a selected record or attribute group has been edited, it must be saved before it can be released. If it is not, the 
release will fail. An error message is displayed. 
Refresh Extracts from Parent Extract 
Refreshes the currently selected extracted records with current data from the parent database only (which itself 
can be an extract). 
Changes are indicated using the specified highlight colors. 
Refresh Extracts from All Ancestors 
Refreshes the currently selected extracted records with current data from all parent databases (which can be 
extracts) up to the top of the database hierarchy. Changes are indicated using the specified highlight colors.

Flush Extracts 
Updates the parent extract or database with saved changes made to extracted records, use the Flush Extract 
window to specify which of the currently selected records are updated. 
The Flush Extract window displays the attribute groups associated with the selected records, and the columns in 
each attribute group. 
Select 
Defines the records or attribute groups that are updated. 
If the check box is selected, the record or attribute group is updated. 
If the check box is not selected, the record or attribute group is not updated. 
Comments 
If required, a comment can be typed into the field. 
The selected records or attribute groups are not released from the extract by this procedure 
Changes that have not been saved are not updated. 
Issue Extracts 
Issues saved changes made to extracted records to the parent extract or database and releases the records, use 
the Issue Extract window to specify which of the currently selected records are issued.

If the check box is not selected, the record or attribute group is not issued. 
Comments 
If required, a comment can be typed into the field. 
If a selected record or attribute group has been edited, it must be saved before it can be issued. If it is not, the

Manually issue the data between extracts 
Use the following procedure to manually issue the data between extracts in a Controlled Object Revisioning 
(COR) project. 
1. In the Configuration module, select the Workflow tab. 
2. Navigate to the location in the Workflow Explorer. 
3. Disable the Controllable element types.

4. In the vercon configuration, disable States to issue extracts. 
5. In the View Detail window, select the Column tab and create the following grid, care should be taken not to 
issue non-relevant data. 
6. Create the following grid, care should be taken not to issue non-relevant data. 
7. Create the following view for the required discipline and category. 
8. From the Team-View Subscription window, allocate the newly created view to the required workflow.

11. Select State in the Extracts group to display the Show Extracts State window. 
12. Select all the required tags (for example dist pro check).
