Notifications 
Notifications related to an action related to a tag (Logical delete, Purge, Rename, Status Changes) of child 
elements (Has Part or Can have parts) at Parent Tag can be displayed in the grid. 
Notifications are displayed when a different discipline issues a new revision of a subscribed record, refer to the 
Lexicon User Guide for further information. The notification is shown as a red flag on the status bar. 
To enable notifications in a grid, on the View tab in the Grid Options select the Show Notifications check box. 
A Tag Notification column is added to the grid 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 654
The number following the flag indicates the number of records that have unaccepted outstanding revisions. 
Notifications can only be accepted for new revisions of data from a different team, it is not possible to return to 
older revisions. 
Click the red flag icon on the status bar to view the revisions. 
Use the Revision Centre window to select, compare and update revisions. 
By default the Select tab is displayed, populated with all the available revisions of subscribed tagged items which 
have been released by other teams. 
Compare 
Defines the revisions that are selected for comparison and update. The revision details are displayed on the 
Compare/Update tab. 
If the check box is selected, the revision is included in the comparison and update. 
If the check box is not selected, the revision is not included in the comparison and update. 
The details for selected revisions are displayed on the Compare/Update tab. 
Compare Latest 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 655
Selects the latest revision of all notified tagged items. The latest revision of every notified item is selected, for all 
filtered items (to a maximum of 99) and the revision details are displayed on the Compare/Update tab. 
The Compare/Update tabs display the details for selected revisions. 
Update 
Updates all selected revisions (if more than 99 are available then the first 99 are selected and the following 
message is displayed). 
Note: 
Once a revision has been accepted, it cannot be rolled back to the previous one. 
Reclassification Notifications 
Teams can be subscribed to receive notifications from cross-discipline operations: for example when a Tag 
Management System controlled tag is reclassified. A user in the subscribed team will receive a notification for 
each classified tag, the notification serves to inform the user that they can proceed with reclassification of 
distributed attribute components where appropriate. 
Note: 
Not all teams will have actions to complete if the reclassified tag does not have distributed attributes for their 
discipline. 
The number of notifications is displayed in the status bar along side the cross-discipline notifications tag and the 
number is updated automatically when a new notification is available. Once selected the Cross-Discipline 
Notifications window is displayed populated with the notifications. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 656
Logically Delete Notifications 
A notification related to a tag is cleared if the linked reference tag is deleted or purged. On deleting any reference 
tag, a notification is displayed in the Tag Notification column on affected parent tag in the grid. Notifications are 
displayed for all the affected tags irrespective of who deleted them. Clicking on the notification in the grid, a 
message window is displayed with details of all deleted tag(s) and other appropriate information. After a 
SaveWork, the notification is visible to all users. 
For example: a cable is connected between a switch and a motor. Upon deleting the cable, both switch and 
motor (parents) are highlighted with notification in the grid. 
Note: 
All notifications in the grid can be deleted from all parent elements in the hierarchy. 
Logical delete of a Flange 
For the purpose of this example - a pump is the parent of a nozzle and the nozzle is the parent of an flange. 
1. Select the flange 
2. Carry out a logical delete, an notification on the flange’s parent (pump and nozzle) is displayed in Tag 
Notification column of the grid. 
3. Double click the notification to display the details of the deleted tag and the other appropriate information. 
4. Perform a SaveWork, the notification is now visible to all users. 
Purge of a Flange 
For the purpose of this example - a pump is the parent of a nozzle and the nozzle is the parent of an flange. 
1. Select the flange which is already logical deleted. 
2. Carry out a purge, an notification on the flange’s parent (pump and nozzle) is displayed in Tag Notification 
column of the grid. 
3. A purge notification will only be created if there is a logical delete notification. 
4. Double click the notification to display the details of the purged tag and the other appropriate information. 
5. Perform a SaveWork, the notification is now visible to all users. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 657
Renamed Notifications 
On renaming any reference tag, a notification is displayed on the parent tag in the grid. Notifications are 
displayed for all the effected tags irrespective of who renamed them. Clicking on the notification in the grid, a 
Notification window is displayed with the details of all the renamed tag(s) and other appropriate information. 
After SaveWork, the notifications is visible to all users 
For example, a pump has a part nozzle if the nozzle is renamed, a notification is displayed in Tag Notification 
column of the grid. 
Notes: 
All notifications in the grid can be deleted from all parent elements in the hierarchy. 
All rename notifications will not be raise if the modified element does not has any status defined. 
All rename notifications on any element are deleted if the status of that element is changed. 
All rename notifications are cleared if the element is set to initial name in the same status. For example: If a 
flange is renamed from A to B. B to C. and then back to A, the notification is cleared as the element’s name has 
reverted back to it’s initial name. 
Clearing Notifications 
To clear all notifications for the tag, in the Notification column of the grid, click the flag to display the 
Notifications window, click Clear All. All notifications in the grid are cleared from the shared services to the 
parent elements in the hierarchy. 
Baselines 
Baselines are views of a project consisting of all disciplines saved at a particular state. Disciplines can continue to 
change data and create new revisions of data without changing the project views saved to baselines. Baselines 
can be created at any time after the initial project configuration has been defined. In practice they are likely to be 
created at key project milestones. Refer to the Administration User Guide for further information. 
Note: 
Access to the Baseline Multiple Databases (MDBs) are reserved for system engineers or engineers with 
responsibility for creating consistent data sets. 
Use the Baseline window to define tags and attributes and populate the baseline. 
Note: 
The project must be in Controlled Object Revisioning (COR) mode and the System Engineer must be logged into a 
Baseline MDB for the option to be available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 658
Attributes 
Use the Discipline Attributes window to define attributes which are displayed for the filtered tags. A set of 
attributes is displayed for each discipline, the required attributes can be moved from the Available discipline 
attributes list to the Selected attributes list by using the arrow buttons. 
Use Clear All to remove all attributes from the Selected attributes list. 
List Tags 
Populates the Baseline window with all tags that can be brought into the current baseline from the discipline 
teams. 
Governing discipline 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 659
The Governing discipline drop-down contains a list of all available disciplines, but it will remain disabled until the 
tags list is populated. The Governing discipline column is populated when the Governing discipline selection is 
changed, but the selected discipline is only applied to the current set of filtered in columns. 
The discipline cell is populated if the tag does not have issued component data for the selected discipline. 
Each cell in the Governing discipline column contains a drop-down list of disciplines available for the tag to 
override the common value. 
Accept Filtered 
Accepts and adds all the filtered-in tags to the current selection, based on what is visible as a result of the 
current filter. A tag cannot be accepted until it has a discipline assigned. 
Clear Filtered 
Clears accepted tags. 
Note: 
Using List Tags clears any accepted tags, filters and the governing discipline. If any attribute selected for filtering 
is not valid on a particular tag then a blank value is displayed. Unset values are displayed blank. 
Accept Column 
Defines the tags that are accepted for baseline population. 
If the check box is selected, the tag is accepted for baseline population. 
If the check box is not selected, the tag is not accepted for baseline population. 
Tag Column 
Displays the tag name (NAME attribute). 
Baseline Column 
Defines all tags that have already been added to the current baseline. 
If the check box is selected, the tag has already been added to the current baseline. 
If the check box is not selected, the tag has already been added to the current baseline. 
Relationship Column 
Set to the project default and can be changed using a drop-down list. 
System 
Displays the systems to which the tag belongs (SCSYSF attribute). 
Revisions Column 
Once the governing discipline has been set for a tag, the list of available revisions for that discipline component 
is populated. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 660
When a different revision is selected, a new set of related component revisions are displayed for the other 
discipline components in the tag. Other discipline component revisions can only be changed by the system 
engineer in the Interface Attributes window. 
Populate 
Populates the current baseline with all of the accepted tags. For each tag, the component that corresponds to 
the selected discipline will be brought down at its latest revision. All other components are brought down using 
the related revisions recorded for the governing discipline component. 
Interface Attributes 
A discipline component has an interface to other discipline components in a tag. The interface is a set of 
attributes that are considered to be of interest to that discipline. In the baseline functionality, the interface 
attributes are used to determine a level of compatibility between revisions of a component. 
For example: consider a tag with mechanical, process and electrical components. 
The mechanical component has an interface with the process and electrical components. The interface with the 
process component can be defined as the operating temperature and operating pressure. The interface with the 
electrical component can be defined as the power load and power rating. 
The process component has an interface with the mechanical and electrical components. The interface with the 
mechanical component can be defined as the fluid code and test medium. The interface with the electrical 
component can be defined as the power load, power rating and current. 
COR takes the attributes in subscriptions set up by a discipline team as the interface attributes. The discipline 
team should create a database view that contains the attributes required as interface attributes. In the first 
example above, the mechanical team would create a database view containing mechanical attributes, plus the 
interface attributes from the other components. 
A discipline team is a team that is associated with a discipline. This association is done on the Team definition 
window in AVEVA Administration. More than one discipline can be associated with a team. The first discipline in 
the list is considered to be the primary discipline. This is the discipline that COR uses in this context. 
Select a single tag using the row selector and clicking Interface or double-click the tag row selector to display the 
Interface Attributes window. 
The Interface Attributes window responds to a new tag selection and is populated with the governing discipline 
or relationship. 
The first row of the Interface Attributes window is populated with the component revisions currently selected in 
the tag list. The second (preview) row shows revisions from the opposite relationship, for example: if the issued 
against relationship is currently set for a tag, the preview row shows component revisions as if the consumed by 
relationship is selected. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 661
The governing discipline is placed first in the row, the available revisions on other components can be selected. 
Differences between attribute values of the current revision and the previous revision are highlighted. 
Update 
Transfers changes back to the main menu and accepts the tag ready for population. 
Related Revisions and Discipline Classification 
A system engineer can use these baseline features to bring down a set of issued tags into the baseline. 
Related Revisions 
When a discipline team issues its component of a tag, Controlled Object Revisioning (COR) records the revision 
numbers of the set of components of the tag. These will be the revision numbers that are in the issuing team's 
Multiple Database (MDB) and reflect the version of data from other disciplines that the team has accepted using 
the Revision Center. The components are the main tag element (ENGITE) and distributed attribute elements 
(XPITEM). The recorded revisions will be used to drive the population of a baseline set of data using relations in 
two ways: ‘issued against’ and ‘consumed by’ relationships. 
Note: 
The exception to this is the tag component. Since the tag is always issued first, no other components have a 
consumed by relationship with the first revision of the tag component. The relationship between the governing 
component and the tag component is always an issued against relationship. If the process discipline is selected 
as governing discipline for this tag in the baseline, and the consumed by relationship is selected then the other 
component revisions are set from this relationship. 
Discipline Classification 
To enable discipline driven functionality, the administrator must classify all User Defined Element Types (UDETs) 
to one of the defined disciplines. Refer to the Lexicon User Guide for further information. 
Note: 
Each UDET must be classified to only one discipline 
Cases 
A case is a named set of alternate data that can be set against an object's attributes (alternate attribute values 
are only available on case dependent attributes). 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 662
Defined Cases 
If an attribute has been marked as case dependent, the cases icon is displayed in the grid list column header. 
Refer to the Lexicon User Guide for further information 
Select the Cases icon to display all the defined cases in the system. 
The selected check boxes control which CASE attributes are displayed in the current grid for the chosen attribute. 
The Base Case indicates the case dependent attribute. 
The grid is updated to display the new case attribute columns. 
Manage Cases 
Use the Manage Cases window to manage all the cases in the system, by adding, deleting or copying cases in the 
system. 
The created or modified cases are stored in the AVEVA Engineering database after Save Work is carried out. 
Set Active Cases 
Use the Set Active functionality to control the active cases for all case dependent attribute columns in current 
grid. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 663
The Set Active Cases window is displayed and populated with all system defined cases. 
The state of each case determines how it is applied in the current grid: 
If the check box is selected, the case is applied for all case dependent attributes in current grid. 
If the check box is not selected, the case is not applied for any case dependent attributes in current grid. 
If the check box is in an intermediate state, the case is applied for some case dependent attributes in current 
grid. 
Auto-populate Case in Case Control Item in Datasheet Template 
Default cases are set in the datasheet template so that all the datasheet instances created based on the 
datasheet template with the desired case values are auto-populated instead of setting the desired cases in each 
datasheet instance. 
The Case values are set at the Engineer by the users and will reside in the first Engineering D/B of the MDB that’s 
being logged into. 
In the Configuration module once the Case Control is mapped under Items -> the user set Case values need to be 
auto-populated. 
This will enable the Administrator to select a default Case. 
Notes: 
For the existing Datasheet Templates, for the Case Controls the Items will be auto-filled only when clicked on the 
control or while loading the Datasheet Template. If the existing controls are having the Items values in it, they 
will be overridden by the instance cases. 
For the existing Datasheet Templates, this functionality will remain as it is today unless they explicitly update the 
default case 
If there are no existing Cases in the project, as in such cases as Items under properties will be empty or have 
"Base Case", the Administrator should be able to give in values manually as today. However, by entering the 
values new Engineering Cases will not be created. 
Querying and Setting Case Values on the Command Line 
Example to query a case value on a User Defined Attribute (UDA): 
Q :caseUDA('Case name' ) 
Example to query an array of cases for a UDA 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 664
!cases = ARRAY() 
!cases[1] = 'Normal' 
!cases[2] = 'Minimum' 
!cases[3] = 'Maximum' 
q var !!ce.attribute(':caseUDA', !cases) 
Example to set a case value 
ATT :caseUDA('Case name' ) = !value 
Refresh Approvals 
Refreshes approved components at the working level. 
When a junior engineer makes changes to data and issues them for approval by a senior engineer (who can 
approve or reject the changes by setting the status), the changes are automatically saved - a save work is not 
needed. 
The option allows the junior engineer to receive changes made by the senior engineer. Any changes to the 
writable discipline Databases (DBs) are received. The action does not receive published data from other 
disciplines. 
Any attribute changes made to the working data is saved before the approval data is received. 
Automatically Create Working Revisions 
If the junior engineer makes changes to attributes on a component and submits them for approval, the approved 
components are refreshed at the working level, and automatically transitioned to the next working revision. This 
requires the status lifecycle to have a defined transition from the approved state to the working state. If there is 
no transition defined, the working revision is not created. 
For example: 
• The junior engineer submits Pump1 at Pending Approval, revision B2* 
• The junior engineer changes an attribute on Pump1 
• The senior engineer approves Pump1 at revision B3 
• The junior engineer refreshes approvals - Pump1 displays as Approved at B3, then Working at B3*. 
Import and Export 
Data can be imported and exported to and from Excel spreadsheets. Data can also be exported to PDF files and 
published to AVEVA NET. 
Grid data can also be updated with data from other databases. Refer to Compare and Update for further 
information. 
Note: 
If a value is not in the restricted list, an error messages is displayed. 
Refer to Restricted Lists for further information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 665
Import Data From Excel 
Imports from a grid that was previously exported to Excel. Imported grids are referred to as lists. 
Use the Excel Import Wizard to map the elements and attributes for the imported data. The Excel Import Wizard
has the same functionality as the Excel Export Wizard, which is explained in Export Data to Excel from Database. 
Elements in an imported grid can be updated only by exporting them to Excel, editing the Excel file, and then 
importing them again. 
Base elements 
Each project type has an associated base type. Each base type has attributes. The base types for Engineer are: 
ENGITE: Engineering Element 
FUNITE: Engineering Functional Element 
SIMITE: Engineering Simulation Element 
To import other base types, such as library elements (LIBITE) and Engineering Typical Item Elements (TYPITE) use 
the Import and Export functionality in the Configuration module. 
Overview of the import process and options 
1. On the Manage tab, select Import>List. Browse to and open the file to import. 
2. Select the data mode to use for the import. 
3. Optionally, select a saved configuration. 
4. Depending on the data mode, select the type of element to import or the database view or database view 
set. 
5. If there are user-defined element types (UDETS), choose whether to include them in the import. 
6. Optionally, if multiple element types or database views were exported, select Show All Worksheets. This 
option acts as a reminder that there are multiple sheets to map. 
7. If multiple element types or database views are included in the Excel file, perform the mapping for each. 
8. Map the appropriate attribute or database view column to each column in the Excel file to import. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 666
9. Optionally, if any elements have cases associated with them, select the required case (attributes can have 
values that vary in different cases. For example, the viscosity of a liquid may vary at different temperatures). 
10. Optionally, choose to remove carriage returns from all text elements. This ensures that element names, for 
example, display on one line. 
11. Optionally, save the import configuration to use for subsequent imports. 
12. Optionally, choose to close the import progress dialog automatically when the import completes. 
Import modes and configurations 
Optionally, if an import configuration has been created, select a configuration to use. A configuration pre-
populates parts of the wizard, making it quicker to specify the import. Configurations are saved in User, Project, 
or Product locations. User configurations are stored locally and can only be used locally. Saved Project and 
Product configurations can be accessed by other users. You can create a configuration at the beginning or the 
end of an import. We recommend that you create it at the end. 
You can import, and export, with these data modes: 
• Element type 
Enables you to select a type of element to import from the Excel file. 
• Database view(s) 
Enables you to select multiple types of element and their associated attributes to import from the Excel file. 
This mode also gives you control over column naming. 
There is no requirement to use the same import mode as for the export. That is, you can use Element type mode 
to import a file exported in Database view(s) mode, or use Database view(s) mode to import a file exported in 
Element type mode. However, if you use Database view(s) mode for both import and export, database view 
columns are mapped automatically to Excel columns. If you use Database view(s) to export but Element type to 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 667
import, that mapping must be done manually. 
Differences in user interfaces of Element type and Database view modes 
You can import the Excel file, in whichever mode it was exported, in either mode. However, the user interfaces of 
steps two and three of the Excel Import Wizard differ in these ways: 
Step 
Element type mode 
Database view(s) mode 
2 
• Title is Worksheet and Element 
Type configuration. 
• Select an element type from 
the Element type list. 
• Title is Worksheet and 
Database View configuration. 
• Select a database view from 
the Choose a Database View or 
View Set dialog. 
3 
• Title is Column and element 
configuration. 
• You map an attribute to each 
Excel column. 
• Title is Excel and Database 
View column configuration. 
• You map a database view 
column to each Excel column. 
The Configuration options step of the Excel Import Wizard provides the option to remove the carriage return 
from text attributes to be imported. If selected, this option ensures that text attributes are displayed on one line. 
Mandatory attributes 
If the Excel file to be imported lacks a mandatory attribute, the file must be edited and imported again. If a file 
must be re-imported in these circumstances, additional attributes become mandatory. 
The NAME attribute is always mandatory. 
Which other attributes are mandatory depends on whether a project is conceptual or non-conceptual, on the 
import mode, and on whether the tags being imported are new or amended. For example, the OWNER attribute 
is mandatory for new tags in a non-conceptual project. Depending on these conditions, you may be prompted 
during an import for the NAME, REF, or OWNER (ENGITE) attributes. 
Accept/Reject Changes 
In Element type mode only, you are prompted to accept or reject some or all the changes. If a large volume of 
data is to be imported, you have the option to skip the Accept/Reject option: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 668
Read-only attributes 
These attributes are read-only: 
• OWNER 
• REF 
• TYPE 
• ACTTYPE 
In the Column and element configuration step (Element type mode) of the Excel Import Wizard, you select the 
attributes to import from the Excel file. By default, the check boxes of all attributes, except those of the read-
only attributes, are selected. You can: 
• Deselect any of the readable attributes to exclude them from the import. 
• Deselect the Name attribute (even though it must be present in the export file). 
Although the check boxes of the read-only attributes are not selected, they are imported. Any differences 
between the current values of these attributes and the values in the Excel file are highlighted, but not updated. 
Excel Import - has parts creation 
If there is a Has Parts relationship in the data model between, for example, nozzles and pumps, then importing 
pump data also automatically imports the associated nozzle data. This contrasts with Can Have Parts and 
Reference relationships. In those cases, to import both pump and nozzle data, the import functionality requires 
separate worksheets containing the pump and the nozzle data. 
Note: 
Automatic Has Parts creation is available only for conceptual model projects. That is, for projects that have an 
associated data model. 
Use this functionality to automatically create the has parts element(s) of a tag. 
Note: 
Has parts elements are created only if the has part element is not created using child information (parent-child 
relationship) to ensure no duplicates are created. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 669
The functionality allows for the following on import of tags or Excel worksheet: 
• If an imported tag does not have any information about the has parts element, the functionality 
automatically creates a has part by default. 
• If an imported tag has any invalid information (part name provided does not exist in the database or excel 
worksheets), no has parts elements are created. 
• If an Excel worksheet is imported which has information for has parts/can have parts/references, the 
functionality uses this information to create has parts/can have parts/references respectively. 
Note: 
An empty value cannot be set on a has parts attribute on a tag and imported for a tag which already has 
associated has parts. 
Excel Import Tags Counter Override on Import 
Engineer updates the counter for the tag from the attribute value of the source element on import using the 
Excel Import functionality and keeps the name consistent with the source element. 
Note: 
This functionality is available only for the TMS enabled projects. In other projects, the Counter mapping step of 
the Excel Import Wizard is not displayed. 
Use the Excel Import Wizard, to map the Excel column with the counters that are to be overridden. Only valid 
write attributes are displayed and the excel columns available for selection are displayed in a drop down list. The 
selection of the excel columns and therefore the configuration is optional 
Note: 
Excel columns which are already mapped cannot be used to map counters. 
When the import completes, the Accept or Reject dialog is displayed with the counter attributes with the 
counter tag in brackets. Single or multiple configurations can be accepted or rejected. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 670
The Import Progress dialog shows the tasks being performed during the import (for example, Apply changes) 
and their statuses. The dialog remains on-screen when the import is completed, enabling you to view any errors 
and warnings, and a summary of the process. Optionally, select Automatically close import progress dialog on 
completion in the Save configuration changes step of the wizard, if you do wish to see confirmation that the 
import has completed or any errors that may have occurred. 
There is also an option to close the import progress dialog on the dialog itself. 
Import a Single Excel file with appropriate parts where cardinality is single 
Use this functionality to import to update existing parent and parts records based on excel data, create parts 
record in existing parent record if parts are missing, replace existing parts if new parts are coming through excel. 
Override counter value for parent and part tag, import case data, import distributed attributes for both parent 
and parts record, preserve name for parent record, create tags as per naming rule if name parts data provided 
through excel. 
Notes: 
Can have parts should only be created if there is attribute mapping available or if the mapping of child parts 
attributes is available. 
Excel should have Name/Ref attributes ofparts so that record/update records created are based on these values. 
Example: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 671
1. In the Configuration module, create a DbView with parents and parts data for example: a pump with nozzle 
and flange, then map with the source element attributes. 
2. On the Manage tab, in the Import group select List, the Select File to Import window. Navigate to the 
required excel file and click Select. The Excel Import Wizard is displayed - Choose a Configuration pane is 
displayed by default. 
3. Select a configuration to use for the import, then click Next. The Worksheet and Database View configuration 
pane is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 672
4. Select the database view to create or update for each worksheet listed. Click Next the Excel and Database 
View column configuration pane is displayed. 
5. Specify the Database view columns to be updated during the import, click Next. The Counter mapping pane 
is displayed. 
6. To override the counter, map the excel column with the write attribute, click Next and save changes. 
Import a Single Excel file to create several records with appropriate parts where 
cardinality is more than one 
Use the functionality to import parent and parts with multi-cardinality where all the records of the parent and its 
parts are displayed in a single sheet. 
1. Create a db view of a pump with a nozzle and flange as parts.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 673
2. Identify the nozzle as a source element. 
3. Identify the flange as a source element. 
4. Map the columns to the source elements attributes. 
5. From the Manage tab in the Import group, select Import then List 
6. From the Select File to Open window, navigate to the excel sheet to be imported. 
Note: 
The excel sheet has different combinations of parent and parts data.

7. Click Open to select the file and to display the Choose a Configuration pane of the Excel Import Wizard 
window.

the Excel Import Wizard window.

element configuration pane of the Excel Import window.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering

10. Select a excel column from the drop-down menu to identify which excel columns values are to be read 
11. Map the destination element type to create the records of defined type. 
Note: 
This is screen optional if no destination element types need to be mapped, all the records imported are 
created with the primary element type defined for the database view. 
12. Click Next to display the Association column configuration pane of the Excel Import Wizard window. 
13. Select an excel column to define the associations between the tags to be imported (which part needs to be 
associated with which parent). 
Note: 
If no excel columns are mapped the associations between the parent and its parts will not be established. 
14. Click Next to display the Excel and Database View column configuration pane of the Excel Import Wizard 
window.

15. Based on the mappings carried out in step 11, dynamic worksheets are created where the view columns are

mapped for the sheets listed.

Import Datasheet from Excel 
Imports a previously created datasheet from a selected location in the format of an Excel file. The imported 
datasheet is compared with the currently opened datasheet and modifications are highlighted. 
Location 
Displays available locations to load an existing 
datasheet in the format of an Excel file. 
File name 
Once selected the file name for the selected Excel file 
is displayed. 
Use the Datasheet Excel Import Wizard to define the mapped tags and attributes for the imported datasheet.

Accept 
Defines the tags that are imported. 
If the check box is selected, the tag is included in the import. 
If the check box is not selected, the tag is not included in the import. 
Use Select All or Clear Selection to select or deselect all check boxes. 
Imported attribute mappings which differ from the current datasheet mappings are highlighted. 
Accept 
Defines the attributes that are imported. 
If the check box is selected, the attribute is included in the import. 
If the check box is not selected, the attribute is not included in the import. 
Use Select All or Clear Selection to select or deselect all check boxes. 
The task summary indicates errors or warnings during import.

Imported attribute mappings which differ from the current datasheet mappings are highlighted in the datasheet. 
Example - Import a Modified Datasheet 
This example assumes that: 
• The currently open datasheet has previously been exported to Excel. 
• The data in the exported datasheet has been modified in Excel. 
1. Click anywhere in the currently open datasheet. 
2. On the Manage tab, select Import>Current Datasheet. 
3. Browse to the exported datasheet and click Open. 
The Datasheet Excel import wizard is displayed. 
4. Select the check boxes of the tags whose attributes you want to import and click Next. 
The import wizard lists the attributes that have been modified in Excel. 
5. Select the check box of each modified attribute to import and click Next. 
A summary of the import's progress is displayed. 
6. Click Finish. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 680
Create CanHaveParts on Datasheet Import 
Note: 
This functionality is only available in TMS enabled projects and only applies to attributes outside the 
continuation area in the datasheet. 
If attribute values for CanHaveParts data has been added or modified (and if the attributes do not already have a 
tag) in an exported datasheet, once imported the application automatically identifies the new or modified 
CanHaveParts data in the Import Wizard and creates a new tag. The new details are displayed in the datasheet. 
If the new attributes being imported override the naming part attributes, the New Tag window is displayed 
prompting for a new name. 
Create multiple CanHaveParts on Datasheet Import 
Note: 
This functionality is only available in TMS enabled projects and only applies to attributes outside the 
continuation area in the datasheet. 
The functionality is identical to that described in Create CanHaveParts on Datasheet Import with the exception 
that multiple attributes can be added or modified. 
Create CanHaveParts by selecting class on Datasheet Import 
Note: 
This functionality is only available in TMS enabled projects and only applies to attributes outside the 
continuation area in the datasheet. 
If element types for CanHaveParts data (DB View Sets) have been added or modified (and if the view does not 
already have a tag) in an exported datasheet, once imported the application automatically identifies the new or 
modified element types in the Import Wizard. Once accepted the Classes window is displayed populated with a 
list of classes that can be added as parts. Select the appropriate class and create a new tag, the new details are 
displayed in the datasheet. 
If the new element type(s) being imported override the naming part attributes, the New Tag window is displayed 
prompting for a new name. 
Export Grid to Excel 
On the Manage tab, select Export>Excel>Current Grid. 
Exports the currently selected grid to a selected location in the format of an Excel file. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 681
Location 
Displays available locations to save the grid. 
File name 
Type a file name for the exported Excel file. 
Export Current Datasheet to Excel 
On the Manage tab, select Export>Excel>Current Datasheet To Excel. 
Exports the currently selected datasheet to a selected location in the format of an excel file. The functionality 
available is identical to exporting a grid to an Excel file. 
Export Current Datasheet to Provider 
On the Manage tab, select Export>Excel>Current Datasheet To Provider. 
Exports the currently selected datasheet in a modifiable format and exports to a selected location in the format 
of an excel file. 
If the Provided By Supplier property is set to True on cells in the datasheet template, then those cells are made 
writable when a datasheet instance created from the template is exported with the Export Current Datasheet to 
Provider option. 
The functionality available is identical to exporting a grid to an Excel file. 
Export Selected Datasheets to Single Excel File 
On the Manage tab, select Export>Excel>Selected Datasheets To Single Excel. 
Exports selected datasheets to a selected location in the format of a single excel file. The functionality available is 
identical to exporting a grid to an Excel file. 
Export Selected Datasheets to Multiple Excel Files 
On the Manage tab, select Export>Excel>Selected Datasheets To Multiple Excels. 
Exports selected datasheets to a selected location in the format of multiple excel files. The functionality available

Look in 
Specifies where the configuration file is stored. 
Configuration name

Delete selected rows

Move the selected row up the grid

View Schematic Diagrams 
The schematic diagram in which an element is located can be viewed, changes made to schematic diagrams 
between selected sessions can be highlighted and listed.

View Selected Item on Diagram 
Displays the schematic diagram in which an element is located from a selected element in a grid.

the diagram and the Viewer - Actions tab is displayed. 
View Diagram 
Opens a schematic diagram, the Diagram Viewer displays the diagram and the Diagram Viewer - Actions tab is 
displayed. 
Use the View Diagram window to navigate to and open the required diagram.

The top left part of the View Diagram window displays the diagram hierarchy. 
The top right part of the View Diagram window displays the diagrams that are available for selection. The 
available diagrams can be filtered. 
Search Diagrams 
Type a string in the field and click Search to search for matching content. The available diagrams are filtered to 
match the search criteria. 
The arrow on the right of the field displays a drop-down list of recent searches that are available for selection. 
Select the required radio button to search either By Diagram Name or By Content. 
Diagrams 
Displays the diagrams that are available for selection. 
Pages 
Displays all pages that are available for selection. 
Use the bottom part of the View Diagram window to preview the currently selected diagram and adjust the 
preview. 
Preview 
Defines the preview display of the currently selected diagram.

If the check box is selected, a screen capture of the currently selected diagram is displayed.

Zoom In 
Zooms into the preview to the next standard zoom level. 
Zoom Out 
Zooms out from the preview to the next standard zoom level. 
Zoom To Window 
Modifies the zoom magnification to include the entire diagram in the preview. 
Information 
Hover over the icon to display additional preview zoom commands. 
Pan 
Hold the Alt key and click the left mouse button to 
drag the preview in any direction. Release the mouse 
button when required. 
Centre and Zoom In 
Hold the Ctrl key and click the left mouse button to 
zoom into the preview to the next standard zoom 
level. The clicked area of the preview is centered in 
the preview. 
Centre and Zoom Out 
Hold the Ctrl and Shift keys and click the left mouse 
button to zoom out from the preview to the next 
standard zoom level. The clicked area of the preview is 
centered in the preview. 
Zoom to Area

to zoom to a defined area in the preview

If the check box is not selected, equipment elements 
are not displayed. 
Piping 
Defines display settings for piping elements in the 3D 
View window. 
If the check box is selected, piping elements are 
displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, piping elements are 
not displayed.

Structure 
Defines display settings for structural elements in the 
3D View window. 
If the check box is selected, structural elements are 
displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, structural elements 
are not displayed. 
Cabling 
Defines display settings for cabling elements in the 3D 
View window. 
If the check box is selected, cabling elements are 
displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, cabling elements are 
not displayed. 
Hangers 
Defines display settings for hanger elements in the 3D 
View window. 
If the check box is selected, hanger elements are 
displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, hanger elements are 
not displayed. 
Others 
Defines display settings for other elements in the 3D 
View window. 
If the check box is selected, other elements are 
displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, other elements are 
not displayed. 
Parallel 
Sets the 3D View window to parallel projection mode. 
Perspective 
Sets the 3D View window to perspective projection 
mode. 
Axes 
Toggles the display of the axes on and off in the 
3DView window. 
Clip Planes 
Displays only the parts of the model which are 
contained within a clipping box. 
Use the 3D View Clip Planes window to define the 
dimensions of the clipping box. 
AVEVA™ Engineering

Display imported elements 
Defines display settings for imported elements in the 
3D View window. 
If the check box is selected, imported elements are 
displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, imported elements 
are not displayed. 
Linked Documents 
Documents and other resources can be linked to the currently selected grid element or a selected element on 
the Database Explorer. 
Use the Linked Documents window to view, add and edit links. 
Create Worlds and Classes 
Before links can be created, the worlds and classes they are assigned to must first be created. 
The Folders/Classes tab on the Linked Documents window displays the existing worlds and classes. 
The left-hand part of the Linked Documents widow displays the existing structure of worlds, folders and classes 
in an explorer tree-view. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 697
The middle part of the Linked Documents window displays the linked documents. 
The right part of the Linked Documents window assigns the classes that the currently selected link is assigned to. 
If a check box is selected, the currently selected link is assigned to that class. 
If a check box is not selected, the currently selected link is not assigned to that class. 
New world 
Right-click within the linked document hierarchy and 
select New world to create a new world. A node for 
the new world is added to the explorer, type a unique 
name for the new world. 
New folder 
Right-click the currently selected world or folder and 
select New folder to create a new folder. A node for 
the new folder is added to the explorer, type a unique 
name for the new folder. 
New class 
Right-click the currently selected world or folder and 
select New class to create a new class. A node for the 
new class is added to the explorer, type a unique 
name for the new class. 
Collapse all 
Right-click within the linked document hierarchy and 
select Collapse all to collapse the explorer view. 
Cut 
Right-click the selected class or folder and select Cut 
to cut the element to the clipboard for a later paste 
operation. 
Copy 
Right-click the selected world, class or folder and 
select Copy to copy the element to the clipboard for a 
later paste operation. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 698
Paste 
Pastes the world, class or folder that is stored on the 
clipboard. 
Rename 
Right-click the selected world, class or folder and 
select Rename to rename the element. 
Delete 
Right-click the selected world, class or folder and 
select Delete to delete the element. 
Add and Edit Links 
Links are organized in link classes which are in turn collected into link worlds. Refer to Database Management for 
further details of link worlds and classes. 
Right-click a selected link on the link part of Linked Documents window and select Add link to create a link from 
a grid element or a selected element on the Database Explorer. 
Use the Add Link to window to define the link type, link properties and the class that the link is assigned to. 
Existing link 
Adds an existing link to the currently selected element (the window defaults to this category if there are any 
existing links). 
The Link options part of the Add link to window displays the hierarchy of worlds, classes and existing links. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 699
Navigate to and select an existing link to assign it to the current element. 
Existing file 
Adds a link to an existing file. 
Name 
Type a unique name for the link. 
Description 
Type a description for the link. 
Path to file 
Type the file path in the field or click Choose file to 
navigate to and select an existing file. 
Web page 
Adds a link to a web page. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 700
Name 
Type a unique name for the link. 
Description 
Type a description for the link. 
Address 
Type the URL in the field or click Browse to navigate to 
and select an existing web page. 
E-mail address 
Adds a link to an e-mail address. 
Name 
Type a unique name for the link. 
Description 
Type a description for the link. 
Email address 
Type the e-mail address for the recipient of the email. 
Subject 
Type the default subject for messages sent to the 
email address. 
Drawing 
Adds a link to an existing drawing. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering

Name 
Type a unique name for the link. 
Description 
Type a description for the link. 
View Links 
The Links tab on the Linked Documents window displays the documents, URLs and e-mail addresses linked to the 
selected element. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 702
Track CE 
Defines the automatic display of links for the currently 
selected element. 
If the check box is selected, links for the currently 
selected element are automatically displayed. 
If the check box is not selected, links for the currently 
selected element are not automatically displayed. 
Assigned Cases 
Displays the classes to which the currently selected 
link is assigned. 
If a check box is selected, the currently selected link is 
assigned to that class. 
If a check box is not selected, the currently selected 
link is not assigned to that class. 
Open 
Right-click the selected link and select Open from the 
drop-down list to open the linked file or URL or to 
send a message to a linked e-mail address. 
Rename 
Right-click the selected link and select Rename to 
rename the link. 
Remove 
Right-click the selected link and select Remove to 
delete the link. 
Tag Properties 
Use the Tag Properties window to display all available data for a grid in a single property grid. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 703
All of the available grid properties are displayed in the window and they are grouped by the Category property of 
the attribute, which is part of the core database schema or the Category property of the User Defined Attributes 
(UDA), as defined in the Lexicon module when the UDA is set up. 
Although it appears that all data is editable, all of the normal access controls are enforced and data can only be 
modified the data that is accessible. Any data displayed that is read-only cannot be modified. 
Items can be organized and displayed using the functionality available. 
Tracking 
Turns automatic tracking on or off. 
In the Engineer module, your 
selection persists within and across 
sessions. 
In the Configuration module, your 
selection persists only within a 
session. 
If tracking is on, the Properties grid 
automatically updates to display 
the properties of the currently 
selected tag. 
If tracking is off, the Properties grid 
does not update when you select 
different tags. To update the 
Properties grid, you must right-click 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 704
the required row and select Tag 
Properties. 
Tabs 
Defines the window display mode 
for categories. 
If selected, each category is 
displayed on an individual tab. 
If not selected, each category is 
displayed sequentially. 
Alphabetical sort 
Sorts the displayed information in 
ascending order (A to Z) or 
descending order (Z to A). 
If Category display is selected, the 
categories are sorted then the 
properties within each category are 
sorted. 
If Alphabetical display is selected, 
the properties are sorted. 
Note: 
Alphabetical sort is not available 
when the window is displayed in 
Tabs mode. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Expand All 
Expands all categories and 
properties, individual groups can be 
collapsed and expanded using the 
plus or minus located to the left of 
each category. 
Collapse All 
Collapses all categories and 
properties, individual groups can be 
collapsed and expanded using the 
plus or minus located to the left of 
each category. 
Select Active Cases 
Controls the display of active cases 
for the currently selected property. 
The available functionality is 
identical to the Set Active Cases 
window. 
Text Filter 
Filters the display to match a text 
string. 
Type a string to be searched in the 
Find field and click Find to search 
the currently displayed window for 
matching content. Matched 
content is highlighted. 
Click Clear to delete text from the 
Find field. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 706
Category/Alphabetical 
Defines the window display mode. 
If Category is selected, attributes 
are sorted by category. 
If Alphabetical display is selected, 
attributes are sorted alphabetically. 
Refer to Set Active Cases for further information. 
Document Packs 
Use the Document packs window to define and export packs of associated documents. A documentation pack 
can be made up of datasheets and drawings. The document packs can be revision controlled. 
1. In the Configuration module, select the Rules tab then navigate to the DataModelViews element in the 
Database View Explorer. 
2. In the Tools group, select Database Views Editor, the Database Views Editor is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 707
3. In the Views pane, right click and select New then View, a new DB View is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 708
4. On the View Properties tab, in the User-defined name field enter Document Pack. 
5. In the Description field, enter Document Pack View. 
6. In the Select element types field, enter EDOCPK. 
7. Select the Data Sources tab, click to check the Allow row creation check box. 
8. Click to check the Auto assign or create owner check box. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 709
9. Select the Columns tab, click Add Standard Attributes, the view is populated with standard attributes. 
Note: 
Add the revision attribute using the RVCURR element type for displaying revisions. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 710
10. Select the Preview tab to display a preview of the database view layout. 
11. Click Save Work. 
12. Exit the Configuration module. 
13. Launch the Engineer module. 
14. On the Home tab in the View group select Grids, the Grids Explorer is displayed. 
15. If the Document Pack category is not displayed in the Grids Explorer, in the Grids group, click New then 
Category. The Manage Grids Category window is displayed. 
Note: If a document packs category is available go to step 19. 
16. Enter Document Packs. 
17. Navigate to Document Packs in the Grid Explorer, right click and select New then Category. 
18. In the Category field, enter Equipment. 
19. Right click on Equipment then select Grids, the Create Grid window is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 711
20. In the Name field, enter AVEVA_GRID_Pumps. 
21. In the Description field, enter Grid for Pumps Document Pack. 
22. In the Discipline field, click to display the discipline drop down list. 
23. Click to check the Mechanical check box. 
24. Navigate to the Document Pack view in the View pane. 
25. Select the Fields tab (standard attributes are selected as default) amend as required. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 712
26. Select the Layout tab, re-arrange the columns of the grid as required. 
Note: 
AVEVA recommends that a sub grid should no be created and configured for the Document Packs grid. 
27. Click Save, the grid is created and is displayed in the Grids Explorer. 
28. Navigate to the AVEVA_GRID_Pumps element in the Grids Explorer. 
29. Select the Home tab in the Tags group click Tags, the New Tag window is displayed. 
Note: 
Currently the Document Pack element is not controlled by Tag Management System. 
30. Select Browse, the Select Class window is displayed. 
31. Click to select Assembly then Component Specification class then click Select. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 713
32. Click Create a new document pack instance is created with a system default name. 
33. Rename the document pack instance as per project rules. 
34. Select the View tab, in the View group click Document Packs the Document packs window is displayed. 
35. From the Document packs window, in the Add, Delete, Reorder and Edit Documents pane, click CoverPage.
The Add Cover Page window is displayed. 
Note: 
The cover page template must be configured by an admin user, refer to .Datasheet Template Editor 
36. From the Add Document window, click to select the required cover template from the list of available 
templates, then click Add, the cover page instance is added to the document pack. 
37. From the Document packs window, in the Add, Delete, Reorder and Edit Documents pane, click TOC, the 
Add Document window is displayed. 
Note: 
The table of contents template must be configured by an admin user, refer to .Datasheet Template Editor 
38. Click to select the required TOC template from the list of available templates, then click Add. The TOC 
instance is added to the document pack. 
Note: 
Only one TOC can be selected. 
39. From the Document packs window, in the Add, Delete, Reorder and Edit Documents pane, click Document.
The Add Document window is displayed 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 714
40. Click to select the required datasheet(s) and/or drawing(s), click Add. The selected datasheet(s) and/or 
drawing(s) are added to the document pack. 
41. Click to select the required datasheet(s) and/or drawing(s) then UP to move the data sheet up in the order of 
the data sheet in the document pack. 
42. Click to select the required datasheet(s) and/or drawing(s) then Remove to delete the datasheet(s) and/or 
drawing(s) from the Document pack. 
43. From the Remove document window, click Yes to remove the data sheet from the document pack. 
44. When a new revision of the document is created these are displayed in the latest revision column. Select the 
required revision in the Update Revisions window to update the document in the document pack. The order 
of documents remains unchanged 
Note: 
Any change to the name, description or revision number and so on are reflected in Manage doc packs along 
with table of contents and cover page whenever applicable. 
45. Select the required datasheet(s) and/or drawing(s) from the document pack grid. 
46. Select the Home tab, then click to select Print DocumentPacks. 
47. Select the Manage tab, in the Export group from the drop-down list select Document Pack. 
48. Select Multiple Formats to export the document pack in multiple formats. 
Note: 
The export to single pdf or multiple formats functionality is only available for documents packs. 
49. In the Browse For Folder window, click to select the location to save the exported document packs, then 
click OK. The Exporting document pack window is displayed show the progress of the export. Once the 
process has been completed a message is displayed in the Messages window. 
Datasheets 
Datasheets are stand-alone documents which you can link to specific grid items. You create datasheets from 
datasheet templates, which are created in the Configuration module. Each datasheet is referred to as a 
datasheet instance. 
Functionality is available to create, revise, view, export and compare datasheets. 
Refer to Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to Excel, Export Datasheet to PDF and Print 
Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
Datasheet Hierarchy 
As with all elements in the software, all Engineer module elements are owned by other elements, therefore 
elements must be created in strict order. 
Use the Database Explorer to display the datasheet hierarchy. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 715
Below the Dictionary world level in the Engineer module, the datasheet specific elements are Datasheet 
Instance World, Datasheet Instance Group and Datasheet Instance for further information. 
Refer to Database Explorer, Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to Excel, Export Datasheet to 
PDF and Print Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
Datasheet Instance World 
A Datasheet Instance World (DSIWLD) element is a container for Datasheet Instance (DSINST) definitions. 
Right-click the required element on the Database Explorer and select New > DSIWLD: Datasheet Instance World 
to create a Datasheet Instance World,. A DSIWLD element is created and displayed on the Database Explorer. 
Datasheet Instance Group 
If multiple elements of a similar type are to be created in the dictionary database then groups can be used to 
simplify navigation of the database hierarchy. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 716
A Datasheet Instance Group (DSIGRO) must be created below a DSIWLD element. 
Right-click the DSIWLD element on the Database Explorer and select New > DSIGRO: Datasheet Instance Group 
to create a DSIGRO element. A DSIGRO element is created and is displayed on the Database Explorer. 
Datasheet Instance 
A prerequisite for creating datasheets is that this hierarchical structure exists: 
Element 
DSIWLD 
Datasheet Instance World 
DSIGRO 
Datasheet Instance Group 
Create this structure in the Database Explorer: 
1. On the Admin tab, click Database Explorer. 
2. Right-click the, top-level, Engineering World element and select New>DSIWLD: Datasheet Instance World. 
3. Right-click the DSIWLD element and select New>DSIGRO: Datasheet Instance Group. 
A datasheet instance is created each time a datasheet is created from a datasheet template. Each datasheet 
instance is stored below the DSIGRO element in the Database Explorer. 
To create a datasheet instance within the Database Explorer (typically a one-time task during initial setup of 
AVEVA Engineering), right-click the DSIGRO element and select New>DSINST:Database Instance. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 717
The datasheet instance is created with a default name, the attributes and properties of the datasheet are 
displayed. 
Create/Open Datasheets and Open Datasheet Revisions 
Use the Datasheet window to create a datasheet, open a datasheet or open a datasheet revision. 
Refer to Array of References, Edit Element Status, Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to 
Excel, Export Datasheet to PDF and Print Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
Create Datasheet 
Use the New tab on the Datasheet window to create a datasheet.

Datasheet Template 
Defines the template used to create the datasheet. 
Name

autonaming functionality.

exceed 50 characters. 
Tags 
Specifies the Engineer grid item from which the 
datasheet is created. 
Click the ellipsis that is displayed when the mouse is 
hovered over the cell to display the Reference Array 
Browser window. 
Misc 
The Misc fields can be updated as required. 
The new DSINST element is displayed on the database explorer and the datasheet is displayed alongside the CE 
editor. 
Additional Pages 
To create additional pages such as notes and sketch pages in the datasheet instance click the new tab and select 
additional page. The Additional page selection form is added and is added at the end of the view. 
Additional page selection form is displayed.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
