Open Datasheet 
Use the Open tab on the Datasheet window to open a datasheet. 
The Datasheet window is populated with existing datasheets that are available for selection. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 720
Open Datasheet Revision 
Use the Revisions tab on the Datasheet window to open a specific revision of a datasheet. 
The Datasheet window is populated with revised datasheets that are available for selection. 
Copy Datasheet 
Creates a copy of the currently open datasheet. 
Use the Copy Datasheet window to define the tags that are copied to the new datasheet. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 721
The list of available tags displays the tags that can be copied to the new datasheet. 
If the check box is selected, the tag is copied to the new datasheet. 
If the check box is not selected, the tag is not copied to the new datasheet. 
Filter 
Type a string to filter the list of available tags that are available for selection, matching content is displayed below 
the field. 
By default, the copied datasheets are opened. If a grid has been setup that displays all datasheets for the project, 
the copied datasheets are highlighted. 
Refer to Array of References, Edit Element Status, Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to 
Excel, Export Datasheet to PDF and Print Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
Copy Selected 
Note: 
The Tag Management System must be installed and running. 
When a tag is copied, the datasheets (all user data and instance) associated with the tag are also copied to the 
new datasheet instance. List datasheets are only copied if the primary tag is set. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 722
Duplicate with Referencing Items 
The tag(s) associated with a datasheet are copied as a link (that is: a datasheet copy is not created but all tags in 
the duplicate datasheet are associated to the existing datasheet). List datasheets are only created if the primary 
tag for the list datasheet is set. Duplicate datasheets are copies of the latest working versions (no revision or 
issue history). 
Refer to Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to Excel, Export Datasheet to PDF and Print 
Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
Compare Datasheet Revisions 
Compares two different versions of a datasheet and highlights the differences. 
First Revision 
Specifies the first version of the datasheet for comparison. 
Second Revision 
Specifies the second version of the datasheet for comparison. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 723
Refer to Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to Excel, Export Datasheet to PDF and Print 
Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
Modify Datasheet 
Mapped attributes which are displayed in the grid can be modified, the corresponding attribute in the datasheet 
is then automatically updated to reflect the modification. 
Refer to Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to Excel, Export Datasheet to PDF and Print 
Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
Multi Language Datasheet 
Use the functionality to produce datasheet(s) in more than one language, the default language can be selected in 
the Engineer module. 
When a datasheet is opened, the contents are displayed in the currently selected language. If the currently 
selected language is changed, any content which is mapped to a language variant is changed to the newly 
selected language. Any content that has not be mapped to a language variant will be displayed in the default 
current language. 
Note: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 724
The language variant can only be a STRING attribute. 
Refer to Languages for further information. 
Bulk Creation 
Use the functionality to create datasheets in bulk. 
Refer to Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to Excel, Export Datasheet to PDF and Print 
Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
New List 
A list datasheet returns a list of the tags in a selected datasource. 
You create a you create a list datasheet in the same way as other datasheets but, additionally, select List 
Datasheet from the list in the toolbar of the Datasheet template editor, in the Configuration module. 
Use the List Datasheet Selection Form window in the Engineer module to create a datasheet from a list 
datasheet template. For list datasheets, you do not need to select a particular row in the grid. 
Open a grid in the Grid Explorer and select Datasheet>New List in the Datasheets group on the toolbar. 
Select a list datasheet and specify a name for the new datasheet. The data source of the grid must be the same 
as the data source on which the list template is based. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 725
If one or more filters is applied on the current grid, the Apply filter from current grid check box is enabled. 
Optionally, select the check box to list only the tags returned by the filters. 
Refer to Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to Excel, Export Datasheet to PDF and Print 
Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
Datasheet Document Properties 
Displays the properties of open datasheets. 
Datasheet properties can be modified, for example, the description. 
Refer to Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to Excel, Export Datasheet to PDF and Print 
Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
Datasheet Configuration 
Use the Datasheet Configuration window to define the datasheet hierarchy and watermark settings, change 
highlighting settings and export folder settings. 
Click Settings in the Datasheets group on the Admin tab. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 726
Owner 
Defines the owner of the datasheet in the hierarchy. 
Type the owner name in the field or select the ellipsis to display the Engineering Reference Browser window. 
Use the Change Highlighting part of the Datasheet Configuration window to specify how differences between 
the last revision and the working version of the datasheet are highlighted. 
Attributes changes 
Defines highlight settings for attribute changes. 
If the check box is selected, all attribute mappings 
with changes are highlighted. 
If the check box is not selected, attribute mappings 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 727
with changes are not highlighted. 
Cell position changes 
Defines highlight settings for mappings with cell 
position changes. 
If the check box is selected, mappings with cell 
position changes are highlighted (the mappings are 
copied to another cell). 
If the check box is not selected, mappings with cell 
position changes are not highlighted. 
Unit of measure changes 
Defines highlight settings for the list of value mappings 
with changes in units of measure. 
If the check box is selected, the list of value mappings 
with changes in units of measure is highlighted. 
If the check box is not selected, the list of value 
mappings with changes in units of measure is not 
highlighted. 
Sort order of list items 
Defines highlight settings for the list of value mappings 
with changes in sort order. 
If the check box is selected, the list of value mappings 
with changes in sort order is highlighted. 
If the check box is not selected, the list of value 
mappings with changes in sort order is not 
highlighted. 
Apply font 
Defines the font and font style for highlighted 
changes. 
If the check box is selected, a font and font style for 
highlighted changes can be defined. The Font, Bold 
and Italic options become available for selection. 
If the check box is selected, a font and font style for 
highlighted changes cannot be defined. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 728
Highlight New Records 
Highlights the newly added records in a datasheet 
between revisions while comparing revisions. 
Newly added records are highlighted in the datasheet 
continuation areas of both list and normal datasheets. 
Note: 
If the Attribute Changes check box is selected, any 
logically deleted records and modified attributes in 
the datasheet are highlighted while comparing 
revisions. Logically deleted records are only visible in 
the datasheets when Show Logically Deleted is 
selected. If both are selected any logically deleted 
rows between the two revisions of the datasheet are 
highlighted. 
Font 
Specifies the font for highlighted changes. 
Bold 
Specifies bold text for highlighted changes. 
If the check box is selected, highlighted text is 
displayed with a bold font. 
If the check box is not selected, highlighted changes 
are not displayed with a bold font. 
Italic 
Specifies italic text for highlighted changes. 
If the check box is selected, highlighted text is 
displayed with an italic font. 
If the check box is not selected, highlighted changes 
are not displayed with an italic font. 
If any changes are made to datasheet with free text, the user is asked if the changes to the data sheet are to be 
saved as a revision. The revised datasheet will be saved to the following location: Project Default folder\
Data\Datasheets\Instances. 
Use the Watermark part of the Datasheet Configuration window to define and format a watermark for the 
datasheet. 
Apply Watermark 
Defines watermark settings for the datasheet 
If the check box is selected, a watermark is applied to 
the datasheet. All of the options in the Watermark 
part of the Datasheet Configuration window become 
active. 
If the check box is not selected, a watermark is not 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 729
applied to the datasheet 
Text 
Specifies the text that is applied to the watermark. 
Direction 
Specifies the watermark display direction. 
Colour 
Specifies the color that is applied to the watermark. 
Font 
Specifies the font that is applied to the watermark. 
Size 
Specifies the text size that is applied to the 
watermark. 
Bold 
Specifies bold text for the watermark. 
If the check box is selected, the watermark is 
displayed with a bold font. 
If the check box is not selected, the watermark is not 
displayed with a bold font. 
Italic 
Specifies italic text for the watermark. 
If the check box is selected, the watermark is 
displayed with an italic font. 
If the check box is not selected, the watermark is not 
displayed with an italic font. 
In front 
Positions the watermark in front of the datasheet text. 
Behind 
Positions the watermark behind the datasheet text. 
All the settings are saved in the DatasheetInstanceSettings.xml file that is stored with the project. 
Use the Export Settings part of the Datasheet Configuration window to export datasheets with or without 
mappings (comments). Multiple datasheets and datasheets with document packs can be exported. 
Note: 
This functionality and default is set by the System Administrator and can be applied to all export options for 
datasheets. 
Use the Export Folder part of the Datasheet Configuration window to define the default datasheet export 
location for Excel and PDF files. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 730
Use the Claim part of the Datasheet Configuration window to define the datasheet claim settings when opened. 
If the check box is selected, the datasheet is claimed when opened. 
If the check box is not selected, the datasheet is not claimed when opened. 
Precision 
Use the functionality to override project defaults, right click in the cell and click Precision, the Precision window 
is displayed. Enter the no of decimals required and remove any trailing zeroes, the Remove Trailing Zeroes check 
box is selected by default. 
Refer to Import Datasheet from Excel, Export Current Datasheet to Excel, Export Datasheet to PDF and Print 
Diagram or Datasheet for further information. 
Datasheet upgrade 
If a tag is reclassified, use the functionality to choose a new datasheet template to upgrade the datasheet 
instance. 
Part of Parts 
Use the part of parts functionality to create new parts or to select existing parts using source elements on the 
parent tag’s datasheets. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 731
Note: 
This functionality is only available with TMR enabled projects and can only be used for the creation of 
CanHaveParts. 
If the parts already exist, there will be no changes, all modification of attributes on these sub-tags have the same 
behaviour as existing datasheets. If a part does not exist, then entering data into any field for the sub-tag 
prompts for selection of an existing sub-tag, or the creation of a new part (for one level parts creation the Select/
Create window is displayed, for multi-level creation, new tags are automatically created). 
If the DB View is configured with multiple element types class picker is displayed and based on the user 
selection. 
Select existing is supported only for one level parts creation, the reference browser is displayed, select the 
unassigned tag from reference browser and the associated attribute is updated in parent tag. 
Parts are created by both normal editing of part attributes on a datasheet and also on datasheet import. Once 
the parts are created, the references of the owning object must be set to point to the new tag. 
Notes 
Part creation in datasheets trigger on edit mode when focusing out of a cell instead of keypress in a cell, or when 
enter is pressed after providing attribute value. The entered value in a cell is updated to db and reflected on the 
datasheet. This applies to all part attributes including both normal cell mappings and user controls. 
Auto Parts 
The auto parts functionality is available to the user by default, if the administrator has added the user to the 
roles functionality available in the configuration module. The user can override this at any time but will not be 
saved. 
If the auto parts checkbox is selected, parts will be created without the need to populate the Select/Create 
window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 732
Notes: 
If the Select /Create window is displayed the user can create a new part tag or select an existing tag. The prompt 
is shown when the parent tag exists or only for a single level. When there are multiple part items in the hierarchy 
the part creation are triggered silently. New part items are not created when there has been no valid value given 
in the attribute cell. 
For user controls, normal cell selection will not trigger part creations. 
Default Cases 
Default cases in are set in the datasheets template so that all the datasheet instances created (based on the 
datasheet template with the desired case values) are auto-populated instead of setting the desired cases in each 
datasheet instance (admin task). The cases are preferably created and added to the MDB in the Engineer module 
before logging in the Configuration module to map the cases under Items properties. Refer to Cases and Default 
Cases for further information. 
Notes: 
Where the case values are changed by the user when loading a datasheet template, a message is displayed to 
update case controls. 
Create/Open - If the case control has a default case defined, then the attribute values should be loaded based on 
the default case, else the default case is treated as the first case as it is today. 
Upon upgrading of datasheet Instance, if the default case has been updated, then while opening the datasheet 
instance the attribute values need to be loaded based on its dependent case. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 733
The case can be modified in the datasheet instance and once changed it should be saved to only that specific 
datasheet instance but not override the datasheet template default value. 
It can be overridden to only the datasheet instance. 
Upon Excel import/export the case control values works as previous. 
Design Reuse 
Design Reuse is a .net addin which simplifies the work required by copying engineering items and datasheet 
instances between projects. 
The following objects can be exported/imported using design reuse: 
• Engineering items 
• Datasheet instances. 
Prerequisites for the import of engineering items 
The required datasheet templates, definitions of distributed attributes, User Defined Attributes (UDAs) and User 
Defined Element Types (UDETs) must be defined in the target database prior to the import of a transfer set. 
Limitations 
• All parents of the exported elements must be named 
• Exported elements should be named, if not, the import may create duplicate items if the imported element 
already exists in the target database. 
Graphical User Interface 
The Graphical User Interface (GUI) is a .net addin and is included in the application framework by using the 
general customization tools provided by AVEVA. The interface works together with the command interface and 
can be mixed. 
Use the Design Reuse window to import or export items and to define the import/export settings. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 734
Export 
Use the Export tab to define the exported items. 
The list of available export items displays the items for export. 
Add selected items to list 
Adds the currently selected item to the list of available 
export items. For example, the currently selected grid 
record. 
Get referred items of selected items in list 
Adds referenced items of the currently selected items 
to the list of available export items. 
Remove selected items from list 
Removes the currently selected items from the list of 
available export items. 
Export 
Exports the items in the list of available export items 
to a transfer set. 
Display the log file 
Displays the export log, only valid after an export has 
been processed. 
Right-click on the list of available export items to display a drop-down list. 
Add external selection 
Adds items to list of available export items using a 
selection from the 3D window or diagram viewer. 
Set to current element 
Zooms to the currently selected item on the diagram. 
Select all 
Selects all items in the list of available export items.

Transfer set properties 
Displays the properties of the transfer set. 
Include all items 
Includes all items in the list of available import items. 
Exclude all items 
Excludes all items in the list of available import items. 
Import 
Imports the items in the list of available import items. 
Display the log file 
Displays the import log, only valid after an import has 
been processed. 
Right-click on the list of available import items to display a drop-down list. 
Set to current element 
Zooms to the currently selected item on the diagram. 
Select all 
Selects all items in the list of available export items

Settings values are saved each time the program exits and are loaded again each time the program starts. 
General Settings are the valid general settings for Design Reuse. Local Settings are the settings for the graphical 
user interface (Design Reuse Addin).

Assign Gland Adapters 
Specifies the glands adapters attached to the cable glands of a cable or enclosure. 
Use the Assign cable gland adapter window to assign the gland adapters. 
Grid 
Displays the cable gland adapters that are available for selection. 
Assign to all cable glands of same type 
Specifies whether or not the selected gland adapters are to be applied to every cable gland that of the same type 
as the current cable gland. 
Change Gland Adapter Type 
Specifies the gland adapter type of glands attached to a cable. 
Use the Assign library window to change the gland adapter type. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 762
Grid 
Lists the gland adapter types that are available to be assigned. 
Cable to Gland 
Specifies the cables attached to a gland. 
Use the Assign Cables window to assign the cables. 
Grid 
Lists the cables that may be assigned. 
Lugs 
Specifies the lugs attached to a cable. 
Use the Assign cable lugs window to assign the lugs. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 763
Available Cable Lugs 
Lists the cable lugs available to be assigned to the cable. 
Add 
Adds the lug selected in the Available Lugs grid to the Selected Cable Lugs grid. 
Remove 
Removes the lugs selected in the Selected Cable Lugs grid from that grid. 
Selected Cable Lugs 
Lists the cable lugs assigned to the cable. 
Lug Position 
Specifies the cable end a selected lug is assigned to, or whether it is a spare. 
Assign is disabled if any selected lugs have a lug type of UNSET. 
Quantity 
Specifies the number of lugs to be assigned of each selected type.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering

Transits 
Specifies the the transit assembly or assemblies that a cable is assigned to, and the order in which the cable 
passes through them. 
Use the Assign cable to cable transit assembly window to assign a cable to transit assemblies 
Upper Grid 
Lists the cable transit assemblies available to be assigned to the cable. 
Add 
Moves the transit assembly selected in the upper grid to the lower grid. 
Remove 
Moves the transit assembly selected in the lower grid back to the upper grid. 
Lower Grid 
Lists the cable transit assemblies assigned to the cable. 
Up and Down Arrows 
Used to change the order of the assigned transit assemblies. 
Transit Parts 
Specifies the transit parts to be assigned to a cable. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Use the Assign cable transit parts window to assign the transit parts to a cable. 
Grid 
Lists the available transit parts. 
Select 
Specifies which transit parts are to be assigned.

Specifies the plugs and sockets of cables with group type core.

Available Cable Connectors 
Lists the cable connectors available to be assigned to the cable. 
Add 
Adds the connector selected in the Available Cable Connectors grid to the Selected Cable Connectors grid. 
Remove 
Removes the connector selected in the Selected Cable Connectors grid from that grid. 
Selected Cable Connectors 
Lists the cable connectors assigned to the cable. 
Connector Ends 
Specifies which end a selected connector is assigned to.

If any of the selected connectors do not have an end specified, the following message is displayed when Assign is

Assign Cable From/To Equipment 
Specifies which equipment items are assigned to a cable. 
Upper Grid 
Lists the equipment available to be assigned to the cable. 
Add 
Moves the equipment selected in the upper grid to the lower grid. 
Remove

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering

Moves the equipment selected in the lower grid back to the upper grid. 
Lower Grid

Assign Cables to Drum 
Specifies which cables drums cables are assigned to. 
If the selected cable drum does not have a catalog cable assigned to it, the following message will be displayed: 
Use the Select cable for cable drum window to add cables to a drum. 
Upper Grid 
Lists the cables available to be assigned to the cable drum, for example, those associated with the same cable 
catalog. Cables already assigned to drums are not listed. 
Add 
Moves the cable selected in the upper grid to the lower grid. 
Remove 
Moves the cable selected in the lower grid back to the upper grid. 
Lower Grid 
Lists the cables assigned to the drum. 
Up and Down Arrows 
Used to change the order of the assigned cables.

Page 769
Assign Cables Using Grid Reference Browser 
Use this functionality to assign cables to cable drums using the grid reference browser. 
Notes: 
Only cables with same catalogue as that of the cable drum may be assigned. 
Cables may not be assigned to more than one cable drum. 
Multiple cables can be assigned to a cable drum. 
Automatically Assign Cables to Cable Drums 
Automatically assigns cables that have not been drummed to a selected cable drum based on their length and 
catalog cable. 
Messages in the System tab of the Messages window will indicate whether or not the cables have been 
successfully drummed. 
Assign Cables Assemblies to Cables 
Specifies the cable assemblies assigned to cables. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 770
Grid 
Lists the cables assemblies that can be assigned to the cable. 
From Connection: 
Displays the From connection (i.e. plug/socket) of the cable assembly selected in the grid. 
To Connection: 
Displays the To connection (i.e. plug/socket) of the cable assembly selected in the grid. 
Swap Connections 
The To and From connections of the cable assembly will be applied to the cable swapped around. 
Auto Terminate Cores 
Automatically terminates the cores of the selected cable or cables. 
If any of the cables have a choice of terminals they can be connected to, the Auto terminate cores window is 
displayed, with details of all selected cables displayed. Otherwise the terminations proceed automatically. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 771
The Auto terminate cores window lists the selected cables and the items and terminals they are to be connected 
to and from. 
Existing and default connections and terminations are displayed when the window is opened in the Connected 
from, Terminated from, Connected to, and Terminated to columns. 
To add or change the equipment/sub-equipment at which a cable core is terminated, click ... in the relevant 
Terminated from or Terminated to field. This accesses the Select sub-equipment window. 
Click OK to make the selected terminations. 
Messages in the System tab of the Messages window will indicate whether or not the cores have been 
successfully terminated. 
Select sub-equipment 
Use the Select sub-equipment window to select the sub-equipment at which a cable core is to be terminated. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 772
The Tag number fields list the equipment and sub-equipment available for selection. 
Click + to expand the sub-equipment list for an item of equipment. 
Click on the required equipment or sub-equipment and click Select to save the selection and close the window. 
Swap Connectors 
Swaps the connectors at the each end of a group type core cable. 
The Assign Cable Connectors window is displayed. 
Click Yes to complete the operation. 
External Calculations 
Performs cable sizing calculations using pre-defined equations stored in an external Excel template. 
Used the Calculation List window to select the required calculation template. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 773
The sizing calculations then take place. 
If the Show execution summary check box is selected in the Calculation Template Details form when a 
calculation template is created or edited, then the results are displayed in the External Calculations Execution 
Summary window. 
External Calculations Execution Summary 
Use the External Calculations Execution Summary window to review the results of external sizing calculations. 
The summary is displayed only if the Show execution summary check box is selected in the Calculation Template 
Details form when the template is created or edited. 
OK 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 774
Must be pressed to save the calculation results. 
Preview Wiring 
Use the Preview Wiring window to view a preview of the wiring of an item. 
Find 
Use to find and select items in the preview. 
Displays the Find window. 
Scale To Fit 
Scales the display of the preview to fit all the items in it. 
-, + and Slider 
Zooms the display of the preview in and out. 
Overview 
Displays an a small window in the bottom-right corner of the window displaying an overview of the preview. 
Refer to Find Items for further information. 
Preview Terminations 
Use the Preview Terminations window to view a preview of the terminations of an item.

Find 
Use to find and select items in the preview.

Scale To Fit 
Scales the display of the preview to fit all the items in it. 
-, + and Slider 
Zooms the display of the preview in and out. 
Overview 
Displays an a small window in the bottom-right corner of the window displaying an overview of the preview.

Equipment 
The AVEVA Engineering and AVEVA Instrumentation Tools - Equipment tab is displayed when an item in a grid, 
drawing, or power distribution diagram is selected for which equipment functionality is applicable, for example, 
electrical or mechanical equipment or instruments. 
Create Equipment Wiring 
Use the Create Wiring For window to create wiring for equipment.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 776
Grid 
Displays details of the selected equipment. 
Select 
Specifies whether an assigned wiring rule is to be applied to an item. 
Wiring Rule 
Specifies the wiring rule applied to the equipment, if this has already taken place. 
The ... control displays the Assign Wiring Rule window, used to assign a wiring rule to the equipment. 
+ / - 
After a wiring rule has been selected for an item, the +/- control displays/hides the connected "from" details of 
that will be assigned to the primary tag when the wiring rule is applied. 
Beneath that, a second +/- control displays/hides the connected to details of that will be assigned to the 
secondary tag(s) when the wiring rule is applied. 
Connected ‘To’ 
Specifies the tag number of the secondary equipment. 
The .. control displays the Assign Tag window, used to select a tag number. 
Sub-Equipment 
If a sub-equipment tag of the assigned tag is selected, it is displayed here. 
Cable Type 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 777
Specifies the catalog cable that a cable derives its basic attributes from. 
The ... control displays a window which is used to select the cable catalog item. 
Cable Length 
Specifies the length of the cable. 
Terminate ‘To’ 
Specifies whether or not the cable is to be terminated at the secondary tag. 
Refer to Catalog Item for further information. 
Assign Tag 
Use the Assign Tag window to assign a tag number to a connection. 
Grid 
Lists the available equipment tag numbers. 
+/- 
Displays/hides the sub-equipment of a tag. 
Edit Supply Fed Items 
Use the Manage Fed and Supply Tags window to assign and manage the supplying and feeding items for 
equipment.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 778
Equipment no, Connection voltage, Output voltages 
Specifies the tag number and basic voltage details of the equipment. 
Check box 
Specifies whether a tag is supplied by (on the Fed tags tab) or feeds (on the Supply tags tab) the equipment. 
Fed tags 
Lists the available tags that the equipment can or does feed. 
Supply tags 
Lists the available tags that the equipment is or could be supplied by. 
Fed Compartment, Supply Compartment 
Specifies the compartment that the selected tag that the equipment is fed from or supplied. 
Fed Device, Supply Device 
Specifies the device in the selected compartment that the equipment is fed from or supplied.

Edit Circuit Breakers 
Use the Manage circuit breakers window to add and edit the circuit breakers of distribution boards. 
Class Type 
Specifies the class of circuit breakers to be added to the distribution board.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering

Page 780
Add Circuit Breaker 
Specifies whether a 1 phase way, 2 phase way, or 3 phase way circuit breaker is to be added. 
+N 
Specifies whether or not 2 or 3 phase way circuit breakers have neutrals. 
L1L2, L1L3, L2L3 
Specifies the phase for 2 phase way circuit breakers 
Load Balance 
Specifies the load on each phase of the distribution board. Updated when loads are assigned, changed, removed 
etc. 
Grid 
Displays the assigned circuits and phases. 
Assign Load 
Specifies the load assigned to each phase of each circuit. 
Status 
Specifies whether a circuit phase is in use, spare, or reserved.

Edit Terminations 
Defines the terminations for an enclosure plate from which a connected cable is terminated. 
Refer to Select Plate and Terminations for further information. 
Assign Busbar Devices 
Use the Devices for Busbar window to assign devices to busbars. 
Power Connections and Spares grid 
Lists the power connections, spares and linked devices assigned to the busbar. The main device, connection type, 
whether or not it is a spare, supply tag, fed tag, enclosure tag and compartment of each connection and spare is 
listed. 
Incomer button 
Adds a spare incomer to the busbar. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 782
Outgoing feeder button 
Adds a spare outgoing feeder to the busbar. 
Compartment and Devices 
Used to view and edit the compartment devices of a power connection selected in the grid. 
Manual 
Shows the main device, linked devices, compartment, and components within that compartment, associated 
with power connection selected in the grid. 
Main Device 
Shows the main device associated with power connection selected in the grid. 
Linked Devices 
Lists the linked devices associated with power connection selected in the grid. 
Compartment 
Shows the compartment associated with power connection selected in the grid. 
Compartment class 
Opens the New window. Used to add a compartment to the power connection selected in the grid and specify its 
class. 
Compartment parts 
Shows the components within the compartment associated with power connection selected in the grid. 
Main devices are indicated with an asterisk prefix *. 
Device class 
Opens the New window. Used to add a device to the compartment and specify its class. 
Remove device 
Used to delete a selected device from the compartment. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 783
Set as main device 
Used to set a selected device as the main device in the compartment. 
Unset main device 
Used to remove the main device status from a device in the compartment. 
Library 
Lists the Library compartments that may be applied to a new or existing compartment to generate details of that 
compartment and create its components. 
In the case of an existing compartments with components the user is given the option to replace the existing 
components with those from the library compartment record, or add the components from the library 
compartment record to the list of existing components. 
If there are multiple main protection devices in the library record, the Select main device window will be 
displayed. 
Refer to Create New Compartment or Device and Select Main Device for further information. 
Create New Compartment or Device 
Use the New window to create compartments and devices for power connections. 
Window displaying compartment classes:

Page 798
Signals grid 
Displays details of the signals that can be assigned to the channels. 
Hide assigned signals 
Specifies whether or not signals already assigned to channels are listed in the Signals grid. 
Show all signals 
Specifies whether or not all signals are to be listed in the Signals grid. 
Select (Signals grid) 
Specifies which signals are to be assigned to the channels selected in the Channels grid. 
Channels grid 
Displays details of the channels of the selected IO module. 
Select (Channels grid) 
Specifies the channels to which signals selected in the Signals grid are to be assigned. 
Also used to specify which signals are to unassigned from terminals using Remove. 
- and + 
Displays and hides details of the signals assigned to a channel. 
Polarity 
Specifies the polarity of a terminal marking. 
Assign 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 799
Assigns the signals selected in the Signals grid to the channels selected in the Channels grid. 
Disabled until a signal and a channel are selected. 
Also disabled if selected channel’s cardinality (the number of signals it can accommodate) would be exceeding by 
assigning the signal, or if the channel types of the channels and signals are not compatible. 
If a selected signal or signals are already assigned to a channel or channels, displays the Remove assigned signal 
from channel(s) window. 
Remove 
Removes signals selected in the Channels grid. 
Refer to Remove Assigned Signal from Channel(s) for further information. 
Remove Assigned Signal from Channel(s) 
Use the Remove assigned signal from channel(s) window to unassign signals from channels so that different 
signals can be assigned in their place. 
Select 
Specifies which signals are to removed. 
Remove 
Removes the selected signals from the channel(s). 
Loop Design Status 
Checks the design status of an instrument loop by checking that all the signal propagating instruments in the 
loop are fully connected to IO modules and that the signal has been assigned to an IO point/channel. If these 
criteria are met, the loop’s design status will be Complete. Otherwise it will be Incomplete. 
The Processing Loop Design Status progress window is displayed: 
Once the check is complete, the Design Status property of the loop will be set to Complete or Incomplete as 
appropriate. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 800
Add Connected Drawing Items 
Use the Connected Items window to add for an item selected on a drawing, equipment and cables that are 
connected to that item in the database, but not on the drawing. 
Click the item in question on the drawing before clicking Add Connected Items. If there is only a single item that 
can be connected, that item is then automatically connected on the drawing. 
If there are multiple items that may be connected, the Connected Items for window then lists the items that are 
connected to that item in the database, but not on the drawing. 
Grid 
Lists the items that can be connected to the selected item on the drawing, on the left and right sides. 
Check box 
Specifies whether or not a specific item is to be added to the drawing and connected to the subject item. 
Add Power Connectors 
Adds power connectors between equipment on Key One Line Diagrams. 
Select the equipment on the drawing (e.g. a motor and a busbar) then click Add Power Connector to add the 
connector(s). 
Not used for Single Line Diagrams. 
Enclosures 
The AVEVA Engineering und AVEVA Instrumentation Tools - Enclosures tab is displayed when an item in a grid or 
drawing is selected for which enclosures functionality is applicable, such as enclosures themselves, and 
equipment and instrument items that may be associated with enclosures.

Display Name 
Specifies the name of the enclosure being edited or the enclosure item selected or being added. 
Class 
Specifies the class of an item to be added to the enclosure, or of an item selected in the enclosure. 
The ... control displays the New window, used to select the class and name of items to add to the enclosure. 
Library items

Preview

For compartments, displays a preview of the layout of the compartment selected under Library items in the 
Compartment Preview window. 
For other items, such as instruments and IO modules, displays a preview of the terminal arrangement of the 
selected item under Library items in the Terminal Arrangements Preview window. 
Number of items to add 
Multiples of certain library items can be added (various device types for example). When such a library item is 
selected, an additional field is displayed next to Add >>. 
Specifies the number of the library items to be added. 
Channel items 
Only displayed if items with channels are selected, for example, an IO module. 
Displays the channels of the item and their descriptions.

Add >> 
Adds the new item to the part (tab) of the currently selected enclosure.

The preview on that tab is updated to display the item. 
Back, Front Door, Door Inside, Left, Right, Top, Bottom 
Used to display the items added to each part of the enclosure, for example, back or front. 
Selected items are highlighted with a blue outline.

Rotates the selected item plus or minus 90°. 
Right-click on an item to display a drop-down menu.

Assign External Equipment 
Use the Assign to Enclosure window to assign external equipment to an enclosure. 
Select all 
Selects all the Select check boxes. 
Add 
Used to add equipment to the grid, which can then be assigned to the enclosure.

Remove 
Used to remove equipment from the enclosure and the grid.

Create Wiring 
Used to create wiring for equipment selected in the grid by applying the wiring rule specified for them. 
Grid 
Lists the equipment selected to be assigned to the enclosure using Add. 
Select 
Specifies whether equipment listed in the grid is to be assigned to the enclosure. 
Wiring Rule 
Specifies the wiring rule applied to equipment in the grid, if this has already taken place. 
The ... control displays the Assign Wiring Rule window, used to assign a wiring rule to the equipment. 
Terminate 
Specifies whether or not equipment is to be terminated. 
Sub Equipment 
Used to assign sub-equipment to equipment. 
Displays the Select Enclosure Sub-Equipment window.

Select all 
Selects all the Select check boxes. 
Show Tags Assigned To Other Enclosures 
Used to list equipment tags already assigned to other enclosures in the grid.

Grid 
Lists tags to be assigned to the enclosure

Quantity

Export to SEE Electrical 
Use the 3D Panel+ option to export the details of selected panel items to SEE Electrical. Select the item to be 
exported and then select the option. 
Simulation 
Note: 
The Simulation tab becomes available if the all required applications are installed and set up. 
Enables the integration of process simulation schematic with Process Flow Diagram (PFD), including all heat and 
material balance data, ensuring process data from simulation is available in the engineering database for use by 
project teams. 
Provides seamless data transfers from the simulation product (AVEVA PRO/II Simulation or AVEVA Process 
Simulation) to AVEVA Engineering. 
If AVEVA Process Simulation is used, data can be be transferred back to the simulation product, the simulation 
can be rebuilt based on the feedback from AVEVA Engineering, and the data can be re-transferred to AVEVA 
Engineering. After the simulation data is available in AVEVA Engineering, the data can be transferred to 
applications such as AVEVA E3D Design, AVEVA Diagrams and AVEVA P&ID based on what stage of detail design is 
available. 
E&I 
Use the E&I tab to perform general actions involving Electrical and Instrument items. These actions do not 
require a specific item to be selected beforehand. 
View Power Explorer 
Displays a hierarchical tree-view of the electrically powered items in a project. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 822
For a busbar tag, the hierarchy of powered items is displayed as follows: 
Busbar Tag 
• Type of Connection (Outgoing Feeder, Incomer, Coupler) 
• Connected Tag 
• Compartment 
• Main Device/Linked Devices 
• Linked Devices linked to Linked Devices 
• Logical Power Cable 
• Physical Cables i.e. found in the Cable Schedule 
• Spares (can be dragged and dropped between busbars in the explorer) 
Other hierarchies are displayed for: 
• Generators 
• Power Centres 
• Transformers 
• Distribution Boards 
• Motors 
• Other Power Equipment 
View Power Distribution 
Displays the current power distribution hierarchy of items in the project. It is updated automatically when the 
hierarchy or any displayed item data is changed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 823
Selected items are highlighted in blue. 
Find 
Use to find and select items in the display. 
Displays the Find window. 
Scale To Fit 
Scales the display of the hierarchy to fit all the items in it. 
-, + and Slider 
Zooms the display of the hierarchy in and out. 
Overview 
Displays an a small window in the bottom-right corner of the window displaying an overview of the hierarchy. 
Refresh 
Used to refresh the hierarchy if it does not automatically update. 
Refer Find Items for further information. 
Export to ETAP 
Use the functionality to export AVEVA Engineering data into the selected ETAP project. AVEVA Engineering 
creates an xml file containing the project data, the location of the where the xml file is placed is determined by 
the user. The file is then converted into a format which ETAP can interpret using the configuration defined in 
Configuration. 
The data contained within the file is then processed by ETAP where it can be reviewed, selected and imported 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 824
into the ETAP format. The progress of the export is displayed: 
Note: 
The AVEVA Integration Service Service is not used when exporting data to ETAP. 
Import from ETAP 
Use the functionality to import ETAP data into the project, AVEVA Engineering sends a request to the AVEVA 
Integration Service Service which translates the data from the ETAP xml file into an format which AVEVA 
Engineering can interpret. Data mappings (stored in AVEVA Engineering) are applied to create a data set which 
can then be viewed in AVEVA Engineering. 
The data set can then be selected and imported into a project using the Compare/Update functionality. The 
progress of the import is displayed, the Compare/Update grids are then displayed in the Compare/Update 
window displaying the differences between the target and source element types. The progress of the import is 
displayed: 
The Compare/Update grids are then displayed. 
Refer to Compare and Update Data for further information. 
Select Changes 
Use the Select Changes functionality to accept or decline the change(s) from the attribute comparison. 
Refer to Accept Changes for Update for further information. 
Update Target Database 
Use the Update Database functionality to apply all the accepted changes to the target database. 
Refer to Update the Target Database for further information.

Drawings Explorer 
Provides an easy to use graphical method for exploring the drawing database and CAD image overlays. 
The Drawings Explorer displays the drawing database elements and CAD image overlays in a hierarchy. Click on 
an element in the tree view to navigate to the element in the database and make it the current element. 
To expand an element hierarchy, click  to the left of the element name. To contract an expanded element, click 
 to the left of the element name. 
To create a new drawing type element, right-click a drawing folder element to display a drop-down menu. 
New 
Engineering Canvas Drawing Type 
Creates a new drawing type element below the 
currently selected drawing folder element. 
To create a new drawing element or to modify a drawing type element, right click on a drawing type element to 
display a drop-down menu.

New

name for the new drawing.

the Drawings Explorer and a blank sheet is created. 
Change Standard 
Changes the standard of symbols on the current 
drawing. 
Attributes 
Displays the attributes that have been specified for the 
CE.

Status Bar 
Positioned at the bottom of the user interface, the status bar displays message information and a number of 
view and selection options. 
Status bar options can be toggled on and off using the mouse, a white background is displayed when a feature is 
turned on. 
Some of the status bar options can also be toggled on and off using keyboard function keys. Where this is the 
case, the keystroke displays in brackets within the tooltip. 
Scale 
Drawings only: 
Displays the current scale. If there 
is no value to display -:- is 
displayed. 
Cursor coordinates 
Drawings only: 
Indicates the cursor position on the 
drawing canvas. 
Selected Record(s) 
Engineer grids only: 
Displays the total number of 
selected grid records. 
Show Notifications 
Engineer only: 
Displays the notifications window. 
Messages 
As actions are carried out, 
messages relating to these actions 
display on the status bar. For 
example, claiming or releasing 
records. 
Session Defaults 
Engineer only: 
Default values for new records can 
be defined for a session. These 
values automatically populate fields 
in the Create element information 
window which is used to create a 
record. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 830
Grid (Ctrl+F7) 
Drawings only: 
Turns the grid and ruler display on 
or off. 
Functionality is available to define 
the display of the grid using the 
Grid Settings window. 
Open Grid Settings 
Drawings only: 
Defines the grid settings. The grid 
settings are defined using the Grid 
Settings window. 
Grid Snap (Ctrl+F9) 
Drawings only: 
Turns grid snapping on or off. 
To temporarily override the current 
snap to grid setting, hold down the 
Shift key when manipulating an 
element. 
Polar Tracking (Ctrl+F10) 
Drawings only: 
Turns the display of polar 
coordinates on or off. 
If polar tracking drawing mode is 
on, objects can only be 
manipulated using angle and 
distance coordinates. 
Orthogonal Lock (Ctrl+F8) 
Drawings only: 
Turns orthogonal drawing mode on 
or off. 
If orthogonal drawing mode is on, 
objects can only be manipulated in 
a horizontal or vertical direction. 
To temporarily override the current 
orthogonal drawing mode setting, 
hold down the Shift key when 
manipulating an object. 
Dynamic Hints (Ctrl+F12) 
Drawings only: 
Turns the display of non-coordinate 
related information on or off. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 831
Alignment Snapping 
Drawings only: 
Turns object snapping using 
alignment guides on or off. 
Alignment guides are used to align 
an object with another object. 
When the two objects are aligned, 
the alignment guides are displayed 
on the drawing canvas. 
Nearest create to grid snapping 
Drawings only: 
Turns nearest create object 
snapping on or off using grid 
spacing. 
The nearest create snaps are 
displayed at the intersection of 
objects and the grid. 
The functionality is only available 
when Grid Snap is turned on. 
Functionality is available to define 
the display of the grid using the 
Grid Settings window. 
Nearest create to locked 
coordination snapping 
Drawings only: 
Turns nearest create object 
snapping on or off when locked 
coordinates are specified in the 
context editor. 
The nearest create snaps are 
displayed as crosshairs on objects 
along the locked coordinate axis. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 832
Additional Grips 
Drawings only: 
Turns the display of additional line 
dimension, connector, outline and 
section plane grips on or off. 
Connector orthogonal mode 
Drawings only: 
Turns orthogonal connector mode 
on or off. 
If orthogonal connector mode is 
on, connectors can only be 
manipulated in a horizontal or 
vertical direction. 
This also applies to validation and 
regeneration of connectors when 
attached elements are modified. 
For example, after moving an 
attached symbol, the connector is 
validated with orthogonal/routing 
mode and updated if not valid. 
Connection Point Routing 
Drawings only: 
Turns connection point routing 
mode on or off. 
If connection point routing mode is 
on, the connector is routed to 
match the connection point 
direction of the first/last segment, 
for example: 
Connections can still be made using 
the symbol position (if the symbol 
has no connection point). 
If connection point routing mode is 
off, no routing segment is created 
on the connector, for example: 
This also applies to validation and 
regeneration of connectors when 
attached elements are modified. 
For example, after moving an 
attached symbol, the connector is 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 833
validated with orthogonal/routing 
mode and updated if not valid. 
Connection full generation on first 
/ last vertex stretch. 
Drawings only: 
Turns full connection generation on 
or off when the first or last vertex 
of a connector is stretched. 
If full connection generation is on, 
the connector is completely 
regenerated when the first or last 
vertex is stretched. 
If full connection generation is off, 
only adjacent connector segments 
are regenerated when the first or 
last vertex is stretched. 
Regeneration is not always full 
(from first to last vertex), if there is 
an inside reference position, full 
regeneration applies from the 
stretched first/last vertex to the 
reference position. 
In-canvas line weights 
Drawings only: 
Turns the display of line weights on 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 834
or off. 
Object Snapping (Ctrl+F3) 
Turns object snapping on or off. 
To temporarily override the current 
object snap setting, hold down the 
Shift key and right click the mouse 
button. 
Functionality is available to define 
the object snap settings using Open 
Object Snap Options.. 
Open Object Snap Options 
Drawings only: 
Click to modify the object snap 
settings. 
Refer to Notifications, Messages and Message Log, Session Default Values for New Records, Grid Settings and 
Object Snaps for further information. 
Grid Settings 
Defines the grid settings. 
Specify the grid spacing, grid snapping, grid style, and ruler display settings. 
Grid On (Ctrl+F7) 
Displays the grid. 
Grid Snap On (Ctrl+F9) 
Snaps objects to the grid. 
Grid Spacing 
Input dimensional values in the X and Y fields to 
define the grid spacing. 
Snap Spacing 
Input dimensional values in the X and Y fields and an 
angular value in the Angle field to define how closely 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 835
the cursor adheres to the grid. 
Style 
Select a grid display style from the drop-down list. 
Draw rulers 
Displays rulers when the grid is displayed. 
Click Save to save the grid settings changes. Click Reset to return the grid settings to the default settings. 
Click Apply to accept the grid settings. 
Click OK to accept the grid settings and close the Grid Settings window. 
Click Cancel to discard any changes and close the Grid Settings window. 
Refer to Status Bar and Object Snaps for further information. 
Object Snaps 
Aids the positioning of new objects by placing them relative to the geometry of existing objects. 
When object snapping is on, if a point is being specified and it is close to the snap point of an existing object, a 
symbol displays to indicate that the snap point is present. 
In the example, a line is being drawn. The orange symbol indicates that the line is intersected with a snap point 
of an existing object (in this case the endpoint of a diamond, as indicated by a box). 
Select the object snap modes to specify the types of snap points that are available. 
Connection 
Snaps to connection points. 
Nearest Create 
Snaps to surface points of primitives. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 836
Endpoint 
Snaps to endpoints of primitives. 
Midpoint 
Snaps to middle points of primitives. 
Center 
Snaps to center points (for example, for circles arcs or 
ellipses). 
Nearest 
Snaps to the nearest points of primitives. 
Perpendicular 
Snaps to points of primitives, or their extensions, 
where the line connecting the point and the previous 
point is perpendicular to the primitive. 
Quadrant 
Snaps to points representing quadrants of circles, arcs 
or ellipses. 
Tangent 
Snaps to points of primitives where the line 
connecting the point and the previous point is tangent 
to the primitive. 
Extension 
The cursor is attracted by virtual extensions of existing 
primitives 
Parallel 
Snaps to points on a virtual line parallel to the 
indicated primitive. 
Node 
Snaps to node points (for example, for text objects). 
Intersection 
Snaps to primitive intersection points. 
Insertion 
Snaps to insertion points of complex objects. 
A snap selection can be retained between different states of the same operation using the PowerWheel™. For 
example, it is possible to draw a rectangle that only snaps to object intersections. 
To use a single snap type, select the required operation, for example, draw rectangles. Press Shift and 
simultaneously right-click the drawing canvas. The PowerWheel™ displays the snapping options: 
Click More to display additional snapping options 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 837
The selected snap option is only available until the operation is finished or None is selected from the 
PowerWheel™. 
Refer to Status Bar and PowerWheel™ for further information. 
Symbol Palette 
Provides an easy to use graphical method for adding symbols to the drawing canvas. 
The Symbol Palette window displays the symbol palettes that can be accessed and the symbols that are available 
for selection. Symbols with inactive in build or deleted classes in the conceptual model are not displayed. 
Symbols can be dragged and dropped onto the drawing canvas. 
The symbol palette displays the connection points for each symbol. If the connection points are associated with 
colors, the colors display on the symbol palette when the mouse is hovered over the symbol. If the connection 
points are not associated with colors, the default orange color displays on the symbol palette when the mouse is 
hovered over the symbol. 
Select a connection point to use it as the base point for the symbol when dragged and dropped onto the drawing 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 838
canvas. 
Options 
Modifies the symbol display size in the Symbol Palette window. 
Small icons 
Displays the symbols at 64 pixels. 
Medium icons 
Displays the symbols at 96 pixels. 
Big icons 
Displays the symbols at 128 pixels which is the default 
display size. 
Search 
Type a string in the Search field, all symbols are searched for matching content. The search results are 
highlighted with a yellow background. 
The arrow on the right of the Search field displays a drop-down list of search filters that are available for 
selection. 
By name 
Filters the search results by name. 
By class name 
Filters the search results by class name. 
Press Ctrl+F to clear the Search field. 
All Symbols 
Displays all available symbols for the currently selected palette (including assemblies). 
If all symbols are displayed, click the All Symbols tab to minimize and maximize the display of the Symbol Palette 
window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 839
Assemblies 
Displays all available assemblies for the currently selected palette. 
If assemblies are displayed, click the Assemblies tab to minimize and maximize the display of the Symbol Palette 
window 
Palette 
Displays the available symbols for the drawing, categorized by palette name. 
• Click on a collapsed palette name to open the palette and collapse all other palettes. 
• Click on an expanded palette name to collapse all palettes. 
• Click + to the left of a palette to expand the palette. All other palettes are unchanged. 
• Click - to the left of a palette to collapse the palette. All other palettes are unchanged. 
Right-click on a palette name to display a drop-down list of available options. 
Toggle 
Toggles the expand and collapse functionality for the 
currently selected palette. 
Collapse all 
Collapses all palettes. 
Expand all 
Expands all palettes. 
Collapse all but this 
Collapses all palettes with the exception of the 
currently selected palette. 
Hover the mouse over a symbol name to display additional information for the symbol. 
Add Symbol 
Adds a symbol to a drawing. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 840
Drag the and drop the required symbol onto the drawing canvas. 
The context editor indicates the position of the symbol and the New Tag window is displayed. Use the New Tag 
window to define the symbol class and the number of created symbols. 
Note: 
If an assembly is added to the drawing which contains multiple symbols, the New Tag window is displayed for 
each symbol in the assembly. The symbol which is currently being created is highlighted on the drawing canvas. 
The item types that can be used on a drawing are defined using the Configuration module. For example, 
Engineering item types or Functional item types. If the symbol cannot be dragged and dropped onto the drawing 
canvas because the item type is not valid, a message is displayed. 
Refer to Create Record in Tag Management System Enabled Project, Add Grid Records and Add Project 
Breakdown Structure Items for further information. 
Add Grid Records 
Provides an easy to use method for adding grid records to the drawing canvas. 
Drag and drop the required grid records onto the drawing canvas. The grid record symbols are displayed on the 
drawing canvas. 
If the grid records cannot be dragged and dropped onto the drawing canvas, 
 is displayed. 
The item types that can be used on a drawing are defined using the Configuration module. For example, 
Engineering item types or Functional item types. If the grid records cannot be dragged and dropped onto the 
drawing canvas because the item type is not valid, a message is displayed. 
Refer to Symbol Palette, Add Project Breakdown Structure Items, Electrical Power Details and Select Terminal 
Tier for further information.

Add Project Breakdown Structure Items 
Provides an easy to use method for adding project breakdown structure items to the drawing canvas. 
Drag and drop the required project breakdown structure items onto the drawing canvas. The project breakdown 
structure item symbols are displayed on the drawing canvas. 
If the project breakdown structure items cannot be dragged and dropped onto the drawing canvas, 
 is 
displayed. 
The item types that can be used on a drawing are defined using the Configuration module. For example, 
Engineering item types or Functional item types. If the project breakdown structure item cannot be dragged and 
dropped onto the drawing canvas because the item type is not valid, a message is displayed. 
Refer to Symbol Palette and Add Grid Records for further information. 
Electrical Power Details 
Use the Electrical power details window to specify power details for items created on an electrical schematic 
diagram. The window is displayed when a powered equipment tag is dragged and dropped onto a power 
distribution drawing.

 button

 button

 button

Select Terminal Tier 
Use the Select terminal tier window to select which tier of a multi-tier terminal to place on an automated loop 
or termination diagram. The window is displayed when a multi-tier terminal is dragged and dropped onto such a 
drawing. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 844
Attributes 
Displays the attributes that have been specified for the Currently selected Element (CE), dependent upon access 
rights, some attributes can be modified. Attributes can also be configured so that they are not displayed in the 
Attributes window. 
By default, the attributes are displayed in a list format, the left hand part of the window displays the attribute 
names, the right hand part of the window displays the attribute values. 
The representation of mapped symbols on the drawing canvas can be modified by changing an attribute value if 
attribute driven mapping has been setup. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 845
For example, the representation of a valve can be modified to open or closed via the attribute Valve Position and 
the value Open or Closed. 
The attribute value can be modified using the drawing canvas or a grid. Modifications to open drawings are 
reflected immediately, modifications to closed drawings are reflected the next time the drawing is opened. 
Issued drawings cannot be modified. 
Refer to Attribute Driven Mapping for further information. 
Properties 
Displays the properties of objects. If the selected object(s) are read only (locked, claimed or unavailable through 
direct access control), the properties are greyed out. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 846
If the Properties window is docked, click the Pin to lock the window in the docked position. 
If a single object is selected, the object type displays in the drop-down list at the top of the window, and the 
window displays the object’s properties. 
If multiple objects are selected, the drop-down list at the top of the window initially displays All (number of 
selected objects) and the window displays only properties common to the selected objects. 
The drop-down list displays each type of object selected. If more than one object type is currently selected, the 
number of each object type displays in brackets. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 847
If an object type is selected from the drop-down list, the properties associated with the object type display, or 
common properties if more than one of that object type is currently selected. 
A new selection can be initiated, a single or multiple selection specified, and all properties can be set to the 
default values. Selections made on the Drawings Explorer are shown in the Properties window and clears the 
graphical selection. 
Specify object selection mode 
Indicates multiple object selection 
mode. Every object clicked on is 
added to the list of selected 
objects. 
Specify object selection mode 
Indicates single object selection 
mode. Only one object can be 
selected at a time. Selecting an 
object de-selects any previously 
selected objects. 
Initiate new object selection 
De-selects all currently selected 
objects and initiates a new 
selection.

the selected objects to default 
Sets all the visual properties of the 
selected objects to their default 
values.

selected object.

The properties list view can be defined.

List the properties by category. 
Property categories are ordered 
logically. By default, some 
categories are expanded and some 
are collapsed. Once set, the 
expanded or collapsed state of a 
category remains the same when a 
different element is selected. 
Alphabetical 
Lists the properties alphabetically.

Select a vertex to display a list of available properties on the bottom part of the Vertex Editor window. The vertex 
properties can be modified.

PowerWheel™ 
Use the PowerWheel™ to access the functionality available from the Fluent UI without leaving the drawing 
canvas. Each PowerWheel™ can be customized to suit the needs of a project, refer to Edit an UIC File for further 
information.

To display the default basic function PowerWheel™, press Ctrl on the keyboard and right click on the drawing 
canvas. 
To display the default general PowerWheel™, right click on the drawing canvas. 
To display an additional PowerWheel™, click any tile displaying >. 
Unhide All 
Use the PowerWheel™ to display all hidden objects on the drawing canvas. The functionality is not available if all 
objects on the drawing canvas are visible. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering

Page 851
Create Drawing 
Creates a new drawing. The drawing name, drawing type and the drawing template can be specified. 
Use the drop-down list to define the drawing type. 
Use the Create new drawing window to specify the name for the new drawing. 
If drawings of the drawing type are created using templates, use the Select Template window to select from the 
list of available templates. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 852
The list of available templates can be filtered by template name or description. 
The drawing is created using the selected template. 
Note: If the drawing is created using the termination drawing template, the start points positioned outside of the 
defined template region are ignored. 
An error message is displayed summarizing the problem. 
Note: If the drawing is created using the termination drawing template or the loop drawing template, items on 
the drawing are represented using the symbol assigned to the library instance of the item for that template. If no 
symbol has been assigned to the library instance for the template, the default symbol for the item type as 
defined for the template will be used instead. 
If the drawing type is not associated with a template, then depending on the drawing type, the Select Drawing 
Border window may then be displayed to select a border for the created sheet. 
Refer to Create Sheet and Bulk Creation for further information. 
Create Sheet 
Creates a new drawing sheet. 
Use the Select drawing border window to select the border for the created sheet. 
Available Borders 
Use the drop-down list to select the border for the created sheet. 
The sheet is created using the selected border. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 853
Bulk Creation 
Creates multiple drawings from selected grid records. 
Use the Bulk Creation window to select the associated drawing type, drawing template and to create the 
drawings. 
The list of selected elements displays the associated grid records for the drawings. 
Drawing Type 
Use the drop-down list to select the drawing type for the created drawings. 
Template 
Use the drop-down list to select the drawing template for the created drawings. 
Show Drawing on Creation 
Defines the display settings for the created drawings. 
If the check box is selected, the drawings are displayed when created. 
If the check box is not selected, the drawings are not displayed when created. 
Close Drawing 
Closes the current drawing or all drawings except the current drawing. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 854
To close the current drawing, right-click the drawing tab and select Close from the drop-down list. 
If more than one drawing is open, you can close all drawings except the currently selected drawing. Right-click 
the drawing tab to remain open and select Close All Tabs Except This from the drop-down list. 
Dock/Undock Drawing 
Undocks a drawing from the main application into a separate window which can be positioned and resized 
independently of the Engineering application window. 
To undock the current drawing, right-click the drawing tab and select Float from the drop-down list. 
An undocked window can be docked in the Engineering application window. 
To dock the current drawing, right-click the Dock option in the bottom-right corner of the drawing window. 
Draw Connectors 
Adds a connector to a drawing. 
Intelligent grip points can be used to connect the connector to an object, if the object is moved or modified, the 
connection is preserved. The connector is drawn using straight line segments. 
Connectors can be drawn using orthogonal connector mode. 
A default connector can be drawn, if a connector class hierarchy association has been defined for the connection 
point definition in the Configuration module. 
Draw Connector with Specified Connection Points 
Draws a connector with specified connection points. 
Use the Symbol Palette to select the required connector, drag and drop the connector onto the drawing canvas. 
The prompt Select connector starting position or snap to connection point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the start point of the connector. 
If connection point routing is on, the prompt Type or select length of starting point segment or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the routing length of the connector. 
The prompt Select connector position or snap to connection point to finish or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the point to which the connector is 
drawn. 
If an object connection point is selected, a connector is drawn between the two specified points and the 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 855
command ends automatically. 
If an object snap point or a position on the drawing canvas is selected, the prompt Select connector position or 
snap to connection point to finish <or Finish> is displayed. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple straight line connector segments can be created. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
A connector is drawn and displays on the drawing canvas. 
Note: 
The Repeat Connector option becomes available on the Fluent User Interface (UI) once the first connector is 
drawn. Additional connectors, using the same format as the first connector, can be added to the drawing canvas 
without the need to drag and drop from the Symbol Palette. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Begin with shield 
Draws a connector with shield vertices at the start 
point of the connector. Refer to Draw Connector with 
Shield Vertices for further information. 
Turn on routing 
Turns on connection point routing. 
Multi connector 
Draws a multi-connector which consists of a parent 
connector and a specified number of child connectors 
that are equally spaced using an offset value. Refer to 
Draw Multi-connector for further information. 
Turn off routing 
Turns off connection point routing. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 856
End with shield 
Draws a connector with shield vertices at the end 
point of the connector. Refer to Draw Connector with 
Shield Vertices for further information. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
Draw Connector with Shield Vertices 
Draws a connector with specified shield vertices at the start point or end point of the connector. The 
functionality to create shield vertices at the connector start point is explained. 
Use the Symbol Palette to select the required connector, drag and drop the connector onto the drawing canvas. 
The prompt Select connector starting position or snap to connection point or is displayed. 
Input B at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Snap to positions to create connections for a loop vertex or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the connection point. 
The prompt Snap to positions to create connection points for a loop vertex or <Finish> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the connection point 
The prompt is repeated, multiple connection points can be created. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the shield vertex creation process. 
The prompt Select connector position or snap to connection point to finish is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the point to which the connector is 
drawn. 
If an object connection point is selected, a connector is drawn between the two specified points and the 
command ends automatically. 
If an object snap point or a position on the drawing canvas is selected, the prompt Select connector position or 
snap to connection point to finish <or Finish> is displayed. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple straight line connector segments can be created. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
A connector is drawn and displays on the drawing canvas. 
Note: 
The Repeat Connector option becomes available on the Fluent User Interface (UI) once the first connector is 
drawn. Additional connectors, using the same format as the first connector, can be added to the drawing canvas 
without the need to drag and drop from the Symbol Palette. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.
