Draw Multi-connector 
Draws a multi-connector which consists of a parent connector and a specified number of child connectors that 
are equally spaced using an offset value. 
The child connectors are drawn using the same format as the parent connector. 
Use the Symbol Palette to select the required connector, drag and drop the connector onto the drawing canvas. 
The prompt Select connector starting position or snap to connection point or is displayed. 
Input M at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Child connectors is displayed. 
Input a value in the field and press Enter to define the number of child connectors. 
The prompt Offset is displayed. 
Input a value in the field and press Enter to define the offset value for the child connectors. 
The prompt Select connector starting position or snap to connection point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the start point of the parent connector. 
The prompt Select connector position or snap to connection point to finish or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the point to which the parent connector is 
drawn. 
If an object connection point is selected, a connector is drawn between the two specified points and the 
command ends automatically. 
If an object snap point or a position on the drawing canvas is selected, the prompt Select connector position or 
snap to connection point to finish <or Finish> is displayed. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple straight line connector segments can be created. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
A multi-connector is drawn and displays on the drawing canvas. 
Note: 
The Repeat Connector option becomes available on the Fluent User Interface (UI) once the first connector is 
drawn. Additional connectors, using the same format as the first connector, can be added to the drawing canvas 
without the need to drag and drop from the Symbol Palette. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.

Refer to Status Bar, Symbol Palette, Modify Connectors and Create Connection Point Definition for further

Draw Stretchy Fed Item Connections on Single Line Diagrams 
As well as using the methods described above, “stretchy” fed item connections can be drawn between items on 
SLDs, e.g. between a busbar and a generator. 
Click on the symbol of one of the items to be connected. The connection points of the symbol are displayed. 
Connection point colours indicate their “direction” i.e. supply tag or fed tag, as defined in the Configuration 
Module. 
Click on and “grab” the required connection point. Holding down the mouse button, drag the mouse pointer 
towards the other symbol. A connector line is drawn along the path of the mouse pointer. 
When the pointer is near the other symbol, its connection points are displayed. Drag the pointer to the required 
connection point and release the mouse button. A fed item connection between the two items is created. 
Fed items connection points can only be connected to supply item connection points, and vice versa. 
Items such as busbars do not have pre-defined connection points. A connection can be drawn from or to any 
point on their symbols. 
Create Text 
Adds annotation text to drawings. 
The prompt Specify the first point is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the insertion point for the text. The Text 
Editor window displays. 
Use the Text Editor window to input and format text. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 863
Select font name 
Defines the font for the text. 
Specify height of the text 
Defines the height of the text. 
Increase font size 
Increases the font size of the text. 
Decrease font size 
Decreases the font size of the text. 
Select text colour 
Defines the color of the text. 
Rotation 
Defines the rotation angle of the text. 
Specify if bold style should be used 
Applies bold style to the annotation text. 
Specify if italic style should be used 
Applies italic style to the annotation text. 
Specify if underline style should be used 
Underlines the annotation text. 
Set left alignment 
Left aligns the text. 
Set center alignment 
Center aligns the text. 
Set right alignment 
right aligns the text. 
Set top alignment 
Aligns the text with the top of the frame 
Set base alignment 
Aligns the text with the base of the first character. 
Set middle alignment 
Aligns the center of the text with the center of the 
frame. 
Set bottom alignment 
Aligns the text with the bottom of the frame. 
Show/Hide Intelligent Text dialog 
Adds intelligent text to the annotation. 
Enter text here 
Specifies the annotation text. 
Intelligent Text 
Input the intelligent text in the Text Editor window. Any attribute class from the conceptual model can be used. 
For example, @Temperature@. 
Note: 
Hash tag syntax is not supported for intelligent text on drawings and sheets. Use attribute value syntax to display 
drawing and sheet information. For example, use @SHTNUM@ to display the sheet number, 
@Sheet.Description@ to display the sheet description, or @Drawing.ECDNAM@ to display the drawing name. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 864
If the intelligent text contains a unit value (for example, @Temperature@), the unit value displayed on the 
drawing is associated with the first unit set in the order of appearance. If a unit set is not defined, the default 
units of measure is displayed. If the unit sets or the default units of measure are modified, the application must 
be restarted to display the modified unit values on the drawing. 
The example formulas can be used to modify the display of units for individual annotations: 
@Temperature{nounits}@ 
{no units} removes the unit of measure from the 
annotation. 
@Temperature{units:K}@ 
{units:K} displays the temperature in Kelvins (if K is a 
defined temperature unit set). 
@Temperature{c1:5}@ 
{c1:5} displays characters one to five of the 
temperature attribute. 
@Temperature{units:K}{nounits}{c1:5}@ 
{units:K}{nounits}{c1:5} displays characters one to five 
of the temperature attribute in Kelvins, the unit of 
measure does not display. 
The example formulas can be used to display attribute values of owner, member and connected objects: 
@Connected To.Default Name@ 
Displays the default name of the object that the 
annotated object is connected to. The connected to 
attribute of the annotated object must be populated. 
@Connected From.Default Name@ 
Displays the default name of the object that the 
annotated object is connected from. The connected 
from attribute of the annotated object must be 
populated. 
@Owner.Description@ 
Displays the description of the object that owns the 
annotated object. The description attribute of the 
owning object must be populated. 
@Members[0].Default Name@ 
Displays the default name of the object that is a 
member the annotated object. The default name 
attribute of the member object must be populated. 
Refer to Unit Sets, Engineering Defaults and Set Default Units of Measure for further information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 865
Change Symbol 
Changes the currently selected symbols on the drawing canvas to another symbol. 
Use the Select Symbol window to specify the symbol that replaces the selected symbols on the drawing canvas. 
Off Page Connectors 
Off page connectors can be connected, disconnected and viewed. 
Connect Off Page Connector 
Connects two currently selected off page connector symbols which are positioned on separate sheets. 
A link is created between the two off page connectors, the drawing number of the linked off page connector is 
displayed. 
Disconnect Off Page Connector 
Disconnects two currently selected off page connector symbols which are positioned on the same sheet or 
separate sheets. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 866
Navigate to Connected Off Page Connector 
Toggles the drawing display between two connected off page connector symbols. 
Not Connected Off Page Connector 
Use the OPC window to display and navigate to a disconnected off page connector symbol. 
The OPC window displays the drawing number, sheet number and associated connector for each disconnected 
off page connector. 
Show only Current Sheet 
Defines the display of disconnected off page connectors in the OPC window. 
If the check box is selected, the OPC window displays disconnected off page connectors on the current sheet. 
If the check box is not selected, the OPC window displays all disconnected off page connectors in the project. 
Navigate 
Navigates to the currently selected disconnected off page connector. 
Highlight Not Connected Off Page Connector 
Highlights all disconnected off page connectors on the drawing. The highlighted objects display using a single 
color and a thick line type. 
To clear the highlight from the disconnected off page connectors on the drawing. On the Drawings tab, in the 
Tools group, click Clear Highlight. 
Modify Objects using Grip Points 
When an object is selected, the grip points display in blue. There are various types of grip point, for example, 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 867
squares and diamonds. 
A grip point can be manipulated to modify the appearance of an object. 
When a grip point is selected, the display color changes to red. 
Stretch Objects using Endpoints 
Stretches objects using endpoints that display as blue squares or blue triangles. 
To stretch an object, click an endpoint. 
The prompt Specify stretch point is displayed. 
Input the distance to move the endpoint at the prompt and press Enter or click to identify the endpoint position. 
The object is manipulated and the command ends automatically. 
Alternatively, an object can be stretched using the stretch command. Refer to Stretch Objects for further 
information. 
In the case of a triangle or hexagon, stretching an end-point increases the size of the object. The shape of the 
object is maintained. For example, an equilateral triangle does not become an isosceles triangle when stretched. 
In the case of a rectangle, stretching an end-point increases the length or height of the object. The shape of the 
object is maintained. 
In the case of diamonds, stretching an endpoint changes the diameter of the shape on the endpoint axis. The 
opposite endpoint is automatically stretched by the same amount as the initial endpoint. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 868
In the case of irregular shapes, stretching an endpoint stretches the selected corner of the shape to a new point. 
In the case of lines and arcs, stretching an endpoint stretches the selected end of the line to a new position. 
The endpoints of arcs display as blue triangles. Stretching the endpoint stretches the end of the arc to a new 
position, along the current curve of the arc. 
Note: 
Alternatively, the prompt Specify Angle is displayed. Input the sweep angle at the prompt and press Enter. 
Stretch Objects using Midpoints 
Stretches objects using midpoints that display as blue squares, blue triangles, blue diamonds or blue crosses. 
In the case of a circle, stretching a mid-point increases the size of the circle. The shape of the object is 
maintained. The circle does not become an ellipse. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 869
The midpoints of ellipses display as two types of grip points: blue squares and blue triangles. 
Stretching a square midpoint grip changes the diameter of an ellipse on that axis. The opposite midpoint is 
automatically stretched by the same amount as the initial midpoint. 
Stretching an ellipse using a triangular midpoint grip increases the ellipse by the same amount along each axis. 
The midpoints of arcs are indicated by blue squares and blue triangles. 
Stretching a square midpoint results in the midpoint being stretched to a new position while the endpoints of 
the arc remain in the same position. 
Stretching a triangle midpoint results in the arc being increased or decreased in size by the amount stretched. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 870
The midpoints of irregular shapes display as two types of grip point: blue diamonds and a line connected to a 
blue cross (vertex midpoint). The grip points can be used to stretch a midpoint in different ways. 
Stretching a blue-diamond midpoint results in the line that the midpoint is on being stretched out into an arc. 
Stretching a vertex midpoint results in the line that the midpoint is on being stretched out into a new point, 
creating a new vertex. 
Note: 
Vertex midpoint grips can be displayed using additional grips on the status bar. Refer to Status Bar for further 
information. 
Stretch Connectors using Endpoints 
The endpoints of connectors display as two types of grip point: circle endpoints and a line connected to a blue 
cross (vertex endpoint). The grip points can be used to stretch an endpoint in different ways. 
Note: 
The display of a stretched connector can be affected by the connector orthogonal mode, connection point 
routing and connection full generation on first / last vertex stretch settings on the status bar. Refer to Status Bar 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 871
for further information 
Stretching a circle endpoint results in the end of the connector being stretched to a new position. 
Stretching a vertex endpoint results in the end of the connector being stretched out into a new point, creating a 
new vertex. 
Vertex endpoint grips can be displayed using additional grips on the status bar. Refer to Status Bar for further 
information. 
Rotate Objects using Grip Points 
Rotates symbols, connection points, rectangles, diamonds, triangles or hexagons using grip points that display as 
blue double-headed arrows. 
To rotate an object, select the rotate grip point and input an angular value in the field and press Enter or click to 
identify the rotation angle of the object. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 872
Fillet Object Corners using Grip Points 
Fillets object corners for rectangles, diamonds and triangles using grip points that display as blue crescents. 
To fillet the corners of an object, input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter. 
Alternatively, drag the grip point away from the object center to increase the fillet radius or towards the center 
to decrease the fillet radius. 
Move Objects using Grip Points 
Moves objects using center point grips that display as blue squares (irregular shaped objects do not have Center 
grip points). 
To move an object, click the center point grip and input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to 
identify the new center grip point location. 
There are other methods for moving objects. Refer to Move Objects and Move Symbols using Connection Points 
for further information. 
Extend Symbols using Grip Points 
Extends symbols using grip points that display as blue arrows. Objects that are defined as repeatable are copied 
during the process. 
To extend a symbol, click the arrow grip point and input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to 
identify the grip point location. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 873
Refer to Create Symbol Template with Extensible Symbols for further information. 
Modify Connectors 
A connector can be modified using grip points and additional grip points. 
A connector can be modified using the Properties window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 874
Connector vertices can be modified using the Vertex Editor window. Text can be associated with each connector 
vertex. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 875
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 876
Text 
Input a value in the field to define the content for the 
connector text. 
Click 
 to add intelligent text. 
Note: 
The connector has a DEXREF attribute which holds a 
reference to an external element. The intelligent text 
is evaluated against this external element if it is valid. 
There are two pseudo attributes on the connector 
CONSRF and CONERF, these hold the DEXREF of the 
symbols at the start and end of the connector 
respectively. The FROM command can be used to 
extract data about the DEXREF, CONSRF and CONERF 
(for example, #BORE<FR DEXREF>). There is a 
limitation on the intelligent text, it is not possible to 
travel the hierarchy from the DEXREF, CONSRF or 
CONERF. This means that while it is possible to display 
the owner of the DEXREF element, it is not possible to 
display any further information about the OWNER. 
Text Offset 
Input a value in the field or select from the drop-down 
list to define the text offset from the center of the 
connector along the X axis and the Y axis. 
Text Colour 
Select from the drop-down list to define the color of 
the connector text. 
Alignment 
Select Base from the drop-down list to align the 
bottom of the connector text with the connector. 
Select BBody from the drop-down list to align the 
connector text above the connector. 
Select HBody from the drop-down list to align the 
center of the connector text with the connector. 
Select TBody from the drop-down list to align the top 
of the connector text with the connector. 
Font 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
text font from the visual style. 
Select from the drop-down list to define the font for 
the connector text. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 877
Text segment 
By Style inherits the connector text position from the 
visual style. 
Start positions connector text adjacent to the first 
connector segment. 
Centre positions connector text adjacent to the center 
connector segment. 
End positions connector text adjacent to the last 
connector segment 
Justification 
Select Left from the drop-down list to left justify the 
connector text. 
Select Right from the drop-down list to right justify 
the connector text. 
Select Centre from the drop-down list to center justify 
the connector text. 
Char height 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
connector text character height from the visual style. 
Input a value in the field or select from the drop-down 
list to define the character height for the connector 
text. 
Character spacing 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
connector text character spacing from the visual style. 
Input a value in the field or select from the drop-down 
list to define the character spacing for the connector 
text. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 878
Line spacing 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
connector text line spacing from the visual style. 
Input a value in the field or select from the drop-down 
list to define the vertical line spacing for the connector 
text. 
Rotation angle 
Input a value in the field to define the rotation angle 
for the connector text. 
Marker glyph 
By Style inherits the connector marker display settings 
from the visual style. 
Select an image from the gallery to modify the 
connector marker display settings. A marker is 
positioned at each connector vertex point. 
Marker Colour 
By Style inherits the connector marker color settings 
from the visual style. 
Select a color to modify the connector marker color. 
Marker size 
By Style inherits the connector marker size from the 
visual style. 
Input a value in the field or select a size to modify the 
size of the connector marker. 
Is major 
By Style inherits the connector jump settings from the 
visual style. 
Yes defines the connector as dominant. A jump is not 
applied to the connector at connector intersections. 
No defines the connector as subordinate. A jump is 
applied to the connector at connector intersections. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 879
Jumping glyph 
By Style inherits the connector jump display settings 
from the visual style. The default jump display is 
indicated with a gap. 
Select an image from the gallery to modify the 
connector jump display settings. 
Jumping size 
By Style inherits the connector jump size from the 
visual style. 
Input a value in the field or select a size to modify the 
size of the connector jump. 
Show add vertex grips 
Yes displays vertex grips on the drawing canvas when 
the connector as selected. 
No removes vertex grips from the drawing canvas 
display when the connector as selected. 
Flow 
By Style inherits the connector flow direction from the 
visual style. 
None modifies the connector flow direction to no 
direction. 
Forward modifies the connector flow direction to 
forwards. 
Back modifies the connector flow direction to 
backwards. 
The flow setting is created for use with asymmetric 
line styles or glyphs 
Shield colour 
By Style inherits the connector shield color settings 
from the visual style. 
Select a color to modify the connector shield color. 
Shield diameter 
By Style inherits the connector shield diameter size 
from the visual style. 
Input a value in the field or select a size to modify the 
diameter of the connector shield. 
Shield extension 
By Style inherits the connector shield extension from 
the visual style. 
Input a value in the field or select a size to modify the 
extension of the connector shield. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 880
Shield line type 
By Style inherits the connector shield line style from 
the visual style. 
Select an image from the gallery to modify the 
connector shield line style settings. 
Shield orientation 
By Style inherits the connector shield orientation from 
the visual style. 
Automatic calculates the shield orientation based on 
the connection point positions. 
Horizontal orientates the shield horizontally. 
Vertical orientates the shield vertically 
Functionality is available on the Vertex Editor window only to modify Flow arrow representation on connectors. 
Angle 
Input a value in the field to define the rotation angle 
of the flow arrow. 
Distance 
Input a decimal value in the field to define the 
position of the flow arrow on the connector segment. 
For example, input 0.5 to position the flow arrow at 
the mid-point of the connector segment, or input 1 to 
position the flow arrow at the end point of the 
connector segment. 
Rotation Strategy 
The angle of the flow arrow is dependent on the 
rotation strategy. 
Select Custom from the drop-down list to allow the 
modification of the flow arrow rotation angle using 
the origin point context. 
Select By segment from the drop-down list to allow 
the modification of the flow arrow rotation angle 
using the segment context. 
Size 
Input a value in the field to modify the size of the flow 
arrow. 
Refer to Create Text, Status Bar, Modify Objects using Grip Points, Vertex Editor, Properties and Draw Connectors 
for further information. 
Merge Connectors 
Merges two connectors. 
The properties of the merged connectors are inherited from the second picked connector. 
Input HEAL directly on the drawing canvas to display the Context Editor. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 881
The prompt Specify source connector is displayed. 
Click to identify the first connector to merge. 
The prompt Specify target connector is displayed. 
Click to identify the second connector to merge. 
The connectors are merged. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. 
If a selected connector is already attached to an element, a user message displays indicating that the connectors 
cannot be merged. 
If there is a circular dependency between the selected connectors, a user message displays indicating that the 
connectors cannot be merged. 
Create Quick Label 
Creates labels which are automatically attached to the currently selected objects. 
The default label format is defined by the administrator. The content and format of the label can be modified 
using the Text Editor window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 882
Copy Objects using Base Point 
Copies objects to the clipboard, ready for pasting to the same sheet, or a different sheet, using a specified base 
point. 
The prompt Select objects is displayed. 
Note: 
The prompt does not display if one or more objects are already selected. 
Click to identify the objects to copy and press Enter. 
Paste Objects 
Various methods are available to paste objects onto the drawing canvas that have been copied to the clipboard. 
Paste New 
Use the functionality to paste a new tagged item from the clipboard to a specified position on the drawing 
canvas. 
Paste Duplicate 
Use the functionality to paste a copy of a tagged item from the clipboard to a specified position on the drawing 
canvas. The copied item is associated with the same tagged item as the original. 
Paste Graphics 
Use the functionality to paste a new tagged item from the clipboard to a specified position on the drawing 
canvas. The tag properties are copied from the original item. 
Paste Objects 
When pasting objects using the Paste New, Paste Duplicate or Paste Graphics commands, the functionality is 
identical. 
The prompt Specify point is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the base point relative to which the 
objects are pasted. 
The objects are pasted to the identified position from the clipboard and the command ends automatically. 
The prompt 
Refer to Create Record in Tag Management System Enabled Project for further information.

Move Objects 
Moves objects to a specified position on the same sheet, the reference positions of connected elements (for 
example, connectors) are retained. 
The prompt Select objects to move is displayed. 
Note: 
The prompt does not display if one or more objects are already selected. 
Click to identify the objects to move and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify base point or <Displacement> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify a base point relative to which the objects 
are moved. 
The prompt Specify the second point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the new location of the objects. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 884
The objects are moved and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Displacement 
Moves the objects, relative to the zero point of the 
user coordinate system Refer to Move Objects using 
Displacement Position for further information. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Refer to Move Objects using Grip Points and Move Symbols using Connection Points for further information. 
Move Objects using Displacement Position 
Moves objects using a specified displacement position on the same sheet, the reference positions of connected 
elements (for example, connectors) are retained. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 885
The prompt Select objects to move is displayed. 
Note: 
The prompt does not display if one or more objects are already selected. 
Click to identify the objects to move and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify base point or <Displacement> is displayed. 
Input D at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify displacement or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the new location of the objects. The 
displacement between the new object position and the original is determined by the position of the cursor with 
relation to the zero point of the user coordinate system. 
The objects are moved and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 886
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Refer to Move Objects using Grip Points and Move Symbols using Connection Points for further information. 
Move Symbols using Connection Points 
Moves symbols using connection points that display as grey circles. 
To move a symbol, click the connection point and input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to 
identify the new connection point location. All existing connections to the symbol are retained. 
If a symbol is moved to a connection point on another symbol, a connection is made using an intelligent grip 
point, the moved symbol becomes a child of the target symbol. 
When the parent symbol is selected, all grip points and connection points are displayed, the connection between 
the parent and child symbols is displayed as an intelligent grip point. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 887
If the parent symbol is moved, the child symbol is also moved. 
When the child symbol is selected, all grip points and connection points are displayed, the origin point of the 
child symbol is displayed as an intelligent grip point. 
The child symbol moves independently of the parent symbol and the connection is removed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 888
Refer to Move Objects using Grip Points and Move Objects for further information. 
Scale Objects 
Sets the overall size of an object. Objects can be scaled using the different methods. 
Change Size of Objects 
Sets the overall size of an object. 
The prompt Select objects to scale is displayed. 
Note: 
The prompt does not display if one or more objects are already selected. 
Click to identify the objects to scale and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify base point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the relative base point to which the 
objects are scaled. 
The prompt Specify scale factor or <#> is displayed. 
Input a value in the field and press Enter or click to identify scale factor. The further the cursor is moved from the 
base point, the larger the objects become. 
The objects are then scaled with respect to the specified base angle and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Copy 
Changes the size of objects and keeps a copy of the 
original objects.

information.

Sets the overall size of an object using a set reference distance. The prompt Select objects to scale is displayed. 
Note: 
The prompt does not display if one or more objects are already selected. 
Click to identify the objects to scale and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify base point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the relative base point to which the 
objects are scaled. 
The prompt Specify scale factor or <#> is displayed. 
Input R at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify reference length or <#mm> is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter or click to identify two points that define the reference 
length. 
The prompt Specify new length or is displayed. 
Press Enter to scale the objects using the specified reference length or input a dimensional value in the field and

Copy 
Changes the size of objects and keeps a copy of the 
original objects. 
Points 
Redefines the reference length points. 
Back 
Undoes the last action.

Rotates objects using a base point or around a base angle. Objects can be rotated using the different methods. 
Note: 
Objects can also be rotated by manipulating grips. Refer to Rotate Objects using Grip Points for further 
information.

The prompt Select objects to rotate is displayed. 
Note: 
The prompt does not display if one or more objects are already selected. 
Click to identify the objects to rotate and press Enter.

Input the array angle in the field. 
Alternatively, this can be specified 
on the drawing canvas. Refer to 
Specify Angle of Array on Drawing 
Canvas for further information. 
Preview 
Displays a preview of the array with 
the current settings. The Array 
window is closed and the preview 
displays on the drawing canvas. 
Press ESC to return to the Array 
window or Enter to create the 
array. 
Specify Row and Column Offsets on Drawing Canvas 
Click 
 to specify both offset distances on the drawing. 
The Array window is closed and the prompt Specify unit cell is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the start point of the unit cell. 
The prompt Other corner is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the other corner of the unit cell. 
The offset distances are the dimensions of the rectangle created with two specified corners of the unit cell. 
The Array window displays, the Column offset and Row offset fields are populated with the identified offset 
distances, by default. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 895
Specify Column Offset on Drawing Canvas 
Click 
 next to the Column Offset field to specify the column offset distance on the drawing canvas. 
The Array window is closed and the prompt Specify the distance between columns is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter to specify the distance between columns or click to 
identify the first point to specify the distance between columns. 
The prompt Specify the second point or <Change reference point> is displayed. 
Click to identify the second point to specify the distance between columns. 
The Array window displays and the Column offset field is populated with the column offset distance, by default. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Change reference point 
Modifies the existing offset reference point. 
Specify Row Offset on Drawing Canvas 
Click 
 next to the Row Offset field to specify the row offset distance on the drawing canvas. 
The Array window is closed and the prompt Specify the distance between rows is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter to specify the distance between rows or click to identify 
the first point to specify the distance between rows. 
The prompt Specify the second point or <Change reference point> is displayed. 
Click to identify the second point to specify the distance between rows. 
The Array window displays and the Row offset field is populated with the row offset distance, by default. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Change reference point 
Modifies the existing offset reference point. 
Specify Angle of Array on Drawing Canvas 
Click 
 next to the Angle of array field to specify the angle of array on the drawing canvas. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 896
The Array window is closed and the prompt Specify array angle is displayed. 
Input an angular value in the field and press Enter to specify the array angle or click to identify the first point to 
specify the array angle. 
The prompt Specify the second point is displayed. 
Click to identify the second point to specify the array angle. 
The Array window displays and the Angle of array field is populated with the array angle, by default. 
For example, if the following settings are applied to a selected rectangle: 
The result is the following array:

Create Polar Array 
Select the Polar tab to create a Polar Array. 
Method 
Select from the drop-down list to specify the array 
creation method. The selection determines the fields 
on the tab that are active. 
Select from: 
• Total number of items and angle to fill 
• Total number of items and angle between items 
• Angle to fill and angle between items 
Centre point 
Input coordinates in the field to specify the array 
center point. 
Alternatively, this can be specified on the drawing 
canvas. Refer to Specify Center Point on Drawing 
Canvas for further information. 
Total number of items 
Input the total number of items in the field to form 
the array. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 898
Angle to fill 
Input a value in the field to define the fill angle of 
array objects. 
Alternatively, this can be specified on the drawing 
canvas. Refer to Specify Angle to Fill on Drawing 
Canvas for further information. 
Angle between items 
Input a value in the field to define the angle between 
the array objects. 
Alternatively, this can be specified on the drawing 
canvas. Refer to Specify Angle between Array Objects 
on Drawing Canvas for further information. 
Rotate items when copied 
Select the check box to rotate array items around the 
center point. 
If the check box is cleared, a base point must be 
specified. 
Set to object's defaults 
Select the check box to rotate objects around the 
default base point. 
Clear the check box to define a base point to rotate 
the objects around. 
Base point 
Input the base point coordinates in the field to define 
a point to rotate the objects around. 
Alternatively, this can be specified on the drawing 
canvas. Refer to Specify Base Point on Drawing Canvas 
for further information. 
Preview 
Click to display a preview of the array on the drawing 
canvas with the current settings. The Array window is 
closed. 
Press ESC to return to the Array window, or Enter to 
create the array. 
Click OK to create the Array or Cancel to discard any inputs and close the Array window 
Specify Center Point on Drawing Canvas 
Click 
 next to the Centre point field to specify the array center point on the drawing canvas. 
The Array window is closed and the prompt Specify centre point of the array is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the center point of the array. 
The Array window displays and the Centre Point field is populated with the center point coordinates, by default. 
Specify Angle to Fill on Drawing Canvas 
Click 
 next to the Angle to fill field to specify the angle to fill with array objects on the drawing canvas,.

Specify Angle between Array Objects on Drawing Canvas 
Click 
 next to the Angle between items field to specify the angle between array objects on the drawing 
canvas. 
The Array window is closed and the prompt Specify second point is displayed. (the first point is the previously 
defined center point). 
Input an angular value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the second point to specify the angle. 
The Array window displays and the Angle between items field is populated with the angle value, by default. 
Specify Base Point on Drawing Canvas 
Click 
 next to the Base point field to specify the base point coordinates to rotate objects around. 
The Array window is closed and the prompt Specify the object's base point is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the base point. 
The Array window displays and the Base point field is populated with the base point coordinates, by default. 
For example, if the following settings are applied to the selected rectangle: 
The result is the following array: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 900
Stretch Objects 
Stretches objects using a box selection or polygon selection. 
Objects can be stretched using the different methods. 
Objects can also be stretched by manipulating grips. Refer to Stretch Objects using Endpoints and Stretch Objects 
using Midpoints for further information. 
Stretch Objects with Box Selection 
Stretches objects using a box selection. 
The prompt Select objects to stretch is displayed. 
Click to identify, or drag a rectangular fence around, the objects to stretch. 
The prompt Specify first corner or <Polygon> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the first corner of the box to enclose the 
objects to stretch. 
The prompt Specify other corner is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the opposite corner of the box. The grip 
points of objects within the box display as red. 
The prompt Specify first corner or <Basepoint> is displayed. 
Note: 
If required, more object grip points can be selected to stretch using multiple box selections. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 901
Press Enter to specify the basepoint position. 
The prompt Specify base point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the basepoint relative to which the grip 
points are stretched. 
The prompt Specify second base point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify a second basepoint to stretch objects 
towards, relative to the first basepoint. 
The objects are stretched and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Polygon 
Stretches objects using a polygon selection. Refer to 
Stretch Objects with Polygon Selection for further 
information. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Basepoint 
Specifies the basepoint relative to which the grip 
points are stretched. 
Displacement 
Stretches the selected objects in relation to the zero 
point of the drawing. The stretch position is 
determined by the position of the cursor in relation to 
the zero point of the user coordinate system. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 902
Stretch Objects with Polygon Selection 
Stretches objects using a polygon selection. 
The prompt Select objects to stretch is displayed. 
Click to identify, or drag a rectangular fence around, the objects to stretch. 
The prompt Specify first corner or <Polygon> is displayed. 
Input P at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify first polygon point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the first point of the polygon that 
encloses the objects to stretch. 
The prompt Specify next polygon point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the polygon. 
The prompt Specify next polygon point or <Close> is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the next point of the polygon. The grip 
points of objects within the polygon display as red. 
The prompt is repeated, allowing you to create multiple polygon points. 
Input C to end the polygon creation process. The grip points of objects within the polygon display as red. 
Note: 
If required, more object grip points can be selected to stretch using multiple polygon selections. 
You are then prompted to Specify first corner or <Basepoint> 
Press Enter to specify the basepoint position. 
The prompt Specify base point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the basepoint relative to which grip points 
are stretched. 
The prompt Specify second base point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify a second basepoint to stretch objects 
towards, relative to the first basepoint. 
The objects are stretched and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 903
Polygon 
Stretches objects using a polygon selection. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Close 
Ends the polygon creation process. 
Basepoint 
SpecifIes the basepoint relative to which the grip 
points are stretched. 
Displacement 
Stretches the selected objects in relation to the zero 
point of the drawing. The stretch position is 
determined by the position of the cursor in relation to 
the zero point of the user coordinate system. 
Explode and Merge Objects 
Explodes multiple objects or outline elements into the primitive components that the objects are composed of, 
or merges multiple elements and forms them into an outline element. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 904
Explode Objects 
Explodes multiple objects or outline elements into the primitive components that the objects are composed of, 
for example lines and arcs. 
The prompt Select objects to explode is displayed. 
Note: 
The prompt does not display if one or more objects are already selected. 
Click to identify the objects to explode and press Enter. 
The selected objects are exploded, for example, a rectangle element is exploded into four straight elements. 
Merge Objects 
Merges multiple elements (for example, straight or arc elements) and forms them into an outline element. 
Note: 
The merge functionality is not intended for use with table elements. 
The prompt Select objects to merge is displayed. 
Note: 
The prompt does not display if one or more objects are already selected. 
Click to identify the objects to merge and press Enter. 
You are prompted to Select option <Finish> 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Close 
Links the two endpoints of the merged object with an 
automatically drawn line and ends the command. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 905
Open 
Merges the objects, the two endpoints of the merged 
object are unchanged. 
Change Length of Objects 
Sets the length of an object. 
Change Length Dynamically 
Sets the length of an object dynamically. 
The prompt Select object to change or is displayed. 
Click to identify the object to change. The object is selected at the end point nearest to the selection point. 
The prompt Specify new end point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the end point position. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple objects can be lengthened. 
Press ESC to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Delta 
Changes the length of objects with a specified delta 
length. Refer to Change Length with Specified Delta 
Length for further information. 
Percent 
Changes the length of objects with a specified 
percentage. Refer to Change Length with Specified 
Percentage for further information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 906
Total 
Changes the length of objects with a specified total 
length. Refer to Change Length with Specified Total 
Length for further information. 
Length 
Displays the length of the currently selected object on 
the status bar. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Change Length with Specified Delta Length 
Sets the length of an object using a specified delta length. 
The prompt Select object to change or is displayed. 
Input E at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Enter delta length or <#mm> is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter to specify the distance to increase or decrease the object 
length (input a negative number to decrease the object length). 
The prompt Select object to change or is displayed. 
Click to identify the object to change, the object length is modified at the end nearest to the selection point. 
The prompt Select object to change or <Finish> is displayed. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple objects can be lengthened. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Angle 
Changes the length of objects with a specified angle 
length. 
Delta 
Changes the length of objects with a specified delta 
length. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Changes the length of objects with a specified 
percentage. Refer to Change Length with Specified 
Percentage for further information.

Changes the length of objects with a specified total 
length. Refer to Change Length with Specified Total 
Length for further information. 
Dynamic 
Changes the length of objects dynamically. Refer to 
Change Length Dynamically for further information. 
Length

Sets the length of an object using a specified percentage.

Input P at the prompt or press the down arrow and select Percent.

100 decreases the object length, a value above 100 increases the object length.

The prompt is repeated, multiple objects can be lengthened.

Trim and Extend Objects 
Trims or extends objects to a specified point. 
Trim Objects 
Trims an object to a specified point. Objects can be trimmed using the various methods. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Trim Objects to Intersecting Objects 
Trims an object to an intersecting object. 
The prompt Select borders for trim is displayed. 
Click to identify the objects to trim to and press Enter. 
The prompt Select entity to trim or <Finish> is displayed. 
Click to identify the objects to trim. 
The selected objects are trimmed. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple objects can be trimmed. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Crossing 
Selects objects using a selection box. 
Edge 
Trims objects to extrapolated edges. Refer to Trim 
Objects to Extrapolated Edges for further information. 
Delete 
Deletes an object. 
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Finish 
Ends the command.

Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Click to identify the objects to trim to and press Enter.

The prompt Enter an implied edge extension mode <Extend> is displayed. 
Input E at the prompt or Enter. 
The prompt Select entity to trim or <Finish> is displayed. 
Click to identify the objects to trim. The selected objects are trimmed. 
The prompt is repeated, multiple objects can be trimmed. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Crossing 
Selects objects using a selection box. 
Edge 
Trims objects to extrapolated edges.

Deletes an object.

Trims objects to extrapolated edges.

Fillet and Chamfer Objects 
Applies a fillet or a chamfer to objects. 
Fillet Objects 
Applies a fillet to objects. Objects can be filleted using various methods. 
Fillet Objects 
Applies a fillet to two selected objects. 
The prompt Select option: <Select object> is displayed. 
Input S at the prompt and Press Enter. 
The prompt Select object is displayed. 
Click to identify the first object. 
The prompt Select object is displayed. 
Click to identify the second object. 
The fillet is created and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 915
Select object 
Starts the fillet process. 
Multiple 
Fillets multiple objects. Refer to Fillet Multiple Objects 
for further information. 
Fillet Multiple Objects 
Applies a fillet to a number of selected objects. 
The prompt Select option: <Select object> is displayed. 
Input M at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Select option: <Select object> is displayed. 
Input S at the prompt and Press Enter. 
The prompt Select object is displayed. 
Click to identify the first object. 
The prompt Select object is displayed. 
Click to identify the second object. The fillet is created. 
The prompt Select option: <Select object> is displayed. 
The process can be repeated to fillet more objects. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Select object 
Starts the fillet process. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Back

Click to identify the second object. 
The fillet is created and the command ends automatically.

Select object 
Starts the fillet process. 
Radius 
Sets the fillet arc radius. Refer to Set Fillet Arc Radius 
for further information. 
Trim 
Sets the trim options. Refer to Turn Trim On/Off for 
further information. 
Multiple 
Fillets multiple objects. 
Back 
Undoes the last action.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

The prompt Specify fillet radius <#mm> is displayed.

Objects with Arc Radius for further information. 
Turn Trim On/Off 
Sets the trimming options for fillets. 
The prompt Select option: <Select object> is displayed. 
Input T at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Select option: <Trim> is displayed. 
Input T at the prompt and press Enter to trim fillet ends. 
Alternatively, Input N at the prompt and press Enter to omit trimming from fillet ends. 
The selected option is used for all subsequent filleting until changed. 
The process can be repeated to fillet more objects. Refer to Fillet Objects with Arc Radius or Fillet Multiple 
Objects with Arc Radius for further information. 
Chamfer Objects 
Applies a chamfer to objects. Objects can be chamfered using various methods. 
Chamfer Objects 
Applies a chamfer to two selected objects. 
The prompt Select option: <Select object> is displayed. 
Input S at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt select first object is displayed. 
Click to identify the first object (which can be a line or a construction line). 
The prompt Select second line is displayed. 
Click to identify the second line. 
The objects are chamfered and the command ends automatically. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available.

Select an object and begin the chamfer process. 
Distance 
Sets the chamfer distance. Refer to Set Chamfer 
Distance for further information. 
Angle 
Sets the chamfer angle. Refer to Set Chamfer Angle for 
further information. 
Trim 
Turns trimming on or off. Refer to Turn Trim On/Off for 
further information. 
Method 
Selects the chamfer method. Refer to Select Chamfer 
Method for further information. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
Set Chamfer Distance 
Sets the chamfer distance measured from the endpoint of a selected edge 
The prompt Select option: <Select object> is displayed. 
Input D at the prompt or press the down arrow key and select Distance. 
The prompt Specify first chamfer distance <#mm> is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter to specify the first chamfer distance. 
The prompt Specify second chamfer distance <#mm> is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter to specify the second chamfer distance. 
The distances are used for all subsequent chamfers until changed. 
The process can be repeated to chamfer more objects. Refer to Chamfer Objects or Chamfer Multiple Objects for 
further information. 
Set Chamfer Angle 
Sets the chamfer angle using a chamfer length and angle measurement. 
The prompt Select option: <Select object> is displayed. 
Input A at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Specify chamfer length on the first line <#mm> is displayed. 
Input a dimensional value in the field and press Enter to specify the chamfer length. 
The prompt Specify chamfer angle from the first line is displayed. 
Input an angular value in the field and press Enter to specify the chamfer angle. 
The distance and angle are used for all subsequent chamfers until changed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 921
The process can be repeated to chamfer more objects. Refer to Chamfer Objects or Chamfer Multiple Objects for 
further information. 
Select Chamfer Method 
Sets the default chamfer method. 
The prompt Select option: <Select object> is displayed. 
Input E at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Select option <Distance / Angle> is displayed. 
Input D at the prompt to set the chamfer method as distance. Refer to Set Chamfer Distance for further 
information. 
Alternatively, input A at the prompt to set the chamfer method as angle. Refer to Set Chamfer Angle for further 
information. 
The process can be repeated to chamfer more objects. Refer to Chamfer Objects or Chamfer Multiple Objects for 
further information. 
Turn Trim On/Off 
Sets the trimming options for chamfered ends. 
The prompt Select option: <Select object> is displayed. 
Input T at the prompt and press Enter. 
The prompt Select option <Trim / No Trim> is displayed. 
Input T at the prompt and press Enter to trim chamfer ends. 
Alternatively, input N at the prompt and press Enter to omit trimming from chamfered ends. 
Objects can then be chamfered. Refer to Chamfer Objects or Chamfer Multiple Objects for further information. 
Remove from Drawing 
Removes the currently selected object(s) from the drawing. 
When the remove option is selected, a Confirm window is displayed by default, asking you to confirm the 
remove by clicking OK or to cancel the remove by clicking Cancel. 
If an object is removed from the drawing canvas, all attached labels are also removed. 
Delete Selection 
Deletes the currently selected object(s), duplicated objects and objects associated with the same tagged item 
from the drawing. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 922
Alternatively, press the Delete key to delete the currently selected object(s) from the drawing. 
Note: 
Tagged items associated with the selected object(s) on the drawing canvas are deleted from the project, or 
logically deleted if the option is on. 
When the delete option is selected, a Confirm window is displayed by default, asking you to confirm the deletion 
by clicking OK or to cancel the deletion by clicking Cancel. 
Protect Drawing 
Sets the read only status of a drawing. 
A protected drawing is read only and cannot be modified, drawing objects can still be modified using the 
associated grid. Attribute changes made to drawing objects are updated regardless of the protection status. 
An unprotected drawing can be modified. By default, generated drawings are protected but can be made 
unprotected if modification is required. For example, loop drawings or hookup drawings. 
By default, manually created drawings are unprotected. 
All issued drawings are read only, the protection status of issued drawings cannot be modified. 
Measure Distance between Two Points 
Measures the distance between two points. 
The prompt Specify the first point is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the point to measure from. 
The prompt Specify the second point or is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the point to measure to. 
The context editor displays the distance between the two points. 
Input F at the prompt and press Enter to end the command. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
You can press the Spacebar to cancel the command. Press the Spacebar again to activate a new measure 
distance process. 
You are prompted to Specify the first point 
Alternatively, you can activate the measure distance functionality by using an in canvas command. Type DI and 
press Enter. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 923
Back 
Undoes the last action. 
Display axis 
Press the down arrow key and select Display axis to 
turn the axes display on or off on the drawing canvas. 
Change unit 
Press the down arrow key and select Change unit to 
display the available units of measure. 
Copy to clipboard 
Press the down arrow key and select Copy to 
clipboard to copy the position value to the clipboard 
ready for a later paste operation. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
Current units 
Press the down arrow key and select Current units to 
display the measured distance in the current default 
units. 
Mm 
Press the down arrow key and select Mm to display 
the measured distance in millimetres. 
Cm 
Press the down arrow key and select Cm to display the 
measured distance in centimeters. 
Metre 
Press the down arrow key and select Metre to display 
the measured distance in metres. 
Km 
Press the down arrow key and select Km to display the 
measured distance in kilometers. 
Thou 
Press the down arrow key and select Thou to display 
the measured distance in thou. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 924
Finch 
Press the down arrow key and select Finch to display 
the measured distance in feet and inches. 
Inch 
Press the down arrow key and select Inch to display 
the measured distance in inches. 
Ft 
Press the down arrow key and select Ft to display the 
measured distance in feet. 
Yd 
Press the down arrow key and select Yd to display the 
measured distance in yards. 
Mile 
Press the down arrow key and select Mile to display 
the measured distance in miles. 
Change Standard 
Changes the standard of symbols on the current drawing. 
Note: 
Symbols standards are defined using the Configuration module and must be configured to enable the 
functionality. 
Use the Change Standard window to select the associated standard. 
Select standard 
Use the drop-down list to select the associated standard. The display of all drawing symbols is modified to 
represent the selected standard. 
The example displays a circuit breaker and a motor displayed using two different standards. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 925
Highlight Selection 
Highlights the currently selected objects on the drawing. The highlighted objects display using a single color and 
a thick line type. 
To highlight the currently selected objects on the drawing: 
1. Right-click on the drawing canvas to display the PowerWheel™. 
2. Click the Annotate tile. 
3. Click the Highlight tile. 
To clear the highlight from objects on the drawing. On the Drawings tab, in the Tools group, click Clear Highlight. 
Refer to PowerWheel™ for further information. 
Add Image, Revision Block, Engineering Table, Database View Table or CAD Overlay to 
Drawing 
Various image types can be added to drawings. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 926
Add Image to Drawing 
Adds an image to a drawing. 
On the Drawings tab, in the Insert group, click Image and select Image from the drop-down menu to display the 
Open window. 
Select the required image from the window and click Open 
The prompt Specify the centre point or <By corner and scale> is displayed. 
A preview of the image displays at the cursor. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the location for the center of the image. 
The prompt enter the Scale <1> is displayed. 
Input a value in the field and press Enter to define the scale factor. Alternatively, move the cursor away from the 
insertion point to increase the size of the image or move the cursor closer to the insertion point to decrease the 
size of the image. Click to define the scale factor. 
The prompt enter the Rotation angle <0deg> is displayed. 
Input an angular value in the field and press Enter to define the rotation angle. Alternatively, move the cursor 
around the centre of the image to visually select the required angle. A ghosted preview of the image rotates to 
reflect the angle. Click to define the rotation angle. 
The image is added to the drawing. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
By corner and scale 
Inserts an image with a specified corner point. 
The prompt Specify the left down corner or is 
displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or 
click to identify the location for the bottom-left of the 
image. 
The image can then be scaled, rotated and positioned. 
Add Revision Block to Drawing 
Adds a revision block to a drawing. 
On the Drawings tab, in the Insert group, click Image and select Revision Block from the drop-down menu to 
display the Select Revision Block window. 
Use the Select Revision Block window to select and add the revision block. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 927
The Select Revision Block window displays the name, the number of rows and the number of columns for each 
revision block that is available for selection. 
The revision block is added to the drawing. 
Add Engineering Table to Drawing 
Adds an intelligent engineering table to a drawing populated with data extracted from items on the drawing 
canvas. 
On the Drawings tab, in the Insert group, click Image and select Engineering Table from the drop-down menu to 
display the Insert Engineering Table window. 
Use the Insert Engineering Table window to define the layout and the items that are displayed in the 
engineering table. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 928
Font Size 
Specifies the font size for the engineering table entries. 
Font Color 
Specifies the font color for the engineering table entries. 
Border Color 
Specifies the color for the engineering table borders. 
Data Source 
Select the Selected symbols radio button to display the selected items on the drawing canvas in the engineering 
table grid. 
Select the Sheet radio button to include all items on the sheet in the engineering table grid. 
Select the Drawing radio button to include all items on the drawing in the engineering table grid. 
Synchronize with Drawing Content 
Select the check box to update the table when the content of the drawing canvas is updated. For example, 
inserted rows for inserted items and deleted rows for deleted items. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 929
Deselect the check box to leave the table unchanged when the content of the drawing canvas is updated. 
Class Filter 
Defines class filters for the engineering table. 
Click Add to add a class filter using the Select Class window. The filtered classes are displayed in the engineering 
table grid. 
Click Remove to remove the currently selected class filter. 
Use 
Defines the drawing items that are included in the engineering table. 
Select the check boxes to include items in the engineering table. 
Deselect the check boxes to exclude items from the engineering table. 
Engineering Table Columns 
Defines the column layout and intelligent text that is included in the engineering table. 
By default, the default name column is added to the engineering table. 
Input intelligent text in the column heading field to define the column content. Any attribute class from the 
conceptual model can be used. For example, @Clone References@. 
By default, a new column is added to the engineering table grid when a column heading is populated. 
Click and hold a column header and drag it to a new position between two other columns to change the column 
order. 
Click [X] in the column header to delete a column. 
The engineering table can be modified using the Text Editor window, Table tab,grip points or properties. Refer to 
Create Text, Table Tab, Modify Intelligent Table using Grip Points and Modify Intelligent Table Properties for 
further information. 
Add Database View Table to Drawing 
Adds an intelligent table to a drawing populated with data extracted from a database view. 
On the Drawings tab, in the Insert group, click Image and select DbView Table from the drop-down menu to 
display the Data Source Wizard. 
Use the Data Source Wizard to define the items that are displayed in the table. The functionality available from 
the Data Source Wizard is explained, refer to Define New Data Source for further information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 930
The engineering table can be modified using the Text Editor window, Table tab, grip points or properties. Refer to 
Create Text, Table Tab, Modify Intelligent Table using Grip Points and Modify Intelligent Table Properties for 
further information. 
Table Tab 
Intelligent tables are formatted using the Table tab. The tab displays when an intelligent table is selected. 
Table group 
Edit 
Displays the Edit engineering table 
window. Refer to Add Engineering 
Table to Drawing for further 
information. 
Note: 
The functionality is not available if 
the currently selected intelligent 
table is populated with data 
extracted from a database view. 
Selection mode 
Turns selection mode on and off.

Selection mode allows you to select 
cells and invoke other table 
operations. 
Selected cells are indicated with a 
green circle. 
Rows group 
Insert above 
Click to insert a row above the 
currently selected cell. If multiple 
cells are selected, the row is 
inserted above the cell at the top of 
the selection. 
Insert below 
Click to insert a row below the 
currently selected cell. If multiple 
cells are selected, the row is 
inserted below the cell at the 
bottom of the selection. 
Remove row(s) 
Click to remove all rows in the 
current cell selection. 
Columns Group 
Insert to the left 
Click to insert a column to the left 
of the currently selected cell. If 
multiple cells are selected, the 
column is inserted to the left of the 
cell furthest to the left of the 
selection. 
Insert to the right 
Click to insert a column to the right 
of the currently selected cell. If 
multiple cells are selected, the 
column is inserted to the right of 
the cell furthest to the right of the 
selection. 
Remove column(s) 
Click to remove all columns in the 
current cell selection. 
Cells group

Merge cells 
Click to merge the currently 
selected cells into a single cell. 
Split cells 
Click to split the currently selected 
cell into multiple cells. 
Data group 
Full regenerate 
If the table is associated with a data 
source, click to regenerate the 
table. 
Note: 
Any data not present in the data 
source is overwritten. Any custom 
data added to the intelligent table 
is overwritten. 
Modify Intelligent Table using Grip Points 
When an intelligent table is selected, the grip points display in blue. You can manipulate a grip point to modify 
the appearance of an intelligent table. 
When a grip point is selected, the display colour changes to red. 
Move Intelligent Table using Grip Points 
Moves an intelligent table using a grip point that displays as a blue square. 
To move an intelligent table, click the square grip point. 
The prompt Specify stretch point is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the grip point position. 
The intelligent table is moved and the command ends automatically. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 933
Rotate Intelligent Table using Grip Points 
Rotates an intelligent table using a grip point that displays as a blue double-headed arrow. 
To rotate an intelligent table, click the double-headed arrow grip point. 
The prompt Specify angle is displayed. 
Input an angular value in the field and press Enter or click to identify the table position. 
The intelligent table is rotated and the command ends automatically. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 934
Stretch Intelligent Table using Grip Points 
To stretch an intelligent table, click the triangle grip point. 
The prompt Specify stretch point is displayed. 
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the grip point position. 
The intelligent table is stretched and the command ends automatically. 
Stretch Intelligent Table Cells using Grip Points 
Stretches an intelligent table cell using a grip point that displays as a blue diamond. 
To stretch an intelligent table cell, click the diamond grip point. 
The prompt Specify stretch point is displayed. 
Input a value in the dimension field and press Enter or click to identify the grip point position. 
The intelligent table cell is stretched and the command ends automatically.

Alignment 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
table text alignment from the table visual style. 
Select Center from the drop-down list to center align 
the table text. 
Select Bottom from the drop-down list to bottom align 
the table text. 
Select Top from the drop-down list to top align the 
table text. 
Letter height 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
table text letter height from the table visual style. 
Input a value in the field or select from the drop-down 
list to define the letter height for the table text. 
Char spacing 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
table text character spacing from the table visual style. 
Input a value in the field or select from the drop-down 
list to define the character spacing for the table text. 
Font 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
table text font from the table visual style. 
Select from the drop-down list to define the font for 
the table text. 
Justification 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
table text justification from the table visual style. 
Select Left from the drop-down list to left justify the 
table text. 
Select Right from the drop-down list to right justify 
the table text. 
Select Center from the drop-down list to center justify 
the table text. 
Line spacing 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
table text line spacing from the table visual style. 
Input a value in the field or select from the drop-down 
list to define the vertical line spacing for the table text. 
Color 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
table text color from the table visual style. 
Select By Layer from the drop-down list to inherit the 
table text color from the layer. 
Select from the drop-down list to define the color of 
the table text. 
Fill color 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the

Visual style

Row color 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the

Select By Layer from the drop-down list to inherit the

Select from the drop-down list to define the row

Row line type 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
row inside line type from the table visual style. 
Select By Layer from the drop-down list to inherit the 
row inside line type from the layer. 
Select from the drop-down list to define the row

Expansion direction 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
expansion direction from the table visual style. 
Select Vertical from the drop-down list to add new 
rows to the bottom of the table and new columns to 
the right of the table. The base point of the table is 
positioned at the top-left of the table. 
Select Horizontal from the drop-down list to add new 
rows to the right of the table and new columns to the 
top of the table. The base point of the table is 
positioned at the bottom-left of the table. 
Resizing mode 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
resizing mode from the table visual style. 
Select Fill from the drop-down list to preserve the 
table height and width when rows or columns are 
added, deleted or modified. If there is no room to 
insert extra rows or columns, the table height/width is 
extended. 
Select Extend from the drop-down list to extend or 
reduce the table height and width when rows or 
columns are added, deleted or modified. 
Max. records count 
Input a value to define the maximum number of 
records displayed. The functionality only applies to 
intelligent tables connected to a data source 
definition. 
Row height adjustment 
Select By Style from the drop-down list to inherit the 
row height adjustment from the table visual style. 
Select On from the drop-down list to adjust the row 
height to the Column length value, if the cell text does 
not exceed the value. 
Select Off from the drop-down list to turn off row

height adjustment. 
Header visibility 
Select On from the drop-down list to include a header 
row in the table.

row from the table. 
Column count 
Input a value to modify the number of columns in the 
table. 
Row count 
Input a value to modify the number of rows in the

Row length

Column length
