Add CAD Overlay to Drawing 
Adds a CAD image to a drawing as an overlay. For example a .DXF or .DWG format image. The CAD overlay can be 
positioned on the drawing canvas and used as a template which can be traced over with engineering symbols 
and objects. 
On the Drawings tab, in the Insert group, click Image and select CAD from the drop-down menu to display the 
Open window. 
Select the required CAD image from the window and click Open. 
A progress bar displays which tracks the progress of the added CAD image. 
The prompt Specify the insertion point or <Finish> is displayed. 
A preview of the CAD image displays at the cursor. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 940
Input values in the dimension fields and press Enter or click to identify the location for the base point of the CAD 
image. 
The CAD image is added to the drawing. 
Press ESC to cancel the command. Additional functionality is also available. 
Scale 
Specifies a scale for the CAD image. 
The prompt Specify X scale <1> is displayed. 
Input a value in the field and press Enter to define the 
scale factor for the X dimension. 
A value of less than 1 decreases the width of the CAD 
image. For example, to reduce the width of the CAD 
image by half, input 0.5 at the prompt. 
The prompt Specify Y scale <1> is displayed. 
Input a value in the field and press Enter to define the 
scale factor for the Y dimension. 
Rotation 
Specifies a rotation angle for the CAD image. 
The prompt Specify rotation angle or <#deg> is 
displayed. 
Input an angular value in the field and press Enter or 
click to identify the rotation angle. 
Finish 
Ends the command. 
The new CAD image overlay is added to the Drawings Explorer. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 941
Overlays can be dragged and dropped from the Drawings Explorer onto the drawing canvas. 
Compare Revisions and Add Revision Clouds 
Compares two different versions of issued drawings and highlights the differences with revision clouds. 
Use the Compare Revisions window to define the drawing revisions for comparison. 
The two revisions for comparison must first be selected. 
Select a revision state on the Base revision list. 
Select a revision state on the Target revision list. 
To compare the selected revisions, click Select. 
The comparison then takes place: 
• Objects that have been created, deleted or modified since the base revision are surrounded by a red cloud. 
• Intelligent tables that have been created, deleted or modified since the base revision are surrounded by a 
red cloud. 
• Changes to symbol or label attribute values using intelligent text, including reclassified drawing objects, are 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 942
also surrounded by a red cloud. 
The drawing revision number in which each change was made is indicated with a triangle. 
In the example, objects have been created, deleted and moved. Each modification is surrounded by a red cloud. 
Revision clouds can be modified until the drawing is issued and becomes read only. Refer to Modify Outline Type 
for further information. 
Export Drawings 
Exports a drawing to a PDF file, Plot File, CAD format or a Raster image. A batch export of multiple drawings can 
be performed using a grid. 
If a drawing contains symbols that have not been updated due to element reclassification, or a drawing symbol 
has been modified or deleted by another user, either from the grid or the database, an error message is 
displayed indicating that the export process has been aborted. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 943
Detailed error information is displayed in the Messages window. 
Use the Export drawing window to define the drawings for export, the file format, the export directory and the 
file export settings. 
Export Settings 
Use the Export tab to define the exported drawings and sheets, the export format and the destination directory 
for the exported files. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 944
Drawing Type 
Use the drop-down list to specify the available drawing types for export. 
If the check box is selected, the drawing type is available for export and displays in the drawings grid. 
If the check box is not selected, the drawing type is not available for export and does not display in the drawings 
grid. 
Select all 
Selects all drawing types, drawings and sheets in the drawings grid. 
Clear selection 
Deselects all drawing types, drawings and sheets in the drawings grid. 
Drawings grid 
Displays the drawing types, drawings and sheets that are available for export. The grid display can be filtered by 
the drawing name, border size, revision, revision date and file name using the drop down lists. 
The check box adjacent to the drawing name column defines the drawing types, drawings and sheets that are 
exported. 
If the check box is selected, the drawing type, drawing or sheet is exported. 
If the check box is not selected, the drawing type, drawing or sheet is not exported. 
Export to 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 945
Use the drop-down list to define the export format. 
PDF 
Exports the drawings to PDF format. 
Plot File 
Exports the drawings to PLT format. 
CAD Formats 
Exports the drawings to a CAD format. For example, 
DWG format. 
Raster image 
Exports the drawings to a raster format. For example, 
BMP format. 
Destination directory 
Defines the location for the exported drawings. Type an explicit location in the field or click Browse. 
Use the standard Browse for Folder window to specify the folder to which the sheets are exported. 
Export selected 
Exports the drawings to the specified file format and location. 
Note: 
The CAD settings or rasterized settings must be saved before the drawing is exported. 
CAD Settings 
Use the CAD Settings tab to define the settings for sheets exported to CAD format. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 946
AutoCAD file version 
Use the drop-down list to specify the AutoCAD version with which the exported sheets are compatible. 
Output format 
Use the drop-down list to define the exported file type. The available output formats are: 
• DWG 
• DXF 
• DGN 
Scale 
Defines a scale factor for the exported file. A value of 1.2 doubles the size of the exported sheets. 
Convert backing sheet to block 
Defines the forming of backing sheets into blocks. 
If the check box is selected, the backing sheets are formed into blocks. 
If the check box is not selected, the backing sheets are not formed into blocks. 
Convert fills to block 
Defines the forming of fills into blocks.

If the check box is selected, the fills are formed into blocks.

Show sheet frame

If the check box is selected, the sheets are exported with frames. 
If the check box is not selected, the sheets are not exported with frames. 
Cutmarks 
Defines the export of sheets with cutmarks. 
If the check box is selected, the sheets are exported with cutmarks. 
If the check box is not selected, the sheets are not exported with cutmarks. 
Border width 
Defines the border width for the exported file. 
Export with black background 
Defines the export of sheets with a black background. 
If the check box is selected, a black background is exported. 
If the check box is not selected, a black background is not exported. 
Rasterized Settings 
Use the Rasterized Settings tab to define the settings for sheets exported to a rasterized (bitmap) format.

Paper Size

Center Plot

If the check box is selected, the exported sheets are centered on the paper. 
If the check box is not selected, the exported sheets are not centered on the paper. 
Fit to Paper 
Defines the scale of the exported sheets in relation to the selected page size. 
If the check box is selected, the exported sheets are scaled to fit the page size. 
If the check box is not selected, the exported sheets are not scaled to fit the page size. 
Is Landscape 
Defines the exported page orientation. 
If the check box is selected, the exported page orientation is landscape. 
If the check box is not selected, the exported page orientation is portrait. 
Output File Extension (rasterized export) 
Use the drop-down list to define the exported file type. The available output formats are:

• PNG - portable network graphics file.

Print Drawings 
Prints a drawing to a specified printer. A batch print of multiple drawings can be performed using a grid. 
Functionality is also available to print a drawing region. 
Print Drawing 
Use the Printing window to preview and print specified drawings and to define the print settings.

Printer 
Use the drop-down list to select the printer or plotter. The default printing device is pre-selected. 
Paper size 
Use the drop-down list to define the printed page size. 
Orientation 
Defines the printed page orientation. 
If the Landscape radio button is selected, the printed page orientation is landscape. 
If the Portrait radio button is selected, the printed page orientation is portrait. 
Fit to paper 
Defines the scale of the printed sheets in relation to the selected page size. 
If the check box is selected, the printed sheets are scaled to fit the page size. 
If the check box is not selected, the printed sheets are not scaled to fit the page size.

Show view frames 
Defines the printing of view frames. 
If the check box is selected, the sheets are printed with view frames. 
If the check box is not selected, the sheets are not printed with view frames. 
Center the print 
Defines the position of the printed sheets on the paper. 
If the check box is selected, the printed sheets are centered on the paper. 
If the check box is not selected, the printed sheets are not centered on the paper. 
Scale lineweight 
Defines the scaling of line weights on the printed output. 
If the check box is selected, line weights are scaled on the printed output. 
If the check box is not selected, line weights are not scaled on the printed output. 
Print area 
Defines a printed area on the sheet using dimension values. 
If the check box is selected, a print area can be defined. The Center X position, Center Y poisson, Width and 
Height options become available. 
If the check box is not selected, a print area cannot be defined. 
Centre X position 
Defines the center of the printed area on the X axis. 
Centre Y position 
Defines the center of the printed area on the Y axis. 
Width 
Defines the width of the printed area. 
Height 
Defines the height of the printed area. 
Margin Left 
Defines the left margin for the printed output. 
Margin Top 
Defines the top margin for the printed output. 
Margin Bottom 
Defines the bottom margin for the printed output. 
Margin Right 
Defines the right margin for the printed output. 
Drawings 
Displays the drawings that are available for print. 
The check box adjacent to the drawing name defines the drawings that are printed. 
If the check box is selected, the drawing is printed. 
If the check box is not selected, the drawing is not printed.

Drawing Type

Select all

Drawings grid

Admin 
Note: 
The Admin tab is only displayed to members of the TAGSADMIN team. Refer to the Administration User Guide for

Refer to Database Explorer, Database Views Editor, Command Window, Design Reuse and Datasheet 
Configuration for further information. 
Enable Tag Properties 
Enables or disables tag properties functionality. 
If the check box is selected, tag properties functionality is enabled. 
If the check box is not selected, tag properties functionality is disabled. 
Refer to Tag Properties for further information. 
Bold Claimed Elements 
Used to enable or disable claimed elements. 
If the check box is selected, claimed elements is enabled. 
If the check box is not selected, claimed elements is disabled. 
Refer to Manage Claims for further information. 
Enable Logical Delete 
Enables or disables the functionality that displays or hides logically deleted records. 
If the check box is selected, the user interface displays or hides logically deleted records. The delete functionality 
in Engineer updates the EXCLEL flag. 
If the check box is not selected, all records are displayed, even if they have been logically deleted. The value of 
the EXCLEL flag is disregarded. 
Note: 
If enable logical delete is disabled, there is no opportunity to undelete a record. Deleted records are removed 
from the database. 
Memory Cache 
The memory cache functionality is particularly intended for use with grids within the Engineer module and 
reduces the number of times that the software needs to access the database for displaying data. 
Note: 
Turning on the memory cache provides a level of performance improvement in grids. The exact amount of 
performance improvement depends upon the specific grid and how it is configured, what data is being displayed 
and what performance the local network gives when accessing the database files.

and staging options for the exported file.

active.

when the export is processed.

Load Calculations in AVEVA Engineering 
The following section explains the how the AVEVA Engineering load calculations work, including all the equations 
used and how load calculation flags can be used. A definition of terms can be found in the glossary at the end of 
the section. 
Note: 
In this section, default field names are used. These default names are created for a new class e.g., Powered 
Equipment, when it is decorated (mapped) with the Power Calculations Vocab class. All the fields in this section 
can be configured at project level to have different names, but they will always retain their attribute level 
decoration. 
Refer to Load Calculations, Load Calculation Flags and Glossary for further information. 
Load Calculations 
Refer to Voltage, Phase, Frequency, Loading Factor, Power Consumed by Tags, Power Flowing through Tag 
Objects, Power Consumed by Tag Objects based on combinations of Utilizations Values and Power Consumed by 
Tag Objects based on ignoring Diversity Factors for further information. 
Voltage, Phase, Frequency 
This data is selected by the designer from the project voltage list created for the project. 
The list will include all valid combinations of voltage, voltage type, frequency and phase identifiers. This includes 
DC power calculations. 
Loading Factor 
Loading Factor has unit value of either Ratio or %. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 958
This unit value is selected for a project via the grid configuration facility in the Configuration module. 
Power Consumed by Tags 
Refer to Nameplate Power and Absorbed Power for further information. 
Nameplate Power 
Nameplate Power 
Nameplate Power has a unit value of Power kW. 
Other unit options such as hp can be added to the project via the grid configuration facility in the Configuration 
module. 
Nameplate Efficiency 
Nameplate Efficiency has a unit value of Ratio. 
Nameplate Power Factor 
Nameplate Power Factor has a unit value between 0 and 1. 
Nameplate Power Factor Unit 
Nameplate Power Factor Unit can have values of LEAD and LAG. 
• When value is set as LAG then Nameplate Reactive Power is positive. 
• When value is set as LEAD then Nameplate Reactive Power is negative. 
• If Nameplate Power Factor =1 then Nameplate Power Factor Unit will be set to null. 
Nameplate Electrical Power 
Nameplate Electrical Power has a unit value of Power kW. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
Nameplate Electrical Power = Nameplate Power / Nameplate Efficiency 
Nameplate Reactive Power 
Nameplate Reactive Power has a unit value of Reactive Power kVAr. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
Nameplate Reactive Power = Nameplate Electrical Power * √ ( (1- Nameplate Power Factor2) / Nameplate Power 
Factor2) 
Note: 
Positive Nameplate Reactive Power values are defined as LAG. 
Negative Nameplate Reactive Power values are defined as LEAD. 
Nameplate Apparent Power 
Nameplate Apparent Power has a unit value of Power kVA. 
Other unit options can be added to the project via the grid configuration facility in the Configuration module. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
Nameplate Apparent Power = √ (Nameplate Electrical Power2 + Nameplate Reactive Power2) 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 959
Nameplate Full Load Current 
Nameplate Full Load Current has a unit value of Current A. 
The Nameplate Full Load Current calculations below depend on the number of phases and therefore on the 
voltage type. 
The equation for voltage is: 
Line Voltage = Phase Voltage * √3 
The equation for a single phase load i.e. Voltage Type = S, is: 
Nameplate Full Load Current = (Nameplate Apparent Power * 1000) / Voltage (Phase voltage) 
The equation for a 2 phase load i.e. Voltage Type = L, is: 
Nameplate Full Load Current = (Nameplate Apparent Power * 1000) / Voltage (Line voltage) 
The equation for a 3 phase load i.e. Voltage Type = TN, T, is: 
Nameplate Full Load Current = (Nameplate Apparent Power * 1000) / (Voltage (Line voltage) * √3) 
DC Calculations 
DC calculations use the same equations as above except that Nameplate Power Factor = 0. This means 
Nameplate Power Factor Unit is null. 
Calculation are as follows: 
Nameplate Electrical Power = (Nameplate Power / Nameplate Efficiency) 
Nameplate Reactive Power = 0 
Nameplate Apparent Power = Nameplate Electrical Power 
Nameplate Full Load Current = (Nameplate Electrical Power * 1000) / Voltage 
Absorbed Power 
Absorbed Power has a unit value of kW. 
Other unit options such as hp can be added to the project via the grid configuration facility in the Configuration 
module. 
Absorbed Power is calculated if the Loading Factor is set. 
The equation is: 
Absorbed Power = (Nameplate Power * Loading Factor) 
Absorbed Efficiency 
Absorbed Efficiency has a unit value of Ratio. 
Absorbed Power Factor 
Absorbed Power Factor has a unit value between 0 and 1. 
Absorbed Power Factor Unit 
Absorbed Power Factor Unit can have values of LEAD and LAG. 
• When value is set as LAG then Absorbed Reactive Power is positive. 
• When value is set as LEAD then Absorbed Reactive Power is negative. 
• If Absorbed Power Factor =1 then Absorbed Power Factor Unit will be set to null. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 960
Absorbed Electrical Power 
Absorbed Electrical Power has a unit value of Power kW. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
Absorbed Electrical Power = Absorbed Power / Absorbed Efficiency 
Absorbed Reactive Power 
Absorbed Reactive Power has a unit value of Reactive Power kVAr. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
Absorbed Reactive Power = Absorbed Electrical Power * √ ( (1- Absorbed Power Factor2) / Absorbed Power 
Factor2) 
Note: 
Positive Absorbed Reactive Power values are defined as LAG. 
Negative Absorbed Reactive Power values are defined as LEAD. 
Absorbed Power Factor Correction 
Absorbed Power Factor Correction has a unit value of Reactive Power kVAr. 
It is always negative i.e. LEAD. 
With correction, the loads Absorbed Reactive Power is calculated as: 
Total Absorbed Reactive Power = Absorbed Reactive Power - Absorbed Power Factor Correction 
Absorbed Apparent Power 
Absorbed Apparent Power has a unit value of Power kVA. Project can add unit option via grid configuration in 
Configuration module. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
Absorbed Apparent Power = √ (Absorbed Electrical Power2 + Total Absorbed Reactive Power2) 
Absorbed Corrected Power Factor 
The Absorbed Corrected Power Factor has a unit value of Ratio. The Absorbed Corrected Power Factor is 
calculated using this equation: 
Absorbed Corrected Power Factor = Absorbed Electrical Power / Absorbed Apparent Power 
Absorbed Corrected Power Factor Unit 
Absorbed Corrected Power Factor Unit can have values of LEAD and LAG. 
• When value is set as LAG then Total Absorbed Reactive Power is positive. 
• When value is set as LEAD then Total Absorbed Reactive Power is negative. 
• If Nameplate Power Factor =1 then Total Absorbed Reactive Power will be set to null. 
Absorbed Full Load Current 
Absorbed Full Load Current has a unit value of Current A. 
The Absorbed Full Load Current calculations below depend on the number of phases and therefore on the 
voltage type. 
The equation for voltage is: 
Line Voltage = Phase Voltage * √3 
The equation for a single phase load i.e. Voltage Type = S, is: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 961
Absorbed Full Load Current = (Absorbed Apparent Power * 1000) / Voltage (Phase voltage) 
The equation for a 2 phase load i.e. Voltage Type = L, is: 
Absorbed Full Load Current = (Absorbed Apparent Power * 1000) / Voltage (Line voltage) 
The equation for a 3 phase load i.e. Voltage Type = TN, T, is: 
Absorbed Full Load Current = (Absorbed Apparent Power * 1000) / (Voltage (Line voltage) * √3) 
DC Calculations 
DC calculations use the same equations as above except Nameplate Power Factor = 0. This means Nameplate 
Power Factor Unit is null. 
Calculations are as follows: 
Absorbed Electrical Power = (Absorbed Power / Absorbed Efficiency) 
Absorbed Reactive Power = 0 
Absorbed Power Factor Correction = 0 
Absorbed Apparent Power = Absorbed Electrical Power 
Absorbed Full Load Current = (Absorbed Electrical Power * 1000) / Voltage 
Power Flowing through Tag Objects 
Refer to Absorbed Diversity Power, Busbar Absorbed Diversity Power, Nameplate Connected Power, Nameplate 
Power per Phase and Nameplate Connected Power per Phase and Total for further information. 
Absorbed Diversity Power 
Absorbed Diversity Active Power 
Absorbed Diversity Active Power is the active power passing through a tag (Supply Tag) that includes the 
diversity ratio (Duty Circle) assigned to each fed tag i.e. power consumer. It should be noted that a fed tag can be 
directly or indirectly connected to this Supply Tag. 
• Absorbed Active Power has a unit value of kW. 
• Fed tag Diversity has a unit of ratio. 
• Absorbed Diversity Active Power is the total active power for all equipment tags that are fed directly or 
indirectly. 
• Absorbed Diversity Active Power is derived from the value of the Utilization for each fed tag. The utilization 
values are: 
• Continuous 
• Intermittent 
• Standby 
• Spare 
Equations for Absorbed Active Power attributes are: 
Continuous Power = Σ (Absorbed Electrical Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) where fed tag Utilization = 
Continuous 
Intermittent Power = Σ (Absorbed Electrical Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) where fed tag Utilization = 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 962
Intermittent 
Standby Power = Σ (Absorbed Electrical Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) where fed tag Utilization = Standby 
Spare Power = Σ (Absorbed Electrical Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) where fed tag Utilization = Spare 
Note: 
For the above equations, if the tag has the "Include in Power Calculations" flag set to true, then for that tag the 
value for the fed tag Diversity Factor = 1. 
Absorbed Diversity Reactive Power 
Absorbed Diversity Reactive Power is the reactive power passing through a tag (Supply Tag) that includes the 
diversity ratio (Duty Circle) assigned to each fed tag i.e. power consumer. It should be noted that a fed tag can be 
directly or indirectly connected to this Supply Tag. 
• Absorbed Reactive Power has a unit value of kVAr. 
• Absorbed Reactive Power can have a positive or negative value. If positive, the associated power factor unit = 
LAG and if negative, the associated power factor unit = LEAD. 
• Fed tag Diversity has a unit of ratio. 
• Absorbed Diversity Reactive Power is the total reactive power for all equipment tags that are fed directly or 
indirectly. 
Equations for Absorbed Reactive Power attributes are: 
Continuous Reactive Power = Σ (Absorbed Reactive Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) where fed tag Utilization = 
Continuous 
Intermittent Reactive Power = Σ (Absorbed Reactive Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) where fed tag Utilization = 
Intermittent 
Standby Reactive Power = Σ (Absorbed Reactive Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) where fed tag Utilization = 
Standby 
Spare Reactive Power = Σ (Absorbed Reactive Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) where fed tag Utilization = Spare 
Note: 
The above equations are only applicable when power has not passed through a busbar class tag. For equations 
for when power has passed through a busbar class tag. Refer to Busbar Absorbed Diversity Power for further 
information. 
Note: 
For the above equations, if the tag has the "Include in Power Calculations" flag set to true, then for that tag the 
value for the fed tag Diversity Factor = 1. 
Absorbed Diversity Apparent Power 
Absorbed Diversity Apparent Power is calculated using Absorbed Diversity Active Power and Absorbed Diversity 
Reactive Power. 
Absorbed Apparent Power has a unit value of kVA. 
Equations for Absorbed Apparent Power attributes are: 
Continuous Apparent Power = √ (Continuous Power2 + Continuous Reactive Power2) 
Intermittent Apparent Power = √ (Intermittent Power2 + Intermittent Reactive Power2) 
Standby Apparent Power = √ (Standby Power2 + Standby Reactive Power2) 
Spare Apparent Power = √ (Spare Power2 + Spare Reactive Power2) 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 963
Absorbed Diversity Power Factor 
Absorbed Diversity Power Factor is calculated using Absorbed Diversity Active Power and Absorbed Diversity 
Apparent Power. 
Absorbed Power Factor has a unit value between 0 and 1. 
Equations for Absorbed Power Factor attributes are: 
Continuous Power Factor = Continuous Power / Continuous Apparent Power 
Intermittent Power Factor = Intermittent Power / Intermittent Apparent Power 
Standby Power Factor = Standby Power / Standby Apparent Power 
Spare Power Factor = Spare Power / Spare Apparent Power 
Equations for Absorbed Power Factor Unit attributes are: 
If Continuous Reactive Power value is positive then Continuous Power Factor Unit = LAG 
If Continuous Reactive Power value is negative then Continuous Power Factor Unit = LEAD 
If Continuous Reactive Power value = 0 then Continuous Power Factor Unit = null 
If Intermittent Reactive Power value is positive then Intermittent Power Factor Unit = LAG 
If Intermittent Reactive Power value is negative then Intermittent Power Factor Unit = LEAD 
If Intermittent Reactive Power value = 0 then Intermittent Power Factor Unit = null 
If Standby Reactive Power value is positive then Standby Power Factor Unit = LAG 
If Standby Reactive Power value is negative then Standby Power Factor Unit = LEAD 
If Standby Reactive Power value = 0 then Standby Power Factor Unit = null 
If Spare Reactive Power value is positive then Spare Power Factor Unit = LAG 
If Spare Reactive Power value is negative then Spare Power Factor Unit = LEAD 
If Spare Reactive Power value = 0 then Spare Power Factor Unit = null 
Absorbed Diversity Current 
Absorbed Diversity Current is calculated using Connection Voltage data (Voltage Type, Project Voltage) and 
Absorbed Diversity Apparent Power. 
Absorbed Diversity Current has a unit value of Amp. 
Equations for Absorbed Current attributes are: 
If Voltage Type = S then 
Continuous Current = (Continuous Apparent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
If Voltage Type = L then 
Continuous Current = (Continuous Apparent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
If Voltage Type = T, TN then 
Continuous Current = (Continuous Apparent Power * 1000) / (Project Voltage * 3) 
If Voltage Type = DC then 
Continuous Current = (Continuous Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
Note: 
In this case Continuous Apparent Power = Continuous Power. 
If Voltage Type = S then 
Intermittent Current = (Intermittent Apparent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
If Voltage Type = L then 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 964
Intermittent Current = (Intermittent Apparent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
If Voltage Type = T, TN then 
Intermittent Current = (Intermittent Apparent Power * 1000) / (Project Voltage * 3) 
If Voltage Type = DC then 
Intermittent Current = (Intermittent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
Note: 
In this case Intermittent Apparent Power = Intermittent Power. 
If Voltage Type = S then 
Standby Current = (Standby Apparent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
If Voltage Type = L then 
Standby Current = (Standby Apparent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
If Voltage Type = T, TN then 
Standby Current = (Standby Apparent Power * 1000) / (Project Voltage * 3) 
If Voltage Type = DC then 
Standby Current = (Standby Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
Note: 
In this case Standby Apparent Power = Standby Power. 
If Voltage Type = S then 
Spare Current = (Spare Apparent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
If Voltage Type = L then 
Spare Current = (Spare Apparent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
If Voltage Type = T, TN then 
Spare Current = (Spare Apparent Power * 1000) / (Project Voltage * 3) 
If Voltage Type = DC then 
Spare Current = (Spare Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
Note: 
In this case Spare Apparent Power = Spare Power. 
Busbar Absorbed Diversity Power 
Busbar Absorbed Diversity Active Power calculations use exactly equations as Absorbed Diversity Active Power 
except there is a Busbar Diversity Factor value that can be set by the user on the busbar class tag. 
Busbar Absorbed Diversity Reactive Power calculations use exactly equations as Absorbed Diversity Reactive 
Power except there is a Busbar Diversity Factor value that can be set by the user on the busbar class tag. 
The attributes are based on the utilization values of: 
• Continuous 
• Intermittent 
• Standby 
• Spare 
Busbar Diversity Factor has a unit between 0 and 1. 
The attributes for the busbar tag diversity factors are: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 965
• Continuous Diversity Factor 
• Intermittent Diversity Factor 
• Standby Diversity Factor 
• Spare Diversity Factor 
In the Seed project, the default value for all 4 attributes is set to 1. This is the recommended value, as usually the 
diversity factor for a project is set at fed tag level. 
Equations for Absorbed Active Power attributes if power flows through busbar class tag are: 
Continuous Power = (Σ (Absorbed Electrical Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) * Continuous Diversity Factor where 
fed tag Utilization = Continuous 
Intermittent Power = (Σ Absorbed Electrical Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) * Intermittent Diversity Factor 
where fed tag Utilization = Intermittent 
Standby Power = (Σ Absorbed Electrical Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) * Standby Diversity Factor where fed tag 
Utilization = Standby 
Spare Power = (Σ Absorbed Electrical Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) * Spare Diversity Factor where fed tag 
Utilization = Spare 
Equations for Absorbed Reactive Power attributes if power flows through busbar class tag are: 
Continuous Reactive Power = (Σ Absorbed Reactive Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) * Continuous Diversity 
Factor where fed tag Utilization = Continuous 
Intermittent Reactive Power = (Σ Absorbed Reactive Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) * Intermittent Diversity 
Factor where fed tag Utilization = Intermittent 
Standby Reactive Power = (Σ Absorbed Reactive Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) * Standby Diversity Factor 
where fed tag Utilization = Standby 
Spare Reactive Power = (Σ Absorbed Reactive Power * fed tag Diversity Factor) * Spare Diversity Factor where fed 
tag Utilization = Spare 
Nameplate Connected Power 
Nameplate Connected Power 
• Nameplate Connected Power has a unit value of kW. 
• Nameplate Connected Power is the total active power for all fed equipment tags. 
• Nameplate Connected Power is derived from Nameplate Power. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Power is: 
Nameplate Connected Power = Σ Nameplate Electrical Power 
Nameplate Connected Reactive Power 
Nameplate Connected Reactive Power is the reactive power passing through a tag (Supply Tag). 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power has a unit value of kVAr. 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power can have a positive or negative value. If positive, the associated power 
factor unit = LAG and if negative, the associated power factor unit = LEAD. 
• Nameplate Connected Power is the total reactive power for all fed equipment tags. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 966
The equation for Nameplate Connected Reactive Power attributes is: 
Nameplate Connected Reactive Power = Σ Nameplate Reactive Power 
Nameplate Connected Apparent Power 
Nameplate Connected Apparent Power is calculated using Nameplate Connected Active Power and Nameplate 
Connected Reactive Power. 
• Nameplate Connected Apparent Power has a unit value of kVA. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Apparent Power is: 
Nameplate Connected Apparent Power = √ (Nameplate Connected Power2 + Nameplate Connected Reactive 
Power2) 
Nameplate Connected Power Factor 
• Nameplate Connected Power Factor is calculated using Nameplate Connected Active Power and Nameplate 
Connected Apparent Power. 
• Nameplate Connected Power Factor has a unit value between 0 and 1. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Power Factor is: 
Nameplate Connected Power Factor = Nameplate Connected Power / Nameplate Connected Apparent Power 
Equations for Nameplate Connected Power Factor Unit attributes are: 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power value is positive then Nameplate Connected Power Factor Unit = LAG 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power value is negative then Nameplate Connected Power Factor Unit = LEAD 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power value = 0 then Nameplate Connected Power Factor Unit = null 
Nameplate Connected Current 
• Nameplate Connected Current is calculated using Connection Voltage data (Voltage Type, Project Voltage) 
and Nameplate Connected Apparent Power. 
• Nameplate Connected Current has a unit value of Amp. 
Equations for Nameplate Connected Current attributes are: 
If Voltage Type = S then 
Nameplate Connected Current = (Nameplate Connected Apparent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
If Voltage Type = L then 
Nameplate Connected Current = (Nameplate Connected Apparent Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
If Voltage Type = T, TN then 
Nameplate Connected Current = (Nameplate Connected Apparent Power * 1000) / (Project Voltage * 3) 
If Voltage Type = DC then 
Nameplate Connected Current = (Nameplate Connected Power * 1000) / Project Voltage 
Note: 
In this case Nameplate Connected Apparent Power = Nameplate Connected Power. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 967
Nameplate Power per Phase 
Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase 
• Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase has a unit value of Power kW. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
If Voltage Type = TN, T then 
Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase = Nameplate Electrical Power / 3 
If Voltage Type = L then 
Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase = Nameplate Electrical Power / 2 
If Voltage Type = S then 
Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase = Nameplate Electrical Power 
Nameplate Reactive Power Per Phase 
• Nameplate Reactive Power Per Phase has a unit value of Power kVAr. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
If Voltage Type = TN, T then 
Nameplate Reactive Power Per Phase = Nameplate Reactive Power / 3 
If Voltage Type = L then 
Nameplate Reactive Power Per Phase = Nameplate Reactive Power / 2 
If Voltage Type = S then 
Nameplate Reactive Power Per Phase = Nameplate Reactive Power 
Nameplate Power Factor Per Phase 
• Nameplate Power Factor Per Phase has a unit value between 0 and 1. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
Nameplate Power Factor Per Phase = Nameplate Power Factor 
Nameplate Power Factor Per Phase Unit 
• Nameplate Power Factor Per Phase Unit can have values of LEAD and LAG. 
• When value is set as LAG then Nameplate Reactive Power is positive. 
• When value is set as LEAD then Nameplate Reactive Power is negative. 
• If Nameplate Power Factor =1 then Nameplate Power Factor Unit will be set to null. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
Nameplate Power Factor Per Phase Unit = Nameplate Power Factor Unit 
Nameplate Apparent Power Per Phase 
• Nameplate Apparent Power Per Phase has a unit value of Power kVA. 
It is calculated using this equation: 
Nameplate Apparent Power Per Phase = √ (Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase12 + Nameplate Reactive Power 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 968
Per Phase12) 
Nameplate Connected Power per Phase and Total 
Nameplate Connected Power per Phase calculations use exactly the same equations as Nameplate Connected 
Power, but with calculation for each phase and totals. 
Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase1 
• Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase1 has a unit value of kW. 
• Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase1 is the total active power for all fed equipment tags associated 
with Phase 1. 
• Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase1 is derived from Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase1 is: 
Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase1 = Σ Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase associated with Phase 1 
Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1 is the reactive power passing through a tag (Supply Tag) 
associated with Phase 1. 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1 has a unit value of kVAr. 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1 can have a positive or negative value. If positive, the 
associated power factor unit = LAG and if negative, the associated power factor unit = LEAD. 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1 is the total reactive power for all fed equipment tags 
associated with Phase 1. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1 attributes is: 
Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1 = Σ Nameplate Reactive Power Per Phase associated with Phase 1 
Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase1 
• Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase1 is calculated using Nameplate Connected Electrical Power 
Phase1 and Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1. 
• Nameplate Connected Power has a unit value of kVA. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase1 is: 
Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase1 = √ (Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase1 + Nameplate 
Connected Apparent Power Phase12 ) 
Nameplate Connected Phase1 Power Factor 
• Nameplate Connected Phase1 Power Factor is calculated using Nameplate Connected Reactive Power 
Phase1 and Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase1. 
• Nameplate Connected Power Factor has a unit value between 0 and 1. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Phase1 Power Factor is: 
Nameplate Connected Phase1 Power Factor = Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase1 / Nameplate 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 969
Connected Apparent Power Phase1 
Equations for Nameplate Connected Power Factor Unit attributes are: 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1 value is positive then Nameplate Connected Phase1 Power 
Factor Unit = LAG 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1 value is negative then Nameplate Connected Phase1 Power 
Factor Unit = LEAD 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase1 value = 0 then Nameplate Connected Phase1 Power Factor Unit 
= null 
Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase1 
• Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase1 is calculated using Connection Voltage data (Project Voltage 
for single phase) and Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase1. 
• Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase1 has a unit value of Amp. 
Equation for Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase1 attributes are: 
Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase1 = (Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase1 * 1000) / 
Project Voltage (Phase voltage) 
Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase2 
• Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase2 has a unit value of kW. 
• Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase2 is the total active power for all fed equipment tags associated 
with Phase 2. 
• Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase2 is derived from Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase2 is: 
Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase2 = Σ Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase associated with Phase 2 
Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2 is the reactive power passing through a tag (Supply Tag) 
associated with Phase 2. 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2 has a unit value of kVAr. 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2 can have a positive or negative value. If positive, the 
associated power factor unit = LAG and if negative, the associated power factor unit = LEAD. 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2 is the total reactive power for all fed equipment tags 
associated with Phase 2. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2 attributes is: 
Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2 = Σ Nameplate Reactive Power Per Phase associated with Phase 2 
Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase2 
• Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase2 is calculated using Nameplate Connected Electrical Power 
Phase2 and Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2. 
• Nameplate Connected Power has a unit value of kVA. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase2 is: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 970
Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase2 = √ (Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase22 + Nameplate 
Connected Apparent Power Phase22) 
Nameplate Connected Phase2 Power Factor 
• Nameplate Connected Phase2 Power Factor is calculated using Nameplate Connected Reactive Power 
Phase2 and Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase2. 
• Nameplate Connected Power Factor has a unit value between 0 and 1. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Phase2 Power Factor is: 
Nameplate Connected Phase2 Power Factor = Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase2 / Nameplate 
Connected Apparent Power Phase2 
Equations for Nameplate Connected Power Factor Unit attributes are: 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2 value is positive then Nameplate Connected Phase2 Power 
Factor Unit = LAG 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2 value is negative then Nameplate Connected Phase2 Power 
Factor Unit = LEAD 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase2 value = 0 then Nameplate Connected Phase2 Power Factor Unit 
= null 
Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase2 
• Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase2 is calculated using Connection Voltage data (Project Voltage 
for single phase) and Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase2. 
• Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase2 has a unit value of Amp. 
Equation for Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase2 attributes are: 
Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase2 = (Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase2 * 1000) / 
Project Voltage (Phase voltage) 
Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase3 
• Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase3 has a unit value of kW. 
• Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase3 is the total active power for all fed equipment tags associated 
with Phase 3. 
• Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase3 is derived from Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase3 is: 
Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase3 = Σ Nameplate Electrical Power Per Phase associated with Phase 3 
Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3 is the reactive power passing through a tag (Supply Tag) 
associated with Phase 3. 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3 has a unit value of kVAr. 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3 can have a positive or negative value. If positive, the 
associated power factor unit = LAG and if negative, the associated power factor unit = LEAD. 
• Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3 is the total reactive power for all fed equipment tags 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Introduction of AVEVA Engineering
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 971
associated with Phase 3. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3 attributes is: 
Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3 = Σ Nameplate Reactive Power Per Phase associated with Phase 3 
Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase3 
• Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase3 is calculated using Nameplate Connected Electrical Power 
Phase3 and Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3. 
• Nameplate Connected Power has a unit value of kVA. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase3 is: 
Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase3 = √ (Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase32 + Nameplate 
Connected Apparent Power Phase32) 
Nameplate Connected Phase3 Power Factor 
• Nameplate Connected Phase3 Power Factor is calculated using Nameplate Connected Reactive Power 
Phase3 and Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase3. 
• Nameplate Connected Power Factor has a unit value between 0 and 1. 
The equation for Nameplate Connected Phase3 Power Factor is: 
Nameplate Connected Phase3 Power Factor = Nameplate Connected Electrical Power Phase3 / Nameplate 
Connected Apparent Power Phase3 
Equations for Nameplate Connected Power Factor Unit attributes are: 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3 value is positive then Nameplate Connected Phase2 Power 
Factor Unit = LAG 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3 value is negative then Nameplate Connected Phase2 Power 
Factor Unit = LEAD 
If Nameplate Connected Reactive Power Phase3 value = 0 then Nameplate Connected Phase2 Power Factor Unit 
= null 
Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase3 
• Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase3 is calculated using Connection Voltage data (Project Voltage 
for single phase) and Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase3. 
• Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase3 has a unit value of Amp. 
Equation for Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase3 attributes are: 
Nameplate Connected Full Load Current Phase3 = (Nameplate Connected Apparent Power Phase3 * 1000) / 
Project Voltage (Phase voltage) 
Note 
Nameplate Connected Electrical Power 3Phase, Nameplate Connected Reactive Power 3Phase, Nameplate 
Connected Apparent Power 3Phase, Nameplate Connected 3Phase Power Factor, Nameplate Connected 3Phase 
Power Factor Unit, Nameplate Connected Full Load Current 3Phase will have the same values as Nameplate 
Connected Power, Nameplate Connected Reactive Power, Nameplate Connected Apparent Power, Nameplate 
Connected Power Factor, Nameplate Connected Power Factor Unit, Nameplate Connected Current as the fed 
tags are identical and the calculations are for all 3 phases.
