Options and Mapping 
Options are provided in the toolbar and context menus to configure how the data will be compared and 
displayed. 
Grid Options 
The grid has a configuration menu accessed via a right-click on any column title, and a context menu accessed via 
a right-click in any row. 
Column Setup 
Column Setup allows you to choose which columns to display in the grid view and which order to display them. 
To display the Column Setup window, right-click on any column title and select Column Setup from the pop-up 
menu. The Column Setup window contains the standard columns for the grid along with the attribute columns 
for the source type. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1102
To display a Column Name in the grid view, select the required checkbox in the Visible column. Clearing a 
checkbox removes the Column Name from the grid view. 
To change the position of a Column Name in the grid view, select the Column Name and click either the up or 
down arrows. 
Click OK to update the grid view. 
Note: 
Grid layouts are automatically saved in the folder. 
C:\Documents and Settings\<Current User>\LocalSettings\Application Data\Aveva\PDMS\
SchematicInstrumentationAddin 
With filenames like 'SchAiInstruments.xml' or 'SchAiLoops.xml'. 
Settings 
Right-clicking on any column title and selecting Settings, displays a settings sub-menu: 
• Enable Filtering 
• Enable Column Summaries 
• Enable Column Sort 
• Feedback Colors 
Enable Filtering allows you to display a filter row in the grid view. The filter row is used to filter the grid to show 
only certain records. Instant "on the fly" filtering is achieved by selecting the icon in the column field and 
selecting a value from the drop-down list. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1103
Further filtering is available by selecting the small icon to the left of a column (field) caption, selecting from the 
drop-down list and entering a value into the highlighted field. The grid view will automatically refresh to reflect 
the selected criteria. 
Enable Column Summaries displays the 
 icon in the No of Changes column. Clicking the icon displays the Select 
Summaries window. 
You can check one or all the boxes and click OK to receive a count displayed at the end of the No of Changes 
column. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1104
Note: 
Clicking Refresh removes the count. 
Enable Column Sort allows you to change the column order. Click and hold on the column title and drag the 
column either left or right into a new position. Two arrows are displayed to assist you in positioning the column. 
Clicking Feedback Colors displays a sub-menu: 
Note: 
New Element Row Color is used when an element is not matched and Compare/Update is able to insert it. The 
Not Matched Row Color is used when an element is not matched and Compare/Update is not able to insert it. 
Note: 
Child Row Deleted Indicator Color is used if child rows contain both differences and logically deleted objects. 
Clicking any of the options displays a Color Selection window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1105
Selecting Reset Default Feedback Row Colors, reverts the options back to their default color settings. 
New Element Row Color 
Salmon 
Candidate for Deletion Color 
Orange

Linked With Changes Row Color 
Pale Yellow

Export to Excel 
The current grid layout and contents can be saved to an Excel file. Right-click in any column title and select 
Export to Excel, then enter a file name in the file browser window. 
Print Preview 
You can view a print preview of the current grid layout and content. Right-click in any column title and select 
Print Preview. 
Grid Context Menu 
Instrument datasheets and details can be displayed when the target is the schematic Database (DB) and the 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1106
source is AVEVA Instrumentation. Right-click in any row in the Instruments or Loops grid view to display a context 
menu. 
The Instruments grid view displays the following options: 
• Instrument Datasheet displays the AVEVA Instrumentation data sheet for the selected instrument. 
• Instrument Details displays the AVEVA Instrumentation window for the selected instrument. 
The Loops grid view has one option: 
• Loop Details displays the AVEVA Instrumentation window for the selection loop. 
Note: 
No context menu exists for the Attribute Details tab. 
The diagram view can be displayed when the source is Schematics DB, the selected source item appears on a 
diagram, and the SVG file is available for that diagram. Right-click in any row in the Elements grid view to display 
a context menu. Show in Diagram displays the diagram view for the selected source object. 
Options 
The types of elements that will be compared and how their attributes will be mapped are set using the Options 
window, from the main toolbar, select Options. 
Note: 
You must be a member of team CUADMIN to use any of these options. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1107
Manage Configurations and Groups 
Click Manage to display the Manage Configurations & Groups window. 
Use the hierarchy of the source Database (DB) to display the available DBs, groups of configurations, parent and 
child configurations. Select a configuration to display its summary details in the right hand pane of the Manage 
Configurations & Groups window. 
Alternatively, click Summary to display read-only configuration summary details. 
Right-click on one of the configurations nodes to create a new configuration with that source DB. 
Right-click one of the configurations to Edit, Copy, Delete, or Rename it. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1108
Selecting New Configuration or Edit will display the Configuration Wizard. 
Right-click one of the groups nodes to create a new group with that source DB. 
Right-click one of the groups to Edit, Copy, Delete or Rename it. 
Selecting New Group or Edit will display the Manage Groups window. 
Configuration Wizard 
The stages to define the configuration consist of: 
1. Define the type of source object. 
2. Define the type of destination object. 
3. Map the attributes. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1109
4. Define member element type mappings. 
5. Set whether to use advanced rules. 
6. Define an expression to identify destination owner objects. 
7. Define filters to be applied to the source object population. 
Note: 
Certain out of the box configurations have specific behaviors associated with them which are not available to 
new created configurations. These out of the box configurations can be edited to add source filters and 
attribute mappings. 
Note: 
In Diagrams, select the out of the box AVEVA Instrumentation source for Instruments in order to compare a 
combined set of SCINSTRUMENTs and SCOINSTRUMENTs. 
Note: 
In Paragon, select the out of the box AVEVA Instrumentation or AVEVA Electrical source for Cable Catalog in 
order to compare the five destination types. 
In AIS URL specify the URL where the data API of the AVEVA Integration Service (AIS) is located. The standard 
format is https://<hostname>:<portno>. 
From the Authentication type options, specify the user authentication method to be used: 
• If AIS is available at on-premises, select NTLM. 
• If AIS is available at on-cloud, select AVEVA Connect. 
Once the AIS URL and Authentication type have been specified, click Connect. The available projects for the 
selected source are then listed in the Source field. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1110
The source data is the data that will be the basis of comparison. 
Set Mode to Local if the source data is to be from a product installed on the same computer. 
Set Mode to Service: 
• If the source data is to be from the AVEVA Integration Service Service. The window changes to show fields for 
Server Name, Port and Source. These should be set according to the Service configuration. An external 
relational DB source is available (only in the service mode and not in 12.1 SP4) using a suitable configuration 
of the AVEVA Integration Service Service. 
• If the source data is to be from an AVEVA ERM source, select a suitable column header for the name 
attribute, for example: MAT_ID, depending on how the views have been configured. Currently integration 
with AVEVA ERM does not support unique attribute so None must be selected. 
• If no unique attribute is available for this source, select None. When a unique attribute is selected, a UDA 
Suffix field is displayed, enter a suffix for the UDA name. The full name for the UDA including the suffix is 
then displayed. 
Note: 
Text UDAs pmg-engi-edb-<suffix>, pmg-sche-edb-<suffix> should be created where <suffix> is 
defined in the configuration. These should be set to be indexed and have a length sufficient to hold the 
unique attribute value length. 
Select the Source DB from the drop-down list. If the source is AVEVA Electrical, AVEVA Instrumentation, or AVEVA 
P&ID, a Login button will appear. Click this and log into the source system. 
Enter the Display name that will be used for this source list in the Compare/Update window. 
Either click on Element type and select the type of object in the source DB, or click on Group and select one of 
the pre-defined source groups. Source groups will be available only in certain contexts such as Design Valves or 
Design Piping Specials where the group represents a combination of element types. 
Select the Name attribute that is to be used for name matching, and the Unique attribute that is to be used for 
internal IDs. 
These are to be set as follows: 
Database 
Type

RefNo

and AVEVA Electrical

CableNo 
CableId

CatalogNo 
CableCatId

EquipmentNo 
EquipId 
Instrument

InstKEY

AVEVA P&ID 
All 
Label 
ID 
If the source is an external DB, select an appropriate table or view for the source type and select the appropriate 
columns for the name and unique attributes. 
Click Next to continue. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1111
The target data is the data in the default DB for the current session, for example: in Tags it will be engineering 
data. Target data is compared against source data. 
In Element type select the type of object in the target DB. 
Enter the Display name that will be used in the Attribute Mapping screen. 
If Compare/Update finds unmatched objects, it can create corresponding new objects in the target DB. Check 
Allow insertion if this is to be allowed. 
By default, inserted objects take the same name as their source objects. An optional Name prefix can be 
specified. 
Note: 
A name prefix will be required where duplicate naming is not allowed between the source and destination DB. 
Check Create Reverse Configuration to automatically create a configuration with source and target swapped 
(this option will be available only when both are DAtaBAse CONtrol Program (DABACON) DBs). 
Click Next to continue. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1112
Certain out of the box configurations such as AVEVA Instrumentation Instruments and AVEVA Electrical Cable 
Catalog have specific behaviors to use multiple destination types. In such cases, select each Element Type in turn 
to define its attribute mappings. 
Note: 
In AVEVA Electrical and AVEVA Instrumentation, the administrative User can restrict the list of attributes 
exposed for integration. Compare/Update will fail if any mapped attributes are not exposed for integration. 
Click Add mapping to add an empty attribute mapping row to the list. On the source side, do one of the 
following: 
• Select an attribute from the drop-down list. 
• Type the name of a pseudo attribute. 
• Type a PML expression, enclosed in brackets, for example: (DESC of OWNE). The expression will not be 
evaluated at this point but will be evaluated at comparison time against the source object. 
• Type a static value, to set a default value onto a target attribute. Any value entered in this way will not be 
validated at this point. 
Select to uncheck the Allow update check box if the attribute is to be compared only and not updated. 
Then on the destination side, do one of the following: 
• Select an attribute from the drop-down list. 
• Type the name of a pseudo-attribute (if this is to be compared only). 
• Type a PML expression (if this is to be compared only), the expression will be evaluated at comparison time 
against the destination object. 
Select a mapping row and click Delete to remove it. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1113
If this configuration is to include parent/child hierarchy, select one or more member element configurations. For 
example, a previously defined nozzle configuration can be selected as a member for an equipment configuration. 
Note: 
When engineering as destination, if there are any child configurations with a parts referenced in the engineering 
DB and there is an associated attribute, the relationship is displayed. The element in the reference attribute will 
be shown as a child for the schematic element and is updated as a child element. 
For example, an element type called :LINETAG in engineering is mapped with SCPLINE in diagrams, in the 
Member Element type mapping wizard. Schematic Pipelines is displayed as a configuration with a parts reference 
and associated attribute :FROMITEM. 
Click Next to continue. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1114
If advanced rules are required for this configuration, select a Project configuration file (this will usually be the 
default filename for the project, for example: for Sample project this would be 
%SAMDFLTS%\ModelManagement\ProjectConfiguration.xml). Then select a Rule set and check Use Advanced 
Rules. 
Select the Restrict the Configuration to Admin Users only check box, if you want to test this configuration before 
publishing it for general use on the project. 
Select the Make this Configuration available standalone check box to allow end-users to be able to run it when 
it is not part of a group of configurations. 
Click Next to continue. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1115
An optional Owner expression can be entered. This will be evaluated against the appropriate source object 
during the Update Database process to determine the owner for an inserted object. 
Or 
Specify an optional owner object for objects inserted from a non-DABACON source DB during Update Database. 
Browse to select an owner object of the appropriate type from the destination DB. 
Note: 
This sets an expression for that ID but where the source is not a DABACON DB, the expression will be read only. 
The expression can be tested by selecting an object of the appropriate source type and pressing Test. 
During the Update Database process, the precedence of determining the owner for an inserted object will be: 
• From the parent/child hierarchy if updating using a child configuration. 
• From a PML callback if defined. 
• From the owner expression if defined. 
• From the setting in the Update Database dialogue. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1116
Add filters to limit the population of source data that will be used in the comparison. 
Note: 
The end-user will have the option to further filter the source data at runtime. 
Two types of filters can be entered. The first is on attribute values. The second is on the evaluation of a PML 
expression. Click the appropriate Add filter to add a row. 
For the attribute filter, select the attribute, operator, and value. The available operators will be appropriate to the 
data type of the selected attribute. 
For the expression filter, enter a PML expression. 
Note: 
Multiple filters will be joined with 'AND' when generating the query. 
Click Finish to complete and save the configuration. 
Note: 
Configuration files are saved in the Project defaults folder, for example: %samdflts%\CUConfigs2.0. 
Additional Configuration Wizard Steps for Engineering Databases running TMS Projects 
If using the Configuration Wizard for an Engineering database running a TMS project, two additional steps are 
provided, . 
Override Destination Base Type Element 
Use to override the destination element type based on a source attribute value, allowing the import of elements 
as a configured destination type based on the source attribute value. Thus enabling elements to be imported as 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1117
configured destination type based in the source attribute value. 
For example: if importing data from AVEVA Schematics and in base configuration Equipment is selected as 
destination element type. 
Click Next to continue. 
Use the functionality available from Step 3 of the Compare/Update Configuration Wizard to override the 
destination element type based on the selection. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1118
Select the None radio button to import destination element types based on the base destination element type 
(this option is the default). 
Select the Override destination element type based on class URI radio button to import destination elements 
types based on the class URI defined in the source application. This option is enabled only for elements from 
AVEVA P&ID and AVEVA Diagrams, as these applications can access the conceptual model to set class URIs. If a 
class URI is not found, the elements are imported with the base destination element type. 
Select the Override destination element type based on Source Attribute radio button to import destination 
element types based on the source attribute value. 
Notes: 
Update/Re import: None: updates matched records, Override destination element type based on class URL:
Updates matched records, if class URL has changed, the product will reclassify as per class URL in the the source 
product and update attributes and Override destination element type based on Source Attribute: Updates the 
matched records, if a mapped source attribute value is changed, the product will reclassify the matched element 
and update the attribute. 
This functionality is supported in group configuration and parent/child configuration. 
Select the Source Attribute from the drop-down list and click Add mapping to add an empty attribute mapping 
row to the list. Populate the Source Attribute Value and Destination Element Type value. If any elements contain 
an unset attribute value, the elements are imported with the base destination element type. 
Click Next to continue. 
Mapping Counter Attributes 
Use to override the counter for the tag by the attribute value from the source element (from the Schematic DB). 
This ensures that, when you import, Engineering uses the same counter in the tag name as is used in the source. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1119
Counter attributes can be text or numeric. When mapping counter attributes an extra step has been added to 
the Compare/Update Configuration Wizard. 
Select the required Schematic Database Attribute. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1120
Select the required Override Engineering Counter from the list of valid write attributes. 
Note: 
If the override type has the same write attribute as the mapped ones then the write attribute of the overridden 
type takes precedence. 
Click Add Mapping, then click Finish, a confirmation window is displayed. 
Click OK. 
Once completed the counter mappings are displayed in the Configuration Summary window. 
Compare/Update Results with Counter Override Mappings 
There is no change in the way the compare results, counter information is displayed as V100 for existing tags and 
V101 for new tags. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1121
Upon updating the selected element, it is created with the counter values against the valid write attributes. 
Update Compare/Update Configurations for AIS Compatibility 
In AVEVA Engineering 15.7.3, when editing configurations created in AVEVA Engineering 15.7.2 or earlier 
versions, a warning is displayed to let the user know that the existing configuration settings are no longer valid. 
The message "Previous AIS settings are no longer valid" is displayed in Step 1 of the Compare/Update 
Configuration Wizard: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1122
The data API of the AVEVA Integration Service (AIS) must be specified and an authentication type selected. 
In AIS URL specify the URL where the data API of the AVEVA Integration Service (AIS) is located. The standard 
format is https://<hostname>:<portno>. 
From the Authentication type options, specify the user authentication method to be used: 
• If AIS is available at on-premises, select NTLM. 
• If AIS is available at on-cloud, select AVEVA Connect. 
Once the AIS URL and Authentication type have been specified, click Connect. The available projects for the 
selected source are then listed in the Source field. 
Update WCF Configurations 
Configurations created in previous versions of Engineering (prior to 15.7.3) can be upgraded to DataAPI 
configurations individually. If the user still wants to use the configurations, then they can run the configurations 
directly without upgrade. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1123
WCF configurations cannot be edited: the message "Previous AIS settings are no longer valid" is displayed in Step 
1 of the Compare/Update Configuration Wizard. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1124
Update WCF configurations and groups of configurations to Data API, by using a PML function. 
The parameters of the PML function are: 
• SourceName: The supported sources and their names are: 
• AVEVA Electrical 
• AVEVA Instrumentation 
• AVEVA P&ID 
• Excel 
• External Datasource 
• AVEVA ERM 
• DataAPI URL: This is the URL where Data API is hosted. The format is: 
<https://<hostname>:<portno> 
• AuthenticationType: The authentication type depends on where AVEVA Integration Service (AIS) is hosted. 
• If AIS is available on-premises, select NTLM. 
• If AIS is available in the cloud, select AVEVA Connect. 
Update WCF Configurations to Data API 
1. Click Manage. 
2. In the Manage Configurations and Groups window, expand the required source database and expand its 
configurations. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1125
3. In Command Window, specify the PML function, with this syntax: 
!!upgradeWcftoDataApi(‘<SourceName>','<Data API URL>','AuthenticationType') 
4. Run the command. 
The Message window displays the success or otherwise of the update. 
Updated configurations can be edited. 
Manage Groups 
Use the Manage Groups window to choose which groups of configurations are to be compared. 
Enter or modify the name of the group. 
Select the Restrict this Group to Admin Users only checkbox to test it before making it available to the Project. 
Use the arrow buttons to choose which configurations will be in the group. 
A configuration can be in one or more groups and can also be available standalone if marked as such. When 
running a Compare operation, Users will be able to select from the available groups and standalone 
configurations. 
Scope selection is available on groups for certain combinations of data. For example, scope by diagrams can be 
set on groups with schematic source and is active for engineering Users. When set the engineering User will be 
prompted to select one or more diagrams, limiting the comparison to engineering items that have equivalent 
schematic objects on those diagrams. 
Note: 
In scope by source, the User can select one or more diagrams from all available, and this will have the effect of 
filtering the source objects according to those diagrams. 
Note: 
In scope by destination, the list of diagrams to select from will be limited to diagrams relevant to the current list 
selection of engineering items. If this results in a single diagram, the User will not be prompted, and the 
comparison will proceed automatically. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1126
To enable scope selection, click to select the Apply scope selection for this group checkbox and select the 
appropriate Scope Criteria from the drop down list. 
Click OK to save the group. 
If a group contains only Service Mode configurations, a common service setting is available for that group. This 
means the entire group of configurations will use a single login connection in place of the individual logins per 
configuration. To enable a common service, click to select the Use same service and Project for all the 
configurations in this group checkbox then enter the appropriate Server and Port settings. 
Advanced Configuration 
Users who are members of team CUADMIN can set up advanced rules by clicking Configuration Explorer. This 
displays the Project Configuration Explorer shared with Schematic 3D Integrator. There you can configure more 
elaborate rules for element matching and updating, in particular key attribute mapping to match multiple pipes 
against schematic or engineering pipelines. An example set of rules using key attributes for engineering to design 
matching is included in the out of the box ProjectConfiguration.xml file. Refer to the Schematic 3D Integrator 
User Guide section on How to Configure Rules. 
Note: 
A target attribute specified in an Assign Action must also be included in the attribute mappings in order to 
appear in a results column. The source attribute in the mapping will be overridden by the advanced 
configuration action. 
Manage Links 
Users who are members of team CUADMIN can click on Manage Links to display this window: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1127
This is an administrative window used to manage Link Worlds. Before using it, create a separate Design 
Reference DB in Admin, and ensure it is current on all MDBs where linking will occur, and all Users who need to 
link can write to it. 
Note: 
The Manage Links window is shared between Integrator and Compare/Update. Design Reference DBs can be 
written to by 3D, schematic and engineering Users. 
The list includes all the Design and Design Reference DBs available, identifying any that contain links (links 
created prior to 12.1 reside in Design DBs). The following functions are available from the File menu, or on a 
right-click context menu. 
Create 
Create a new Link World in the selected DB. 
Valid 
Output a list of links and any errors to the command 
line for the selected DB. This action will also merge 
partially populated links, for example: 3D object "A" is 
linked to schematic object "A" by one link and 
schematic object "A" is linked to engineering object 
"A" by another link, as can occur when links are 
created in separate extract DBs. 
Validate All 
As for Validate for all DBs in the list. 
Remove invalid references 
Remove invalid references from links in the selected 
DB. Invalid references can occur when elements are 
deleted and the Link World DB cannot be updated. 
Caution: 
This operation should be used only when all of the DBs holding objects that have been linked are current on 
the MDB. 
Properties 
Display the number of links in the selected DB. 
The Edit menu provides Cut, Copy, Paste, and Delete functions to move Link Worlds between DBs. You can also 
drag-and-drop from one DB to another to move or merge a Link World. 
Restrict Link Option to Admin Users 
Use the Restrict link option to admin Users functionality to control whether general Users can change the 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1128
Update Engineering Database settings for linking, updating attributes, and unlinking. If the option is checked, 
these settings are disabled, so that End-Users must use the default settings for link and update, and are not able 
to unlink. 
Update Engineering Database 
This feature creates or updates data in the Engineering database according criteria that you select. The criteria 
are: 
• Unmatched - The supplier's data does not have the same name as any data in the Engineering database. 
• Matched - The supplier's data has the same name as data in the Engineering database. That is, for example, 
both the supplier and the Engineering database have an item named Pump 1. 
• Linked - The supplier's data was previously imported but requires updating in the Engineering database. 
1. Select the source from the lists in the Source Selection group on the Compare/Update tab. 
2. The Compare/Update pane lists the items read. They are color-coded according to whether they are 
unmatched, matched, or linked. 
3. In the Compare/Update pane, select the checkboxes of the items to update. 
4. Click Update Database>Update Database with Options in the Destination group of the Compare/Update 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1129
tab. 
The Update Engineering Database form is displayed. If you selected no items in the Compare/Update pane, 
only the top, summary, section of the form is displayed. If you selected one or more items, then either or 
both the Unmatched items to be inserted and Matched or linked items to be updated sections are also 
displayed, according to the items you selected. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1130
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1131
5. (Optional) For unmatched, matched, or linked items, clear the Linked inserted items or Update attributes 
for linked items checkboxes. 
6. (Optional) Click the ellipsis to select the owner for the selected item types. 
7. (Optional) For matched or linked items, select the Unlink selected items checkbox 
8. Click Update. 
The progress and then the result of the update are displayed:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Configuration with Inherited Types 
Using the inherited types concept makes it easy to group all related types by creating a single configuration, 
instead of several configurations, and display all the relative types in the Compare/Update grid. 
Single Configuration 
The Inherited Element Types option is available only in the Diagrams module while creating configuration with 
Engineering as source.

Click the Browse button to open the window with all the inherited types (or rather all the sub-classes) existing 
under Pump (which is the selected type). These have now been moved to the n-level of the tree and are listed in 
the grid. The classes listed here can be found in the Configuration module. 
AVEVA™ Engineering

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Select the classes in the Inherited Element Types window. The selected classes are shown in the text box as 
displayed above. This creates the configuration to include the inherited types. 
Run the above created configuration. This lists the elements of type pump and also the included types as shown 
above.

Link Database Administration including Global Projects 
All Compare/Update Users require write access to a single Link World (CYMWRL). If this is not present when a 
Link function is performed then Compare/Update will return an error. Project administrators are recommended 
to use the following procedures: 
• Create a separate Design Reference Database (DB) in Admin, and ensure it is current on all MDBs for 
Compare/Update Users, and all Compare/Update Users can write to it. 
• Use Options to create a Link World in the new DB, or to merge links from an old link world (prior to 12.1 link 
worlds resided in Design DBs). 
• For a Global Project, create variant extracts of the Link World DB and make one extract primary at each 
location that needs to use Compare/Update. This will ensure the link data can be issued back to the parent 
DB and combined from the various locations with no claim conflicts. 
Links created prior to 12.1 reside in Design DBs. These must be migrated to Design Reference DBs in order to be 
used by Compare/Update in schematic and engineering modules. Project Administrators are recommended to 
use the following procedures: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1138
• If using extract DBs, then in PDMS DESIGN or Marine Outfitting, use the Extract Control form to select the 
existing CYMWRL and issue all changes. Repeat this step for all levels of extract DB. The next step should 
then be carried out in the parent DB. 
• In PDMS DESIGN or Marine Outfitting, use Options to merge links from the Design DB to a new Design 
Reference DB (e.g. by drag and drop). Then click on the Design DB and delete the old Links. 
• Refresh any extracts of the Design and Design Reference DBs. 
When working in an extract DB, issuing, flushing or dropping an element that has a referencing link will 
automatically include the link object. The reference array attribute INCFLU (include flush) returns any link 
elements that reference the current element. Flushing, issuing or dropping an element with referencing link 
objects will act on those link objects even if the element itself is not claimed or modified. The Extract Control 
form will display an "R" label on such elements. 
Integration UDA Definition 
The UDAs must be defined in LEXICON if the matching process is to use them for storing source Database (DB) 
references. For further information, refer to Instrumentation, Electrical and P&ID Elements Matching. 
The UDAs may be created in any UGROUP in a current dictionary DB. If used in conjunction with the specific 
module, it is suggested to include these UDAs in the same group. 
The Integration UDAs should be created as Indexed UDAs with the following details:

Note: 
In order to store the source DB references for elements already imported, re-run Compare/Update on the 
existing data, accept all rows even though there are no changes listed, and complete the Update Database 
process. 
Update Time Stamping 
Compare/Update automatically sets the date and time when an object is updated if it finds the UDA pmg-
UpDateTime with UDA type datetime defined for the destination element type. 
Reclassification of Tags 
If the classification of a tag has been changed in AVEVA Diagrams and/or AVEVA P&ID, the class URL has changed. 
On import of the schematic data into AVEVA Engineering the tag is reclassified to match the tag to the same class 
as the schematic and/or P&ID elements. 
Note: 
This functionality is enabled only when the override destination element option is enabled in configuration and 
the override destination element option is enabled in the Configuration Summary pane of the Manage 
Configurations & Groups window. 
Note: 
Updates are not allowed if the element does not belong to the hierarchy or is not a child of the owning element. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1140
In the Compare and Update module the software checks for any matched tags that have been reclassified (in 
Diagrams and/or P&ID). Any identified tags are displayed in the Compare/Update grid as Reclassified to Source 
type for example: Reclassified to: CentrifugalPump. 
Click to select the Accept checkbox on the highlight lines in the grid of the Compare/Update grid and click 
Update Destination, the Reclassify Tags window is displayed populated with the selected tags. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1141
Select one or all of the tags, once the reclassification is complete the Compare/Update grid is refreshed as 
shown below. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Compare and Update
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1142
Design in Context 
Design In Context allows you to view AVEVA AIM documents from within AVEVA Everything3D 2.1, AVEVA E3D 
Design 3.1, AVEVA Diagrams, AVEVA Engineering and AVEVA Marine 12.1.SP5 based on objects selected in these 
applications. Design In Context is able to search for and present data differently depending on what type of data 
is selected. For example, searching for documents related to an existing system, which is not yet modelled in the 
current model, but contains information useful to the designer. 
Accessing the Design In Context commands differs between AVEVA Everything3D 2.1, AVEVA E3D Design 3.1, 
AVEVA Diagrams, AVEVA Engineering and AVEVA Marine 12.1.SP5 but perform the same operations. 
Design in Context in AVEVA Everything3D 2.1

Dashboard Server Selection in Design in Context 
To use Design In Context you need to set up a server connection with AVEVA AIM Dashboard in order to access 
the data. To set up a server connection, click Add Server to display the Dashboard Server Selection window. 
Add New Server 
Click to add a new server 
connection. A new line is displayed 
with attribute fields. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Design in Context
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1146
You must enter the correct values into the attribute fields and then select the Default Server from the drop 
down list. 
Click Apply to accept the entry or Dismiss to close the window. 
When you have added a server, the Dashboard Server Selection window will display that the connection was 
successful. 
Multiple server connections can be added and the required server connection can be set as default by selecting 
from the Default Server drop down list. 
Delete a Server 
Highlight the server connection in 
the list and click to delete. 
Configure Design in Context 
You can configure the default settings for Design In Context by clicking Configure to display the Configuration 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Design in Context
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1147
window. 
If the Auto Tracking check box is selected, the current element is displayed in the Search field of the Associated 
Documents window and an automatic search will be performed and the results displayed. 
If the Auto Tracking check box is not selected, the current element is displayed in the Search field of the 
Associated Document window but no automatic search is performed. To perform a search you must click Search. 
Group Document by 
Allows you to change the way the results on the Associated Documents window are displayed, either by 
Document Type or by Tag. 
If Document Type is selected, all of the Tags are grouped and displayed in different groups for a particular 
Document Type. 
List View 
Thumbnail View 
When the documents are opened, the documents in each group will open as tabs in the same window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Design in Context
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1148
Each group of documents will open in it’s own window. 
If Tag is selected, all of the Document Types are grouped and displayed in a list. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Design in Context
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1149
List View 
Thumbnail View 
When the documents are opened, the documents will open as tabs in the same window.

mode by clicking either Recent Documents, Collections, Favourites or Restore from the Manage group on the 
AIM tab. 
Search in Design in Context 
The current item selected in the application will be displayed in the Search field. Click on 
 to list the

search is performed and the results displayed. 
The displayed view can be either a List View or a Thumbnail View based on the selection.

Collections in Design in Context 
You can create a user defined set of documents called Collections. Each collection can be named and a number 
of different documents can be saved within the collection. 
To add a new collection click Collections to display the Associated Documents window in collections mode. 
Click New Collection and enter a name in the displayed field. 
Save 
Click to save the new collection. 
The collection is added to the 
Collections list. 
Cancel 
Click to cancel the new collection 
and return to the previous screen. 
To add documents to an existing or a new collection, select the check box(es) preceding the document name in 
the List View or select the check box(es) located bottom left in the Thumbnail View. 
List View 
Thumbnail View 
Select either an existing collection from the drop down list or create a New Collection by entering a name into 
the field. 
After selecting an existing collection you have two options. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Design in Context
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1153
Save 
Click to save the selected 
documents to the selected 
collection. 
Cancel 
Click to cancel. The selected 
documents will not be added to the 
selected collection 
To create a new collection, you can either select New Set from the drop down list and click Save which will 
create a new collection named New Set or type a new name for the collection in the field and click Save. 
Clicking Cancel with return you back to the document list without creating a new collection. 
To rename a collection, select the collection and click 
. A text field with the name of the collection will be 
displayed. 
Type the new name for the collection in the text field and click 
. A confirmation message will be displayed 
showing the collection name has been updated. 
Clicking Cancel will return you back to the collection list without editing the collection name. 
To remove a collection from the collection list select the collection check box(es) and click 
. You will be asked 
to confirm the removal. 
You have two options. 
Delete Collection 
Click to delete the collection from 
the collection list. A confirmation 
that the collect has been deleted 
will be displayed. 
Cancel 
Clicking Cancel will return you back 
to the collection list without 
deleting the collection. 
Favourite Documents 
You can add documents to a favorite group by clicking 
 preceding the document name in the List View or 
located top right in the Thumbnail View.

List View 
Thumbnail View 
After clicking it’s colour will change to blue 
 to indicate that it has been added to Favourites. 
To remove a documents from Favourites, click the blue star in either the List View, Thumbnail View or the 
Favourites View. In the List View and Thumbnail View the star will revert back to grey. In the Favourites view 
the document will be removed. 
AVEVA AIM Dashboard 
The currently selected item in the explorer can be displayed in the AVEVA AIM Dashboard by clicking AVEVA 
AIM Dashboard. 
In AVEVA Everything3D™ 2.1/ AVEVA Everything3D™ 3.1 the AVEVA AIM Dashboard can also be displayed from 
the PowerWheel (Ctrl+Right Click).

Design in Context

Page 1155
ERM Integration - Browse 
Use the Design in Context functionality to view live data from AVEVA ERM directly from within AVEVA E3D Design 
based on the element selected in the application. Or use to view live data as pseudo attributes. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Design in Context
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1156
Note: 
Data can also be viewed for objects that have been created in AVEVA Engineering and then published to AVEVA 
ERM and also imported to AVEVA E3D Design using the Compare/Update functionality. For such objects, Design 
in Context uses the object's reference id, as it is known in AVEVA Engineering, to synchronize and display the 
data in AVEVA E3D Design. 
To view live data from AVEVA ERM in AVEVA E3D Design, the AVEVA ERM settings must be configured, refer to 
Dashboard Server Selection in Design in Context for further information. 
Associated Information 
The data displayed in the Associated Information window is delivered live from AVEVA ERM. 
Note: 
If the connection is down the Associated Information window is displayed blank. 
When only AVEVA ERM is Configured 
When only AVEVA ERM is configured the Associated Information window is displayed as follows populated with 
Material Information and Planning Information. 
When Both AVEVA ERM and AVEVA AIM are Configured 
When both AVEVA ERM and AVEVA AIM are configured, the Associated Information window is displayed as 
follows with Material Information and Planning Information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Design in Context
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1157
Design in Context Application Programming Interface 
This document lists the Design In Context Application Programming Interface (API) and its usage with c#. 
Test_connection 
Checks whether given URL is valid live DashBoard site. 
Syntax 
Public static bool Test_connection ( 
string URL, 
out string strMessage 
) 
Parameters 
URL 
The dashboard URL to be tested. 
strMessage 
When this method returns, contains the error 
message, if test fails otherwise, contains String.Empty. 
Return value 
true if success; otherwise, false. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Design in Context
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1158
Example 
string strMessage = string.Empty; 
if (Test_connection("http://inhyzvms8r09:16000/", out strMessage)) 
{ 
Console.WriteLine("Success - Test Connection"); 
} 
else 
{ 
Console.WriteLine("Failed - Test Connection. Error - {0}", strMessage); 
} 
GetJSONResponse_GET 
Gets JSON response string from given DashBoard site, using HTTP GET method. 
Syntax 
Public static bool GetJSONResponse_GET ( 
string strURL, 
string strQuery, 
out string strErrorMessage, 
out string strJSONReturn 
) 
Parameters 
strURL 
The dashboard URL to be queried with HTTP GET 
method. 
strQuery 
Query for HTTP GET method. 
strErrorMessage 
When this method returns, contains the error 
message, if fails otherwise, contains String.Empty. 
strJSONReturn 
When this method returns, contains the JSON 
response from DashBoard, if query gets response 
otherwise, contains String.Empty. 
Return value 
true if success; otherwise, false. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Design in Context
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1159
Example 
string strMessage = string.Empty; 
string strJSON = string.Empty; 
if (GetJSONResponse_GET( 
"http://inhyzvms8r09:16000/", 
"/api/v1/domain/items?fullId=IPE|905675", 
out strMessage, out strJSON)) 
{ 
Console.WriteLine("Returned JSON - {0}", strJSON); 
} 
else 
{ 
Console.WriteLine("Failed - Error - {0}", strMessage); 
}

string strURL, 
string strQuery, 
string payload, 
out string strErrorMessage,

) 
Parameters 
strURL 
The dashboard URL to be queried with HTTP POST 
method. 
strQuery

payload 
Header body for POST method. 
strErrorMessage 
When this method returns, contains the error 
message, if fails otherwise, contains String.Empty. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Design in Context
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1160
strJSONReturn 
When this method returns, contains the JSON 
response from DashBoard, if query gets response 
otherwise, contains String.Empty. 
Return value 
true if success; otherwise, false. 
Example 
string strMessage = string.Empty; 
string strJSON = string.Empty; 
if (GetJSONResponse_POST( 
"http://inhyzvms8r09:16000/", 
"/api/v1/domain/searches/run", 
"{\"criteria\":[{\"booleanOperator\":\"AND\",\"handle\":0,\"value\":\"J-9002A%\",\"secondValue\":nu
(& OTHER PHYSICAL) 
OBJECT\",\"secondValue\":null,\"valueOperator\":\"Equals\",\"bracket\":\"None\",\"listOfValues\":fa
out strMessage, out strJSON)) 
{ 
Console.WriteLine("Returned JSON - {0}", strJSON); 
} 
else 
{

}

Public static bool GetDataForTag ( 
string strURL, 
string strCriteria, 
string strObjectTypeHandle, 
out string strErrorMessage, 
out string strJSONReturn 
) 
AVEVA™ Engineering

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Parameters 
strURL

When this method returns, contains the error 
message, if fails otherwise, contains String.Empty. 
strJSONReturn 
When this method returns, contains the JSON 
response of data related to criteria from 
DashBoard, if tag has any data on DashBoard

string strMessage = string.Empty;

"http://inhyzvms8r09:16000/", 
"J-9002A", 
"LOGICAL (& OTHER PHYSICAL) OBJECT",

{

else

) 
Parameters

"http://inhyzvms8r09:16000/", 
"J-9002A", 
" LOGICAL (& OTHER PHYSICAL) OBJECT", 
true, 
out strMessage, out dtTable)) 
{

else

QuickDataSearchAll 
Checks whether there are any data / files on DashBoard site for given criteria and for given searchable object

Page 1171
Monitor 
The Monitor module controls your entry into the system which is normally done via the Interface. 
Access Rights 
This chapter describes what is happening at each stage of entering the application. It explains the way access to 
data and modules is controlled when the System Administrator sets up projects, users, teams and databases. 
Then it describes how you can use MONITOR commands to query information about access rights to databases 
and modules, and about who is using the application. 
Entering the Application 
When you start up the application, you must first give the name of the project you will be working on using the 
PROJECT command. You can query the projects available. Then you specify a user name and password by giving 
the USERNAME command. You can query the users, and the MDBs, available in the Project before they are set. 
Note: 
When working in non-graphics mode, it is recommended that you enter the USERNAME command on its own, 
and you will then be prompted to enter the username and password, which will not be echoed on the screen. Do 
not follow the command immediately by the username and password, as this may be a security risk. 
When you have entered the username and password correctly, you will be in MONITOR. 
You will be able to use the facilities described in this manual, or start up another module. 
Databases 
All data is stored in databases. To work on design data you must specify the name of a Multiple Database (MDB) 
before you enter another module. An MDB is a collection of databases with defined access rights. Specifying an 
MDB sets your access rights to the databases. 
A MDB can contain up to 1000 DBs, and any number of the 1000 DBs can be described as current and accessed 
at one time. Databases which cannot be accessed are described as non-current or deferred. The status of a DB 
can be changed to Current or Defer at any time. Refer to Current and Non-current Databases for further 
information. 
MONITOR checks that an MDB has been selected before it will allow you to enter another module (except 
ADMIN). 
Note: 
When you select an MDB and enter a design module, UNITS settings are taken from the first current catalog DB. 
Connection compatibility (COCO) tables in all the current catalog DBs are available to the designer. 
Current Working Units Initialization 
Current units are set on session start. Their configuration is dependent on the setting of the BUNIT and DUNIT 
attributes in the first UNIT element found in a catalogue database in the MDB. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Monitor
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1172
The value of the BUNI and DUNI are used for setting BORE and DISTANCE current working units. The BUNI value 
is applied to current BORE units (one of MM, INCH or FINCH) and the DUNI value is the current DISTANCE unit 
(any distance standard unit including MM, INCH or FINCH). 
The setting of DUNI also determines which of imperial or metric units are to be generally used. 
Some physical quantities will be initialized to use their database unit regardless of imperial or metric DUNI 
settings (e.g. angles, voltages, and current, time, power). 
The current working units of most other quantities are directly dependent on (for example, DERIVED from) the 
setting of the current distance unit (and sometimes current units of other quantities. For example, Area, Volume, 
Surface and Linear Density, Content, Temperature gradient. 
Some quantities have special initial imperial or metric settings applied which are dependent on whether the 
distance unit is imperial or metric 
• Mass will be kg or lb 
• Temperature will be degC or degF 
• Pressure will be pascal or PSI 
• Force will be newton or lbf 
• Density will be kg/m3 or lb/ft3 
Unit mass will be kg/distance or lb/distance 
Database Types 
A Plant project can contain the following types of database: 
System Databases 
• The SYSTEM database itself, which holds the access control data for the model data and modules. 
• The COMMS (Communications) database, which stores information about who is using which module and 
which model databases are available. 
• The MISC database which store inter-user messages, and inter-database macros. 
• In a Global project there will also be the Global database and a transaction database. 
Model Databases 
• The DESIGN database which contains all the design information for the project. 
• The CATALOG database where the project catalog and specifications are stored. 
• The PADD database which contains data about drawings produced by DRAW. 
• The PROPERTIES database which can contain material properties, and can be referenced by the catalog. 
• The DICTIONARY database which holds the definitions of user-defined attributes (UDAs), as set up in 
LEXICON. 
• The ISOD database holds spool drawings generated by SPOOL. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Monitor
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1173
Database Access Control 
Users have one of the following types of access to a DB: 
Read/Write 
You can change the data held in the DB. 
Read-only 
You can interrogate the contents of the DB (for 
example, by using ISODRAFT to produce an isometric), 
but you cannot alter it. 
None 
You have no access to the DB. 
Each database is set up with UPDATE, CONTROLLED or MULTIWRITE access rights, where: 
UPDATE 
The database can be accessed by many users with 
Read-only rights, but only one user with Read/Write 
right 
CONTROLLED 
Controlled by an external system. 
MULTIWRITE 
The database can be accessed by many users with 
Read/Write rights. 
You can see the access rights to a database by using the LIST DB command. 
Foreign Databases 
Foreign databases are databases which have been included from a different project. This is often used for 
Catalog databases, so that many users can have Read access to the same database without needing multiple 
copies. For example, in the Sample project supplied with the product, there are several databases shared with 
the Master project. Foreign databases are listed as such when databases are queried. Refer to QUERY for further 
information. 
Teams 
A team is a user or a group of users who have read-write access to a database (and read access to several other 
databases). The database must be named as: 
teamid/identifier 
teamid 
is the name of the team which is given full Read/Write 
access to the DB 
identifier 
is the name of the DB. This is a normal Plant name 
beginning with a slash (/). 
For example, the PIPING team would have read-write access to design databases containing piping design, the 
STRUCTURES team would have read-write access to databases containing steelwork design, but they would both 
have read-only access to a catalog database. So, the MDB for the PIPING team could contain the following 
databases (and normally several others): 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Monitor
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1174
PIPING/PIPING-A 
A Design database PIPING-A, to which the PIPING 
team will have read/write access. 
MASTER/CATALOG-1 
A Catalog database CATALOG-1, to which the PIPING 
team will have read-only access. 
Access Rights of Users to Databases 
Each user has a level of access assigned by the System Administrator: 
• Free users have read-write access to all databases. 
• General users have read-write access to databases owned by their team in the current MDB, and read-only 
access to other copies of databases in the current MDB. 
Module Access Control 
Any module can be classified as Free, or Restricted. Whether a user has access to a module will depend on the 
user’s access rights and the access classification of the module, as follows: 
• Only a Free user may use a Restricted module 
• A General user may use Free modules 
In the system configuration as supplied, only ADMIN and LEXICON are Restricted. All the other modules are Free. 
Using MONITOR 
This chapter is an overview of MONITOR commands. They are mainly used to select databases and modules, to 
query information about the system status, users and databases, and to communicate with other users. 
Refer to Command Summary for further information. 
Changing Your Password 
You can change your password from that allocated to you by the System Administrator. The command PASS is 
followed by a slash and then the new password, consisting of up to fifteen characters, excluding I’@$/*". 
User Status 
The STATUS command outputs information about your current status and the databases to which you have 
access. Refer to STATUS for a detailed description of the status command. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Monitor
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 1175
System Status 
The SYSTAT (System Status) command outputs information about the current active status of the project. It lists 
all users who are accessing the project at that time, the modules and databases which they are using, and 
whether they are examining (Read-only status) or modifying (Read/Write status) the database. It also gives the 
unique identifier of each user. Refer to SYSTAT for a detailed description of the SYSTAT command. 
Setting the Current Team 
The SET command sets the specified team as the current one. Once a team has been set, DBs owned by that 
team can be referred to by using the name part only. For example, after the command 
SET TEAM SAMPLE 
You could give the command 
Q DB /DESI 
Instead of the command 
Q DB SAMPLE/DESI 
Listing and Querying 
The LIST and QUERY commands can be used to display information about Users, Teams, MDBs and Databases. 
LIST outputs general information, and QUERY outputs information about a named item. Note that there are 
many other options in the QUERY command available in other modules; the ones described here are those 
available in MONITOR. 
The LIST command outputs information about: 
Users 
MDBs 
DBs 
Teams 
Copies of databases 
Modules 
LIST USERS 
An example of typical output from the command LIST USERS is shown below. It lists all the users in the project, 
with their access level (Free or General), and the teams they belong to:
